words.nativeDefinition: Unimpressive or mediocre (informal).
words.etymology: meh（うーん）が口語で「特に感動しない」ニュアンスを表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: （分野を）熟知している、本物のプロ

examples.label: B
examples.text: My tax account is always like, let me do some research and circle back. I'm like, do you really know your stuff?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 税理士がいつも「調べて折り返します」って言うんだ。本当に熟知してるの？

examples.label: C
examples.text: Goeys down Matt, he's still a newbie, so of course he doesn't know his stuff yet. Give him more time to find his feet and he'll be alright.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ジョーイはまだ新人だから、当然まだ熟知してない。足を見つけて落ち着く時間をあげれば大丈夫。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Have expertise in one's field.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 精通している；本当にわかっている

words.nativeDefinition: To be knowledgeable and skilled in one's field.
words.etymology: know one's stuff（自分の分野を知っている）が専門知識を持つ意味。

examples.label: B
examples.text: My professor said that I only need to tweak the wording here and there to make my report perfect. It ended up taking me roughly 12 hours.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 教授はレポートは言い回しを少し直せば完璧だって。結局12時間くらいかかったけど。

examples.label: C
examples.text: The game went down to the wire, but we ended up losing again. We're going to have to tweak our game plan.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 試合はギリギリまで競ったけど、また負けた。作戦を微調整しないと。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Modify something slightly.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 少し調整する

words.nativeDefinition: To make small adjustments to improve something.
words.etymology: tweak（微調整する）が細かい修正を表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Why are they trying to keep their relationship hush? Is it against company policy?
examples.japaneseTranslation: なんで交際を秘密にしようとしてるの？会社の規則違反？

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm planning on moving you to the next year. Everything's still up in the air though, so can you please keep it as harsh as for now?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 来年昇進させる予定なんだけど、まだ未定だから内緒にしてくれる？

examples.label: D
examples.text: s secret. you
examples.japaneseTranslation: 秘密の；内緒の

words.nativeDefinition: Kept secret; confidential.
words.etymology: hush（静かに）の重ねで、口外禁止の秘密を表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: His TED Talk really resonated with me. It made me realize that real happiness comes from within myself, not from other people.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼のTEDトーク、本当に心に響いた。本当の幸せは自分の中から来るんだって気づいた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Do you think my speech resonated with the crowd? It was off the cuff, but I think it was pretty good. If I do say so myself.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 僕のスピーチ、聴衆の心に響いたと思う？即興だったけど、悪くなかったと思う。

examples.label: D
examples.text: A pier of somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 心に響く；共感を呼ぶ

words.nativeDefinition: To evoke a strong feeling of understanding or connection.
words.etymology: resonate（共鳴する）が心に響く意味で使われる。

words.japaneseMeaning: どこまで話してたっけ？

examples.label: B
examples.text: Where were we? Oh yeah, we were talking about, you think being street smart is equally important as being book smart.
examples.japaneseTranslation: どこまで話してたっけ？ああ、ストリートスマートとブックスマートが同じくらい大事かって話。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Where were we? That's right. You were telling us about what your kids did over the weekend.
examples.japaneseTranslation: どこまで話してたっけ？そうだ、子供たちが週末何してたか教えてくれてたね。

examples.label: D
examples.text: What will we talk about?
examples.japaneseTranslation: どこまで話したっけ

words.nativeDefinition: Used when resuming a conversation after an interruption.
words.etymology: where were we（どこにいた？）が会話の続きを思い出す表現。

examples.label: B
examples.text: My go-to cafe bombed out the price of coffee by 50 cents, sad day.
examples.japaneseTranslation: いつも行くカフェがコーヒーの値段を50セント引き上げた。悲しい日だ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I need to bump up my GPA to at least 3.0 so I can apply for the Study of Broad Program.
examples.japaneseTranslation: GPAを最低3.0まで引き上げないと留学プログラムに応募できない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Increase something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 引き上げる；増やす

words.nativeDefinition: To increase something, such as a price or amount.
words.etymology: bump up（上に押し上げる）が値上げや増加を表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: （音・空気が）クリアな、さわやかな

examples.label: B
examples.text: I just bought these Sony headphones. The sound is so crisp and the bass is awesome. Definitely the gold standard for headphones.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このSonyヘッドホン買った。音がすごくクリアで低音も最高。ヘッドホンのゴールドスタンダードだ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Picture this, crisp air, beautiful smell, and crispness. I can't find a reason not to like winner.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 想像してみて、さわやかな空気、いい匂い、清々しさ。冬が嫌いな理由が見つからない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Clean and clear.
examples.japaneseTranslation: さっぱりした；明晰な

words.nativeDefinition: Clear, sharp, and fresh.
words.etymology: crisp（パリッとした）が音の明瞭さや空気の清々しさを表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜の）意味をなくす、本末転倒になる

examples.label: B
examples.text: Walking your customer's shoes is just a process to take stock out of what they need, so if you don't put anything in practice after doing it, it defeats the purpose.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 顧客の立場で考えるのは彼らのニーズを把握するプロセスだから、やった後に実践しなければ意味がない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: wearing a mask on your chin clearly defeats the purpose doesn't it?
examples.japaneseTranslation: マスクを顎に付けてるの、明らかに本末転倒じゃない？

examples.label: D
examples.text: Make something pointless.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 本末転倒になる；意味がなくなる

words.nativeDefinition: To make something pointless or counterproductive.
words.etymology: defeat the purpose（目的を打ち負かす）が本来の意味を無効にすること。

words.japaneseMeaning: （人を）混乱させる、戸惑わせる

examples.label: B
examples.text: The Australian accent threw me off at first, but now I find it the easiest to understand.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 最初はオーストラリア訛りに戸惑ったけど、今は一番わかりやすいと思う。

examples.label: C
examples.text: The interview was so weird, the question that threw me off the most was, how would you get this pandemic under control? I mean, if I knew the answer, I wouldn't be here.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 面接が変すぎた。一番戸惑った質問は「このパンデミックをどう収束させる？」だった。答えわかってたらここにいないよ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Conf magnesium based on cats. It contains calcium, vitamin C, sodium. Treat and herb pneumonia, vitamin C of sodium. Sin bladder is poisonous, freurib well. risks, moisturizing, calm power,
examples.japaneseTranslation: 混乱させる；惑わせる

words.nativeDefinition: To confuse or unsettle someone.
words.etymology: throw off（外す）が予想外のことで混乱させる意味。

words.headwordAudio: juicy/112A there's a fine line (between st.mp3

examples.label: B
examples.text: There's a fine line between genius and insanity. They are like two sides of the same coin.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 天才と狂気の境界線は微妙だ。表裏一体みたいなもの。

examples.label: C
examples.text: There's a fine line between being confident and thinking that you know everything under the sun.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 自信があることと、何でも知ってると思うことの境界線は微妙だ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: There's a small distinction between sending and sending.
examples.japaneseTranslation: わずかな違い；一線の差

words.nativeDefinition: There is only a small difference between two things.
words.etymology: fine line（細い線）が二つの状態の微妙な境界を表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Kubo aka the Japanese Messi scored a wonderful goal. It was magnificent.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 久保、別名日本のメッシが素晴らしいゴール決めた。壮観だった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This is my sacred place, aka my bedroom. It's pretty cool, isn't it?
examples.japaneseTranslation: ここが僕の聖域、別名寝室。かっこいいでしょ？

examples.label: D
examples.text: Also known as
examples.japaneseTranslation: 別名；いわゆる

words.nativeDefinition: Also known as; an alternative name.
words.etymology: a.k.a.（also known as＝別名）の略。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I thought about rewriting this part of my essay, but I think I'm going to leave it as is.
examples.japaneseTranslation: エッセイのこの部分書き直そうか考えたけど、そのままにすることにした。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Why are you so OCD? The report is fine as is. Let's not reinvent the wheel.
examples.japaneseTranslation: なんでそんな完璧主義なの？レポートはそのままで十分。車輪の再発明はやめよう。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Unchanged.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 現状のまま

words.nativeDefinition: Without any changes; as it currently is.
words.etymology: as is（あるがままに）が変更なしの状態を表す。

words.headword: have one's head in the clouds
words.headwordAudio: juicy/115A have one's head in the clouds.mp3
words.phonetic: hæv wənz hɛd ɪn ðə klaʊdz
words.japaneseMeaning: 夢見がちな、現実離れした

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'd love to discuss this with Emma, but she seems to have her head in the clouds right now. Maybe next time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: エマと議論したいんだけど、今は夢見がちみたい。また今度かな。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I always sense check Terry's work because he always seems to have his head in the clouds.
examples.japaneseTranslation: テリーの仕事はいつもセンスチェックする。いつも夢見がちに見えるから。

examples.label: D
examples.text: The Enfogist
examples.japaneseTranslation: 夢見がちな；現実離れした

words.nativeDefinition: To be unrealistic or daydreaming.
words.etymology: head in the clouds（頭が雲の中）が空想にふけっている比喩。

examples.label: B
examples.text: You should use Airbnb instead of hotels. You get more bang for the buck.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ホテルの代わりにAirbnb使った方がいいよ。コスパがいい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Which MacBook is the best bang for the buck? It's hard to choose because there are so many models.
examples.japaneseTranslation: どのMacBookが一番コスパいい？モデル多すぎて選びにくい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: value for money.
examples.japaneseTranslation: コスパがいい

words.nativeDefinition: Good value for the money spent.
words.etymology: bang for the buck（ドルの価値に見合うインパクト）が費用対効果を表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: My mom called me this morning and told me that I'm not allowed to go out and play till I finish my homework.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今朝母から電話があって、宿題終わるまで外で遊んじゃダメだって。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My boss is always breathing out my neck. I wish he would stop it because it makes me feel cornered.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 上司がいつも後ろから監視してる。やめてほしい。追い詰められた気分になる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: For somebody into a situation that they can't escape from.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 追い詰める；逃げ場をなくす

words.nativeDefinition: To force someone into a situation they cannot escape.
words.etymology: corner（角）が比喩的に逃げ場のない状況に追い込む意味。

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜について）自分を責めすぎる

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm beating myself up a bit about dropping out of college, but what's done is done.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 大学中退のこと、少し自分を責めてる。でも済んだことは仕方ない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I have to stop beating myself up about things I can't control.
examples.japaneseTranslation: コントロールできないことで自分を責めるのはやめなきゃ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Criticize oneself or something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 自分を責める；後悔する

words.nativeDefinition: To criticize or blame oneself excessively.
words.etymology: beat oneself up（自分を殴る）が過度に自分を責める比喩。

words.japaneseMeaning: （人が）生まれる前の時代、知らない時代

examples.label: B
examples.text: This song was before my time, but it's great. I like it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この曲、僕の生まれる前の時代だけど、すごくいい。好き。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I heard there was a guy with the same name as me before my time here. Is that true?
examples.japaneseTranslation: ここに来る前に同じ名前の人がいたって聞いた。本当？

examples.label: D
examples.text: Before somebody was born, started working or living.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ～の時代より前

words.nativeDefinition: Before someone was born or started a particular role.
words.etymology: before one's time（自分の時代より前）が経験していない過去を表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: You should step back and seriously think about why you're working so hard to make big bucks for someone else and only getting peanuts.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 一度立ち止まって、なんで他人のために大金を稼ぐために必死なのか考えた方がいい。自分はピーナッツしかもらってないのに。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I know he's earning big bucks now, but I don't think it's fair to have him foot the bill. Let's just chip in.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼、今大金稼いでるのはわかるけど、全部払わせるのは公平じゃない。割り勘しよう。

examples.label: D
examples.text: A lot of money.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 大金；たくさんのお金

words.nativeDefinition: A large amount of money.
words.etymology: big（大きい）＋bucks（ドル）で大金を表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Can you jog my memory? I think it's a big name and F1 but I just can't remember who it is.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 思い出せないんだけど、F1の有名人だと思う。誰だっけ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Look at how many big names are involved in this project, KPMG, Deloitte, Accenture, so on and so forth, this will be an absolute slam dunk.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このプロジェクトに関わってる有名人の数見て。KPMG、デロイト、アクセンチュア…絶対大成功するよ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Famous version company or organization.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 大手；有名な

words.nativeDefinition: A famous or well-known person, brand, or company.
words.etymology: big name（大きな名前）が著名な人物や企業を表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I heard about this bar by word of mouth. It's a hidden gem that only locals know.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 口コミでこのバーを知った。地元の人しか知らない隠れた名店。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This is a great product in and of itself, so I think it will spread by word of mouth pretty naturally.
examples.japaneseTranslation: これ自体すごくいい商品だから、口コミで自然に広がると思う。

examples.label: D
examples.text: because the people talking about it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 口コミで；評判で

words.nativeDefinition: Through people talking to each other, not through advertising.
words.etymology: word of mouth（口から口へ）が口コミによる広がりを表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: （人の）言い訳をするとすれば、彼女の身を挺して言えば

examples.label: B
examples.text: Sorry for scuffing your shoes but my defense you shouldn't wear expensive stuff at work.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 靴を傷つけてごめん。でも言い訳をするとすれば、職場で高いもの履くべきじゃない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I heard Shannon yelled at you yesterday and heard a fan just completely out of character. Maybe something's up with her.
examples.japaneseTranslation: シャノンが昨日怒鳴ったって聞いた。彼女らしくない。何かあったのかも。

examples.label: D
examples.text: and support somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人を）擁護して；言い訳をする

words.nativeDefinition: In defense of someone; speaking in their favor.
words.etymology: in one's defense（誰かの防御として）が擁護の意味。

words.japaneseMeaning: （予定に）押し込む、時間を作る

examples.label: B
examples.text: My toothache is killing me, but my dentist can't squeeze me until next week. I'll just have to soldier on.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 歯が痛くて死にそう。でも歯医者は来週まで時間作ってくれない。耐えるしかない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I try to squeeze in a workout before work every morning, but sometimes things just don't go according to plan.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 毎朝仕事前にワークアウトを押し込もうとしてるけど、計画通りにいかないこともある。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Make time for something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 時間を作る；ねじ込む

words.nativeDefinition: To find time for something in a busy schedule.
words.etymology: squeeze in（押し込む）が忙しい中で時間を確保する意味。

examples.label: B
examples.text: If you're paranoid about getting in a car accident, just don't drive a car, problem solved.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 交通事故が怖いなら、車に乗らなければいい。問題解決。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Oh no, I got zero on my test again. My mom would kill me if I showed this to her, so I'm going to sweep it out of the carpet for now. Problem solved.
examples.japaneseTranslation: テストまた0点。母に見せたら殺される。とりあえず隠しておこう。問題解決。

examples.label: D
examples.text: The issue has been resolved.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 解決した；問題なし

words.nativeDefinition: Said when a problem has been easily resolved.
words.etymology: problem solved（問題解決）が手っ取り早い解決を表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: 包括的な、誰もを受け入れる

examples.label: B
examples.text: My colleagues are trying to be more inclusive because they think I'm shy but the thing is I just don't like hanging out in big groups.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 同僚がもっと包括的になろうとしてるけど、僕は shy なんじゃなくて大人数が嫌いなだけ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My friends are all pretty inclusive, so don't worry.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 友達はみんな包括的だから、心配しないで。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Proactively friendly.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 包括的な；誰も排除しない

words.nativeDefinition: Including and welcoming all types of people.
words.etymology: include（含む）から、誰もを受け入れる姿勢を表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: シャウトアウト、公の感謝

examples.label: B
examples.text: A big shout out to everyone about this book.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この本に関わったみんなに大きなシャウトアウト。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My girlfriend got a shout out from her favorite actor last night on his YouTube live and she was fan-growing so hard.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼女、昨夜お気に入りの俳優のYouTubeライブでシャウトアウトもらって、めちゃくちゃ興奮してた。

examples.label: D
examples.text: credit mention
examples.japaneseTranslation: 紹介；名前を挙げる

words.nativeDefinition: A public acknowledgment or mention of praise.
words.etymology: shout out（叫びかける）が公に称賛する意味。

words.japaneseMeaning: 人間の本性、人間らしさ

examples.label: B
examples.text: Don't feel bad. It's only human nature to forget things. Let me give you a quick refresher.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 落ち込まないで。物忘れするのは人間の本性だよ。簡単におさらいさせて。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Do you think human nature is fundamentally good or evil?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 人間の本性は根本的に善なのか悪なのか、どう思う？

examples.label: D
examples.text: innate human behavior.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 人間の本性

words.nativeDefinition: Natural human behavior or tendencies.
words.etymology: human（人間）＋nature（本性）で、人間の自然な性向を表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Did you know that Louis Vuitton's signature monogram was actually designed by the son of Louis Vuitton?
examples.japaneseTranslation: ルイ・ヴィトンの代名詞モノグラム、実はルイ・ヴィトンの息子がデザインしたの知ってた？

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm gonna order this travel nookie. It's their signature dish.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このトラベルヌードルを注文する。看板メニューなんだ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Most well now.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 代表的な；お馴染みの

words.nativeDefinition: Characteristic or distinctive of a person or brand.
words.etymology: signature（署名）が特徴的なもの・代名詞を表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: （会話に）口を挟む、一言加える

examples.label: B
examples.text: I can't just chime in when I don't even know what you've been talking about.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 何の話してたかもわからないのに口挟めないよ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I won't remember everything I want to say so just chime in if I miss anything during the meeting.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 言いたいこと全部覚えてないから、会議中に漏れたら口を挟んで。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Break into a conversation.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 口を挟む；割り込んで言う

words.nativeDefinition: To join a conversation with a comment.
words.etymology: chime in（鐘が鳴る）が会話に加わる意味。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I actually think this film is overrated. I don't know why there's so much hype around it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この映画、過大評価だと思う。なんでこんなに話題になってるのかわからない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I really want to go to this bar, but there's just way too much hype around it. I'll wait until it's less crazy.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このバー行きたいけど、話題性が高すぎる。落ち着いてから行こう。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Excitement and attention.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 盛り上がり；話題性

words.nativeDefinition: Excitement and publicity surrounding something.
words.etymology: hype（大宣伝）が過剰な期待や話題性を表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: The new manager has raised the standard of this club and brought out the best in every player.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 新マネージャーがクラブの基準を引き上げて、全選手のベストを引き出した。

examples.label: C
examples.text: If online English conversation lessons were incorporated into compulsory education, it definitely raises the standards of English in this country.
examples.japaneseTranslation: オンライン英会話を義務教育に組み込めば、この国の英語の基準は確実に引き上がる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: and crease the overall quality level.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 基準を上げる

words.nativeDefinition: To improve the overall quality level.
words.etymology: raise the standard（基準を上げる）が品質水準の向上を表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: $200 for a t-shirt? That's so expensive. I would spend $50 tops because they wear out pretty quickly.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Tシャツ200ドル？高すぎ。せいぜい50ドル。すぐダメになるし。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My iPhone 6 battery is on the way out. It lasts two hours tops. I think my MacBook is going to kick the bucket soon too. Give me a break.
examples.japaneseTranslation: iPhone 6のバッテリー、せいぜい2時間持つ。MacBookもそろそろダメかも。勘弁して。

examples.label: D
examples.text: the most
examples.japaneseTranslation: 一番；最高の

words.nativeDefinition: At most; no more than.
words.etymology: tops（頂点）が口語で「最大でも」という上限を表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: ちょっと聞いて、注目して

examples.label: B
examples.text: Can I get you guys to listen up for a second? I know you'll want to just get out there and kick the ball But there's some safety stuff we need to go through first
examples.japaneseTranslation: ちょっと聞いてくれる？すぐ外に出てボール蹴りたいのはわかるけど、先に安全の話をしなきゃ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I need you to listen up here. This course is geared towards beginners so intermediate and advanced students shouldn't be taking this course, okay?
examples.japaneseTranslation: ここ聞いて。このコースは初心者向けだから、中級以上は受けないでね。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Pay attention.
examples.japaneseTranslation: よく聞け；注目して

words.nativeDefinition: Used to command attention before speaking.
words.etymology: listen up（聞き上げろ）が注意を引く呼びかけ。

words.japaneseMeaning: 読みにくい、予測しにくい

examples.label: B
examples.text: I am so hard to read, she texted me every day, but whenever I ask her out on a date, she rejects me.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 僕、すごく読みにくい人間だ。毎日メッセージくるのに、デート誘うと断られる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My boss keeps me on my toes because he's really hard to read.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 上司は読みにくいから、常に気を引き締めてる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Difficult to understand.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 読み取りにくい；わかりにくい

words.nativeDefinition: Difficult to understand or predict.
words.etymology: hard to read（読みにくい）が人の気持ちや意図がわかりにくい意味。

words.japaneseMeaning: 偏見のない、オープンマインドな

examples.label: B
examples.text: You think Kylie is an open-minded person? She didn't say anything when I thought I was going to get a huge Mickey Mouse tattoo on my stomach. So I guess so.
examples.japaneseTranslation: カイリーはオープンマインドな人だと思う？お腹に巨大ミッキーマウスのタトゥー入れようとしても何も言わなかったし。

examples.label: C
examples.text: The reason he's so successful is because he's really open-minded. He really listens when people speak.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼が成功してる理由はオープンマインドだから。人の話を本当に聞く。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Unpurgidness and receptive to new ideas.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 偏見のない；心が広い

words.nativeDefinition: Willing to consider new ideas and opinions.
words.etymology: open-minded（心が開いている）が柔軟な思考を表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜を）シャットアウトする、無視する

examples.label: B
examples.text: If you want to make a career out of being an influencer, you'll have to get better at blocking out all the haters.
examples.japaneseTranslation: インフルエンサーとして生計立てたいなら、ヘイター全部シャットアウトするの上手くならないと。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Once the best way to block out all these distractions, I really need to study for next week's exam.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この邪魔全部シャットアウトする最善の方法は何？来週の試験勉強しなきゃ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Ignore something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 無視する；遮る

words.nativeDefinition: To ignore or shut out distractions.
words.etymology: block out（遮断する）が邪魔を排除する意味。

words.japaneseMeaning: 年齢がバレる、世代がわかる

examples.label: B
examples.text: I know using too many emoji shows my age, but I just can't help myself.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 絵文字使いすぎると年齢バレるのはわかってるけど、止められない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I showed my age today by saying Pokémon Red was the first game I ever played.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今日、ポケモン赤が初めてプレイしたゲームだって言って年齢バレしちゃった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Review how old somebody is.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 年齢がわかる；老けて見える

words.nativeDefinition: To reveal how old someone is.
words.etymology: show one's age（年齢を示す）が世代を露呈する意味。

words.japaneseMeaning: 負けず嫌い、負けて悔しがる人

examples.label: B
examples.text: He just logs out every time it looks like he's about to lose. Such a sore loser.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 負けそうになると毎回ログアウトする。本当に負けず嫌い。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Paul is the biggest sword loser. I thought it would be water under their bridge by now, but he still isn't talking to me because I beat him at Table 10 last week.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ポールは最大の負けず嫌い。先週テーブルテニスで負けたからまだ話してくれない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Version gets upset when they lose.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 負け惜しみが激しい人

words.nativeDefinition: Someone who becomes upset when they lose.
words.etymology: sore（痛い）＋loser（負け者）で、負けを認められない人。

examples.label: B
examples.text: You got some questions? Give me a second to get my notebook. Okay, I'm ready fire away.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 質問ある？ノート取るからちょっと待って。よし、どうぞ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Did you say you had something to tell me far away?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 何か言いたいことがあるって？どうぞ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Go ahead.
examples.japaneseTranslation: どうぞ；遠慮なく

words.nativeDefinition: Go ahead; start asking or saying something.
words.etymology: fire away（撃て）が「遠慮なく始めて」という許可の表現。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I've been hooked on this manga for the last few weeks. It's so bad it's good.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ここ数週間この漫画にハマってる。ダメなほど面白い。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I started getting cabin fever, so I went for a walk and started listening to the podcast you sent me. I'm absolutely hooked on it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 閉じこもり症候群になって散歩したら、送ってくれたポッドキャスト聴き始めた。完全にハマってる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I take you to something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ハマっている；中毒になっている

words.nativeDefinition: Addicted to or very enthusiastic about something.
words.etymology: hooked（釣り針にかかった）が比喩的に夢中を表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: This key to a part of the planned Google Maps says that we're going the wrong way.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Googleマップによると逆方向に行ってる。計画の一部？

examples.label: C
examples.text: Was the surprise birthday cake part of the plan or did the restaurant just organize it?
examples.japaneseTranslation: サプライズバースデーケーキ、計画の一部？それともレストランが勝手に？

examples.label: D
examples.text: intentional.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 計画の一部；意図的な

words.nativeDefinition: Intentionally part of a plan.
words.etymology: part of the plan（計画の一部）が意図的な要素を表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: attendance is looking a bit touching go. I hope today's workshop doesn't get called off.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 参加者数が微妙だ。今日のワークショップ中止にならないか心配。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Let's call off the picnic. The weather looks like it's going to be terrible and I hate picnic anyway.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ピクニック中止にしよう。天気最悪だし、ピクニック自体嫌いだし。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Can't do something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 中止する

words.nativeDefinition: To cancel something that was planned.
words.etymology: call off（呼び止める）が予定を取りやめる意味。

words.japaneseMeaning: 準備できてるよ、いつでも

examples.label: B
examples.text: Let me know when you want me to give you a call. I'm ready when you are.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 電話かけてほしいタイミング教えて。いつでも準備できてる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Ready when you are, so give me a shout when you want to leave.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 準備できてるから、出発したくなったら声かけて。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I'm ready now.
examples.japaneseTranslation: いつでも準備OK

words.nativeDefinition: I'm prepared and waiting for you to be ready.
words.etymology: ready when you are（あなたの準備ができたら準備OK）が待機の意思表示。

examples.label: B
examples.text: You have an interesting story to tell me? Okay, okay, I'm all ears
examples.japaneseTranslation: 面白い話あるの？オッケーオッケー、聞いてるよ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You'll finish work around sixish on Friday. Ok, ok, give me a text when you finish so I can come and pick you up. We'll play it by ear from there.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 金曜6時頃仕事終わる？了解。終わったら連絡して迎えに行く。あとはその場のノリで。

examples.label: D
examples.text: OK.
examples.japaneseTranslation: オッケー；わかった

words.nativeDefinition: OK; all right (informal, playful).
words.etymology: OK の冗談めいた言い換え okey-dokey。

words.japaneseMeaning: なぜか、理由はわからないけど

examples.label: B
examples.text: Thomas planning to go on a vacation in New Zealand next week, but for whatever reason he did a 180 about the trip.
examples.japaneseTranslation: トーマス来週ニュージーランド旅行の予定だったのに、なぜか180度考えを変えた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: For whatever reason, Boris has become the poster boy for bad haircuts, I think he looks great.
examples.japaneseTranslation: なぜかボリスがダサい髪型の代表になっちゃった。僕はかっこいいと思うけど。

examples.label: D

examples.japaneseTranslation: なぜか；理由はともかく

words.nativeDefinition: For an unknown or unexplained reason.
words.etymology: for whatever reason（どんな理由であれ）が原因不明を表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜だと思った、〜だろうと考えた

examples.label: B
examples.text: Don't be sorry about ignoring my messages. I figured you were busy.
examples.japaneseTranslation: メッセージ無視してごめん。忙しいんだろうと思った。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I've never read a book by Hurricane Mara Commu before, so I figured it was about time to pick one up.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ハリケー・マラコミュの本読んだことないから、そろそろ読もうと思った。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I thought.
examples.japaneseTranslation: だと思った；思った通り

words.nativeDefinition: I thought or assumed (based on reasoning).
words.etymology: I figured（推測した）が推測に基づく判断を表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Korean stuff is all the rage at the moment. I don't understand what the fuss is all about.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 韓国ものが今大流行。なぜそんなに騒がれてるのかわからない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Cryptos are all the rage right now, but I think that all the low hanging fruit is gone.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 暗号通貨が今大流行だけど、簡単に取れる果実はもうないと思う。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Very popular and trendy.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 大流行の；巷で話題の

words.nativeDefinition: Very popular and fashionable right now.
words.etymology: all the rage（全ての怒り→熱狂）が大流行を表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: 釣りタイトル、クリックベイト

examples.label: B
examples.text: On Never Visit This News website again, it's just for the clickbait articles.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このニュースサイト二度と行かない。釣り記事ばっかり。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I thought this video was legit, but it was just clickbait.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 本物かと思ったら、ただのクリックベイトだった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Attention seeking deceptive, Attention seeking and deceptive things.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 釣りタイトル；煽り系

words.nativeDefinition: Online content designed to attract clicks through sensationalism.
words.etymology: click（クリック）＋bait（餌）で、クリックを釣るコンテンツ。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Let's say for argument sake, you have to choose between having a rich head or poor intelligent person as your boyfriend. Which would you choose?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 仮に選ばなきゃいけないとして、金持ちのバカか貧しい賢い人、どっちが彼氏？

examples.label: C
examples.text: Man, Joanne's such an airhead. She forgot to include the attachment again.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ジョアン、本当に天然。また添付ファイル忘れてた。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Stupid person.
examples.japaneseTranslation: お空上人；頭がスカスカな人

words.nativeDefinition: A silly or unintelligent person.
words.etymology: air（空気）＋head（頭）で、中身のない頭を比喩。

words.headwordAudio: juicy/151A in the middle of nowhere.mp3
words.japaneseMeaning: ど真ん中のどこにもない場所、僻地

examples.label: B
examples.text: I kind of live in the middle of nowhere, but I'm a lot happier now because I have so much more space than my old apartment in New York.
examples.japaneseTranslation: かなり僻地に住んでるけど、NYのアパートよりずっと広くて幸せ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I got so drunk that I ended up in the middle of nowhere with no idea how I got here. I'm never drinking Ticula again.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 酔い潰れてどこにもない場所にたどり着いた。ここまで来た経緯がわからない。テキーラ二度と飲まない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: in a very remote area.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 辺鄙な場所；ど真ん中のどこでもない場所

words.nativeDefinition: In a very remote or isolated location.
words.etymology: middle of nowhere（どこでもない真ん中）が人里離れた場所を表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Many smart people are late bloomers, for instance this science is putting a lot of hard work day in day out and you finally want to know about price at 97.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 多くの賢い人は大器晩成型。例えばこの科学者、毎日コツコツ努力して97歳でノーベル賞。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I work day and day outside I'll be able to retire early.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 毎日コツコツ働けば早期退職できる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Constantly.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 毎日のように；繰り返し

words.nativeDefinition: Every day without exception.
words.etymology: day in, day out（日々）が毎日繰り返すことを表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: 危ないところだった、ヒヤリハット

examples.label: B
examples.text: I just had a close call with a train. I'm still pretty rattled by it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 電車とのヒヤリハットがあった。まだビクビクしてる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Phew, how's it close call? We almost missed a plane.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 危ないところだった。飛行機乗り遅れるところだった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Narr Escape from Danger
examples.japaneseTranslation: 危なかった；ギリギリセーフ

words.nativeDefinition: A narrow escape from danger or disaster.
words.etymology: close call（近い呼び出し）が危うく事故になる状況を表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: The burger was good but four beef patties is a bit of an overkill. How could anyone finish this?
examples.japaneseTranslation: バーガーは美味しかったけど、ビーフパティ4枚はやりすぎ。誰が食べ切れるの？

examples.label: B

examples.text: You don't need a little bit of weight, you already skin and bones.

examples.label: B
examples.text: It's a bit of a stretch to say that what this company does is groundbreaking. There's so many other companies that do the same thing.

examples.text: On the face of it, he seems over the hill, but he could just be having a bad season.

examples.text: Have limited knowledge of current events.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 世間知らず；情報に疎い

words.nativeDefinition: To be unaware of current events or popular culture.
words.etymology: live under a rock（岩の下に住む）が情報に疎い比喩。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Online sales have grown by 66% from last year and now they're neck and neck with our brick and mortar stores. We might have finally reached a tipping point.
examples.japaneseTranslation: オンライン売上は去年比66%増で、実店舗と肩を並べるレベル。ついに転換点かも。

examples.label: C
examples.text: To take this thing out of lumped some payments for expensive products, our brick and mortist stores allow you to pay an installment interest free.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 高額商品の分割払い、実店舗なら無利息でできる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: physical
examples.japaneseTranslation: 実店舗の；物理的な

words.nativeDefinition: Physical stores as opposed to online businesses.
words.etymology: brick（レンガ）and mortar（モルタル）が物理的な建物の店舗。

words.japaneseMeaning: （偏見などを）助長する、強める

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm worried that your videos with Nick playing to the stereo type that Americans love to hog the spotlight.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ニックとの動画が「アメリカ人は注目を独占したがる」ステレオタイプを助長してるんじゃないか心配。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Using the fact that I'm quite pleasing to the fact that everybody thinks I'm shy.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 見た目が良いことを利用して、みんなが僕は shy だと思うの。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Reinforce something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 都合よく働く；拍車をかける

words.nativeDefinition: To contribute to or reinforce something, often negatively.
words.etymology: play into（〜に加わる）が偏見などを強める意味。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Hold up, can you run that idea by me again? I didn't quite get it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ちょっと待って、そのアイデアもう一度説明して。よくわからなかった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Hold up, my laptop is being a bit buggy. I'm going to have to restart it and start streaming. Sorry guys, I'll keep you posted about when we can restart.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 待って、ノートPCがバグってる。再起動して配信再開する。ごめん、再開したら連絡する。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Wait.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 待って；ちょっと待ち

words.nativeDefinition: Wait; used to pause or ask someone to stop.
words.etymology: hold up（持ち上げる）が「待て」の口語表現。

words.headwordAudio: juicy/186A not in sb's nature (to do).mp3

examples.label: B
examples.text: It's not in my nature to get upset over work stuff, but I was disappointed to not get promoted. I'll speak to my boss later, but I'm just going to get on with my work for now.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 仕事のことで upset になるのは性分じゃない。昇進できなくて残念だけど、後で上司と話す。今は仕事続ける。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I never make a fuss about small things, it's not in my nature.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 小さなことで騒ぐことはない。性分じゃない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: That's something somebody would do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ～の性格ではない

words.nativeDefinition: Not typical of someone's character or nature.
words.etymology: not in one's nature（性質にない）が本来の性格と合わないこと。

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜に）真剣に取り組む、努力する

examples.label: B
examples.text: Part 5 they started applying himself to his studies, but it might be too little too late. The only two months ago until his college entrance exam.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 最近勉強に真剣に取り組み始めたけど、手遅れかも。大学入試まであと2ヶ月。

examples.label: C
examples.text: What's most important in life is to apply yourself to things that you like rather than what other people want you to do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 人生で一番大事なのは、他人が望むことじゃなく、自分が好きなことに真剣に取り組むこと。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Make one self-work hard or something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 真剣に取り組む

words.nativeDefinition: To work hard and diligently at something.
words.etymology: apply oneself（自分を適用する）が努力を注ぐ意味。

examples.label: B
examples.text: When it comes to writing a business, I don't think there's such thing as a recipe for success. You just need to chip away at it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ビジネス書を書くことに成功の秘訣なんてないと思う。コツコツやるしかない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This team seems to have the recipe for success. They always find a way to win, even when they have a lot of players out of action.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このチームは成功の秘訣を持ってる。選手が欠けてても必ず勝つ方法を見つける。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Method 4 succeeding
examples.japaneseTranslation: 成功の秘訣

words.nativeDefinition: A proven method or formula for achieving success.
words.etymology: recipe（レシピ）が成功の方法論の比喩。

words.headwordAudio: juicy/189A a few minutes into sth.mp3

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm only a few minutes into this book and I'm already bored.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この本、数分読んだだけで退屈。

examples.label: C
examples.text: The main character died if you managed to move you. What the hell?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 主人公が数分で死んだ。何これ？

examples.label: D
examples.text: A few minutes after the start of something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 始まって数分後

words.nativeDefinition: A short time after something begins.
words.etymology: a few minutes into（数分経って）が開始直後を表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: 仕事をやり遂げる、任務を果たす

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'll get the job done so please don't micromanage me.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 仕事はやり遂げるから、マイクロマネジメントしないで。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Helim will get the job done if you take the time to coach her.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ヘレンはコーチさえすれば任務を果たす。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Complete the task.
examples.japaneseTranslation: やり遂げる；目的を達成する

words.nativeDefinition: To successfully complete a task.
words.etymology: get the job done（仕事を終える）が任務完了を表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: 無名の人、誰でもない人

examples.label: B
examples.text: I can't believe Troy is all over TV now. He was a nobody when I met him three weeks ago. Give her take.
examples.japaneseTranslation: トロイが今TV中だなんて信じられない。3週間前会ったときは無名だった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You know how the bartender treated us like nobody's? I think it's because of our outfits. How about we go there wearing suits next time? I'm game if you are.
examples.japaneseTranslation: バーテンダー、俺たちを誰でもない扱いした。服装のせいか。次はスーツ着て行こう。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Unknown or unimportant person.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 誰でもない人；無名の

words.nativeDefinition: An unknown or unimportant person.
words.etymology: nobody（誰でもない人）が無名・取るに足らない人。

words.headwordAudio: juicy/192A not feeling a hundred percent.mp3

examples.label: B
examples.text: Maybe you should try taking a few days off if you're not feeling a hundred percent.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 体調万全じゃないなら、数日休んだ方がいいよ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I haven't been feeling 100% for a few days now, but I should be fine by tomorrow, knock on wood.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 数日体調万全じゃない。明日には大丈夫なはず。 touch wood。

examples.label: D
examples.text: And well.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 体調が優れない

words.nativeDefinition: Not feeling completely well or healthy.
words.etymology: not feeling 100 percent（100%の気分じゃない）が体調不良。

examples.label: B
examples.text: This YouTuber has rubbed off on me so much that I've been studying all day to day. Not gonna lie, I hated it at first, but now I love it. Learning new things is fun.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このYouTuberの影響で毎日勉強してる。最初は嫌だったけど、今は好き。新しいことを学ぶの楽しい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Oh man, I'm starting to rub off on you since when did you like rock music?
examples.japaneseTranslation: お前、いつからロック好きになった？僕の影響受けてる？

examples.label: D
examples.text: influence somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 影響を与える

words.nativeDefinition: To influence someone, often unconsciously.
words.etymology: rub off on（擦り付けて）が影響が及ぶ比喩。

examples.label: B
examples.text: It's below the belt to post someone's private information on social media. You should think twice before you post something like this.
examples.japaneseTranslation: SNSに個人情報投稿するのは卑劣。そんなこと投稿する前によく考えて。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Think twice about buying from this site. It looks super sketchy to me.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このサイトから買う前によく考えて。すごく怪しい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Stop and reconsider.
examples.japaneseTranslation: よく考える；一度立ち止まる

words.nativeDefinition: To consider carefully before acting.
words.etymology: think twice（二回考える）が慎重に検討する意味。

examples.label: B
examples.text: You have a new boyfriend? That's news to me. You just broke up with your axe.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 新しい彼氏いるの？初耳。元カレと別れたばっかりじゃない？

examples.label: C
examples.text: You can speak Chinese, that's Nizumi.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 中国語話せるの？知らなかった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I didn't know that.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 初耳だ；知らなかった

words.nativeDefinition: I didn't know that; that's new information to me.
words.etymology: that's news to me（それは私にとってニュース）が初耳の意味。

words.japaneseMeaning: 口外しない、秘密は守る

examples.label: B
examples.text: Don't worry, I won't tell anybody that you were watching Netflix at work all day today. My lips are always sealed.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 心配しないで、今日仕事中Netflix見てたこと誰にも言わない。口は固いよ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: What do you have in your mind? Just tell me. My lips are sealed.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 何考えてるの？教えて。口外しない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I promise not to tell your secret to anyone.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 口外しない；秘密を守る

words.nativeDefinition: I promise to keep a secret.
words.etymology: lips are sealed（唇は封じられている）が秘密保持の誓い。

words.japaneseMeaning: 不幸中の幸い、逆に良かったこと

examples.label: B
examples.text: I know being ghosted by a crush feels terrible, but I think this is a blessing in disguise. You don't have to waste any more time and you can finally move on with your life.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 片思いに ghost されたのは辛いけど、不幸中の幸いだと思う。もう時間無駄にしなくていい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: It was a blessing in disguise that the shirt was sold out in my size because now I don't have to spend money on it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: サイズ売り切れで買えなかったの、不幸中の幸い。お金使わなくて済んだ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Something that is actually positive.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 不幸中の幸い

words.nativeDefinition: Something that seems bad but turns out to be good.
words.etymology: blessing in disguise（変装した祝福）が見かけ倒しの幸運。

words.japaneseMeaning: あの頃が良かった、良き時代だった

examples.label: B
examples.text: Remember when a hamburger from McDonald's was around 6pm, those were the days.
examples.japaneseTranslation: マクドナルドのハンバーガー60円だった頃覚えてる？良き時代だった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: When I was a kid, my dad and I would watch Dragon Ball together. Those were the days.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 子供の頃、父と一緒にドラゴンボール見てた。良き時代だった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: That was such a great time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: あの頃が良かった；懐かしい

words.nativeDefinition: Said nostalgically about a happy past time.
words.etymology: those were the days（あれが良き日々だった）が懐かしさの表現。

words.japaneseMeaning: 独り言、考えながら口に出す

examples.label: B
examples.text: Hey buddy, if you don't like their videos, maybe just don't watch them. I don't know, just thinking I'll loud here.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 動画嫌いなら見なければいいんじゃない？わからない、独り言。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Thinking out loud, what's the point of perfume ads if you can't smell them?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 独り言だけど、香水のCMって匂いわからないのに意味ある？

examples.label: D
examples.text: Just verbalizing one's thoughts.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 独り言；思ったことをそのまま言う

words.nativeDefinition: Saying one's thoughts aloud without necessarily expecting a response.
words.etymology: thinking out loud（大声で考える）が独り言の表現。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I don't want kids right now, but I do want to start a family down the track.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今すぐ子供は欲しくないけど、将来的には家族を持ちたい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I took the plunge to quit my full-time job to focus on my startup. I hope my decision will pay off down the track.
examples.japaneseTranslation: フルタイム辞めてスタートアップに集中する決断をした。将来的に報われるといい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: in the future.
examples.japaneseTranslation: いずれ；将来的に

words.nativeDefinition: At some point in the future.
words.etymology: down the track（線路の先）が将来の比喩（主に豪英）。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Sorry, I know you pencil me in for lunch on Saturday, but can we push it back the next week?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 土曜ランチ仮押さえしてたけど、来週にずらせる？

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm not sure if I can make it yet, but can we pencil that in for now? I'll let you know once my schedule is set in stone.
examples.japaneseTranslation: まだ行けるかわからないけど、とりあえず仮押さえしていい？スケジュール確定したら連絡する。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Book something intendibly.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 仮予約する；予定に入れる

words.nativeDefinition: To schedule something tentatively.
words.etymology: pencil in（鉛筆で書く）が仮の予定を表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: （困難を）乗り越える、押し通す

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm going through a rough patch right now, but I just need to push through it for the next few weeks and things will get better.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今つらい時期だけど、あと数週間乗り越えれば良くなる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Having clear goals is to keep it pushed through obstacles.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 明確な目標があれば、障害を乗り越え続けられる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Progress through something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 乗り越える；押し進める

words.nativeDefinition: To continue through difficulty despite obstacles.
words.etymology: push through（押し通す）が障害を乗り越える意味。

words.japaneseMeaning: （自分を）さらけ出す、積極的に行動する

examples.label: B
examples.text: Don't be afraid to put yourself out there. If you go through life terrified of rejection, you'll miss out on so many opportunities.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 自分をさらけ出すのを恐れないで。拒絶を恐れて生きると、機会を逃し続ける。

examples.label: C
examples.text: If you want to become a people person, you only just step out of your comfort zone and put yourself out there.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 人当たりの良い人になりたいなら、コンフォートゾーンから出て積極的に行動しないと。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Be more sociable.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 積極的に出る；勇気を出す

words.nativeDefinition: To make oneself more visible or sociable.
words.etymology: put oneself out there（自分を外に出す）が積極的に行動する意味。

words.japaneseMeaning: ほどほどに、節度を持って

examples.label: B
examples.text: There's nothing wrong with stockpiling if done in moderation, but this is too much toilet paper. It's kind of like you're trying to build a casler or something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ほどほどにやれば備蓄に問題ないけど、トイレットペーパーこれは多すぎ。城作ってるみたい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I need to stop binging Netflix because I always lose track of time, everything and moderation.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Netflixの binge はやめないと。いつも時間を忘れる。何事もほどほどに。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Within a reasonable limit.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 適度に；ほどほどに

words.nativeDefinition: Within reasonable limits; not excessively.
words.etymology: in moderation（節度を持って）が適度な範囲を表す。

words.headword: have all the time in the world
words.headwordAudio: juicy/205A have all the time in the world.mp3
words.phonetic: hæv ɔl ðə taɪm ɪn ðə wərld
words.japaneseMeaning: 時間はいくらでもある

examples.label: B
examples.text: People tend to think they have all the time in the world, but life is very short.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 人は時間はいくらでもあると思いがちだけど、人生は短い。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm on summer break so I'll have all the time in the world but I have no idea what to do with it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 夏休みだから時間はいくらでもある。でも何すればいいかわからない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Have a lot of time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 時間はたっぷり

words.nativeDefinition: To have plenty of time available.
words.etymology: all the time in the world（世界中の時間）が時間に余裕がある比喩。

words.japaneseMeaning: 今度こそ完全に、完全に終わらせる

examples.label: B
examples.text: I could face book once and for all because I was so tired of seeing boomers lecturing young people.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Facebook完全にやめた。年寄りが若者説教するの見るのにうんざり。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Let's settle this once and for all, where the beetles are black-eyed peas the best band of all time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: これで完全に決着つけよう。ビートルズとブラック・アイド・ピース、どっちが史上最高のバンド？

examples.label: D
examples.text: Completely and finally.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 完全に；これっきりに

words.nativeDefinition: Completely and finally, so it won't happen again.
words.etymology: once and for all（一度で全て）が完全に解決することを表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Up on I need to be somewhere in half an hour but I can drop you off on the way.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 30分後どこか行く必要あるけど、途中で降ろしてあげられる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm heading to the mall now. Do you want to hop on?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今モール向かってる。一緒に乗る？

examples.label: D
examples.text: Get in.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 乗る；参加する

words.nativeDefinition: To get into a vehicle; to join someone going somewhere.
words.etymology: hop on（飛び乗る）が車などに乗る口語表現。

examples.label: B
examples.text: What happens to kids during childhood can stay with them in their adult years, so parents should be extra careful of what they say to them.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 子供時代の経験は大人になっても心に残る。親は言葉に気をつけるべき。

examples.label: C
examples.text: That picture of you eating a frog really gross me out. I can't see it. I think it's going to stay with me forever.
examples.japaneseTranslation: カエル食べてる写真、本当に気持ち悪かった。一生忘れられない。ずっと心に残る。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Remaining the memory of somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 心に残る

words.nativeDefinition: To remain in someone's memory or affect them long-term.
words.etymology: stay with（一緒にいる）が記憶や影響が残る意味。

words.headword: scrape the bottom of the barrel
words.headwordAudio: juicy/209A scrape the bottom of the barrel.mp3
words.phonetic: skreɪp ðə ˈbɑtəm əv ðə ˈbɛrəl
words.japaneseMeaning: （アイデアなどの）底をつかせる、使い古す

examples.label: B
examples.text: Hang on, didn't they have a similar show about a year ago? They're definitely scraping the bottom of the barrel for ideas.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 待って、1年前に似た番組なかった？アイデアの底をつかしてる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I can't believe this political party supporting a celebrity is a candidate again. They're really scraping the bottom of the barrel.
examples.japaneseTranslation: また有名人を候補に推すなんて。本当に底をつかしてる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Run out of good options.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 当たり障りのない選択肢がなくなる

words.nativeDefinition: To use the last remaining poor-quality options.
words.etymology: scrape the bottom of the barrel（樽の底をこする）が最後の手段を使い果たす比喩。

words.japaneseMeaning: （会話などを）始める、切り出す

examples.label: B
examples.text: I want to strike up a conversation with her, but I just get too nervous.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼女と会話切り出したいけど、緊張しすぎる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I struck up a great relationship with my brother and lost straight away, even though everybody else thinks he's an oddball. Does that make me weird?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 兄とすぐ仲良くなった。他のみんなは変人だと思うけど。僕も変？

examples.label: D
examples.text: ustoat's online properly!
examples.japaneseTranslation: 始める；切り出す

words.nativeDefinition: To begin a conversation or relationship.
words.etymology: strike up（始める）が会話や関係の開始を表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: （選択肢から）外れた、もう無理

examples.label: B
examples.text: Sorry, the offer is already off the table. Time is of the essence.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ごめん、オファーはもう無効。時間が重要。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I want to focus on my career now, so marriage is out to table for me at this point.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今はキャリアに集中したいから、結婚は選択肢から外れた。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Not an option.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 選択肢から外れる；不可能

words.nativeDefinition: No longer available as an option.
words.etymology: off the table（テーブルの上から外れた）が選択肢から除外された意味。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I've never taught Russian history before, but give me a few days to read up on it, should be a piece of cake.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ロシア史教えたことないけど、数日調べれば大丈夫。簡単なはず。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I've been reading up on a company that I'm going to do an interview with next week.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 来週面接する会社について調べてる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Research something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 調べる；勉強する

words.nativeDefinition: To research or study a topic.
words.etymology: read up on（読んで調べる）が情報収集を表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: バケットリスト、死ぬ前にやりたいこと

examples.label: B
examples.text: Going to a Beyonce concert is at the top of my bucket list, our tickets are really hard to score those so I wouldn't bet my house on it happening.

examples.label: C
examples.text: As it stands, the only thing I haven't checked off on my bucket list is space travel.

examples.label: B
examples.text: She was condoned and moving all her money to a Swiss bank account in it. Vanished and then air. How stupid is that?

examples.label: C
examples.text: I heard this guy conned people out of money. I can't prove it, but a lot of people have been talking about it. Where they're smoke, they're fire, right?

examples.label: D

words.headwordAudio: juicy/215A think the world of sb.mp3

examples.label: B

words.japaneseMeaning: エリート、最優秀な人材

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm having dinner with the best and brightest of silicon valley tonight. I have butterflies in my stomach.

examples.label: D

examples.label: B
examples.text: Check out our website for the latest and greatest information.

examples.label: C
examples.text: What's the point of getting the latest and greatest iPhone every time a new one comes out? They're not that different.

examples.label: B

examples.label: C
examples.text: I model is a side hustle, it makes me some money here and there, but it's not something I can do forever.

examples.label: B

words.japaneseMeaning: 意見を述べる、声を上げる

examples.label: B
examples.text: I mean, I'm not against people voicing their opinion, but I just wish they would listen a bit more.

examples.label: C
examples.text: I really voiced my opinions on social media because I don't want to dig myself in or hold by saying something dumb.

examples.label: D
examples.text: Express one's position.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 意見を述べる

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm off. We're going to pick up again next week so I'm going to relax while I can. Catch you later.

examples.label: C
examples.text: I don't understand why, but our financial performance is really picked up over the last couple months.

examples.label: D

words.japaneseMeaning: セカンドオピニオン、別の意見

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm sure oranges are good for your skin because they're rich in vitamin C, but you're welcome to get a second opinion.

examples.label: C
examples.text: I don't agree that Americans never say surely. I'm pretty sure I've heard someone use it. We need a second opinion on this
examples.japaneseTranslation: アメリカ人がsurely使わないって同意しない。聞いたことある。セカンドオピニオン必要。

examples.label: D

examples.japaneseTranslation: セカンドオピニオン；他の意見

words.etymology: second opinion（第二の意見）が別の専門家の見解。

examples.label: B
examples.text: The buzzer on my tweet is finally died down. I can't believe I got more than 10,000 likes in a couple of days.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ツイートの通知、ようやく収まった。数日で1万いいね超えたなんて。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This restaurant has been going gangbusters since opening last week. Look at that line, it's insane. I'm going to wait for it to die down a bit.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このレストラン、先週オープンから大盛況。あの行列見て。少し落ち着くまで待とう。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Become quieter!
examples.japaneseTranslation: 静まる；収まる

words.japaneseMeaning: カチンとくる、感情的に反応する

examples.label: B
examples.text: I got really triggered when I heard it was throwing shade at me behind my back, but I'll let it slide this time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 陰口叩かれてるって聞いて本当にカチンときた。今回は許す。

examples.label: C

examples.japaneseTranslation: ジェームズが体重いじり始めたとき、彼女かなりカチンときてた。卑劣な攻撃だから当然。

examples.label: D
examples.text: obset
examples.japaneseTranslation: ムカつく；過剰反応する

words.headwordAudio: juicy/225A be on one's best behavior.mp3

examples.label: B
examples.text: Why don't have to keep reminding you to be on your best behavior? You should know by now that you can't raise your voice at fancy restaurants.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 何度言えば良い子にするの？高級レストランで大声出しちゃダメなの、もうわかってるでしょ。

examples.label: C

examples.label: D

examples.label: B
examples.text: Alright kids, it's time for bed. You know the drill. Make your bed turn off your phone, switch off the lights.

examples.label: B

words.headwordAudio: juicy/228A go back a few years.mp3

examples.label: B
examples.text: If I go back a few years to when I first came to Japan, I only knew a couple of simple phrases. I've come such a long way.

examples.text: Go back a few years and you find that most people didn't even know what Siri was. Virtual assistants have come such a long way since then.

examples.label: D

words.japaneseMeaning: オープンブック、読みやすい人

examples.text: I'm such an open book, people can tell what I'm thinking just by looking at my face.

examples.label: C

examples.label: B

examples.label: C

words.japaneseMeaning: 当てにならない、フレーキー

examples.label: B
examples.text: My internet's being really flaked at everything. It's just laggy.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ネットがすごく不安定。ラグがひどい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: He never shows up when he says he will. He's so flaky.

examples.label: B
examples.text: Look at these mosquito bites. I usually bring bugs spray when I go camping, but it's slip my mind yesterday. That really taught me a lesson.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 蚊に刺されまくり。キャンプ行くとき虫除け持つの忘れた。良い教訓だった。

examples.japaneseTranslation: すべての失敗が貴重な教訓を教えてくれる。

examples.label: D

examples.japaneseTranslation: 教訓を与える

words.etymology: teach a lesson（教訓を教える）が経験から学ばせる意味。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Adam and I both work at the same company, but we don't really talk to each other that much because we work in different arenas.
examples.japaneseTranslation: アダムと同じ会社だけど、分野が違うからあまり話さない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Having a good understanding of mouth will come in handy in many arenas.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 数学の理解は多くの分野で役立つ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Field
examples.japaneseTranslation: 分野；舞台

examples.label: B
examples.text: You think I'm handsome? I get that a lot.

examples.label: C
examples.text: The color of your eyes are so beautiful. Sorry, do you get that a lot?

examples.label: B
examples.text: Getting exposure to new work is great in small doses, but I start feeling stressed out when it happens too often.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 新しい仕事を少量ならいいけど、頻繁だとストレス。

examples.label: C
examples.text: She takes a duct face selfie and posted on Instagram every day to fish for compliments. I can take it in small doses, but this is too much.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 毎日ド派手な自撮りインスタ投稿して褒め求めてる。少量なら我慢できるけど、これは多すぎ。

words.headword: have got a good thing going
words.headwordAudio: juicy/236A have got a good thing going.mp3
words.phonetic: hæv gɑt ə gʊd θɪŋ goʊɪŋ
words.japaneseMeaning: うまくいってる、良い関係／状況

examples.label: B
examples.text: Are you sure you want to break up with her? You got a good thing going here. It'd be a shame to straight over a stupid argument.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 本当に別れるの？ここうまくいってるのに。バカな口論で台無しにするのもったいない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I've got a good thing going in this company so I think I'm going to stick around for a while.

words.etymology: have a good thing going（良いことが進行中）が好調な状態。

words.japaneseMeaning: それは別の話、状況が変わる

examples.label: B
examples.text: Normally, I'd say paying somebody under the table to do something illegal isn't right. If it's to say somebody's life though, that's a different story.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 普通、裏金で違法なこと頼むのはダメ。でも命を救うなら別の話。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I love my dog, but when she eats my chicken nuggets, that's a different story.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 犬は好き。でもチキンナゲット食べられたら別の話。

examples.label: D
examples.text: That would change my opinion.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 話が違う；それは別問題

words.etymology: that's a different story（それは別の話）が条件次第で変わること。

examples.label: B
examples.text: And never cross my mind to publish this list of words as a book until a couple of years ago.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この単語リストを本にするなんて、数年前まで思いもよらなかった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Picking up Spanish is cross my mind, but I think I'm better off focusing on Italian for now. There's still a lot to learn.
examples.japaneseTranslation: スペイン語習おうかと思ったけど、イタリア語に集中した方がいい。まだ学ぶことが多い。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I encourage somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 思い浮かぶ

words.headwordAudio: juicy/239A need a bit of work.mp3

examples.label: B
examples.text: The garden at my new place still needs a bit of work. I'm scrambling to get it done before December because I'm planning to have a Christmas party here.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 新居の庭、まだ少し手入れが必要。12月前に終わらせたい。クリスマスパーティー予定だから。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My web design skills need a bit of work, so I'm going to pick Kate's brand about it later. She's a pro when it comes to this stuff.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ウェブデザインスキル、もう少し必要。後でケイトに相談する。プロだから。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Require some improvement.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 改善が必要

words.nativeDefinition: To require some improvement.
words.etymology: need a bit of work（少しの仕事が必要）が改善が必要な状態。

examples.label: B
examples.text: This economist says the Chinese economy might soon run out of steam but you never know.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この経済学者、中国経済はすぐ勢いが尽きるかもって。わからないけど。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I finished writing most of this book, but I'm starting to run out of steam. I'm going to put designing the cover on the back burner until after Christmas.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 本の大部分書き終えたけど、勢いが尽きてきた。表紙デザインはクリスマス後に回す。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Please move.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 息切れする；力が尽きる

words.nativeDefinition: To lose energy, momentum, or enthusiasm.
words.etymology: run out of steam（蒸気が尽きる）が勢いが失われる比喩。

examples.label: B
examples.text: You hate milk, right? Try this. You'll change your tune about it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 牛乳嫌いでしょ？これ試して。考え変わるよ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Why do you hate children so much? I bet you change your turn if you had kids.
examples.japaneseTranslation: なんで子供そんなに嫌いなの？自分の子供いたら考え変わると思うよ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Change one's opinion.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 口を改める；態度を変える

words.nativeDefinition: To change one's opinion about something.
words.etymology: change one's tune（調子を変える）が意見変更の比喩。

words.headwordAudio: juicy/242A carry a lot of weight.mp3
words.japaneseMeaning: （意見などが）重みがある、影響力がある

examples.label: B
examples.text: Make sure you attach reference letters to your resume. They carry a lot of weight in the assessment process.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 履歴書に推薦状添えて。評価プロセスで重みがある。

examples.label: C
examples.text: He shouldn't have made that comment as an influencer. He should have been more aware that his words carry a lot of weight.
examples.japaneseTranslation: インフルエンサーとしてあのコメントすべきじゃなかった。言葉の重みをもっと意識すべきだった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Be important or influential.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 重みがある；影響力がある

words.nativeDefinition: To be very important or influential.
words.etymology: carry weight（重みを持つ）が重要度や影響力を表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I mean, events that higher a new coach will put us back to square one. We're making good progress under Carlo and it's way too soon to make a call on him.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 新コーチ雇うイベントで最初からやり直しになる。カルロの下で順調に進んでる。判断するには早すぎる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm convinced that Steve Jobs would have created a lot more innovative iPhone models if he wasn't live.
examples.japaneseTranslation: スティーブ・ジョブズが生きてたら、もっと革新的なiPhone作ってたと確信してる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I'm certain.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 確信している

words.nativeDefinition: To be certain that something is true.

examples.label: B

examples.text: Stasis is always playing up our boyfriend has a heart of goal, but I've never actually seen him do anything nice for anybody.

words.headword: be on the tip of sb's tongue

examples.label: B
examples.text: Lucy's last name is on the tip of my tongue. It definitely starts with an A, but I just can't remember the rest of it.

examples.label: C
examples.text: Oh, I know this guy. What was his name? It's on the tip of my tongue, but I can't remember.

examples.label: B

examples.japaneseTranslation: 緊張やストレスのとき、口の中側を噛んでる自分に気づく。なんでだろう。

examples.label: D

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm not going to sink to your level and have a fight with you on the internet. Who are you anyway? Sorry, but I have a life.

examples.label: B

examples.label: C

words.japaneseMeaning: ポリティカルコレクトな

examples.label: B
examples.text: I think we're too politically correct these days. It feels like everybody's always policing each other's language.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 最近ポリティカルコレクトすぎる。みんないつも互いの言葉を監視してる気がする。

examples.label: C
examples.text: He keeps referring to Japanese people as jobs, but I don't think it's politically correct.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 日本人のことをJapsって呼び続けてる。ポリティカルコレクトじゃないと思う。

examples.japaneseTranslation: 配慮した言い回しの；政治的に正しい

examples.label: B
examples.text: I was so tired last night that I forgot to set my alarm. Good thing I woke up early.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昨夜すごく疲れてアラーム設定忘れた。良かった、早起きできた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Good thing you were there with me. I don't think I could have stood up to that rip scary guy by myself.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 一緒にいてくれて良かった。あの怖い男一人では立ち向かえなかった。

examples.label: D

words.japaneseMeaning: 少ない方がいい、シンプルイズベスト

examples.label: B
examples.text: Our school principal is always long-winded. I'm like just get straight at the point. She just doesn't seem to understand that less is more.

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm a big believer that less is more, which is why I only have a couch and a table in my apartment.

examples.label: D

words.japaneseMeaning: ゴーストタウンのように誰もいない

examples.label: B
examples.text: Costco is like a ghost down this morning. I think it might have a lot to do with the weather.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今朝のコストコ、ゴーストタウンみたい。天気のせいかも。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This restaurant becomes like a ghost town after Christmas when all the tourists leave.
examples.japaneseTranslation: クリスマス後に観光客が去ると、このレストランはゴーストタウンみたいになる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: deserted.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 人気のない；ガラガラの

words.nativeDefinition: Completely deserted with no people around.
words.etymology: ghost town（幽霊町）が人気のない場所の比喩。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I don't know why Connie randomly started dancing around the kitchen last night. Did it where you out as well?
examples.japaneseTranslation: コニーが昨夜キッチンで突然踊り始めたのなんで？君も気持ち悪かった？

examples.label: C
examples.text: Green man goes weird me out. I just expect them to always be yellow.

examples.label: D
examples.text: Make somebody feel uncomfortable.

words.etymology: weird out（変にする）が不快感を与えるスラング。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I've been wondering why Pete cut ties with me out of the blue, but I can't work it out. Do you think I should do with me spilling coffee or his favorite scarf?
examples.japaneseTranslation: ピートが突然縁を切った理由ずっと考えてる。わからない。コーヒーこぼした？好きなスカーフ？

examples.label: C
examples.text: I cut ties with Josh because he spilled the beans on the birthday surprise that I was planning for my girlfriend. I've been prepping for it for more than two months.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ジョシュとは縁を切った。彼女へのサプライズバースデーをバラした。2ヶ月以上準備してたのに。

examples.label: D
examples.text: and a relationship with somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 縁を切る；関係を断つ

words.headwordAudio: juicy/278A have it in for sb.mp3
words.japaneseMeaning: （人に）意地悪する、恨みを持っている

examples.label: B
examples.text: For my me again, why do you think Paul has it in for you? I was chatting to him last week and he made it sound like you guys are best friends.
examples.japaneseTranslation: また？なんでポールが意地悪してると思う？先週話したら親友みたいに言ってた。

examples.label: C

examples.japaneseTranslation: 怒鳴られるから意地悪してるわけじゃない。

examples.label: D

words.japaneseMeaning: フレネミー、敵か味方か

examples.label: B
examples.text: Ice cream is my friend of me, it takes so good but it makes me fat.

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm supposed to go to a college friend's party today, but I don't really want to. They're more of a friend of me anyway. What should I tell him?

examples.label: D
examples.text: person who pretends to be a friend but is actually an enemy.

words.japaneseMeaning: うっかり屋、ボーッとしてる

examples.label: B
examples.text: I've been pretty absent-minded lately, I hope it's not because of old age.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 最近かなりうっかり屋。加齢のせいかな。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm so absent minded. I spent 20 minutes looking for my phone. It was right under my nose.
examples.japaneseTranslation: すごいうっかり屋。20分スマホ探した。目の前にあった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: For Gap4 in Attentive.

words.japaneseMeaning: 想像力豊かすぎる、大げさな想像

examples.label: B
examples.text: I get really scared of the dark sometimes because I've an overactive imagination. I always think that there's something hiding in the shadows.

examples.label: C
examples.text: Does that cloud not look like dry-mond view? Must just be my overactive imagination.
examples.japaneseTranslation: あの雲、ドラゴンに見えない？僕の想像力豊かすぎか。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Imagination that exaggerates things.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 想像力が豊かすぎる

words.etymology: overactive（過剰に活発な）＋imagination（想像力）。

examples.label: B
examples.text: This comedian is doing a pilgrimage to parapherify his soul because he got cancelled. I don't think it's going to help much.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このコメディアン、キャンセルされて魂のパリナイ中。あまり効果なさそう。

examples.label: C
examples.text: His terrible sexist comments were the straw that broke the camel's back. I'm canceling him.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ひどい性差別コメントが最後の一押し。キャンセルする。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Boy, got somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ノーコンマンド；追放する

words.etymology: cancel（取り消す）がSNS時代の公開的な排斥。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Just because you tell everybody that you're an alpha male doesn't mean that you are, action speak louder than words.
examples.japaneseTranslation: アルファ男性だって誰にでも言うだけじゃ意味ない。行動が物語る。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Josh's smart, confident, courageous and adored by women. He's definitely an alpha.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ジョシュは賢く、自信があって、勇気があって、女性に慕われる。間違いなくアルファ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: dominant dominant person.

words.etymology: alpha（アルファ）が動物の社会性から転じた支配的な人。

words.japaneseMeaning: 出世する、コーポレートラダーを登る

examples.label: B
examples.text: I used to be obsessed with climbing the corporate ladder, but after my first son was born, I realized that there's more to life than my job.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 出世に夢中だったけど、長男が生まれて仕事以外にも大切なものがあると気づいた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My dad told me there's only two career options. Climb the cordu ladder or build your own business. I'm old over it for a few weeks and decided to quit my job.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 父はキャリアは2択だって。出世するか、自分のビジネスを作るか。数週間悩んで辞めた。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Progress at a company through promotions.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 出世する

words.japaneseMeaning: ベストプラクティス、最善の方法

examples.label: B
examples.text: It's best practice to watch your rice before you cook it, but I just can't be bothered.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 米を炊く前に洗うのがベストプラクティスだけど、面倒でやってない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You should watch this video if you want to learn about best practice for being minimalistic.

examples.label: D

words.japaneseMeaning: 形だけこなす、やらされ仕事

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm leaving next week so I'm just going through the motions right now. I don't care if my boss is happy or not anymore.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 来週辞めるから、今は形だけこなしてる。上司が喜ぶかどうかもうどうでもいい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Successful players never just go through the motions in practice.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 成功する選手は練習で形だけこなすことはない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Do you something without putting in much effort?

words.japaneseMeaning: シルバーフォックス、白髪のイケオジ

examples.label: B
examples.text: My current life goal is to become a sexy, well-spoken silver fox.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今の人生目標は、セクシーで話上手なシルバーフォックスになること。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I know you think that my dad looks like a real silver fox in this photo, but you'd be disappointed if you met him in person.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この写真の父、本物のシルバーフォックスに見えるって言うけど、会ったらがっかりするよ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Handsomolder man.
examples.japaneseTranslation: シルバーフォックス；イケオジ

words.etymology: silver（銀）＋fox（キツネ）が魅力的な高齢男性のスラング。

examples.label: B
examples.text: My mom almost threw away my favorite plush toy, not on my watch.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 母が好きなぬいぐるみ捨てようとした。僕が見てる限りはダメ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I will not allow racers for sex as comments on my watch.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 私が責任者の間、性差別コメントは許さない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Now, while somebody's in charge.

words.japaneseMeaning: バブルの中で生きている、世間知らず

examples.label: B
examples.text: I can't believe that they think there are no homeless people in their city. They really do live in a bubble.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ホームレスがいないと思ってるなんて信じられない。本当にバブルの中で生きてる。

examples.text: I traveled the world last year and I realized that I've been living in a bubble my whole life. It was a great wake-up call.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 去年世界旅行して、ずっとバブルの中で生きてたと気づいた。良い目覚めだった。

words.japaneseMeaning: 大きな靴を履く、高い期待

examples.label: B
examples.text: I took over my dad's company because he retired last year. They're pretty big shoes to fill, but I'm managing to keep my head above water so far.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 父が引退して会社引き継いだ。大きな靴を履くことだけど、今のところ水際で踏ん張ってる。

words.japaneseMeaning: リセットボタンを押す、やり直す

examples.label: B
examples.text: Lockdown sucks, but it gave me the opportunity to hit the reset button on many parts of my life.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ロックダウンは最悪だけど、人生の多くの部分でリセットボタンを押す機会になった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm feeling pretty overworked so I'm going to go on vacation and hit the reset button.
examples.japaneseTranslation: かなり働きすぎてるから、休暇行ってリセットボタン押す。

examples.label: D

examples.label: B
examples.text: Back to the future is the best. There's nothing like it. I watched it when I was a teenager and I blew my mind to me. It's the gold standard of sci-fi movies.
examples.japaneseTranslation: バック・トゥ・ザ・フューチャー最高。勝るものない。10代のとき見て衝撃。SF映画のゴールドスタンダード。

examples.label: C
examples.text: There's nothing like waking up the sound of the ocean and the smell of fresh coffee.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 海の音で目覚めて、新鮮なコーヒーの香り。勝るものない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Something is the best.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ～に勝るものはない

words.etymology: there's nothing like（〜のようなものはない）が最高の比較。

words.japaneseMeaning: 涙を誘うもの、ティアジャーカー

examples.label: B
examples.text: This film is such a tear-jerker, but I can't put my finger on why, maybe it's the music.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この映画、ティアジャーカー。理由は特定できない。音楽かも。

examples.label: C

examples.japaneseTranslation: この本、本当のティアジャーカー。読むたびに感情が動く。

examples.label: D

words.japaneseMeaning: 控えめに言っても、遠慮なく言えば

examples.label: B
examples.text: As performance so far has been to put it mildly disappointing.
examples.japaneseTranslation: これまでのパフォーマンスは、控えめに言ってもがっかり。

words.headwordAudio: juicy/295A put sb on a pedestal.mp3

examples.label: B
examples.text: Please don't put me on a pedestal. I'm just sharing my thoughts so I could be wrong.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 神格化しないで。ただ考えを共有してるだけ。間違ってるかも。

words.headwordAudio: juicy/296A not cut out to do.mp3

examples.label: B
examples.text: I don't think I'm cut out to be a surgeon. I freak out every time I see blood.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 外科医向いてないと思う。血見るたびパニック。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Just because I'm alone, well, doesn't mean I hate people, I just like spending time on my own.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 一匹狼だからって人嫌いじゃない。一人の時間が好きなだけ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: She's such a lone wolf, I don't think I've ever seen her have lunch with anybody.

words.headword: there's no such thing as a free
words.headwordAudio: juicy/298A there's no such thing as a free.mp3
words.phonetic: ðɛrz noʊ səʧ θɪŋ ɛz ə fri

examples.label: B
examples.text: You can get a free iPhone, no strings attached, that's definitely a scam. There's no such thing as a free lunch.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 無条件でiPhone無料？絶対詐欺。タダ飯はない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Success is not luck of the drop, but the result of hard work there's not such thing as a free lunch.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 成功は運任せじゃない。努力の結果。タダ飯はない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Nothing a value is free.

words.japaneseMeaning: ノーペイン・ノーゲイン

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm just going to be eating chicken and broccoli for the next three weeks so that I can fit in my suit for the wedding. No pain, no gain, I guess.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 結婚式のスーツに入るために3週間チキンとブロッコリーだけ。ノーペイン・ノーゲイン。

examples.label: C
examples.text: The high-guided on the week can absolutely wreck my knees, but the view of the end of it was incredible. Not paying no gain, right?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 週末のハイキング、膝ボロボロ。でも頂上の景色最高。ノーペイン・ノーゲイン。

examples.label: D

words.etymology: no pain no gain（痛みなしに得なし）が努力の必要性の格言。

words.headwordAudio: juicy/300A a small price to pay.mp3
words.japaneseMeaning: 小さな代償、許容できるコスト

examples.label: B
examples.text: Taxi rates are really high in Japan, but it's a small press to pay to live in this beautiful country.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 日本のタクシー料金高いけど、この美しい国に住む小さな代償。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I know my huge down jacket looks a bit ridiculous, but it's a small price to pay for keeping warm.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 巨大ダウンジャケットバカみたいに見えるけど、暖かさの小さな代償。

examples.label: D
