words.etymology: small price to pay（払う小さな代償）が許容できる犠牲。

words.japaneseMeaning: （地位などを）確固たるものにする

examples.label: B
examples.text: This movie cemented him as one of my favorite actors.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この映画で彼はお気に入りの俳優としての地位を確固たるものにした。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I think winning the gold medal has some energy pan status as a world's best baseball team.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 金メダル獲得で世界最高の野球チームとしての地位を確固たるものにした。

examples.label: D
examples.text: establish something firmly.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 固める；確立する

words.nativeDefinition: To establish something firmly in people's minds.
words.etymology: cement（セメントで固める）が地位や評価を固定する比喩。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Can I borrow car for a few hours? We've hit a snag and I think it'll be able to sort it out for us.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 車数時間貸してくれる？問題に直面して、彼が解決してくれると思う。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I was planning to travel to Australia next week but hit a snag with a visa application.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 来週オーストラリア旅行の予定だったけど、ビザ申請で問題に直面した。

examples.label: D
examples.text: encounter an unexpected problem.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 思わぬ障害にぶつかる

words.nativeDefinition: To encounter an unexpected problem or delay.
words.etymology: hit a snag（障害物にぶつかる）が思わぬ問題の比喩。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I never believe it when girls say they like me because I have deep rooted trust issues.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 女の子に好かれてるって言われても信じない。根深い信頼問題がある。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Gender bias is still deep rooted in this country, and most of the time it's unconscious.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この国ではジェンダーバイアスがまだ根深い。多くは無意識。

examples.label: D
examples.text: F rimlaying plற it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 根深い

words.nativeDefinition: Deeply ingrained and difficult to change.
words.etymology: deep-rooted（深く根付いた）が長年の固定観念を表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: それ自体としては、厳密には

examples.label: B
examples.text: I don't hate my job because the work that I do per se. It's more to do with the people I work with.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 仕事自体は嫌いじゃない。一緒に働く人の問題。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm not a dog person per se, I just really don't like cats.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 犬派というわけじゃない。猫が本当に嫌いなだけ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Exactly.
examples.japaneseTranslation: それ自体は；本来の意味では

words.nativeDefinition: By itself; in the strict sense.
words.etymology: per se（それ自体で）がラテン語由来の「厳密に言えば」。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I hate to shoot a good of what up a damn I wish I'd invested more in Bitcoin.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 後悔の念じ…ビットコインにもっと投資すればよかった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I wish I had saved up some of my money during college instead of spending it all on booze. Should I get a water?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 大学時代お金貯めておけばよかった。全部酒に使っちゃった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: For grants.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ～すればよかった；後悔

words.nativeDefinition: Expressing regret about missed opportunities.
words.etymology: woulda shoulda coulda が後悔の口語短縮形。

examples.label: B
examples.text: My trip to work this morning with the whole saga. I'll tell you all about it over lunch.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今朝の通勤、長話だった。ランチで全部話す。

examples.label: C
examples.text: We're a nice and Christmas dinner with the whole family, it's always such a saga, even when we have all hands on deck, it still takes so much time and effort.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 家族全員のクリスマスディナー、いつも長話。全員動員しても時間と労力かかりすぎ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Long involves story.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 長い物語；一連の騒動

words.nativeDefinition: A long, complicated series of events.
words.etymology: saga（サガ）が北欧文学から長い物語の意味に。

words.headwordAudio: juicy/307A come out of one's shell.mp3
words.japaneseMeaning: 殻から出る、外向的になる

examples.label: B
examples.text: I used to be a lot more shine quiet, but playing basketball really helped me come out of my shell.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昔はもっと shy で静かだった。バスケが殻から出るのに役立った。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I hate how I'm so introverted so my goal for this year is to come out on my shell a bit more and meet new people.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 内向的すぎるのが嫌。今年の目標はもう少し殻から出て新しい人に会うこと。

examples.label: D
examples.text: become more sociable.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 内向的から解放される

words.nativeDefinition: To become more confident and sociable.
words.etymology: come out of one's shell（殻から出る）が内向的から解放される比喩。

words.japaneseMeaning: 細かいことにこだわる

examples.label: B
examples.text: I know the devils in the detail, but complaining that the logo on the website is off by one pixel is just splitting hairs, isn't it? Who's going to notice?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 細部が重要なのはわかるけど、ロゴが1ピクセルズレてるって文句は細かいことにこだわりすぎ。誰が気づく？

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm really frustrated how these politicians are splitting hairs and not addressing the elephant in the room.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 政治家が細かいことにこだわって、部屋の象（問題の核心）に触れないのにイライラする。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Be too picky about small details.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 細かいことを気にする

words.nativeDefinition: To argue or worry about insignificant details.
words.etymology: split hairs（毛を分ける）が些細な違いにこだわる比喩。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Questions about his divorce aside, Andrew answers basically everything that I ask him. He's such a good sport.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 離婚の話は別として、アンドリューは僕の質問にはほぼ全部答えてくれる。いい人。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Defending aside, Joe is a very intelligent player.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 守備は別として、ジョーは非常に知的な選手。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Apart from something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ～は別として

words.nativeDefinition: Apart from or except for something.
words.etymology: aside（脇に）が本題から除外する意味。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm not sure if I want to live in Tokyo or Osaka next year, it's a bit of a coin flip.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 来年東京か大阪か住むかまだ決めてない。コイントスみたい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I think you should play the long game in investing, day trading and short-term investing on more like a coin flip in my opinion.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 投資は長期ゲームすべき。デイトレードや短期投資はコイントスみたい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: on certain situation.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 五分五分；運次第

words.nativeDefinition: A situation where the outcome is essentially random.
words.etymology: coin-flip（コイン投げ）が50/50の不確実性の比喩。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Here's a little thought experiment. Would you rather have a 20% chance of winning $10,000 or $1,000 for sure? For this worth, I take the first option.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ちょっとした思考実験。1万ドル20%の確率か、1000ドル確実か？僕は前者。

examples.label: C
examples.text: What three things are you bringing to a deserted island? It's an interesting dot experiment, isn't it?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 無人島に3つ持っていくものは？面白い思考実験。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Hi, Bethedico question.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 思考実験

words.nativeDefinition: A hypothetical scenario used to explore an idea.
words.etymology: thought experiment（思考実験）が仮定による考察。

examples.label: B
examples.text: The only way to solve the problem of climate change is for nations to work together and tackle the problem head on.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 気候変動問題を解決するには、各国が協力して正面から取り組むしかない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I find avoiding my problems just feeds into my anxiety so I prefer to rip off the band aid and face them head on.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 問題回避は不安を増やすだけ。絆創膏を一気に剥がして正面から向き合う方が好き。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Directly.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 正面から；真っ向から

words.nativeDefinition: Directly and without avoidance.
words.etymology: head-on（正面から）が直接対峙することを表す。

words.headword: on a first name basis with sb
words.headwordAudio: juicy/313A on a first name basis with sb.mp3
words.phonetic: ɔn ə fərst neɪm ˈbeɪsɪs wɪθ ˈsəmˌbɑdi

examples.label: B
examples.text: Hold your horses, how did you end up on a first name basis Kim Jong-un? You're not a spy, are you?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 待って、どうやって金正恩と名前で呼び合う仲になったの？スパイ？

examples.label: C
examples.text: My goal is to be on a first name basis with Ariana Grande. I'm serious.
examples.japaneseTranslation: アリアナ・グランデと名前で呼び合う仲になるのが目標。マジで。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Having a close relationship with somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 名前で呼び合う仲

words.nativeDefinition: To know someone well enough to call them by their first name.
words.etymology: first name basis（名前の関係）が親しい関係の表現。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I think racism is still alive and well all over the world. It just that people are better at hiding it now.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 人種差別は世界中で今も健在。今は隠すのが上手になっただけ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Yeah, makes you still exist, but to say that it's the live in kicking is pushing it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: うん、まだ存在する。でも健在と言うのは言い過ぎ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Actively exist.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 元気に活動している

words.nativeDefinition: Still active and thriving.
words.etymology: alive and well（生きていて健康）が依然として存在・活発を表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: My laptop is screwed up with viruses and whatnot, so I won't turn on. Maybe it's kicked the bucket.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ノートPC、ウイルスだらけで起動しない。寿命かも。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I live for adrenaline rushes, you know skydiving, bungee jumping and whatnot.
examples.japaneseTranslation: アドレナリンラッシュが生きがい。スカイダイビング、バンジージャンプなど。

examples.label: D
examples.text: and so on.
examples.japaneseTranslation: などなど

words.nativeDefinition: And similar things; et cetera.
words.etymology: what-not（何とか）がその他諸々を表す口語。

words.japaneseMeaning: ことわざにもあるように

examples.label: B
examples.text: We haven't won this game yet, so I don't know why some people are already celebrating. As the saying goes, don't count your chickens before they hatch.
examples.japaneseTranslation: まだ勝ってないのに祝ってる人がいる。ことわざにもあるように、孵る前に数えるな。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm going to take next year off to backpack around South America. As the saying goes, travel is the only thing you spend money on that makes you richer.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 来年南米をバックパックで旅する。ことわざにもあるように、旅行はお金を使っても豊かにしてくれる唯一のもの。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Like the maximum says.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ことわざの通り

words.nativeDefinition: As the proverb or saying goes.
words.etymology: as the saying goes（ことわざにあるように）が格言の引用。

words.headwordAudio: juicy/317A (even) a broken clock is right.mp3
words.japaneseMeaning: 時計が止まっても2回は合う

examples.label: B
examples.text: While the stock price for cast on the site were right for once, I guess even a broken clock is right twice a day.
examples.japaneseTranslation: カストロンの予測、今回だけ当たった。壊れた時計も1日2回は合う。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Almost everything he says is absolute nonsense. Sure, sometimes his stuff he says makes sense, but even a broken clock is right twice a day.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼の言うことほとんどナンセンス。時々筋は通るけど、壊れた時計も1日2回は合う。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Even a normally unreliable person or thing can be occasionally correct.
examples.japaneseTranslation: たまには当たる

words.nativeDefinition: Even unreliable people or things can occasionally be correct.
words.etymology: broken clock is right twice a day（壊れた時計も1日2回合う）の格言。

words.headword: would be late for one's own fun
words.headwordAudio: juicy/318A would be late for one's own fun.mp3
words.phonetic: wʊd bi leɪt fər wənz oʊn fən
words.japaneseMeaning: 自分の葬式にも遅刻する人

examples.label: B
examples.text: Owen can be super annoying because he be late for his own funeral but he's been with me through thick and thin. I don't have many friends like that so I really cherish him.
examples.japaneseTranslation: オーウェン、自分の葬式にも遅刻しそうで超イライラする。でもずっと一緒にいてくれた。そんな友達少ないから大切にしてる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Man, where's Dan? I swear the man will be late for his own funeral.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ダンはどこ？マジで自分の葬式にも遅刻しそうな男だ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Be on puncture.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 自分の葬式に遅刻しそう；のんびり屋

words.nativeDefinition: Someone who is always late for everything.
words.etymology: late for one's own funeral（自分の葬式に遅刻）が極端な遅刻の比喩。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Starbucks customer service is just something else they always go the extra mile to make customers smile.
examples.japaneseTranslation: スタバのカスタマーサービス、本当に別格。いつも一歩余計にやって客を笑顔にする。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I've tried so many headphones but these both ones are truly something else. They're so cost-effective too.
examples.japaneseTranslation: たくさんヘッドホン試したけど、このボーズは本当に別格。コスパもいい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Supportding...
examples.japaneseTranslation: 別格の；すごい

words.nativeDefinition: Exceptionally good or impressive.
words.etymology: something else（別の何か）が並外れて優れている意味。

words.japaneseMeaning: 家計を支える、稼いでくる

examples.label: B
examples.text: Maybe it's just me, but I think there's still a lot of pressure on men to bring home the bacon.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 僕だけかもしれないけど、男にはまだ家計を支えるプレッシャーがあると思う。

examples.label: C
examples.text: We haven't been playing well lately, but I'm still confident that we can bring home the bacon tonight if we go for broke.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 最近調子悪いけど、今夜全力出せば勝てる自信ある。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Make money for the household become successful.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 家計を支える；稼ぐ

words.nativeDefinition: To earn money to support one's family.
words.etymology: bring home the bacon（ベーコンを持ち帰る）が稼ぎを家に持ち帰る比喩。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Pull up your chair, everyone. Dinner's almost ready.
examples.japaneseTranslation: みんな座って。夕食もうすぐ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Pull up your chair and watch the movie with us.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 椅子引いて一緒に映画見よう。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Take a seat.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 座る；席につく

words.nativeDefinition: An invitation to sit down.
words.etymology: pull up a chair（椅子を引く）が着席を促す表現。

words.japaneseMeaning: 二者択一、どちらか一方

examples.label: B
examples.text: Building a career in raising a child isn't either or there's definitely a way to do both.
examples.japaneseTranslation: キャリアと子育ては二者択一じゃない。両方できる方法がある。

examples.label: C
examples.text: A lot of people ask me if they should focus on quality or quantity when it comes to studying, but I don't think it's in either or.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 勉強は質か量かってよく聞かれるけど、二者択一じゃないと思う。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Bye, Neri Choice.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 二択

words.nativeDefinition: Presenting only two mutually exclusive choices.
words.etymology: either-or（どちらか）が二択の選択を表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: It's weird how that penned by an apple apple pensong took the internet by storm and then disappeared within a year or so.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Apple PencilのCMソング、インターネット席已して1年くらいで消えた。変。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Pokemon Goetheck the World by Storm a few years ago, but are people still playing it? I haven't installed it ages ago.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ポケモンGO、数年前世界を席巻したけど、まだやってる人いる？僕はもうアンインストールした。

examples.label: D
examples.text: become suddenly very popular in something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 大ブレイクする；一気に流行る

words.nativeDefinition: To become suddenly very popular.
words.etymology: take by storm（嵐のように取る）が急速な流行を表す。

words.headword: let the cat out of the bag
words.headwordAudio: juicy/324A let the cat out of the bag.mp3
words.phonetic: lɛt ðə kæt aʊt əv ðə bæg

examples.label: B
examples.text: I try to keep my wife in the dark about getting a pet snapping turtle but Greg let the cat out of the bag earlier today.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 妻にカメのペット内緒にしようとしてたのに、グレッグが今日バラしちゃった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Just my two cents, but you really shouldn't tell Nina that you're quitting the company if you want to keep it a secret. She's going to let the cat out of the bag for sure.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 秘密にしたいなら、会社辞めることニーナに言っちゃダメ。バラすから。

examples.label: D
examples.text: reveal the secret.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 秘密をバラす

words.nativeDefinition: To reveal a secret unintentionally.
words.etymology: let the cat out of the bag（袋から猫を出す）が秘密暴露の比喩。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I want to break my smoking habit before I move in with my girlfriend so I guess it's now or never.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼女と同棲する前に喫煙習慣断ちたい。今しかない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I haven't managed to break this habit of eating chips after work every day. On a positive note, I'm shaking way more water than I used to.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 仕事後にポテチ食べる習慣、まだ断ててない。良い点として、水はたくさん飲むようになった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Stop or retain something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 習慣をやめる

words.nativeDefinition: To stop doing something habitual.
words.etymology: break a habit（習慣を壊す）が悪習慣を断つ意味。

words.japaneseMeaning: 街に出る、（製品が）発売される

examples.label: B
examples.text: Today we're going to hit the streets of New York to ask people about the pros and cons of living in New York.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今日はNYの街に出て、NY生活の長所短所を人々に聞く。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I can't wait for this new product to hit the streets.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この新製品、早く発売されるの待ちきれない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Be walking on the streets. Be released.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 街に出る；発売される

words.nativeDefinition: To go out onto the streets; to be released publicly.
words.etymology: hit the streets（通りを歩く）が街頭に出る・発売される意味。

words.japaneseMeaning: （物事を）枠組みで捉える、言い方を変える

examples.label: B
examples.text: Depending on how you frame it, you could either say that I was lucky to pass the exam by the skin of my teeth or that I study just the right amount.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 捉え方次第で、試験ギリギリで合格したのは運が良かったとも、勉強不足とも言える。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I know racism exists in this country, but trying to frame every single problem as a race issue is nonsense.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この国に人種差別は存在する。でも全ての問題を人種問題として枠組みするのはナンセンス。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Consider something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 捉える；枠組みで考える

words.nativeDefinition: To present something in a particular way.
words.etymology: frame（枠）が物事の見せ方・捉え方を表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜を）ジロジロ見る、目分量で測る

examples.label: B
examples.text: Oh my god, the man sitting across from his eyeballing me so creepy.
examples.japaneseTranslation: うわ、向かいの男がジロジロ見てきて気持ち悪い。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I had to eyeball the seasoning because I didn't have a recipe but it actually turned out almost perfect.
examples.japaneseTranslation: レシピなかったから目分量で調味料入れたけど、ほぼ完璧だった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Steared something. Measure something roughly by sight.
examples.japaneseTranslation: じろじろ見る；目測する

words.nativeDefinition: To look at someone or something intently; to estimate by sight.
words.etymology: eyeball（目玉）が動詞で「凝視する」「目分量で測る」。

words.japaneseMeaning: ひらめき、電球が点灯した瞬間

examples.label: B
examples.text: I had a light bulb moment in the shower just now, but I don't remember what the idea was anymore.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今シャワー中にひらめいたけど、何のアイデアだったか忘れた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I had this light bulb moment during the workshop that my passion is in robotics and programming.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ワークショップ中にひらめいて、ロボティクスとプログラミングに情熱があると気づいた。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Moment of sudden realization or inspiration.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ひらめき；閃き

words.nativeDefinition: A moment of sudden realization or inspiration.
words.etymology: light-bulb moment（電球の瞬間）がアイデア闪现の比喩。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Languages are always logical. You have to accept the fact that it has different shades of gray or you'll be forever frustrated.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 言語は常に論理的じゃない。グレーゾーンがあることを受け入れないと、永遠にイライラする。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Life is not black and white, but shades of gray. That's why most of us are just stumbling our way through it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 人生は白黒じゃなくグレーゾーン。だからみんなたどたどしく生きてる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: ambiguity.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 灰色地帯；曖昧さ

words.nativeDefinition: Areas of ambiguity rather than clear right and wrong.
words.etymology: shades of gray（灰色の色合い）が白黒はっきりしない領域。

words.headwordAudio: juicy/331A float an idea by sb.mp3
words.japaneseMeaning: （アイデアを）さりげなく提案する

examples.label: B
examples.text: It was Fiona that floated this idea by me, so I can't really take credit for it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このアイデア提案してくれたのフィオナ。僕の手柄じゃない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Can you lend me your ear for a minute? I just wanted to float an idea by you.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ちょっと耳貸してくれる？アイデア一つ提案したい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Proposed an idea to somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 案を出す

words.nativeDefinition: To suggest an idea to someone for their reaction.
words.etymology: float an idea（アイデアを浮かべる）が試水的に提案する意味。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I think people should stick up for themselves, but they could be hated for it, so it's kind of like a double-edged sword.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 自分の意見をはっきり言うべきだけど、嫌われるかも。諸刃の剣。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I think the internet is a double-edged sword. It gives you opportunities to make your dreams come true, but on the flip side is made things more competitive.
examples.japaneseTranslation: インターネットは諸刃の剣。夢を叶える機会をくれる反面、依存や誹謗中傷のリスクも。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Think that has positive negative aspects.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 諸刃の剣

words.nativeDefinition: Something with both positive and negative consequences.
words.etymology: double-edged sword（両刃の剣）が一長一短の比喩。

words.headword: the deck is stacked against sb
words.headwordAudio: juicy/333A the deck is stacked against sb.mp3
words.phonetic: ðə dɛk ɪz stækt əˈgɛnst ˈsəmˌbɑdi
words.japaneseMeaning: 不利な状況、勝負が決まってる

examples.label: B
examples.text: Simply put, the deck has stacked against me, so I won't be getting promoted no matter how well I do this year.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 要するに、勝負は最初から不利。今年どれだけ頑張っても昇進しない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: If you don't go to college, the deck will be stacked against you when you look for work. Having said that is not impossible, so you do you.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 大学行かないと就職で不利。とはいえ不可能じゃない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: The situation is unfavorable to somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 不利な状況

words.nativeDefinition: The situation is unfairly disadvantageous to someone.
words.etymology: deck is stacked（トランプの山を操作）が不正な不利の比喩。

examples.label: B
examples.text: People don't usually show their hand in business negotiations so the ability to read between the lines is critical.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ビジネス交渉では手の内を見せない。行間を読む能力が重要。

examples.label: C

words.headword: put sb out of sb's misery

examples.label: B
examples.text: Use the nasi for dogs as a controversial topic, but for terminally ill dogs, I think it should at least be an option to put them out of their misery.

words.headword: like a deer in the headlights

examples.label: C

examples.label: D
examples.text: B Flughorn 6.

words.japaneseMeaning: 構成要素、基本ブロック

examples.label: B
examples.text: One of the key building blocks of competence is to have a lot of success.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 有能さの重要な構成要素の一つは、たくさん成功体験を積むこと。

words.headwordAudio: juicy/339A make an example of sb.mp3

words.headwordAudio: juicy/340A stand the test of time.mp3
words.japaneseMeaning: 時代に残る、長く愛される

examples.label: C

examples.label: C

examples.label: B
examples.text: My boss hasn't assigned me to a project for four weeks now. Is he trying to tell me I'm damaged goods in a roundabout way?

examples.label: B
examples.text: It's common to obviously uncold for it. I think you're just trying to get a rise out of me. I'm going to block him.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 明らかに冷たい態度。わざと怒らせようとしてる。ブロックする。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Sorry, it was just a joke. I wasn't trying to get a rise at you.

words.japaneseMeaning: 正直に言うと、本音を言うと

examples.label: B
examples.text: Can I be real with you for a second? I don't actually like drinking but I do it because everyone else does.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ちょっと本音言っていい？実は酒好きじゃない。みんな飲むから付き合ってる。

words.headwordAudio: juicy/346A in the nick of time.mp3

examples.label: B
examples.text: I managed to get to the store in the nika time. It was only a few minutes from closing.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 店に間一髪で着いた。閉店数分前。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I got to the airport in the Nicaraguan. Thank God.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 空港に間一髪。よかった。

examples.label: D

words.headword: have a score to settle (with sb
words.headwordAudio: juicy/347A have a score to settle (with sb.mp3
words.phonetic: hæv ə skɔr tɪ ˈsɛtəl (wɪθ ˈsəmˌbɑdi

examples.label: B
examples.text: And in all how to score this deal with Joker Vitch in the final, he did.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ジョーカーとの因縁、決着つけた。決勝で。

examples.label: C

words.japaneseMeaning: 全然わからない、何も知らないまま

examples.label: B
examples.text: Artificial intelligence has definitely opened Pandora's box, but a lot of people are still none the wiser. We'll take over the world someday.
examples.japaneseTranslation: AIは間違いなくパンドラの箱を開けた。でも多くの人は何も知らないまま。仕事奪われる。

examples.label: C

examples.label: B
examples.text: The cat returns is not in the same vein as most of the other jibbly films, but it's a fun film.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 『ニャンコの大活躍』は他のジブリ作品と同じ路線じゃないけど、楽しい映画。

examples.label: D

examples.label: B
examples.text: My air conditioner broke, my fridge light is gone off, and now my car won't start when it rains at pores.
examples.japaneseTranslation: エアコン壊れて、冷蔵庫の灯消えて、今度は車が動かない。泣きっ面に蜂。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Payraise, new girlfriend and a vacation coming up in a couple of weeks when it rains at pause.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昇給、新しい彼女、数週間後に休暇。幸せも続くね。

examples.label: B

examples.japaneseTranslation: 宇宙人信じる？どんでん返し、僕宇宙人。

examples.label: C

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜が）最高／最悪、他の追随を許さない

examples.label: B
examples.text: Bill's made a lot about decisions before, but adopting a pet snake takes the cake.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ビルは変な決断多いけど、ヘビをペットにするのは最高（最悪？）。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Ken's tattoo really takes a cake. I've seen some interesting ones before, but the Pikachu he has on his back is amazing.

examples.label: B
examples.text: Last but not least, let me introduce today's facilitator, Mr. Douglas Kapel.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 最後に、今日のファシリテーター、ダグラス・カペルさんを紹介します。

examples.text: And last but not least, thank you for being with us today. That's all for today's session, and I hope to see you at the next one. Take care!
examples.japaneseTranslation: 最後に、今日来てくれてありがとう。以上です。次回お会いしましょう。

words.etymology: last but not least（最後だが最も重要ではない）が締めの紹介。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Businesses is a doggy dog world. You can't trust anybody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ビジネスは弱肉強食の世界。誰も信用できない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I know it's a doggy dog world, but I still think Adam would have my back no matter what. We've been best friends for ages.

examples.label: D

examples.japaneseTranslation: 弱肉強食の

words.headwordAudio: juicy/356A put a sock in it.mp3

examples.label: B
examples.text: I wish Fred would put a socket in, he's been blowing his own horn the whole time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: フレッド黙ってほしい。ずっと自分の自慢話してる。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Are you still sad about Josh moving to another company? It's a dead deal so stop crying over spilled milk after all. Money talks.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ジョシュが転職したことまだ悲しい？もう終わった話。泣き寝入りしろ。結局金の力は偉大。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This app used to be so much better when it didn't have as many ads. It's sad, but money talks.

examples.japaneseTranslation: 金の力は偉大

words.headword: the wheels are already in motio
words.headwordAudio: juicy/358A the wheels are already in motio.mp3
words.phonetic: ðə wilz ər ɔˈrɛdi ɪn motio
words.japaneseMeaning: （もう）歯車は回り始めている

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm serious, I'm running for President in the next election. The wheels are already in motion.
examples.japaneseTranslation: マジで次の選挙大統領出馬する。もう歯車は回り始めてる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Helen never tells anybody her plans until the wheels are already in motion.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ヘレンは歯車が回り始めてからでないと計画を誰にも言わない。

examples.label: D

examples.label: B
examples.text: My dad taught me to take the high road no matter how difficult the situation is and I think that helped me develop strong moral values.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 父は状況がどんなに難しくても高潔な道を選ぶよう教えてくれた。強い道徳観の形成に役立った。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Hey, there's gonna hate, so just ignore them and always take the high road.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ヘイトはある。無視して、いつも高潔な道を選んで。

examples.label: D

words.nativeDefinition: To choose the morally superior course of action.
words.etymology: take the high road（高い道を取る）が道徳的に正しい選択。

examples.label: B
examples.text: These tissues with my face printed on them are hilarious. They'll sell like hot cakes for sure.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 僕の顔印刷ティッシュ、面白い。絶対飛ぶように売れる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I know customers are only just starting to trickle in, but it's pretty early in the day. I still think that these pins will sell like hotcakes. They're so cute.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 客はまだ少しずつだけど、まだ早い。これピンズ飛ぶように売れると思う。可愛いし。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Be sold quickly and in large numbers.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 飛ぶように売れる

words.nativeDefinition: To sell very quickly and in large quantities.
words.etymology: sell like hotcakes（ホットケーキのように売れる）が大売れの表現。

examples.label: B
examples.text: The fact that he thinks there's a gray area with climate change speaks volumes, he just doesn't seem to believe in science.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 気候変動にグレーゾーンがあると思うって事実、物語ってる。科学を信じてない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I think Alex is really falling for Laura, his body language when he's around her speaks volumes.
examples.japaneseTranslation: アレックス、ローラに本気で落ちてる。一緒にいるときのボディランゲージが物語ってる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Provide a lot of information.

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜を）強調しすぎることはない

examples.label: B
examples.text: If you want to be good at English, you need to immerse yourself in English every day. I can't emphasize this enough.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 英語上達には毎日英語に浸る必要がある。強調しすぎることはない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I can't emphasize enough how important it is to commit to improving a little bit each day because that's what leads you to success.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 毎日少しずつ改善にコミットすることの重要性、強調しすぎることはない。それが成功につながる。

examples.label: D

examples.label: B
examples.text: Do you want to take your ex back? Just send her a message to test the waters. If she doesn't reply, move on.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 元カノと戻りたい？メッセージ送って様子見て。返事なければ次。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm listing this chart as a card on eBay for $1,200 just to test the waters. I don't expect anybody to cough up that much money for it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このカードeBayで1200ドル出品して様子見。そんな大金出す人いないと思うけど。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Find out the chances of something being successful.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 様子を見る；試す

words.nativeDefinition: To try something cautiously to see the reaction.
words.etymology: test the waters（水を試す）が慎重に試す比喩。

words.headword: have a field day (with sth/ sb)
words.headwordAudio: juicy/394A have a field day (with sth_ sb).mp3
words.phonetic: hæv ə fild deɪ (wɪθ ˈsəmθɪŋ/ ˈsəmˌbɑdi)
words.japaneseMeaning: （〜を）大いに楽しむ、ネタにする

examples.label: B
examples.text: This guy's mannerisms are so funny. I'm sure Jeff creators will have a field day with him.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この男の仕草、面白すぎ。ジェフ・クリエイターが大いにネタにしそう。

examples.label: C
examples.text: The president quickly corrected the typos in his tweet, but social media is already having a feel day with it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 大統領、すぐツイートの typo 直したけど、SNSはもう大いにネタにしてる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Get a lot of enjoyment from something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 大いに楽しむ

words.nativeDefinition: To enjoy something greatly, often at someone's expense.
words.etymology: have a field day（一日中野原で遊ぶ）が大いに楽しむ意味。

words.japaneseMeaning: （人は）バカじゃない、騙されない

examples.label: B
examples.text: Your explanation doesn't stock up. Did you think that I believe there's savings account in Japan that will earn me 30% interest? I wasn't born yesterday.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 説明筋通ってない。日本に年利30%の普通預金あると信じると思った？バカじゃない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Come on, I wasn't born yesterday. You really expect me to believe that you dog it your homework?
examples.japaneseTranslation: おい、バカじゃない。犬が宿題食べたなんて本当に信じろって？

examples.label: D
examples.text: Somebody is not that stupid.
examples.japaneseTranslation: バカじゃない

words.nativeDefinition: Someone is not naive or easily fooled.
words.etymology: wasn't born yesterday（昨日生まれたわけじゃない）が経験者を表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: 酸っぱいブドウ、負け惜しみ

examples.label: B
examples.text: Don't let the negative comments take away from your success. They're just sour grapes.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ネガティブコメントで成功を台無しにしないで。ただの負け惜しみ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Chris didn't get promoted and he was like, I didn't want to get promoted anyway, too much responsibility and not enough pay. That's just sour grapes, isn't it?
examples.japaneseTranslation: クリス昇進できなくて「昇進なんか望んでなかった、責任多いし給料少ない」って。負け惜しみでしょ？

examples.label: D
examples.text: Betterness.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 酸っぱい葡萄；負け惜しみ

words.nativeDefinition: Disparaging something because you cannot have it.
words.etymology: イソップ寓話「酸っぱいブドウ」から、得られないものを贬める。

words.japaneseMeaning: 手のかかる、要求が多い

examples.label: B
examples.text: You shouldn't compete on prices because high prices attract high maintenance customers.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 価格競争すべきじゃない。高い価格は手のかかる客を引き寄せる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: She's high maintenance and hard to please so I stop chasing her.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼女、手のかかるし満足させにくいから追うのやめた。

examples.label: D
examples.text: demanding.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 手のかかる；要求が多い

words.nativeDefinition: Requiring a lot of attention and effort to maintain.
words.etymology: high-maintenance（メンテナンスが高い）が手間のかかる人・物。

words.headword: out of sight, out of mind
words.headwordAudio: juicy/398A out of sight, out of mind.mp3
words.phonetic: aʊt əv saɪt, aʊt əv maɪnd

examples.label: B
examples.text: I just block people that annoying me. I'll decide out of mind.
examples.japaneseTranslation: うるさい人はブロック。去る者日々に疎し。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Here's the thing, working from home is great most of the time, but it makes getting promotions way harder at a side out of mind.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 在宅勤務、だいたい良いけど、昇進は去る者日々に疎しで難しくなる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: It's easier to ignore when you can't see it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 見えなければ忘れる

words.nativeDefinition: If you can't see something, you forget about it.
words.etymology: out of sight, out of mind（見えなければ心にもない）の格言。

words.japaneseMeaning: 家系に受け継がれる、血筋

examples.label: B
examples.text: Well, your kids are also tall. It looks like it runs in the family.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 子供たちも背が高い。家系に受け継がれてるみたい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Now that I think about it, both my dad and my grandpa had really deep voices as well, so it probably runs in the family.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 考えてみると、父も祖父も低い声だった。家系に受け継がれてるんだ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Be something genetic.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 家系に受け継がれる

words.nativeDefinition: To be a characteristic shared by members of a family.
words.etymology: run in the family（家族の中を走る）が遺伝・家系の特徴。

words.japaneseMeaning: （人を）いじめる、当たり散らす

examples.label: B
examples.text: My teacher starts picking and I'm going to answer questions where she knows I'm not listening.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 先生、聞いてないのわかってるのに質問してくる。いじめ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: John says that it's just banner, but he's obviously picking on Emma.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ジョンは冗談だって言うけど、明らかにエマをいじめてる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Target somebody to the bully.
examples.japaneseTranslation: いじめる；からかう

words.nativeDefinition: To bully or repeatedly criticize someone unfairly.
words.etymology: pick on（選んで攻撃する）が特定の人を狙う意味。

words.japaneseMeaning: （口語）当たり前じゃない、変な、見知らぬ

examples.label: B
examples.text: I know this sound super random, but one of the things on my bucket list is to eat snake. I heard it tastes like chicken.
examples.japaneseTranslation: すごく変な話なんだけど、死ぬまでにやりたいことリストの一つにヘビを食べるのが入ってるんだ。鶏肉みたいな味がするって聞いたよ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I got followed by so many random people on Insta. I don't even know how they found me
examples.japaneseTranslation: Instaで見知らない人にフォローされまくった。どうやって見つけたのか全然わからない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: unexpected.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 思いがけない

words.nativeDefinition: Unexpected, odd, or unrelated; used casually to describe something that seems out of place or arbitrary.
words.etymology: ラテン語の random（無作為）から。口語では「思いがけない」「関係なさそうな」というニュアンスで使われる。

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜が）どうしても欲しい、〜に夢中になる

examples.label: B
examples.text: I don't know why, but I've been craving KFC all day. Perhaps the Emma forgetting me a family feast.
examples.japaneseTranslation: なぜか今日一日中KFCが食べたくてたまらない。家族でケンタッキーでも行こうかな。

examples.label: C
examples.text: If you're craving something sweet, our chocolate cheesecake fits the bill.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 甘いものが食べたくなったら、当店のチョコレートチーズケーキがぴったりですよ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Desire something intensely.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かを）強く欲しがる

words.nativeDefinition: To have a strong desire for something, especially food.
words.etymology: 古英語 crāfan（懇願する）が語源。強い欲求を表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: （驚き・強い感情）なんてこった、マジで

examples.label: B
examples.text: My god, that code so sick. I literally just did a double take
examples.japaneseTranslation: マジか、そのコードやばすぎ。二度見しちゃった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My god, Nick is such a narcissist. He's always talking about how handsome he is. I wish he'd put a sock in it
examples.japaneseTranslation: マジで、Nickって完全ナルシストだよ。いつも自分がイケメンだって言ってる。黙ってほしい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Wow!
examples.japaneseTranslation: わあ！

words.nativeDefinition: An exclamation of surprise, shock, or strong emotion.
words.etymology: 宗教的な my God（我が神よ）から転じた感嘆詞。

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜を）やり方を考える、取り組む、進め方を決める

examples.label: B
examples.text: You should check out this online seminar if you want to know how to go about building a business.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ビジネスの立ち上げ方を知りたいなら、このオンラインセミナーをチェックした方がいいよ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: We can't miss this deadline. What's the best way to go about this?
examples.japaneseTranslation: この締め切りは絶対に守らないと。どう進めるのがベストかな？

examples.label: D
examples.text: Thank you for listening.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （物事を）進める、取り組む

words.nativeDefinition: To approach or handle something in a particular way; to set about doing something.
words.etymology: go（行く）＋ about（〜について）の句動詞で、物事への取り組み方を表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: （過去の〜を）振り返る、回想する

examples.label: B
examples.text: Looking back on our performance last year, I think our company is on shaky ground, hopefully sales pick up soon.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 去年の業績を振り返ると、うちの会社は不安定な状況だと思う。売上が回復してくれればいいんだけど。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Don't waste your time looking back on the negative things. Every single one of them will probably turn out to be a blessing in disguise.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ネガティブなことばかり振り返るのは時間の無駄だよ。どれも最終的には隠れた恵みになるはず。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Reflect on something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （過去の何かを）振り返る

words.nativeDefinition: To think about something in the past, often with reflection.
words.etymology: look back（振り返る）＋ on（〜について）で、過去を思い返すイメージ。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Don't think of me as a weirdo. I'm just a long wolf.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 変人だと思わないで。ただの一匹狼なだけだよ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Ryan is a bit of a weirdo, but he means well.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Ryanはちょっと変わり者だけど、悪気はないよ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Strange person.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 変わった人

words.nativeDefinition: A person who behaves in a strange or eccentric way.
words.etymology: weird（変な）＋ -o（人を表す接尾辞）の口語形。

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜を）完璧にこなす、うまくやり遂げる

examples.label: B
examples.text: I wasn't sure that I nailed my math test last week, so when I saw that aidedake, it was a site for sore eyes.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 先週の数学のテスト、うまくいったか自信なかったから、満点見てホッとした。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm super hungover, but I still think I nailed my interview today. I really should lay off the booze though. I feel like garbage.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 二日酔いだけど、今日の面接はうまくいったと思う。酒は控えないと。体調最悪だ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Do something successfully.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かを）うまくやる

words.nativeDefinition: To do something successfully or perfectly, especially under pressure.
words.etymology: 釘を打つ nail から、的を射る・成功するという比喩的用法。

words.headword: start (sth) off with sth/by doing sth
words.headwordAudio: random/008A start (sth) off with sth_by doing sth.mp3
words.phonetic: stɑrt (ˈsəmθɪŋ) ɔf wɪθ something/by duɪŋ ˈsəmθɪŋ

examples.label: B
examples.text: Let's start off by introducing ourselves. You know the drill, say your name, where you're from, and what you like.
examples.japaneseTranslation: まず自己紹介から始めよう。名前、出身、好きなものを言うの、お決まりだよね。

examples.label: C
examples.text: We can start the day off with a run around the park and then brunch at Mr. Eggs. chime in if you have any other restaurant recommendations.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 公園を走って一日を始めて、そのあとMr. Eggsでブランチしよう。他におすすめの店があれば教えて。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Again something or something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かを）始める

words.nativeDefinition: To begin something with a particular action or item.
words.etymology: start off（始める）＋ with/by（〜で）の句動詞。

words.japaneseMeaning: （人・物を）見つけ出す、追い詰める

examples.label: B
examples.text: I finally tracked Andrew down and made him pay me back the money. He was totally into nylon so that he'd already done it
examples.japaneseTranslation: やっとAndrewを見つけ出して、借りたお金を返してもらった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I finally tracked down the shirt that I wanted. It took the whole day, but it was a small price to pay.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 欲しかったシャツをやっと見つけた。一日かかったけど、それくらいの価値はあった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Find something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かを）見つける

words.nativeDefinition: To find someone or something after searching.
words.etymology: track（跡を追う）＋ down（見つけ出す）の句動詞。

words.japaneseMeaning: （時間が経つと好きになる）慣れの味、好かれ方が分かれるもの

examples.label: B
examples.text: You think I'm a weirdo? I get that a lot. I'm an acquired taste
examples.japaneseTranslation: 変人だと思う？よく言われるよ。慣れると好きになるタイプなんだ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Red wine is an acquired taste, but once you get used to it, there's nothing like it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 赤ワインは acquired taste だけど、慣れると他に代えがきかなくなる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: A person or thing that one grows to like over time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 時間をかけて好きになる人・物

words.nativeDefinition: Something one learns to appreciate over time rather than liking immediately.
words.etymology: acquired（後天的に身につく）＋ taste（好み）の複合語。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I was way and over my head doing my first presentation of the client today. I did prepare ahead of time, but I just got too nervous.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今日初めてのクライアント向けプレゼンで手一杯だった。事前に準備はしたけど、緊張しすぎた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: They might be able to squeeze you in, but I would make a reservation ahead of time. This restaurant can get really busy on weekends.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 当日空きが出るかもしれないけど、前もって予約した方がいいよ。この店、週末はすごく混むから。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Advanced
examples.japaneseTranslation: 事前に、前もって

words.nativeDefinition: Before the scheduled or expected time.
words.etymology: ahead（前に）＋ of time（時間より）の成句。

words.headword: what do you make of sth
words.headwordAudio: random/012A what do you make of sth.mp3
words.phonetic: wət du ju meɪk əv ˈsəmθɪŋ
words.japaneseMeaning: （〜について）どう思う？、どう解釈する？

examples.label: B
examples.text: What do you make of this message I got from Steve? Kind of mean, right? I feel like he's been picking on me recently.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Steveから来たこのメッセージ、どう思う？ちょっと意地悪じゃない？最近私のこといじめてる気がする。

examples.label: C
examples.text: What do you make in the new boss? Everyone seems to like him because he buys drinks for everyone every Friday. I guess money talks.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 新しい上司、どう思う？毎週金曜にみんなに飲み物を奢るから好かれてるみたい。金の力ってやつだね。

examples.label: D
examples.text: What is your opinion about something?
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かについて）どう思う？

words.nativeDefinition: What is your opinion or interpretation of something?
words.etymology: make of（〜をどう捉える）の慣用表現。

words.japaneseMeaning: 絶対厳守の締め切り、譲れない期限

examples.label: B
examples.text: The hard deadline for your essay is December 11th. No ifs and her butts.
examples.japaneseTranslation: レポートの絶対締め切りは12月11日。例外は一切なし。

examples.label: C
examples.text: The good thing about owning your own companies that there aren't really any hard deadlines.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 自分の会社を持ついいところは、厳密な締め切りがないことかな。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Definitely do it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 厳守の期限

words.nativeDefinition: A deadline that absolutely cannot be missed.
words.etymology: hard（厳しい）＋ deadline（締め切り）で、交渉の余地のない期限。

examples.label: B
examples.text: She won two back to back Olympic gold medals. Her mom and grandma are also Olympic gold medalists, so I think it runs in the family.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼女はオリンピック金メダルを連続で2つ取った。お母さんとおばあちゃんも金メダリストだから、血筋なんだろうね。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I watched two Disney movies back to back yesterday. The second one was a real tear-jerker.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昨日ディズニー映画を2本続けて見た。2本目は号泣モノだった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Consecutive. Consecutively.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 連続した；連続的に

words.nativeDefinition: Happening one immediately after another with no break.
words.etymology: back to back（背中合わせ）から、間を置かず続くことを表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜とか〜とか（うんざりする話を省略する）

examples.label: B
examples.text: My wife gets furious when I forget to turn off the light. She's like, you're like a 10 year old kid blah blah blah blah
examples.japaneseTranslation: 妻は電気を消し忘れると激怒する。「10歳児みたいだ〜とか〜とか」って。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You always like, I'm busy, I'm tired, and I'm not ready yet blah blah blah. Stop making excuses, just go for it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: いつも「忙しい」「疲れた」「まだ準備できてない〜」とか言って。言い訳やめて、やってみろよ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: etc.
examples.japaneseTranslation: などなど

words.nativeDefinition: Used to dismiss or summarize boring, repetitive talk; etc.
words.etymology: 意味のない繰り返しを表す擬音語 blah の連続。

words.japaneseMeaning: 共感できる、（人に）わかりやすい

examples.label: B
examples.text: To be a pro-committing, you need to write jokes that are relatable to everyone. To do this, you're going to need to walk in the audience's shoes.
examples.japaneseTranslation: プロのコメディアンになるには、誰もが共感できるジョークを書く必要がある。聴衆の立場に立つんだ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Your English is great, but I don't think the way you learn English is relatable to the audience.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 英語は上手だけど、英語の学び方が聴衆に共感しにくいと思う。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Understandable by somebody
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人に）共感できる

words.nativeDefinition: Easy to identify with or understand from personal experience.
words.etymology: relate（共感する）＋ -able（〜できる）の形容詞形。

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜に）共感する、身に覚えがある

examples.label: B
examples.text: I love this song because I can relate to Lerick so much. It's almost like it was created for me.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この曲が大好き。Lyricsにすごく共感できる。まるで自分のために作られたみたい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Thanks for sharing your story. I can definitely relate to it because I also struggled with building good study habits.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 話を聞いてくれてありがとう。勉強習慣を身につけるのに苦労したから、すごく共感できる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: And Padaju was suddenly
examples.japaneseTranslation: （〜と）共感する、関連する

words.nativeDefinition: To understand and empathize with someone or something from personal experience.
words.etymology: relate（関連づける）＋ to（〜に）で、自分の経験と結びつけるイメージ。

examples.label: B
examples.text: He's lying bad? Depends. They're all sort of lies. For instance, is it bad to lie to your kids about Santa?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 嘘は悪い？場合によるよ。例えば、子供にサンタの存在を嘘つくのは悪い？

examples.label: C
examples.text: Should you go to university? It depends and this day in an age you can learn almost everything from YouTube
examples.japaneseTranslation: 大学に行くべき？場合によるよ。今はYouTubeでほとんど何でも学べる時代だし。

examples.label: D
examples.text: It's hard to give a definite answer.
examples.japaneseTranslation: はっきり答えられない

words.nativeDefinition: The answer varies depending on the situation; it is not definite.
words.etymology: depend（依存する・左右される）から、条件次第で変わることを表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: （習慣で）身についたこと、自然にできること

examples.label: B
examples.text: I used to find it really challenging to study for more than three hours, but now it's become second-aged to me.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 3時間以上勉強するのは最初は大変だったけど、今では身についた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: wearing a mask has become so second-aged to me that I would be a little embarrassed being outside without it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: マスクをつけるのが当たり前になりすぎて、外でマスクなしだとちょっと恥ずかしい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Natural behavior.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 自然な行動

words.nativeDefinition: Something that becomes natural and automatic through habit.
words.etymology: second（第二の）＋ nature（天性）で、第二の本能＝習慣化したこと。

words.japaneseMeaning: （人に）様子を聞く、連絡して確認する

examples.label: B
examples.text: Let's check in with Kevin to see how he's doing. Ever since his girlfriend broke up with him, he's been a real mess.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Kevinに連絡して様子を聞こう。彼女に振られてから、すごくボロボロなんだ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Here are some tips on how to impress your boss. First things first, check in with your boss regularly to see if you're on the same page.

examples.label: B

examples.text: BAN!

examples.text: But you won't let him..."

words.japaneseMeaning: 標準的な、普通の、ありきたりの

examples.text: I know you're happy that you won, but did you have to rub it in? I don't mean to be a buskill, but you're just going a little overboard.

examples.label: B
examples.text: If you think wearing that extravagant suit is going to get Jessica's attention, then go for it. Put in my opinion, less is more.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 派手なスーツ着てJessicaの注目を集めたいなら、やってみな。私としては控えめな方がいいと思うけど。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Do you want to use this chair? Sure go for it
examples.japaneseTranslation: この椅子使う？いいよ、どうぞ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Do it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: やってみろ；やる

words.nativeDefinition: To try something or encourage someone to do it.
words.etymology: go for（〜を目指す）の口語用法。

words.japaneseMeaning: 口を閉ざす、黙っている

examples.label: B
examples.text: Keep your mouth shut about me and Amy. If you spill the beans, I'll be really mad.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 俺とAmyのこと、口外しないで。バラしたら本当に怒る。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You can comment, and keep my mouth shut about your upcoming promotion.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昇進のこと、口を閉ざしておいて。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Stay silent.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 黙っている

words.nativeDefinition: To stay silent and not reveal information.
words.etymology: keep one's mouth shut（口を閉ざす）の直訳的成句。

words.japaneseMeaning: （自分の）やり方でやる、好きにやらせる

examples.label: B
examples.text: Please don't force your ideas and values on me like that. Bottom line is I don't care what you do. You do your thing and I'll do mine.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 自分の価値観を押し付けないで。君が何をしようと関係ない。君は君のやり方で、俺は俺のやり方で。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You want to study abroad? Go ahead do your thing you only live once but if you do it right once is enough
examples.japaneseTranslation: 留学したい？好きにすればいい。YOLO だけど、正しく生きれば一度で十分。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Do it one once.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 自分のやり方でやる

words.nativeDefinition: To do things one's own way without interference.
words.etymology: do one's thing（自分のことをする）の口語表現。

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜を）試してみる、チャレンジする

examples.label: B
examples.text: Let me have a go at presenting this project. Don't worry. I know my stuff when it comes to crypto
examples.japaneseTranslation: このプロジェクトのプレゼン、私がやらせて。暗号のことは詳しいから。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Have a good that punching bag. You'll feel better. I promise. But wrap your hands just to be safe.
examples.japaneseTranslation: サンドバッグ、殴ってみな。気分良くなるよ。手は包帯巻いてね。

examples.label: D
examples.text: attempt something
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かに）挑戦する

words.nativeDefinition: To attempt or try something.
words.etymology: have a go（試みる）のイギリス英語表現。

words.japaneseMeaning: （以前より）状況が悪い、余計に困っている

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm worse off after this knee surgery than I was with the injury. It's like I can't win and I always seem to draw the short straw.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 膝の手術後、怪我してた時より状況が悪くなった。どうやっても負け続けてる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Correct me if I'm wrong, but isn't it obvious that people in the UK are financially worse off because of Brexit?
examples.japaneseTranslation: Brexitのせいでイギリスの人々は経済的に余計に困ってるよね？

examples.label: D
examples.text: in a poor position.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 不利な立場にある

words.nativeDefinition: In a worse situation than before.
words.etymology: worse（より悪い）＋ off（状態）の比較表現。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Don't change the subject. Answer my question. Why did you buy so many centuries? Are you opening a restaurant or something?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 話題をそらすな。質問に答えて。なんでセンチュリー買ったの？レストラン開くの？

examples.label: C
examples.text: My bad for changing the subject, but isn't carrot cake just the best of that stuff.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 話題変えて悪かったけど、キャロットケーキって最高じゃない？

examples.label: D
examples.text: Switch the topic of conversation.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 話題を変える

words.nativeDefinition: To deliberately switch to a different topic of conversation.
words.etymology: change（変える）＋ the subject（話題）の直訳的成句。

words.japaneseMeaning: （何度やっても）飽きない、いつまでも面白い

examples.label: B
examples.text: My favorite movie? It's a bit cliche, but I like Star Wars. Never gets old.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 好きな映画？クリシェだけどスターウォーズ。何度見ても飽きない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: The Steve Jaws speech at Stanford University never gets old.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Steve Jobsのスタンフォード大学スピーチ、何度聞いても飽きない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Be always good
examples.japaneseTranslation: 飽きない；いつも楽しい

words.nativeDefinition: To remain enjoyable or interesting no matter how often experienced.
words.etymology: never get old（古くならない）の比喩的用法。

examples.label: B
examples.text: You need to learn to push back on the work that your manager dumps on you. I've never seen you so stressed in my life.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 上司が押し付ける仕事には抵抗する習慣をつけないと。こんなにストレス見たことない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My teenage son has started pushing back a lot recently. Maybe we were all like that at his age, but it's still annoying.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 10代の息子、最近よく反抗する。俺たちもあの年頃そうだったけど、うざい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Resist doing something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （〜するのを）拒む

words.nativeDefinition: To resist or oppose something, especially unreasonable demands.
words.etymology: push（押す）＋ back（戻す）で、押し返すイメージ。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm so tired of my boss always making me do his dirty work and pour me I end up doing it every time because I don't want to get on his bad side
examples.japaneseTranslation: 上司の汚れ仕事ばかり押し付けられるのにうんざり。断れなくて結局やってる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Andrew does all the dirty work and knick ends up with all the credit. Andrew is going to need to draw the line at some point.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Andrewが全部汚れ仕事やって、Nickが手柄を横取り。Andrewもそろそろ線引きしないと。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Undas ourable tasks.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 嫌な仕事

words.nativeDefinition: Unpleasant or dishonorable tasks that someone else avoids.
words.etymology: dirty（汚い）＋ work（仕事）で、誰もやりたがらない仕事。

words.japaneseMeaning: （人の）相手をする、一緒にいてあげる

examples.label: B
examples.text: Are you going to a bar tonight? I don't drink but I can keep it company with a soft drink if you want
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今夜バー行く？私は飲まないけど、ソフトドリンクで付き合うよ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Dad kept me company on the phone yesterday while I drove home so nice of him.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昨日、帰り道で父が電話で付き合ってくれた。優しかった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: A company somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人に）付き添う

words.nativeDefinition: To stay with someone so they are not alone.
words.etymology: keep company（付き合う）の古い表現。

words.japaneseMeaning: （人を）イライラさせる、神経に障る

examples.label: B
examples.text: I really get some of my nerves when he pretends he doesn't understand Japanese to get out of doing a job. I wasn't born yesterday, you know.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 日本語わからないふりして仕事をサボるの、本当にイライラする。

examples.label: C
examples.text: That tongue-clicking sound old Japanese guys make when they get angry seriously gets on my nerves. It's so passive aggressive
examples.japaneseTranslation: 怒ると舌打ちする年配の日本人男性、マジで神経に障る。受け身攻撃的。

examples.label: D
examples.text: annoy somebody
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人を）イライラさせる

words.nativeDefinition: To annoy or irritate someone.

words.japaneseMeaning: いつかは、どこかの時点で

examples.label: B
examples.text: You're gonna have to face the facts at some point. You won't get in back, but I say turn this into an opportunity to meet someone new
examples.japaneseTranslation: いつかは現実と向き合わないと。取り戻せないけど、新しい出会いのチャンスに変えよう。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Oh my god, your phone screen is cracked as hell. I think you should upgrade at some point
examples.japaneseTranslation: マジで画面割れてる。そろそろ買い替えた方がいいよ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: at some time in the future.
examples.japaneseTranslation: いつか；そのうち

words.nativeDefinition: At some unspecified time in the future or past.
words.etymology: some point（ある時点）を強調する表現。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I think Specialized Knowledge will be required going forward in this doggy dog world of social media.
examples.japaneseTranslation: SNSの激戦時代、今後は専門知識が必要になると思う。

examples.label: C
examples.text: There will be many issues we will have to deal with going forward, but let's face some head on.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今後対処しなければならない問題はたくさんあるけど、正面から向き合おう。

examples.label: D
examples.text: in the future.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今後；これから

words.nativeDefinition: From now on; in the future.
words.etymology: going forward（前進しながら）のビジネス英語表現。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I see you rockin those new Jordans, speaking of which, Nike is releasing a new special edition Air Force 1 next week. Let me check it out.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 新しいジョーダンかっこよく着こなしてるね。来週ナイキの限定エアフォース1出るよ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: The Black Prada Jacket he was rocking last night really blew me away.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昨夜着てた黒のプラダジャケット、本当にかっこよかった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Where's something looking cool?
examples.japaneseTranslation: （〜を）かっこよく決める

words.nativeDefinition: To wear something stylishly; to look great in something.
words.etymology: rock（揺るがす）のスラング用法で、堂々と身につけるイメージ。

words.japaneseMeaning: （声を）出せなくなる、声が枯れる

examples.label: B
examples.text: I always lose my voice at the club trying to talk over the music. What a terrible way to pass the time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: クラブで音楽に負けて大声出して、いつも声枯れる。最悪な時間の過ごし方。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I almost lost my voice screaming at my son when he drove our car into a polio yesterday. I barely kept it together.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 息子が車をポールにぶつけた時、叫びすぎて声が出なくなりかけた。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Become a Navalist Speak
examples.japaneseTranslation: 声が出なくなる

words.nativeDefinition: To lose the ability to speak, often from overuse.

words.japaneseMeaning: （いつもと）違うことをして、気分転換に

examples.label: B
examples.text: Life was feeling a bit repetitive so I decided to work from New York for a change, problem solved.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 生活がマンネリ化したから、気分転換にニューヨークでリモートワークすることにした。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I think I'm going to wear something nice for a change. Can you help me pick something out?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今日くらいはおしゃれしてみようかな。服選び手伝って。

examples.label: D
examples.text: for the sake of variety.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 気分転換に

words.nativeDefinition: For the sake of variety or as a change from the usual.
words.etymology: for a change（変化のために）の成句。

words.japaneseMeaning: （口語）チーズくさい、安っぽい、ベタすぎる

examples.label: B
examples.text: Her boyfriend wrote her such a cheesy romantic poem, and even had to copyright similar next to his name.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼氏がチーズくさい恋愛詩書いて、名前の横に©まで入れてた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I totally feel you, this film is way too cheesy, let's bounce.
examples.japaneseTranslation: わかる、この映画ベタすぎ。帰ろう。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Of low quality or bad taste.

examples.label: B
examples.text: Is Jim playing dumb or is he actually that stupid? He's so out of touch with reality sometimes.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Jimは本当にバカなの？それともとぼけてるの？現実感覚ない時ある。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Stop playing down. You know what you did. You better think twice next time you use the company's funds throw party.
examples.japaneseTranslation: とぼけるな。自分が何したかわかってる。会社の金でパーティーするの、次は考えろ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Act ignorant.
examples.japaneseTranslation: とぼける

words.etymology: play（演じる）＋ dumb（口のきけない）の成句。

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜に）固執する、方針を変えない

examples.label: B
examples.text: Don't try to be a hero, stick to the plan. We just need one more goal. Remember, team work is a recipe for success.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ヒーローになろうとするな、計画通りにやれ。あと1ゴール必要。チームワークが成功の秘訣。

examples.label: C
examples.text: It's best practice to stick to doing the things you're good at, then create a team to help you with the things you lack.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 得意なことに集中して、苦手なことはチームに任せるのがベスト。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Thank you for sharing.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （〜を）守り続ける

words.headwordAudio: random/070A is it just me, or.mp3

examples.label: B
examples.text: Is it just me or does Kevin like me? He's always smiling at me. It kind of weirds me out
examples.japaneseTranslation: 私だけ？Kevinって私のこと好き？いつも微笑んでくる。ちょっと気持ち悪い。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Is it just me or is the server down? I can't log into my account
examples.japaneseTranslation: 私だけ？サーバー落ちてない？ログインできない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Am I alone in thinking this or
examples.japaneseTranslation: 私だけがそう思うのか

words.nativeDefinition: Am I the only one who thinks this, or do others agree?
words.etymology: is it just me（私だけ？）＋ or（それとも）の疑問形。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Every single time I meet up with John he cracks the same joke like a broken record.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Johnに会うたびに同じジョーク言う。壊れたレコードみたい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I worked out every single day for the last year. Look at these before and after pictures. Amazing, right? This is something I always wanted to do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 1年間毎日ワークアウトした。ビフォーアフター見て。すごいでしょ？

examples.label: D
examples.text: Every time, every day.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 毎回；毎日

words.nativeDefinition: Every time or every day without exception.
words.etymology: every single（一つ一つ）を強調する表現。

words.headword: how was I supposed to know (that)
words.headwordAudio: random/072A how was I supposed to know (that).mp3
words.phonetic: haʊ wɑz aɪ səˈpoʊzd tɪ noʊ (ðət)
words.japaneseMeaning: （〜だなんて）知るはずない、どうやって知れって

examples.label: B
examples.text: How was I supposed to know you can't eat fish? You live in Japan. You think someone living in the land of the rising sun would like their sushi?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 魚食べちゃダメなの知るはずないでしょ。日本に住んでるんだから。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I just accidentally warned a ketchup packet with my fries in the microwave. How was I supposed to know it was inside the box?
examples.japaneseTranslation: フライドポテトと一緒にケチャップ袋を電子レンジに入れた。中に入ってるなんて知らなかった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I didn't know
examples.japaneseTranslation: 知る由もなかった

words.nativeDefinition: How was I expected to know that? (expressing that one had no way of knowing)
words.etymology: how was I supposed to know の口語的疑問形。

examples.label: B
examples.text: The series is crazy. I can't believe he was the bad guy all along. It didn't even cross my mind. What a plot twist
examples.japaneseTranslation: このシリーズやばい。ずっと黒幕だったなんて信じられない。全く思いもしなかった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Was she throwing shade on me? I'm not surprised at all. I knew she was a friend of me all along
examples.japaneseTranslation: 陰口言ってた？全然驚かない。最初から敵だと思ってた。

examples.label: D
examples.text: From the very beginning.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ずっと；最初から

words.nativeDefinition: From the very beginning; all along.
words.etymology: all along（ずっと）の成句。

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜について）ショックを受けている、落ち込んでいる

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm still cut up about getting that DNA English. I'm American for God's sake. I figured I'd at least understand my own language.
examples.japaneseTranslation: DNAテストの結果、まだショック。アメリカ人なのに、自分の言語すら理解できないなんて。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Are you still cut up about getting called out by David last week? Don't sink to his level, forget about it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Davidに先週指摘されたこと、まだ落ち込んでる？彼のレベルに下がるな、忘れろ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: upset about something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （〜について）落ち込んでいる

words.nativeDefinition: Upset or distressed about something.
words.etymology: cut up（切り刻まれた）の比喩で、心が傷ついた状態。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Check out this 10-eye pitch in the backyard. It's so warm and comfy.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 裏庭のこのテント、暖かくて居心地いい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This sofa is so comfy. I never thought a piece of furniture could be this inviting.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このソファ、本当に居心地いい。家具がこんなに魅力的だとは思わなかった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: comfortable
examples.japaneseTranslation: 快適な

words.nativeDefinition: Comfortable and cozy.
words.etymology: comfortable の短縮形。

words.headword: a bit too adj for my liking
words.headwordAudio: random/076A a bit too adj for my liking.mp3
words.phonetic: ə bɪt tu adj fər maɪ ˈlaɪkɪŋ
words.japaneseMeaning: （〜が）ちょっと〜すぎる、好みには少し〜

examples.label: B
examples.text: This curry is a bit too spicy for my liking. This will have me sweating the whole time I eat it
examples.japaneseTranslation: このカレー、ちょっと辛すぎる。食べてる間ずっと汗かく。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Starbucks coffee is a bit too bitter for my liking but I still go sometimes to charge my phone.
examples.japaneseTranslation: スタバのコーヒー、ちょっと苦すぎるけど、充電する時は行く。

examples.label: D
examples.text: slightly too adjective for me.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （私には）少し〜すぎる

words.nativeDefinition: Slightly too [adjective] for one's personal preference.
words.etymology: for my liking（私の好みには）の成句。

words.japaneseMeaning: （怒って）声を荒げる、大声を出す

examples.label: B
examples.text: I try not to raise my voice of my children when I'm angry. It's not my nature to lose my temper easily. Besides, it doesn't help the situation.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 怒っても子供に声荒げないようにしてる。普段怒りっぽくないし、効果もない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Don't raise your voice like that, but won't make your argument better.
examples.japaneseTranslation: そんな大声出すな。議論が有利になるわけない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Shout!
examples.japaneseTranslation: 大声を出す

words.etymology: raise one's voice（声を上げる）の直訳的成句。

words.japaneseMeaning: （習得に）時間と努力がかかる、急な学習曲線

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm really good at making coffee now, but it was a steep learning curve when I first started out.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今はコーヒー上手く作れるけど、最初は習得に時間かかった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm thinking of switching from baseball to softball. Do you think it'll be a steep learning curve?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 野球からソフトボールに変えるの、習得大変かな？

examples.label: D
examples.text: challenging experience.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 大変な経験

words.nativeDefinition: A difficult learning process requiring significant effort.
words.etymology: steep learning curve（急な学習曲線）のビジネス・教育用語。

words.japaneseMeaning: （耳に残る）キャッチーな、覚えやすい

examples.label: B
examples.text: I love KitKat's slogan, have a break, have a KitKat. It's so catchy, isn't it? No wonder it stood the test of time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: KitKatの「Have a break, have a KitKat」、キャッチーでしょ？時代を超えて残ってる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: A lot of Japanese comedians come up with catchy earworms like Pimpa and Upbo Upbo Band. Unfortunately, they lose popularity really quickly.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 日本のお笑い芸人、ピンポンやウッピーみたいな耳に残るフレーズ作るけど、すぐ流行り終わる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: memorable
examples.japaneseTranslation: 耳に残る

words.nativeDefinition: Memorable and easy to remember, especially a tune or phrase.

words.japaneseMeaning: （流行が）広まる、（理解が）浸透する

examples.label: B
examples.text: The new baggy pants trend is really starting to catch on again. I'm too tight to spend my money on those things, sorry for the dad joke.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ゆったりパンツのトレンド、また流行り始めてる。お金かけるのは無理。

examples.label: C
examples.text: 40 years ago, they said, Rape will never catch on, but it's still as popular as ever. Who would have known how groundbreaking it was at the time?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 40年前「ラップは流行らない」って言われたけど、今も人気。どれだけ画期的だったか。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Become fashionable
examples.japaneseTranslation: 流行る

words.nativeDefinition: To become popular or widely understood.
words.etymology: catch on（理解する・流行る）の句動詞。
