words.japaneseMeaning: （口語）参加しない、もう関わらない

examples.label: B
examples.text: Am I coming to dinner tonight? No, I'm out. Sorry to let you down, but I have a lot of work to do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今夜のディナー？参加しない。ごめん、仕事が山ほどある。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Hey boss, I just want to let you know. I'm out. I'm done with this company in your ridiculous demands. You really force my hand in this
examples.japaneseTranslation: 上司、もう関わらない。この会社も理不尽な要求ももううんざり。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I'm not going to be involved.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 関わらない

words.nativeDefinition: I am not going to participate or be involved.
words.etymology: I'm out（外れる）の口語表現。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Did you iron everything out with your wife? I don't think divorce is the answer you guys should stick together
examples.japaneseTranslation: 奥さんとの問題、解決した？離婚は答えじゃない、一緒にいるべき。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This game is fantastic, but unfortunately there are some bugs here and there. I really hope they are in the Mount Sinner rather than later.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このゲーム最高だけど、バグがちょこちょこある。早く修正してほしい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Fix something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かを）解決する

words.nativeDefinition: To resolve problems or smooth out difficulties.
words.etymology: iron out（しわを伸ばす）の比喩的用法。

words.japaneseMeaning: （別の）角度から、視点を変えて

examples.label: B
examples.text: It's taking way too long to solve this issue and I think we need to approach it from a different angle.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この問題の解決に時間かかりすぎ。別の角度からアプローチする必要がある。

examples.label: C
examples.text: After having kids, I really started looking at life from a different angle.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 子供ができてから、人生を別の角度から見るようになった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: from a different perspective.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 別の角度から

words.nativeDefinition: From a different perspective or approach.
words.etymology: angle（角度）から、見方を変えるイメージ。

words.japaneseMeaning: （一線を）越える、許容範囲を超える

examples.label: B
examples.text: Jim crossed the line when he punched me in the face. I knocked him out right then and there and I foreign I
examples.japaneseTranslation: Jim、顔を殴った時点で一線越え。その場でノックアウトした。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You shouldn't downplay the seriousness of inappropriate remarks made at work, once crossed the line you can't go back.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 職場での不適切な発言の深刻さを軽視するな。一線越えたら戻れない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: You'll get far
examples.japaneseTranslation: 一線を越える

words.nativeDefinition: To do something unacceptable that goes too far.
words.etymology: cross the line（線を越える）の比喩。

words.headword: you gotta do what you gotta do
words.headwordAudio: random/116A you gotta do what you gotta do.mp3
words.phonetic: ju ˈgɑtə du wət ju ˈgɑtə du
words.japaneseMeaning: （必要なら）やるしかない、仕方ない

examples.label: B
examples.text: The Simon is due tomorrow, so I may have to pull it all out of today. It sucks, but you gotta do what you gotta do
examples.japaneseTranslation: 試験が明日だから、今夜徹夜かも。最悪だけど、やるしかない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I would never condone violence, but he did kiss your girlfriend. You can't just walk it off. You gotta do what you gotta do sometimes.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 暴力は賛成しないけど、彼女にキスしたんだ。スルーできない。やるしかない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: You have to do what is necessary.
examples.japaneseTranslation: やるしかない

words.nativeDefinition: You have to do what is necessary, even if unpleasant.
words.etymology: gotta do what you gotta do（やるべきことをやる）の口語。

words.japaneseMeaning: （理論上は）うまくいくが、実際は

examples.label: B
examples.text: Economic policies were great on paper but not in reality.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 経済政策は理論上は素晴らしいけど、現実は違う。

examples.label: C
examples.text: The democracy looks good on paper, but it also has its problems. For instance, decision-making can be real slow in a democratic system.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 民主主義は理論上は良いけど、問題もある。例えば意思決定が遅い。

examples.label: D
examples.text: And dairy.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 理論上；書面上

words.nativeDefinition: In theory but not necessarily in practice.
words.etymology: on paper（紙の上では）の比喩。

words.headwordAudio: random/118A what's that supposed to mean.mp3

examples.label: B
examples.text: I should try to be kind? What's that supposed to mean? I'm the kindest person I know.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 優しくすべき？どういう意味？私、一番優しい人だよ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Olivia said on the most handsome short guys she knows I was like what's that supposed to mean? I hate mixed messages.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Oliviaが「知ってる中で一番イケメンな低身長の男」って。どういう意味？

examples.label: D
examples.text: I'm offended.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （嫌な気持ちに）させる言い方

words.nativeDefinition: What do you mean by that? (often offended or confused)
words.etymology: supposed to mean（意味することになっている）の疑問形。

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜する）余裕がない、〜してはいけない

examples.label: B
examples.text: This company is in the red so we can't afford to make any stupid mistakes.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 会社が赤字だから、愚かなミスは許されない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Our store is offering some deal that you just can't afford to miss out on. Please check out our website for more information.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 見逃せないお得情報。ウェブサイトをチェックして。

examples.label: D
examples.text: must not do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （〜しては）いけない

words.nativeDefinition: Cannot afford to do something because the risk or cost is too high.
words.etymology: can't afford（余裕がない）の比喩的拡張。

words.japaneseMeaning: （人を）ゾッとさせる、不気味に感じさせる

examples.label: B
examples.text: His fake smile always gives me the creeps. It's just so obvious
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼の作り笑い、いつもゾッとする。バレバレ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Roach is seriously giving the creeps. I don't like them. Don't you think that's pretty self-explanatory?
examples.japaneseTranslation: ゴキブリ、マジでゾッとする。好きじゃないのは自明でしょ？

examples.label: D
examples.text: Make somebody uneasy.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人を）ぞっとさせる

words.nativeDefinition: To make someone feel uneasy or frightened.
words.etymology: give the creeps（鳥肌が立つ）の口語表現。

words.japaneseMeaning: 真実は痛い、耳の痛い話

examples.label: B
examples.text: I know the truth hurts, but this guy is a scammer. Good thing you haven't sent him money yet.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 真実は痛いけど、この男は詐欺師だ。まだ金送ってなくてよかった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I just calculated how much I spent on coffee last year. The truth hurts, doesn't it?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 去年コーヒーに使った金額計算した。真実は痛いでしょ？

examples.label: D
examples.text: Reality is painful.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 真実は辛い

words.nativeDefinition: The truth can be painful to accept.
words.etymology: the truth hurts（真実は痛い）の直訳的成句。

examples.label: B
examples.text: What was I saying? Oh yeah, logosh here so much better than yoga she'd die for girl are absolutely fire
examples.japaneseTranslation: 何の話してたっけ？あ、そうそう、ここのロゴス、ヨガよりずっといい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: So I opened the front door and wait, I got a call hold on. Okay, what was I saying? Oh yeah, in my parents' room, they kicked in dancing like usher.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 玄関開けて、待って電話——何の話してたっけ？あ、両親の部屋に鍵が。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I forgot what I was saying.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 何を言ってたっけ

words.nativeDefinition: I forgot what I was saying.
words.etymology: what was I saying（何を言ってた）の口語表現。

words.japaneseMeaning: （人の）好みではない、好みに合わない

examples.label: B
examples.text: That painting really isn't in my taste. This is just my take, but it seems like the artist just copied Van Gogh.
examples.japaneseTranslation: あの絵、私の好みじゃない。Van Goghのコピーに見える。

examples.label: C
examples.text: When our sports are just not to my taste, I hate wearing those heavy clothes and boots.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 冬スポーツは好みじゃない。重い服とブーツが嫌。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Not somebody's liking.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人の）好みではない

words.nativeDefinition: Not to someone's liking or preference.
words.etymology: not to one's taste（好みに合わない）の成句。

words.japaneseMeaning: 金目当ての人、金の亡者

examples.label: B
examples.text: His ex-wife was a real gold digger. She wrote them in the bind of Ferrari, the mansion and all those designer clothes for her.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 元妻は本当の金目当て。フェラーリ、豪邸、ブランド服全部。

examples.label: C
examples.text: John stay away from Susie. I know she's pretty, but she's a gold digger. She only likes you because you have money
examples.japaneseTranslation: John、Susieから離れろ。可愛いけど金目当て。お金があるから好きなだけ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: A person who forms a relationship for financial gain.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 金目当ての人

words.nativeDefinition: A person who pursues a relationship for financial gain.
words.etymology: gold（金）＋ digger（掘る人）の複合語。

words.japaneseMeaning: （遅れを）取り戻す、追いつこうとする

examples.label: B
examples.text: Thanks to our Terrible Start, our team has been playing catch-up all season.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 最悪のスタートのせいで、シーズン中ずっと遅れを取り戻そうとしてる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Can't go to the barbecue today. I have to play catch up at the office. I'm filling in for my boss, so it comes with a territory.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今日バーベキュー行けない。オフィスで遅れ取り戻さないと。上司の代わりで大変。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Try to catch up.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 追いつこうとする

words.nativeDefinition: To try to catch up after falling behind.
words.etymology: play catch-up（追いつく遊び）の比喩。

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜したくて）うずうずする、〜したい

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm really itching to go to karaoke tonight when I'm singing I can block all the negative thoughts out
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今夜カラオケ行きたくてうずうずしてる。歌ってる時、ネガティブな考え全部消える。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I've been hitching to devour a cheeseburger since this morning, but I'll settle for some nachos.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 朝からチーズバーガー食べたくてうずうず。ナチョスで我慢する。

examples.label: D
examples.text: You want him to do very much.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （〜したくて）うずうずしている

words.nativeDefinition: To want very much to do something.
words.etymology: be itching to（かゆがって）の比喩的用法。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Ticks into the Super Bowl are still up for grabs, but prices are going up quickly.
examples.japaneseTranslation: スーパーボウルのチケット、まだ争奪戦。値段上がってる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I joined their pub quiz contest last night and it was pretty fun. It was freeing they had some amazing prizes up for grabs as well.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昨夜パブクイズ参加した。すごい景品が争奪戦だった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: available.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 誰でも取れる；入手可能

words.nativeDefinition: Available for anyone to take or win.
words.etymology: up for grabs（掴みに行ける）の口語表現。

words.japaneseMeaning: 誤解しないで、そういう意味じゃない

examples.label: B
examples.text: Don't get me wrong. ChatGBT is impressive, but there's still a lot of room for improvement.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 誤解しないで。ChatGPTはすごいけど、改善の余地はまだある。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Don't get me wrong. It's not that I don't like vanilla. I just think chocolate is better Sometimes I do switch it up and go for vanilla though
examples.japaneseTranslation: 誤解しないで。バニラが嫌いなわけじゃない。チョコの方が好き。たまには変えるけど。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I don't disagree.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 誤解しないで；反対じゃない

words.nativeDefinition: Don't misunderstand me; I am not disagreeing entirely.
words.etymology: don't get me wrong（誤解しないで）の前置き表現。

examples.label: B
examples.text: We've been friends for 15 years, wow, time flies. We've had our ups and downs, but our friendship has definitely been an experience.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 15年も友達なんだ。時間経つの早い。色々あったけど、友情は宝物。

examples.label: C
examples.text: It's already 10pm? Time really flies. I can't believe we've been studying for 12 hours straight.
examples.japaneseTranslation: もう10時？時間経つの早い。12時間勉強してたなんて信じられない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Time passes quickly.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 時間が経つのは早い

words.nativeDefinition: Time passes surprisingly quickly.
words.etymology: time flies（時間が飛ぶ）の直訳的成句。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Come again, did you just say you lost at $1,000 that they could see now? The deck was really stuck against you, no pun intended.
examples.japaneseTranslation: もう一度？1000ドル負けたって？運が悪すぎ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Come again, you still haven't left the house? Come on, I swear you'd be late your own funeral.
examples.japaneseTranslation: もう一度？まだ家出てないの？自分の葬式に遅刻しそう。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Please repeat.
examples.japaneseTranslation: もう一度言って

words.nativeDefinition: Please repeat what you said.
words.etymology: come again（もう一度来て）の口語的疑問。

words.headword: have a nice ring to it
words.headwordAudio: random/131A have a nice ring to it.mp3
words.phonetic: hæv ə nis rɪŋ tɪ ɪt
words.japaneseMeaning: （耳に）いい響きがする、かっこいい

examples.label: B
examples.text: What do I think the next Nintendo console would be called maybe super Nintendo Switch or something? It has a nice ring to it, don't you think?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 次の任天堂機の名前？スーパーNintendo Switchとか？いい響きする。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Distinction just has such a nice ring to it and the content is so good. I'm sure we'll stand the test at time
examples.japaneseTranslation: Distinctionって本当にいい響きする。内容も最高。時代を超えると思う。

examples.label: D
examples.text: siangovy red
examples.japaneseTranslation: いい響きがする

words.nativeDefinition: To sound appealing or attractive.
words.etymology: have a ring to it（響きがある）の成句。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I finally taught my personal record in Bowling today. It's a great feeling.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今日ボウリング自己ベスト更新した。最高の気分。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Nothing to have playing Mario Party with my friend, it's so fun.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 友達とマリオパーティやるの、本当に楽しい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Be something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （〜の）頂点だ

words.nativeDefinition: To exceed or be the best; to top something.
words.etymology: top（頂点に立つ）の動詞用法。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Being an actor's rough, but I learned a lot along the way. It took me hundreds of performances to come out of my shell, but now it's easy.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 俳優は大変だけど、過程でたくさん学んだ。何百回も演じて、やっと殻を破れた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I've been doing jiu jitsu for years and I got stronger along the way. It's tough at first, but if you push through it, it becomes second nature.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 柔術を何年もやって、途中で強くなった。最初は大変だけど、乗り越えれば。

examples.label: D
examples.text: During the process.
examples.japaneseTranslation: その過程で

words.nativeDefinition: During the process; as things progress.
words.etymology: along the way（道の途中で）の成句。

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜を）求めている、目指している

examples.label: B
examples.text: We'll do after anyway, is it money or status? I'm convinced that it's impossible to have both.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 結局何を求めてるの？金？地位？両方は無理だと思う。

examples.label: C
examples.text: What we're trying to achieve through this exercise is strip the body fat. That's what we're after. We're not trying to build muscle here, okay?
examples.japaneseTranslation: このエクササイズの目的は体脂肪を落とすこと。それが求めているもの。筋肉増量じゃない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Secret Desire, certainly.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かを）密かに狙っている

words.nativeDefinition: To want or seek something.
words.etymology: be after（〜を追い求める）の句動詞。

examples.label: B
examples.text: You have a thing hanging off your chin, wipe it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 顎に何かついてる、拭いて。

examples.label: C
examples.text: What's that thing in your bag? Is that a toothbrush?
examples.japaneseTranslation: カバンに入ってるあれ何？歯ブラシ？

examples.label: D
examples.text: Object.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 何か（名前が出ない物）

words.nativeDefinition: A thing whose name one cannot remember; thingamajig.
words.etymology: thing（物）＋ -y（愛称）の口語形。

words.headwordAudio: random/136A when you get a chance.mp3

examples.label: B
examples.text: Can you call me when you get a chance? Sorry, I haven't contacted you recently
examples.japaneseTranslation: 時間できたら電話して。最近連絡できなくてごめん。

examples.label: C
examples.text: When you get a chance, can you send me the pictures you took yesterday? I want to post them on Instagram
examples.japaneseTranslation: 時間できたら、昨日撮った写真送って。Instagramに載せたい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: When you have free time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 暇なときに

words.nativeDefinition: When you have free time or an opportunity.
words.etymology: when you get a chance（機会があれば）の丁寧表現。

words.japaneseMeaning: （そこから）引き継ぐ、あとは任せる

examples.label: B
examples.text: My laptop got broken yesterday. I've been using it for five years now, so I could get a new one, but I'll get a quote for a pair of first and take it from there.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ノートPC壊れた。5年使ったから新しいの買うかも。見積もり取ってから判断する。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Thank you so much for your hard work, Bella. Leave the folders out of the desk and I'll take it from there. Don't worry. They're in good hands.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Bella、お疲れ様。フォルダは机に置いとくから、あとは私が引き継ぐ。心配しないで。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Take the lead from that point.
examples.japaneseTranslation: そこから先は任せる

words.nativeDefinition: To continue from that point; to take over.
words.etymology: take it from there（そこから取る）の成句。

examples.label: B
examples.text: This sounds crazy but just hear me out. If we all chip in 3,000 bucks, the H will be able to afford a seat on Elon ship tomorrow's in 2050. Are you with me?
examples.japaneseTranslation: クレイジーに聞こえるけど、最後まで聞いて。3000ドルずつ出せば、H君もElonの宇宙船に乗れる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You shouldn't waste your time dealing with people who don't hear you out, life's too short.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 最後まで聞いてくれない人に時間使うな。人生短い。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Listen until the end.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人の話を）最後まで聞く

words.nativeDefinition: To listen to someone until they finish speaking.
words.etymology: hear out（聞き終える）の句動詞。

words.japaneseMeaning: （世の中に）存在する、世の中には

examples.label: B
examples.text: There are some really scary people out there. This could be all in my head, but I've had this weird feeling that somebody is following me
examples.japaneseTranslation: 世の中には本当に怖い人がいる。気のせいかもしれないけど、誰かに見られてる感じ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: There's just too much delicious food out there. I can't choose my favorite.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 世の中においしいもの多すぎ。好きなもの一つ選べない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: in the world.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 世の中に；世間に

words.nativeDefinition: In the world; out in society.
words.etymology: out there（そこ外に）の口語表現。

words.japaneseMeaning: （リスクを避けて）安全策を取る

examples.label: B
examples.text: Hold up. We need to be at the airport by 11. Let's play it safely the house at 9 then.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 待って。11時までに空港着かないと。9時に家出る安全策取ろう。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I would play it safe and make a backup of your essay. Oh, you already made one? Sweet!
examples.japaneseTranslation: 安全策取ってレポートのバックアップ作った方がいい。もう作った？いいね。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Avoid risks.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 安全策を取る

words.nativeDefinition: To avoid taking risks; to be cautious.
words.etymology: play it safe（安全にプレイする）の成句。

words.headword: where is sb going wrong (with sth)
words.headwordAudio: random/141A where is sb going wrong (with sth).mp3
words.phonetic: wɛr ɪz ˈsəmˌbɑdi goʊɪŋ rɔŋ (wɪθ ˈsəmθɪŋ)
words.japaneseMeaning: （〜で）何が間違ってる？、どこがダメ？

examples.label: B
examples.text: Where am I going wrong with my calorie counting? I've been on a diet for more than two months now, but I'm not losing weight at all
examples.japaneseTranslation: カロリー計算、何が間違ってる？2ヶ月以上ダイエットしてるのに痩せない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Where am I going wrong with my pronunciation? Fireway and be completely honest.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 発音、何が間違ってる？遠慮なく正直に言って。

examples.label: D
examples.text: What is somebody doing incorrectly and relation to something?
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人は）何を間違えている？

words.nativeDefinition: What is someone doing wrong in relation to something?
words.etymology: going wrong（うまくいかない）の疑問形。

words.japaneseMeaning: （失敗した時の）予備プラン、Bプラン

examples.label: B
examples.text: I admire your courage, but you can't just decide to quit school to become a comedian. You should think about a backup plan in case you fail.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 勇気は認めるけど、学校辞めてコメディアンになるなんて。Bプラン考えないと。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm tired of being Judy's backup plan when she calls me I know she's scraping the bottom of the barrel
examples.japaneseTranslation: JudyのBプラン扱いされるのうんざり。電話くる時、他に誰もいない時だけ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I'll turn it off.

words.japaneseMeaning: （人を）当てさせる、わからなくさせる

examples.label: B
examples.text: That movie really kept me guessing all the way until the end. There's something about Spielberg movies that keep me on the edge of my seat.
examples.japaneseTranslation: あの映画、本当に当てさせられた。結末予想つかなかった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I just might profess your keep us guessing about when the next test is. At the bare minimum, she should let us know a month in advance.

examples.label: D

words.japaneseMeaning: たいていは、多くの場合

examples.label: B
examples.text: I work between 50 to 80 hours a week more often than not. Do you think that's normal?

examples.label: B

examples.label: C

examples.label: B

examples.label: C

words.japaneseMeaning: （思い出の）価値、感情的な価値

examples.text: This guitar has sentimental value to me because it was my first guitar It's dumb, but sometimes you just feel attached to something, you know

words.phonetic: hæv ðə taɪm əv wənz laɪf

examples.label: B
examples.text: I had the time of my life in Hawaii. The airline did lose my luggage on the way back home, but I guess it's just one of those things

words.headword: in the back of sb's mind

examples.label: B

examples.label: D

examples.label: B
examples.text: Come on, I ordered my food half an hour ago. What's the hold-up?

examples.label: C

examples.label: B
examples.text: I got carried away in button NFT for 100 grand but surprise surprise is now $10. I'm never making such a large impulse by again.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 勝利の勢いに乗って、つい調子に乗った。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I can't believe I ate five birds today. They look so tasty that I just got carried away.

examples.label: B
examples.text: You're dating my sister? Wow, I didn't see that coming. Honestly, this is kind of a shock to the system
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼女が辞めるなんて予想外。全く思わなかった。

examples.label: C

examples.label: D
examples.text: Didn't expect that.

examples.label: B
examples.text: This is me getting the 3. Shot and 1 go pretty cool photo, huh?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 5000語のレポート、一気に書き上げた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I pass my driving test in one go. I can give you a ride anytime you need one now.

examples.text: I'm not technically a vegetarian because I eat meat every now and then I guess you could call me a very open-minded vegetarian.

examples.label: C
examples.text: I've technically already graduated university. I just haven't received my diploma yet.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 厳密に言えば、まだ締め切り前。あと5分。

examples.text: Strictly speaking.

examples.label: B
examples.text: When he called, my first thought was that he got into an accident or something. Turns out he just wanted to ask if I'd seen his charger.

examples.label: C
examples.text: When my dog gave me a dirty look, my first thought was, oh my god, he looks super mad. What did I do doing?

examples.label: B
examples.text: I know you want to prove you right, but just let it go. It's not worth it. I'm not trying to take anyone side here. I just want you to relax

examples.label: C

examples.text: Hey, tune into the news. There's a story about a guy who lifted an entire car up to save an old woman. What an alpha?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 授業中、集中できない時は一時的に切る。瞑想みたい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: They're time when I stop what I'm doing, put some classical music on and just tune out. It's like a form of meditation for me.

words.japaneseMeaning: （目の）覚めるような、啓発的な

examples.label: B

examples.label: B

examples.label: C

words.japaneseMeaning: （ギリギリ）間に合う、余裕がない

examples.label: B

examples.label: D

examples.label: B
examples.text: I know you feel like you have a score to settle, but things are going to get ugly if you don't apologize.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 清算したい気持ちはわかるけど、謝らないと状況悪化する。

examples.label: C
examples.text: inflation is getting really ugly in this country last year a cup of coffee was like four dollars and now it's five.

words.japaneseMeaning: （借りは）チャラにする、互いに相殺

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm not quite sure how much you owe me. If you get me lunch next time, I'm happy to call it even if you are.

examples.label: C
examples.text: I really didn't mind driving you around for the last few weeks, but if you still feel guilty, give me a lift to the airport tomorrow and we'll call it even.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 数週間送迎してくれたの、気にしないけど、罪悪感あるなら空港まで送って。チャラ。

examples.label: D

examples.japaneseTranslation: 互いに帳消し

examples.label: B
examples.text: My teacher said I need to rewrite my entire essay. Give me a break.

examples.label: C
examples.text: You're saying that your dog ate your homework, give me a break. I know you don't have a dog, so you don't have a leg to stand on

words.japaneseMeaning: （冷静に）考える、正しく判断する

examples.label: B
examples.text: I slept so little last night that I can barely think straight. The fact that I have to work on night tonight as well really takes the cake
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昨夜ほとんど寝てなくて、冷静に考えられない。今夜も夜勤。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm so addicted to caffeine, before coffee I can't think straight or focus.
examples.japaneseTranslation: カフェイン依存。コーヒー前は冷静に考えられない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Sinc incredibly.

examples.label: B
examples.text: Japan is really ahead of the curve with some technology but a bit backwards with others. It's like there are bits stuck in their ways sometimes.

examples.text: My town has no public transportation so you could say it's a bit backwards. It's in the middle of nowhere so it can't be helped.
examples.japaneseTranslation: うちの町、公共交通ない。時代遅れと言える。ど真ん中の田舎。

examples.text: Not advanced.

examples.label: B
examples.text: The police have been working around the clock and they finally found the best way for it to catch the murderer.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 警察は昼夜休みなく捜査して、ついに犯人逮捕。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm working around the clock to find a cure for this disease, but no luck yet.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この病気の治療法探し、昼夜休みなく働いてる。まだ見つからない。

examples.label: B
examples.text: It serves me right for copying my friend's math homework. I got every question wrong. Next time, I'll copy someone's smarter.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 友達の数学宿題コピーしたら全部間違い。自業自得。次は賢い奴のコピーする。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This prank YouTuber just got sued for one of his stupid pranks. So is him right.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このドッキリYouTuber、バカなドッキリで訴えられた。自業自得。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Somebody deserves it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 自業自得

examples.label: B
examples.text: The FOMC press conference is happening today. Let's wait and see what happens a bit coin.
examples.japaneseTranslation: FOMC記者会見今日。様子見よう、ビットコイン。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Just finish my job interview. Now I need to wait and see if I made the cut.

examples.label: D

examples.label: C
examples.text: You could just got a dog. Funny thing is she's afraid of dogs. If I was her I would have just gotten a cat

examples.label: D

words.japaneseMeaning: （シンプルに）簡潔に、複雑にしない

examples.label: B
examples.text: When it comes to app design, the best thing to do is to keep it simple.
examples.japaneseTranslation: アプリデザインは、シンプルに保つのがベスト。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Sometimes I catch myself giving explanations that are over-complicated. Usually it's best to keep it simple and get to the point.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 説明が複雑になりすぎる時ある。シンプルに要点を伝える方がいい。

examples.label: D

examples.japaneseTranslation: シンプルに

words.etymology: keep it simple（シンプルに保つ）の成句。

words.japaneseMeaning: （選択肢を）残しておく、決めない

examples.label: B
examples.text: I was really into her, but she wasn't willing to commit. She probably just wanted to keep her options open.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼女のこと本気だったけど、彼女は本気じゃなかった。選択肢残してたんだ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You should submit job applications to more than one company. Their pros and cons to each place, so it's good to keep your options open.

examples.label: B
examples.text: You're seriously gonna run this marathon with no training. I think you're asking for trouble. I would pull out and train for the next one
examples.japaneseTranslation: トレーニングなしでマラソン走るの？問題招いてる。棄権して練習しろ。

examples.label: C

words.japaneseMeaning: （本音を）さらけ出す、心の内を話す

examples.japaneseTranslation: 躊躇なく本音さらけ出せる友達がいて幸せ。みんな大好き。

examples.label: D

words.nativeDefinition: To reveal one's deepest feelings honestly.

examples.label: B

words.japaneseMeaning: （不足分を）カバーする、フォローする

words.japaneseMeaning: （正直に）言わせてもらえば、言わざるを得ない

examples.label: B

examples.text: Not always not for everyone, but smell and texture aside, it's extremely healthy and good for you.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 納豆は全員向けじゃないけど、匂いや食感 aside、超健康的。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You want to take three months of paid leave this year? I really want to give you the okay right now, but let me talk this over with a president first.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 3ヶ月有給取りたい？今すぐOKは出せない。上司と話し合う。

examples.label: D
examples.text: math sh fini
examples.japaneseTranslation: （〜について）話し合う

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜は）交渉可能、議論の余地あり

examples.label: B
examples.text: I got a job interview today and the employer seemed pretty flexible. She said salary and workload are both up for discussion.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今日面接、雇用主は柔軟だった。給与も仕事量も交渉可能。

examples.label: C

examples.text: There's no quick fix for a hangover, but drinking a lot of water could help you bounce back faster.

words.headword: get off on the wrong foot
words.headwordAudio: random/189A get off on the wrong foot.mp3
words.phonetic: gɪt ɔf ɔn ðə rɔŋ fʊt

examples.label: B
examples.text: I got off on the wrong foot with my boss by being late on my first day. Everyone must staring at me and I felt like a deer in the headlights.

examples.label: C
examples.text: I don't want to get off on the wrong foot with your pants, so I'll try and dress a little formally.

words.japaneseMeaning: （むしろ）どちらかと言えば、かえって

examples.text: You shouldn't exercise with that injury. I wouldn't worry about losing muscle if anything and we'll make things worse, I think
examples.japaneseTranslation: その怪我で運動するな。筋肉落ち心配より、かえって悪化する。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I don't think messaging her again will make her like you more if anything it will just turn her off.
examples.japaneseTranslation: またメッセージ送っても好印象にはならない。かえって引く。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Anything That mattered? diameter passed

words.japaneseMeaning: （人間だから）ミスするのは当然

examples.label: B
examples.text: Sometimes it can be embarrassing to make mistakes, but we're only human, nobody's perfect.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ミスは恥ずかしいけど、人間だから。完璧な人なんていない。

words.headwordAudio: random/192A a bump in the road.mp3

examples.label: B
examples.text: I put my heart and soul into the interview, but I still got rejected from the job. I still got an interview lined up, so it's just a bump in the road.

words.japaneseMeaning: （つまり）言いたいのは、本当に言いたいのは

words.japaneseMeaning: （男らしい）マッチョ、男っぽい

examples.label: B
examples.text: I think one simple reason why we have so few men in nursing is that it's not seen as a macho job.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 看護師に男性が少ない理由の一つは、マッチョな仕事と見なされないから。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Jacob is always wearing sleeveless tops to show off his muscles. He's too much to put on a jacket even in winter.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Jacob、いつもノースリーブで筋肉見せてる。冬でもジャケット着ない。

words.headword: give sb the benefit of the doubt
words.headwordAudio: random/195A give sb the benefit of the doubt.mp3
words.phonetic: gɪv ˈsəmˌbɑdi ðə ˈbɛnəfɪt əv ðə daʊt

examples.text: When Jack told me he was slacking off, I gave you the benefit of the doubt, but it became impossible when I caught you gaming at work.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Jackがサボってるって言った時、信じてやった。ゲームしてるの見たら無理。

words.headwordAudio: random/196A do more harm than good.mp3
words.japaneseMeaning: （かえって）悪化させる、逆効果

examples.label: B
examples.text: At this point, scolding Tim is going to do more harm than good. He already knows he made a mistake.

examples.label: C
examples.text: If you want the stupid controversy on Twitter to die down, just ignore it, getting involved will do more harm than good for sure.

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜には）全然及ばない、遠く及ばない

examples.label: B
examples.text: My Japanese isn't anywhere near perfect, but it's getting better gradually.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 日本語、完璧には全然及ばない。でも少しずつ上達してる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: That performance wasn't anywhere near as good as I expected it would be. Those actors have definitely seen better days.

examples.label: B
examples.text: I hit a thousand followers on TikTok with only one post. Pretty impressive if you ask me.

examples.label: C
examples.text: If you ask me, working in Australia is a really good idea. I really liked their low stress work culture when I was there.

words.headwordAudio: random/199A give sb the thumbs up_down.mp3
words.japaneseMeaning: （〜に）賛成／反対のサインを出す

examples.label: B
examples.text: I applied for a scholarship, but my university gave me the thumbs down.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 奨学金申請したけど、大学から反対サイン。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I gave my son the thumbs up to travel around the world before starting college. I thought it would be a good experience for him.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 息子に世界旅行の賛成サイン出した。大学前のいい経験。

examples.label: D

words.japaneseMeaning: （記憶が正しければ）確か、〜だったはず

examples.label: B
examples.text: If I remember correctly, we're supposed to take a right turn here.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 記憶が正しければ、ここで右折するはず。

examples.label: C
examples.text: If I remember correctly, it was you that asked me to cook dinner tonight. I've been over backwards for you and you still can play.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 記憶が正しければ、今夜夕飯作るのは君が頼んだ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I think.

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm ready when you are, come on we don't have all day.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 準備できたら言って。急いで、時間ない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Get to the point we don't have all day.

examples.label: D
examples.text: Be in a hurry.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 急いでる

examples.label: B
examples.text: It strikes me that Japanese people care more about what the people around them think compared to Americans.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 日本人はアメリカ人より周囲の目を気にする、そう思う。

examples.label: C
examples.text: When I was Googling first date restaurants, it struck me that all the ones that came up were really dim and quiet.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 初デートのレストラン検索して、出てきた店全部暗くて静か、気づいた。

examples.label: D
examples.text: It seems to me that.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜のように思える

words.headword: strike a balance (between A and B)
words.headwordAudio: random/203A strike a balance (between A and B).mp3
words.phonetic: straɪk ə ˈbæləns (bɪtˈwin ə ənd bi)

examples.label: B
examples.text: You need to strike a balance between how much you work out and how much you rest if you want to maintain your health.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 健康維持には、運動と休息のバランスが必要。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm trying to strike a balance between work and life. Last year I was at work too much. When I saw my friends I felt like I'd been living under a rock.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 仕事と生活のバランス取ろうとしてる。去年は仕事しすぎ。

examples.label: D

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜を）正確に特定する、ピンポイントで見つける

examples.label: B
examples.text: Can you try and pinpoint what in your past is still subconsciously bothering you today? Close your eyes and think.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 過去の何が今も潜在意識で悩ませてるか、特定できる？目を閉じて考えて。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I can't quite pinpoint why I don't like John. They're just something off about him. Maybe it's because he's so nosy.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Johnが好きじゃない理由、特定できない。何か変。詮索好きかも。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Identify something with great accuracy.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ピンポイントで特定する

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜を）盛り上げる、スパイスを加える

examples.label: B
examples.text: My life started feeling really monotonous, so I started to side hustle to spice it up. Now my life is full of action and really busy, but I love it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 生活が単調になったから、副業始めて盛り上げた。今はアクション満載。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Let's spice this party up. Come on guys call your friends the more the Mario
examples.japaneseTranslation: パーティー盛り上げよう。友達呼べ、人が多いほど楽しい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Make ceiling more exciting.

words.nativeDefinition: To make something more exciting or interesting.

words.japaneseMeaning: （期待以上に）頑張る、期待を超える

examples.label: B
examples.text: To climb the corporate ladder, it's not enough to go above and beyond. People have to like you as well.

words.japaneseMeaning: （他の人に）倣う、同じことをする

examples.label: B

words.japaneseMeaning: （現場で）手を汚す、実務をやる

examples.text: My biggest pet peeve is people biting their nails. The sound of it makes me want to die.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 最大の嫌なことは爪を噛む音。聞いてると死にたくなる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My pet bee is a breeze to is when people say express own set of espresso. It makes me cringe.

examples.label: B
examples.text: Come on guys you're only down by two points. You've got this. Yep, that's the spirit. Go get him

examples.label: B

examples.label: B

words.japaneseMeaning: （リズム／ルーティンに）慣れる、入る

examples.text: I got COVID pretty bad last week, but I'm on the men now. I'm still feeling a little blavo.

words.headword: sb's memory is a bit hazy

words.japaneseMeaning: （どうしても）〜できない、どう考えても

examples.label: B
examples.text: When I was in junior high school, I couldn't figure out for the life of me why we had to learn factorization. I actually stood on a Y.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 中学の時、因数分解なんで勉強するのか、どうしてもわからなかった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I can't find the spreadsheet for the life of me even though I know I saved it somewhere.
examples.japaneseTranslation: スプレッドシート、どうしても見つからない。保存したはずなのに。

examples.label: D
examples.text: No matter how hard I try.
examples.japaneseTranslation: どうしても

words.nativeDefinition: No matter how hard I try; for the life of me.
words.etymology: for the life of me（命がけでも）の強調表現。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'll do the talking during the meeting. Can you take them in?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 会議では私が話す。メモ取って。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I bought this beautiful bouquet from Mell. I couldn't find the right words to express my love door so I decided to let the flowers do the talking
examples.japaneseTranslation: Melから花束買った。愛の言葉見つからなくて、花に任せた。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Speak
examples.japaneseTranslation: 代わりに話す

words.nativeDefinition: To do the talking on behalf of someone.
words.etymology: do the talking（話す役）の成句。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Just so I'm clear when you said let's meet on Sunday you meant next Sunday not this upcoming one right?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 確認だけど、日曜日って来週の日曜で合ってる？今週じゃなくて。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Just so I'm clear, I want fried rice for dinner, not fried rice. Stupid autocorrect.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 確認、夕飯はチャーハンね。フライドライスじゃなくて。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Just a confirmed.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 確認のため

words.nativeDefinition: Just to confirm; just so I'm clear.
words.etymology: just so I'm clear（明確にするため）の成句。

words.headword: what's the worst that could happen
words.headwordAudio: random/273A what's the worst that could happen.mp3
words.phonetic: wəts ðə wərst ðət kʊd ˈhæpən
words.japaneseMeaning: （最悪でも）何が起きる？、大丈夫でしょ

examples.label: B
examples.text: When I'm scared of doing something, I always ask myself, what's the worst that could happen?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 怖い時、いつも「最悪何が起きる？」って自分に聞く。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Just go up to an asker out. What's the worst that could happen?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 声かけてみな。最悪何が起きる？

examples.label: D
examples.text: Everything will be fine.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 最悪でもどうなる？

words.nativeDefinition: What is the worst that could happen? (reassuring)
words.etymology: what's the worst（最悪は何）の口語表現。

examples.label: B
examples.text: If she postpones her date time and again, it's probably a sign that she's not really that interested. Tana move on.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 何度も延期されたら、興味ないサイン。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Did you hear about Greg? He got dropped from the team. I heard it was because he was late to training time and time again
examples.japaneseTranslation: Greg、チーム落とされた。練習遅刻、何度も。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Repeatedly.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 何度も

words.nativeDefinition: Repeatedly; time and time again.
words.etymology: time and again（時々）の強調形。

examples.label: B
examples.text: You don't have to turn your webcam on during the workshop, so if you're camera-shide, that's completely fine.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ワークショップ、カメラオン不要。カメラ苦手なら全然OK。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I've been debating for a few months if I should start a YouTube channel. I have a lot of things I want to talk about, but I'm a bit camera-shy.
examples.japaneseTranslation: YouTube始めるか数ヶ月悩んでる。話したいことはたくさんあるけど、カメラ苦手。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Reluctant to be photographed.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 写真を撮られるのが苦手

words.nativeDefinition: Reluctant to be photographed or on camera.
words.etymology: camera shy（カメラ照れ）の複合語。

words.japaneseMeaning: （自分の）発言を撤回する、間違いを認める

examples.label: B
examples.text: My friends that I never get famous when they see me on TV in Japan, but have to eat their words.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 友達、有名になれないって言ったけど、日本TV出て撤回。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I said this restaurant was suck because it's so cheap, but I guess I'm going to have to eat my words. This steak tastes amazing.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この店ダメだと言ったけど、撤回する。ステーキ美味しい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: The track but one has said and be apologetic.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 言ったことを撤回する

words.nativeDefinition: To admit one was wrong about something one said.
words.etymology: eat one's words（言葉を食べる）の比喩。

words.japaneseMeaning: （雰囲気・調子を）作る、基調を決める

examples.label: B
examples.text: Good morning routine really sets the tone for their whole day. I hit the gym every morning and it gives me energy for the rest of my day.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 良い朝のルーティンは一日の基調を決める。毎朝ジム。

examples.label: C
examples.text: When playing a gig, the first band is crucial because it's set to tone for the rest of the show. If it's just me, the entire show falls apart.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ライブ、最初のバンドが基調決める。盛り上がらないと終わり。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Create the mood.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 雰囲気を作る

words.nativeDefinition: To establish the mood or atmosphere.
words.etymology: set the tone（調子を設定する）の成句。

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜を）アピールする、見せびらかす

examples.label: B
examples.text: I feel like Japanese people love to advertise their wealth by wearing high and fashion brands.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 日本人、ハイブランド着て富裕層アピールするの好き。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Stuart loves to advertise his muscles by posting topless selfies all the time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Stuart、筋肉アピールのトップレス自撮りばかり。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Show something off.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 自慢する

words.nativeDefinition: To show off something proudly.
words.etymology: advertise（宣伝する）の口語的用法。

examples.label: B
examples.text: This coffee tastes burnt. My dad is a coffee snob and it's rubbed off on me.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このコーヒー、焦げてる。父がコーヒー通で、私も染み付いた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I wouldn't buy wine for David if I were you. He's a real Venus snob, so probably hate whatever you get him.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Davidにワイン買うな。本当のワイン通だから、何買っても嫌がる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Person who has extremely high standards for something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （〜に）うるさい人

words.nativeDefinition: A person with extremely high standards for something.
words.etymology: snob（スノッブ）＋ 対象 の複合語。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Wait, I thought you guys were supposed to be talking about the finances for this month. How did my marital status come up in conversation?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今月の財務の話のはずだったのに、私の婚姻状況がどう出てきた？

examples.label: C
examples.text: It's funny you mentioned Australia because it came up in conversational lunch yesterday. I'm actually planning on going next year.
examples.japaneseTranslation: オーストラリアの話、昨日ランチで出た。実は行く予定。

examples.label: D
examples.text: be talked about in conversation.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 会話に出る

words.nativeDefinition: To be mentioned during a conversation.
words.etymology: come up in conversation（会話に出る）の成句。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I need to have a rock solid understanding of what I'm going to talk about my presentation. Otherwise, I get so nervous I can't talk.
examples.japaneseTranslation: プレゼンの内容、盤石の理解必要。でないと緊張で声震える。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I can't believe they didn't take your side. I think your argument is rock solid.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼らがあなたの味方取らなかったなんて。議論は盤石だと思う。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Very robust.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 盤石の；非常に堅い

words.nativeDefinition: Very reliable and robust.
words.etymology: rock-solid（岩のように固い）の複合語。

words.japaneseMeaning: （飾り気ない）シンプルな、無駄のない

examples.label: B
examples.text: This new Pixel phone is only 300 bucks. It's pretty basic and no frills, but for web browsing and other simple stuff, it's more than enough.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 新Pixel、3万円。基本機能だけだけど、Web閲覧には十分。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I've been craving this for ages. This is the best no-friles burger in the city. There's the cheese, there's the bun, there's the beef, that's it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ずっと食べたかった。街で最高の無飾気バーガー。チーズ、バン、パティ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Basic.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 飾り気のない

words.nativeDefinition: Simple and basic without extras.
words.etymology: no frills（飾りなし）の複合語。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I swear the last election was rigged, the government is so corrupt now, you just can't trust it anymore.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 前回の選挙、不正操作された。政府腐敗しすぎ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: One reason why boxing lost so much popularity for a while is because there was a long period when the match was obviously rigged.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ボクシング人気落ちた理由、八百長の時期が長かった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: fraudulently manipulated.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 不正に仕組まれた

words.nativeDefinition: Dishonestly manipulated or fixed.
words.etymology: rig（不正操作する）の過去分詞形。

words.japaneseMeaning: （総合的に見て）すべてを考慮すると

examples.label: B
examples.text: Don't downplay your performance last night, all things considered you held court very well throughout the concert.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昨夜のパフォーマンス、総合的に見てよくやった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: All things considered, I still don't think it's worth upgrading to the new iPhone.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 総合的に見て、新iPhoneにアップグレードする価値ない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Taking everything into account.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 総合的に見れば

words.nativeDefinition: Taking everything into account.
words.etymology: all things considered（すべて考慮すれば）の成句。

words.headword: bite off more than one can chew
words.headwordAudio: random/285A bite off more than one can chew.mp3
words.phonetic: baɪt ɔf mɔr ðən wən kən ʧu
words.japaneseMeaning: （能力以上のこと）背負い込む、口が大きい

examples.label: B
examples.text: I signed up for eight classes as a semester. Now I barely have time to sleep. I think I've bitten off more than I can chew
examples.japaneseTranslation: 8科目履修登録。睡眠時間ほぼない。能力以上背負った。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Sarah, going on five dates in a week is too much. I know you're mispopular, but don't bite off more than you can chew.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Sarah、週5デート多すぎ。人気あっても、背負い込むな。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Try to do more than one is able to do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 手に負えない量を抱える

words.nativeDefinition: To try to do more than one is capable of.
words.etymology: bite off more than one can chew（噛める以上噛む）の比喩。

words.japaneseMeaning: （使った後）片付ける、後始末する

examples.label: B
examples.text: If you want to buy new toys, you kids are going to have to learn to clean up after yourselves.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 新しいおもちゃ買いたいなら、後始末覚えなさい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You can use the kitchen if you clean up after yourself.
examples.japaneseTranslation: キッチン使っていいけど、後始末して。

examples.label: D
examples.text: tidy up after one is done.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 後片付けする

words.nativeDefinition: To tidy up after oneself.
words.etymology: clean up after oneself（自分の後片付け）の成句。

words.japaneseMeaning: （パーティーで見せる）芸、ネタ

examples.label: B
examples.text: Oh no, there goes Logan showing off his same old party trick. We've seen him in his Rubik's Cube 1-2 many times.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Logan、いつものパーティー芸見せてる。ルービックキューブ何度も見た。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My party trick is drinking a bottle of coke and seeing the ABCs without burping once. People get excited to do a degree, but usually not the girls.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 私の芸はコーラ1本飲んでABC言ってゲップしない。みんな興奮。

examples.label: D
examples.text: You need performance for entertainment.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 芸；見せ場のネタ

words.nativeDefinition: A skill or trick used to entertain at parties.
words.etymology: party（パーティー）＋ trick（芸）の複合語。

words.japaneseMeaning: （中年の）危機、ミッドライフクライシス

examples.label: B
examples.text: In terms of having a midlife crisis, he's just body convertible and he's getting Botox next week to get rid of his wrinkles. Pretty pathetic.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ミッドライフクライシスで、オープンカー買って来週Botox。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Today we're talking to men in their quarries about whether or not they're having a midlife crisis. Let's hit the streets and see how they feel.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 40代男性、ミッドライフクライシスしてるか街頭インタビュー。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Midlife anxiety
examples.japaneseTranslation: 中年の危機

words.nativeDefinition: A period of anxiety and self-doubt in middle age.
words.etymology: mid-life（中年）＋ crisis（危機）の複合語。

words.japaneseMeaning: （人にとって）最善の選択、ベストな方法

examples.label: B
examples.text: If you're looking for a car, your best bet is at that cafe. He's always drinking a coffee and working on his computer there.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 車探してるなら、あのカフェがベスト。いつもコーヒー飲んでPC。

examples.label: C
examples.text: If you want a good burger, your best bet is bucks. Interestingly enough, they aren't brick and mortar. They only do delivery.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 良いバーガーならBucksがベスト。店舗なくデリバリーのみ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Somebody's best course of action.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 一番いい手

words.nativeDefinition: Someone's best course of action.
words.etymology: best bet（最善の賭け）の成句。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Our team came up short on sales this month, we are really going to have to apply ourselves next month to make up for it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今月の売上、目標届かなかった。来月挽回しないと。

examples.label: C
examples.text: We came up short in the final, but getting there in the first place is already a great achievement.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 決勝届かなかったけど、そこまで行っただけですごい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Fare the rich ago…
examples.japaneseTranslation: 目標に届かない

words.nativeDefinition: To fail to reach a goal or standard.
words.etymology: come up short（短く出る）の比喩。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I was sick with the flu last week, but now I'm back in business. My doctor even gave me the okay to come into the office.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 先週インフルだけど、もう本調子。医者もオフィス来ていいって。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Looks like your car just needs some new brake pads and the oil change and you should be back in business.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ブレーキパッドとオイル交換すれば、また本調子。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Active again.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 復活した

words.nativeDefinition: Active and functioning again after a break.
words.etymology: back in business（再び営業中）の成句。

words.japaneseMeaning: （どちらにも）なりうる、五分五分

examples.label: B
examples.text: What again for all of these sport teams are neck and neck? This really could go either way.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 両チーム互角。本当に五分五分。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This election is really close, looking at the current number of votes, it could really go either way.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 選挙接戦。現時点の票数的に五分五分。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Could finish with either outcome.
examples.japaneseTranslation: どっちにもなりうる

words.nativeDefinition: Could result in either outcome.
words.etymology: go either way（どちらにでも行く）の成句。

words.headword: I don't know about you, but
words.headwordAudio: random/293A I don't know about you, but.mp3
words.phonetic: aɪ doʊnt noʊ əˈbaʊt ju, bət
words.japaneseMeaning: （君はどうかわからないけど）私は〜

examples.label: B
examples.text: I don't know about you, but I could really go for some donas right now.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 君はどうかわからないけど、今ドーナツ食べたい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I don't know about you, but this he's killing me. Can you grab two juices from the fridge? I call Dave's on grape.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 君はどうかわからないけど、この暑さキツい。冷蔵庫からジュース2本取って。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I'm not sure if you feel the same but
examples.japaneseTranslation: 君はどうかわからないけど

words.nativeDefinition: I'm not sure if you feel the same, but...
words.etymology: I don't know about you（君はどうかわからない）の成句。

words.japaneseMeaning: （気分転換の）環境の変化、場所替え

examples.label: B
examples.text: I moved out of Tokyo because I needed to change a scenery. The time I live now is like a ghost town, but I love being so close to the beach.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 東京出て、環境変えたかった。今の町はゴーストタウンだけど、静かで好き。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My go-to productivity hack is a change my environment. It's interesting how the change of scenery gets me in the groove.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 生産性ハックは環境変え。場所替えで集中モード入る。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Temporary shift from ordinary surroundings.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 環境を変える

words.nativeDefinition: A change from one's usual surroundings.
words.etymology: change of scenery（景色の変化）の直訳的成句。

words.japaneseMeaning: （理由もなく）わけもなく、筋道がない

examples.label: B
examples.text: We change our logo because we just felt like it. There was no rhyme or reason for it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: なんとなくロゴ変えた。理由もなく。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My boss all of a sudden gave me the boot for the life of me. I can't figure out why. No rhyme or reason at all
examples.japaneseTranslation: 突然クビ。理由もなく。全然わからない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: No logical reason or meaning.
examples.japaneseTranslation: わけもなしに

words.nativeDefinition: No logical reason or pattern.
words.etymology: rhyme or reason（韻と理由）の否定形。

words.japaneseMeaning: （一歩）リードしている、有利な立場

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm already studying from next semester because I like being a head of the game. It also sets a good example for my little brother.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 来学期から勉強始めてる。一歩リード好き。弟にもいい例。

examples.label: C
examples.text: In the 80s Japan was ahead of the game when it came to technology. I'm not sure about it now though.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 80年代、日本は技術で一歩リード。今はどうか。

examples.label: D
examples.text: and then advantageous position.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 先行している

words.nativeDefinition: In an advantageous position; ahead of others.
words.etymology: ahead of the game（ゲームより前）の成句。

words.headwordAudio: random/297A can't have it both ways.mp3
words.japaneseMeaning: （両方は）無理、一石二鳥はない

examples.label: B
examples.text: There's no way to make big bucks with little effort. If you want something big, you need to work hard. You can't have it both ways.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 楽して大金は無理。大きく稼ぎたいなら努力。両方は無理。

examples.label: C
examples.text: If you want to lose weight, there's only two options. Eat less or exercise more, you can't have it both ways.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 痩せたいなら、食べる量減らすか運動増やす。両方は無理。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Can't hide them both.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 両方は無理

words.nativeDefinition: Cannot have both options at once.
words.etymology: can't have it both ways（両方持てない）の成句。

words.japaneseMeaning: （事前に）調べる、準備する、宿題をする

examples.label: B
examples.text: I did my homework before the interview, so I think I did pretty well. Plus, one of the interviewers is a friend, so she'll put in a good word for me.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 面接前に調べたから、うまくいった。面接官の一人は友達。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You should do your homework before posting that video. If any of it's wrong, people are going to have a field day in the comments section.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 動画投稿前に調べろ。間違ってたら炎上する。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Do one's preparation or research.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 下調べをする

words.nativeDefinition: To do thorough preparation or research.
words.etymology: do one's homework（宿題をする）の比喩。

words.japaneseMeaning: （最高級の）トップクラス、最高峰

examples.label: B
examples.text: Many people don't know this, but Japanese denim is top of the line. They've surpassed every other country.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 日本のデニム、最高級。他国を凌駕。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Excuse me, sir. That gaming PC you've been eyeballing is top of the line. It's so powerful that you can play any game with no lag.
examples.japaneseTranslation: あのゲーミングPC、最高峰。どんなゲームも動く。

examples.label: D
examples.text: of the best quality.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 最高級の

words.nativeDefinition: Of the highest quality.
words.etymology: top of the line（ラインの頂点）の成句。

words.japaneseMeaning: （見た目の）美学、おしゃれ、美的

examples.label: B
examples.text: It's important for cafes to be Instagramable, so many young people just visit them for the aesthetic.
examples.japaneseTranslation: カフェはInstagram映え大事。若者は美学だけで来る。

examples.label: C
examples.text: How's your profile picture so aesthetic? Did you use Photoshop?
examples.japaneseTranslation: プロフィール写真、なんでこんなおしゃれ？Photoshop使った？

examples.label: D
examples.text: appearance, beautiful.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 見た目；美しさ

words.nativeDefinition: Concerned with beauty and visual appeal.
words.etymology: ギリシャ語 aisthētikos（感覚的な）から。

words.japaneseMeaning: （長話は抜きに）さっそく、前置きはこのくらいに

examples.label: B
examples.text: Without further ado, I'd like to introduce the fastest men in the world. Do say in bold, please welcome in with a round of applause.
examples.japaneseTranslation: さっそく、世界最速の男を紹介します。盛大な拍手を。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Without further ado, I'd like to show you all the features of our latest model. Everyone, pull up a chair and prepare to have your mind's blown.
examples.japaneseTranslation: さっそく、最新モデルの機能をお見せします。席について。

examples.label: D
examples.text: without delaying any longer.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 早速；さっそく

words.nativeDefinition: Without any further delay.
words.etymology: without further ado（これ以上の騒ぎなく）の成句。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Girls can sniff out a guy with no confidence from him all the way.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 女の子は自信のない男を嗅ぎ分ける。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Tim is definitely trying to pull the wall over your eyes. I know how to sniff out a liar.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Tim、嘘ついてる。嘘つきの嗅ぎ分け方わかる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: To text something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （〜を）嗅ぎ分ける

words.nativeDefinition: To detect or discover something through instinct.
words.etymology: sniff out（嗅ぎ出す）の句動詞。

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜の）噂を耳にする、風の便りを聞く

examples.label: B
examples.text: I knew Kanye West was in Paris last month. I'm surprised the proper U.S. didn't catch wind of it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Kanye West先月パリにいたの知ってた。パパラッチが風の便り聞かなかったの驚き。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I hope our parents don't catch one of what we've been up to. I might be imagining things, but Mom has been asking so many questions.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 両親にバレないといい。Mom最近質問多い、気のせい？

examples.label: D
examples.text: Become aware of something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 風の便りを聞く

words.nativeDefinition: To become aware of something, often indirectly.
words.etymology: catch wind of（風を捕まえる）の比喩。

words.japaneseMeaning: （安心して）眠れる、心配なく寝られる

examples.label: B
examples.text: Finally finished my macro economics final. I'm sure I used it. Now I can sleep easy.
examples.japaneseTranslation: マクロ経済学の期末終わった。合格確信。安心して眠れる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm just thinking out loud here, but you might be able to finally sleep easy if you install an alarm system.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 警報システム入れれば、ようやく安心して眠れるかも。

examples.label: D

words.headwordAudio: random/305A come from a good place.mp3

examples.label: B
examples.text: Before taking someone's advice, you should consider whether the advice is actually coming from a good place.
examples.japaneseTranslation: アドバイス受ける前に、善意から来てるか考えるべき。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I know you're coming from a good place, but I'm totally happy being single. Stop trying to hug me out with someone.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 善意はわかるけど、独身で全然幸せ。無理に紹介しないで。

examples.label: D

examples.japaneseTranslation: 善意から来ている

words.nativeDefinition: To be motivated by good intentions.
words.etymology: come from a good place（良い場所から来る）の比喩。

examples.label: B
examples.text: It's too early to try and calm bend down, he's still pretty angry so you're just going to fan the flames.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Ben落ち着くの早い。まだ怒ってるから、油注ぐだけ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This politician never misses a chance to fan the flames of division and racism. I know it's a strategy to get elected, but it's disgusting.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この政治家、分断と人種差別煽る機会逃さない。当選戦略。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Increased negative emotions.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 火に油

words.japaneseMeaning: （困難に）柔軟に対応する、受け流す

examples.label: B
examples.text: I used to get really stressed out by unexpected things happening to me, but at some point I just learned to roll with the punches.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 予想外のことでストレス受けてたけど、ある時から受け流すようになった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You got a few root commons on your tweet, just roll with a punch as a move on.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ツイートにクソリプ来たら、受け流して次へ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Adapt to difficulties.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 柔軟に対処する

words.japaneseMeaning: （この部屋から）出さない、内緒

examples.label: B
examples.text: What I'm about to tell you about my brother doesn't leave this room, okay?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 兄について話すこと、ここから出さないで。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Don't worry, the chat we just had won't leave this room, my lips are sealed.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今の会話、ここから出さない。口封じする。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Be kept secret.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ここだけの話

words.japaneseMeaning: （気持ちを）立て直す、取り繕う

examples.label: B
examples.text: My mental health has taken a beating over the past few days. I'm starting to pull it together, but still I can't wait for the weekend.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ここ数日メンタルボロボロ。立て直し始めたけど、週末待ち遠しい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: When I'm down in the dumps, I usually pull it together by giving myself a pep talk.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 落ち込んだ時、自分に pep talk して立て直す。

examples.label: D

words.headword: sb's heart is in the right place
words.headwordAudio: random/310A sb's heart is in the right place.mp3
words.phonetic: ˈsəmˌbɑdiz hɑrt ɪz ɪn ðə raɪt pleɪs

examples.label: B
examples.text: Jimmy gets in trouble at school sometimes, but his heart is in the right place. He's only 8, give him a break.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Jimmy、学校でトラブル起こすけど根は良い。8歳だ、許して。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Natasha can be a little too blunt, but her heart is in the right place. I wish she would stay in her lane with all the unsolicited advice though.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Natasha、少しぶっきぼうだけど根は良い。余計なお世話控えてほしい。

examples.label: D

examples.japaneseTranslation: 根はいい人

words.nativeDefinition: Someone has good intentions despite their actions.
words.etymology: heart is in the right place（心が正しい場所）の成句。

words.headword: take sb up on sb's offer
words.headwordAudio: random/311A take sb up on sb's offer.mp3
words.phonetic: teɪk ˈsəmˌbɑdi əp ɔn ˈsəmˌbɑdiz ˈɔfər

examples.label: B
examples.text: My friend invited me to his party so I took him up on his offer.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 友達がパーティー招待してくれたから、申し出受けた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Seriously, I can give you right if you want, so I was like, I'll take you up on your offer then.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 「乗せってあげる」って言われて、申し出受けた。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Except somebody's offer.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 申し出を受ける

words.etymology: take up on an offer（申し出を受ける）の成句。

words.japaneseMeaning: （人の）頭から離れない、気になって仕方ない

examples.label: B
examples.text: That warm movie we saw has been playing on my mind. I usually have a high pain threshold for those kind of films, but this one really got to me.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 見た映画、頭から離れない。普段は感動系に強いのに。

examples.label: C
examples.text: The last words my grandma said to me are still playing on my mind. If you're not doing what you love, you're wasting your time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 祖母の最後の言葉、まだ頭から離れない。「好きなことやらないと無駄」。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I'll keep by somebody's thoughts.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 頭から離れない

words.nativeDefinition: To preoccupy someone's thoughts.
words.etymology: play on one's mind（心で再生される）の成句。

words.japaneseMeaning: （有名人の）名前を出して自慢する

examples.label: B
examples.text: Mark is always name dropping his French look cool in front of people. I wish he'd knock it off, it's not like they're even household names.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Mark、いつも有名人の名前出してカッコつけようとする。やめて。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I named dropped the guy I met at a career event in the interview was like, oh yeah, she said you're great
examples.japaneseTranslation: キャリアイベントで会った人の名前、面接で出したら「ああ、彼女が君すごいって言ってた」って。

examples.label: D

examples.japaneseTranslation: 有名人の名前を出す

words.headword: play the cards one is dealt
words.headwordAudio: random/314A play the cards one is dealt.mp3
words.phonetic: pleɪ ðə kɑrdz wən ɪz dɛlt
words.japaneseMeaning: （与えられた）カードで最善を尽くす

examples.label: B
examples.text: Life can suck sometime, but you just gotta play the card you dealt. Trust me. You'd be miserable if you had to play life on God mode.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 人生は厳しい時もある。配られたカードで最善を尽くせ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I support people get rich and rich people become poor. At the end of the day, what matters most is how you play the cards you're dealt.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 貧乏人が金持ち、金持ちが貧乏。大事なのは配られたカードでどうプレイするか。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Make the best of one situation.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 与えられたカードで頑張る

words.japaneseMeaning: （和解の）申し出、和平のオリーブの枝

examples.label: B
examples.text: After I break up, I sent Kary and Nice card as a piece offering. I didn't want to end things on a bad note.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 別れた後、Katyに素敵なカード送って和平の申し出。最後悪く終わりたくなかった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My brother and I fight all the time, but in the end one of us always gives the other some type of peace offering.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 兄弟、いつもケンカするけど、最後はどっちかが和平の申し出。

words.headword: not want anything to do with sb
words.headwordAudio: random/316A not want anything to do with sb.mp3
words.phonetic: nɑt wɔnt ˈɛniˌθɪŋ tɪ du wɪθ ˈsəmˌbɑdi

examples.label: B
examples.text: I don't want anything to do with Bill anymore. Every time he loses any type of game he has a fit, what a sword loser.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Billとはもう関わりたくない。負けたら癇癪起こす、最悪。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I don't want anything to do with him. I've heard so many bad things about him.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼とは関わりたくない。悪い噂聞きすぎ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Not want to be involved with somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人と）関わりたくない

words.nativeDefinition: To want no involvement with someone.
words.etymology: want nothing to do with（関係したくない）の成句。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Sorry, I left the keys in the door. I've been so scatterbrain recently. I guess it's stress.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ごめん、鍵ドアに入れたまま。最近散漫すぎ。ストレスかな。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I know I'm always scattered rain and have my head in the clouds, I'm so lucky my partner accepted me, words and all.
examples.japaneseTranslation: いつも散漫で上の空。パートナーが受け入れてくれて幸せ。

examples.label: D

examples.japaneseTranslation: うっかり屋

words.nativeDefinition: Forgetful and disorganized.
words.etymology: scatter（散らばる）＋ brained（頭）の複合語。

words.headwordAudio: random/318A come knocking on the door.mp3

examples.label: B
examples.text: The other day, my neighbor came knocking on the door and gave me freshly baked cookies. She's so kind.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 隣人がドアノックして焼きたてクッキーくれた。優しい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I wouldn't give up your dream of being a singer just yet. You don't know what opportunities may come knocking on the door for you in the future.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 歌手の夢、まだ諦めないで。思いがけないチャンス来るかも。

examples.label: D
examples.text: See you, honey.

words.japaneseMeaning: （口だけ）賛成する、形だけの支持

examples.label: B
examples.text: My managers are just paying lip service to work life balance. They don't actually do anything about it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: マネージャー、ワークライフバランス口だけ賛成。何もしない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Most politicians pay lip service to climate change policies, but do nothing about it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 政治家、気候変動政策口だけ賛成。行動なし。

examples.label: D

words.japaneseMeaning: （電気・ネットから）切り離された、オフグリッド

examples.label: B
examples.text: I went camping off the grid for a few weeks, it was great to hit the reset button.
examples.japaneseTranslation: オフグリッドで数週間キャンプ。リセットできた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: One day I'm going to delete my social media accounts, destroy my phone and go off the grid. Social media is useful, but it can be addictive though.
examples.japaneseTranslation: いつかSNS削除、スマホ壊して、オフグリッドになる。SNS便利だけど。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Disconnect it from a central utilities or the internet.
examples.japaneseTranslation: インフラから切り離された

words.headword: see the error of one's ways
words.headwordAudio: random/321A see the error of one's ways.mp3
words.phonetic: si ðə ˈɛrər əv wənz weɪz
words.japaneseMeaning: （自分の）非を認める、改心する

examples.label: B
examples.text: She saw the air ever weighs and apologized to you right wasn't that enough?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 非認めて謝ったでしょ。それで十分じゃない？

examples.label: C
examples.text: No, it used to be a bad do, but he saw the air of his waist and changed for the better.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昔は悪ガキだったけど、非認めて良い方向に変わった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: acknowledge one mistakes.

words.etymology: see the error of one's ways（道の誤りを見る）の成句。

words.japaneseMeaning: （一つしか）得意がない人、一芸のみ

examples.label: B
examples.text: This is my fifth time cooking curry this week. I'm a one-trip pony as far as cooking is concerned.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今週5回目のカレー。料理に関しては一芸のみ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: When it comes to singing, I'm a one-trick pony. I can sing Michael Jackson's thriller and nothing else.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 歌に関して一芸のみ。Michael JacksonのThrillerしか歌えない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Person that has only one thing that they're good at.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 一手だけの人

words.etymology: one-trick pony（一芸のポニー）の比喩。

words.japaneseMeaning: （はっきり）明確な、白黒はっきり

examples.label: B
examples.text: Why there's so many exceptions to English grammar rules? I wish things were a bit more cut and dry like math.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 英語文法、例外多すぎ。数学みたいにもっと明確だったら。

examples.label: C
examples.text: There's no cut and dry answer for why most animals sleep. It's such an inefficient process if you think about it. Don't you think?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 動物がなぜ眠るか、明確な答えない。非効率なプロセス。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Clear and definite.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 白黒はっきり

words.japaneseMeaning: （まだ）結論出てない、評価が分かれる

examples.label: B
examples.text: The jury is still out on whether in-person learning is actually more beneficial than online learning.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 対面学習がオンラインより有益か、まだ結論出てない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: The trees still out on whether six hours or eight hours is the optimal amount of sleep time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 最適睡眠時間6時間か8時間か、まだ結論出てない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: The conclusion hasn't been reached yet.
examples.japaneseTranslation: まだ結論が出ていない

words.nativeDefinition: No conclusion has been reached yet.
words.etymology: the jury's still out（陪審員はまだ）の法廷比喩。

examples.label: B
examples.text: We have a lot of exciting events lined up for this year. Don't miss out on any of these opportunities to join in.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今年、ワクワクするイベントたくさん手配済み。見逃さないで。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You better have a plan be lined up because there's no guarantee that this company will never go bankrupt.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Bプラン手配しておいて。会社倒産保証ない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: How's something organized?
examples.japaneseTranslation: （〜が）準備できている

words.nativeDefinition: To have something arranged or planned.
words.etymology: have lined up（並べてある）の成句。

words.japaneseMeaning: （顔色が）死にかけ、ボロボロに見える

examples.label: B
examples.text: After her pet golfer's died, I always looked like death for months. She seems to be doing better now though.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ペットの金魚死後、何ヶ月も死にかけに見えた。今は良くなってる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: What's up, cause you look like death. Are you sick or something?
examples.japaneseTranslation: どうした、死にかけに見える。病気？

examples.label: D
examples.text: Look extremely ill.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 死にそうに見える

words.nativeDefinition: To look extremely ill or exhausted.

examples.label: B
examples.text: Messy was definitely the major driving force behind Argentina winning the World Cup for the third time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Messi、Argentina W杯3度目優勝の原動力。

examples.label: C
examples.text: What was the driving force behind your decision to invest all your money into creating this book?
examples.japaneseTranslation: この本制作に全財産投じる決断の原動力は？

examples.label: D
examples.text: Empress.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 原動力

words.nativeDefinition: The main force or factor behind something.
words.etymology: driving（駆動する）＋ force（力）の複合語。

words.japaneseMeaning: （経済的に）うまくやっている

examples.label: B
examples.text: Harold doesn't have a full-time job, but he seems to be doing well for himself.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Harold、正社員じゃないけど、うまくやってる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You know that guy in Shabby clothes that we saw at the cafe, he's actually doing pretty well for himself. He just doesn't care how he looks.
examples.japaneseTranslation: カフェで見たボロボロの服の男、実は結構うまくやってる。見た目だけ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Drive financially.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 経済的に成功している

words.etymology: do well for oneself（自分のためにうまくやる）の成句。

words.japaneseMeaning: （話題を）避けて回る、曖昧にする

examples.label: B
examples.text: Don't try and dance around the topic. I want to know where you were in the afternoon of October 28th.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 話題避けて回るな。10月28日午後、どこにいた？

examples.label: C
examples.text: I wonder why this politician always dances around the topic of China. I smell a rat.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この政治家、中国の話題いつも避けて回る。怪しい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Avoid the topic.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 話題を濁す

words.nativeDefinition: To avoid addressing a topic directly.
words.etymology: dance around（踊りながら回る）の比喩。

words.japaneseMeaning: （万が一）〜の可能性で、念のため

examples.label: B
examples.text: I lost my phone on campus last night. This is a long shot, but on the off-chance that anyone picked it up, could you please DM me? It's a red iPhone.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昨夜キャンパスでスマホ落とした。万が一拾った人いたら連絡ください。

examples.label: C
examples.text: On the off chance you're having download of the free course yet, click on the link below.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 万が一無料コースダウンロードしてなければ、下のリンク。

examples.label: D
examples.text: In case.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 万が一に備えて

words.nativeDefinition: In case; on the small chance that.
words.etymology: on the off-chance（可能性が薄い）の成句。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Sorry, I can't do lunch today. I got a laundry list of things to get done by this afternoon.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今日ランチ無理。午後までに終わらせる項目、山ほどある。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I got fired today. The plot with this is that I wanted to quit a month ago, so when they gave me a laundry list of reasons, I didn't care.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今日クビ。1ヶ月前に辞めたかった。クビ理由、山ほど言われた。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Very long list.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 長々としたリスト

examples.text: With the economy in shambles, many people in this city are at breaking point financially. The government is going to have to fix this ASAP.

words.japaneseMeaning: （人を）手のひらで転がす、翻弄する

examples.label: B

examples.label: D

examples.label: B
examples.text: I think Tim is struggling to put his stamp on the company. You might disagree, but the company hasn't changed much since Jobs death.

examples.label: C
examples.text: The California chef tried to put his stamp on sushi and Tokyo but ended up failing miserably. Japanese people would never consider his food sushi.

examples.label: D

examples.label: B
examples.text: It so strange that the flat Earth conspiracy theory made it come back in the 2000s. Who would the right mind would believe it?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 地球平面説、2000年代に復活。正気の沙汰じゃない。

examples.label: C

examples.label: D

examples.label: B
examples.text: Passing exams is just a means to an end. If you're studying just for the sake of passing them then you can completely missing the point.

examples.label: C

examples.label: B
examples.text: Elon, last but not least, here's a million dollar question that everyone wants to know. And humans live on Mars?
examples.japaneseTranslation: Elon、最後に100万ドル問題。人類は火星に住める？

examples.label: C
examples.text: Can Japan make itself great again? Well, I think that's a million dollar question, but I'm skeptical about that for a number of reasons.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 日本再び偉大に？100万ドル問題だけど、懐疑的。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I saw my company out by telling the press all the shady things they do behind the scenes. Oh well, serves them right.

examples.label: C

words.japaneseMeaning: （よく）練られた、計画がしっかりした

examples.label: B
examples.text: I don't know if business plan is well thought out. Not surprising because he's very clinical when it comes to this kind of stuff.

words.headword: have a lot going for sth/sb

words.phonetic: hæv ə lɔt goʊɪŋ fər something/somebody

examples.label: B
examples.text: Fiona has a lot going for her. She's intelligent. She's got a lot of experience and she gets along well with almost everybody

examples.label: C

words.japaneseMeaning: （無視）サイレントトリートメント、沈黙

examples.label: B

examples.label: D

examples.label: B
examples.text: I always seem to shape my leg when I get nervous. I'm trying to change this for years, but old habits die hard.

examples.label: B
examples.text: The interview is going to start now. Please relax, take a seat and pay no mind of the man sitting in the corner. You just take a note.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 面接始まる。リラックスして座って。隅の男気にしないで。

examples.label: C

examples.label: C

examples.text: That pinball on my nose makes me look like a red nose reindeer, but all jokes aside, you know any good acne medications?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 鼻のニキビ、トナカイみたい。冗談はさておき、ニキビ治療薬知ってる？

examples.label: C
examples.text: I found an article about a protein shaker that doubles as a dumbbell. Looks great, jokes aside, I'm sure it's just clickbait.

examples.label: C

words.japaneseMeaning: （大当たり）ジャックポット、大成功

examples.label: C

words.headwordAudio: random/383A you are what you eat.mp3

examples.label: B
examples.text: You should chill out with a burglars. No wonder you're gaining weight. How some veggies for a change? Remember you are what you eat!

examples.label: C
examples.text: Ever since I started only eating steak and chicken, my muscles have gotten huge, you are what yee!
examples.japaneseTranslation: ステーキとチキンだけ食べ始めて、筋肉デカくなった。食べたものが体！

examples.label: D
examples.text: What do you eat to term as your health and appearance?
