examples.label: B

examples.text: This doesn't taste bad, but no matter how you dress it up, instant coffee is instant coffee. It'll never be as good as properly brew coffee.

examples.label: C

examples.label: B
examples.text: It's just a matter of time before I get axed so many people have been fired recently. I'll just take it as it comes.

examples.text: Don't take too long making up your mind about whether or not you want to work overseas. The clock is ticking

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜について）話し続ける、〜の話ばかり

examples.label: B
examples.text: I think I just met my new best friend. I was at a barn for it and I just talked baseball with this guy all night long

examples.label: C
examples.text: It's no fun hanging out with Jason because he just talks money. It's like he has no other interests.

examples.label: D
examples.text: written

words.japaneseMeaning: （自慢する）資格、自慢話のネタ

examples.label: B
examples.text: I came first to Truvianite. I mean I didn't win anything except for bragging rights, but still good, right?
examples.japaneseTranslation: トライアスロン1位。賞品ないけど自慢する資格はある。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Creme of Dual Jabar earned his bragging rights by becoming the NBA player with the highest scoring record, 38,387 points.

words.japaneseMeaning: （絶好の）チャンス、黄金の機会

examples.label: B
examples.text: This exchange program is a golden opportunity for you to study abroad. You'll learn new things and broaden your horizons.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この交換留学、留学の絶好のチャンス。新しいこと学んで視野広がる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Alright everyone, it's a big day today. Our team has a goal not opportunity to show this company how much we are worth to them.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今日大事な日。チーム、会社に価値を示す絶好のチャンス。

examples.label: D

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm just using this company as a stepping stone to a bigger company. I've no intention of staying here long-term.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この会社、大きい会社への踏み台。長期滞在の予定なし。

examples.label: C
examples.text: For me, going to university is just a stepping stone to a good career. I actually hate studying.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 大学は良いキャリアへの踏み台。勉強は嫌い。

examples.label: D
examples.text: enteruimstrap
examples.japaneseTranslation: 足がかり

words.etymology: stepping stone（踏み石）の比喩。

examples.label: B
examples.text: When I was growing up, my dad was pretty much out of the picture, but my mom gave us so much love that we barely noticed.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 育った時、父はほぼいなかった。母の愛で気づかなかった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Gina and her boyfriend have been on and off for a while now, but I think he's finally out of the picture for good.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Ginaと彼氏、何度も別れたり戻ったり。今度こそ完全にいない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Absent.

words.etymology: out of the picture（絵の外）の比喩。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I thought crypto was a future so I put all my money into it and surprise a prize. It's all gone. I shouldn't have put all my eggs in one basket.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 暗号が未来だと思って全財産投じた。案の定全部なくなった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Wow, you lost your keys again, surprise surprise!
examples.japaneseTranslation: また鍵なくした？案の定！

examples.label: D
examples.text: Not surprisingly.
examples.japaneseTranslation: あるある；案の定

words.nativeDefinition: Sarcastically: as expected.

words.japaneseMeaning: （人を）踏みにじる、いいように使う

examples.label: B
examples.text: I feel like I'm getting walked all over at work. My boss tries to dump all the office chores on me
examples.japaneseTranslation: 職場で踏みにじられてる。上司、雑用全部押し付ける。

examples.label: C
examples.text: What do you let Jen walk all over you? I know it's tough, but you have to stand up to where every now and again.

examples.label: D

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜の）はったりを見破る、本気か試す

examples.label: B
examples.text: He told me at 5 people offering a million for the place which sounded ridiculous to me. I called his bluff and got the place for 800,000.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 5人が100万オファーって？馬鹿げてる。はったりを見破って安く取った。

examples.label: C
examples.text: How did I end up unemployed? Well, my boss said he'd sack me if I were jeans the office again, and I started to call his bluff and did it again
examples.japaneseTranslation: どうやって失業？上司、ジーンズ着たらクビって。はったりを見破ったら本当にクビ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Challenge somebody to carry out their intended action.

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜には）可能性がある、続く見込み

examples.label: B
examples.text: This idea definitely has legs, but to make it a reality, that's going to require a lot of time and money.

examples.text: I don't think this TV series really has legs. I've watched a few episodes and they're just so repetitive.

examples.label: D

words.japaneseMeaning: （孤立して）真空の中で、単独では

examples.label: B
examples.text: The rise of terrorism in this country did not happen in a vacuum. Many factors were at play including political changes around the world.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この国のテロリズム増加、真空では起きなかった。政治変化など要因多数。

examples.label: C
examples.text: No great work of art is created in a vacuum. There are always influences from past creators.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 偉大な芸術作品、真空では生まれない。常に過去の影響。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Internal isolation
examples.japaneseTranslation: 孤立した状態

words.nativeDefinition: In isolation, without external influence.
words.etymology: in a vacuum（真空中で）の比喩。

words.japaneseMeaning: （余裕・伸びしろ）ヘッドルーム、余地

examples.label: B
examples.text: The grand I got from the government is really going to give me some headroom and guards to my research.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 政府の助成金、研究に余裕ができる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I know the Japanese economy is pretty mature, but I think there's still a lot of headroom for growth.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 日本経済成熟してるけど、成長の余地まだある。

examples.label: D
examples.text: leeway
examples.japaneseTranslation: 余裕；余地

words.nativeDefinition: Room for growth or maneuver; leeway.
words.etymology: headroom（頭上の空間）の比喩的用法。

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜が）パイプラインに入っている、準備中

examples.label: B
examples.text: We have some great new products in the pipeline including a collab with Nike. It's going to be huge. We'll keep you updated.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ナイキコラボ含む素晴らしい新製品、準備中。続報。

examples.label: C
examples.text: We've got some exciting giveaways in the pipelines to stay tuned.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ワクワクするプレゼント、準備中。お楽しみに。

examples.label: D
examples.text: to progress your plan.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 準備中；進行中

words.nativeDefinition: Planned or in development.
words.etymology: in the pipeline（パイプラインの中）のビジネス比喩。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Unicles flagship item is definitely heat tech. I can't imagine living without it
examples.japaneseTranslation: ユニクロの看板商品は間違いなくヒートテック。なくてはならない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I just visited the Bergeri flagship store in London. It was so packed.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ロンドンのBurberry旗艦店行った。すごく混んでた。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Most important.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 最も重要な

words.nativeDefinition: The most important or representative product.
words.etymology: flagship（旗艦）の比喩的用法。

words.japaneseMeaning: 「〜って」「〜みたいに」（口語で誰かの発言を引用する際の言い淀み）

examples.label: B
examples.text: Grey can vitamin you to this event that he's going to after work. They're serving free drinks so I'm like, don't have to ask me twice.
examples.japaneseTranslation: グレーが仕事のあと行くイベントに誘ってくれたんだ。フリードリンクがあるって言うから、俺は「二つ返事で行くよ」って言っちゃった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My siblings were having a pretty heated discussion about the proper way to cut a eggplant. I was just like, can you guys just get it done? It's just an eggplant.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 兄弟がナスの切り方で盛り上がってた。俺は「とにかく切れよ、ただのナスだろ」って思った。

examples.label: D
examples.text: So yesterday I called Jean and she was like, why do you always call me when I'm at the gym? I've literally only done it once before.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 誰かが言った、または考えた

examples.label: E
examples.text: I can blame about my wife snoring yesterday and she's like, you can't talk. I snore like a cute little kitty but you snore like a lion.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昨日妻のいびきについて文句言ってたら、彼女が「あんたこそ言えないでしょ。私は可愛い子猫みたいにいびくのに、あんたはライオンみたい」って言われた。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Somebody said or thought.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 誰かが言った、または考えた

words.nativeDefinition: A casual filler used to introduce what someone said or thought, often before a direct quote.
words.etymology: like の引用用法。カジュアルな会話で「彼女が言ったんだけど」の代わりに she was like と言う現代口語表現。

words.japaneseMeaning: ただ〜なだけ／単に〜というだけ（本当の理由を補足する）

examples.label: B
examples.text: I don't normally play table tennis. It's just that it's going to be raining nonstop for a while, so I don't really want to play baseball right now.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 普段は卓球なんかしない。ただ、しばらく雨が降り続くらしいから、今は野球したくないだけなんだ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm not scared of flying or anything. It's just that I prefer taking the train because it's a lot more comfortable.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 飛行機が怖いとかじゃない。ただ電車の方がずっと快適だから、そっちを選ぶってだけ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I think we should live our lives the way we want. It's not a hard and fast rule though. It's just that I think we would be happier that way.
examples.japaneseTranslation: しかし／でも

examples.label: E
examples.text: I love burgers. It's just that I had three of them for dinner last night, so I don't really feel like having one right now.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ハンバーガーは大好き。ただ昨夜3個食べたから、今はあんまり食べたい気分じゃないんだ。

examples.label: F
examples.text: However
examples.japaneseTranslation: しかし／でも

words.nativeDefinition: Used to explain or clarify the real reason behind something, as a mild correction or addition.
words.etymology: just（ただ）と that の組み合わせで、「理由はこれだけ」と焦点を当てる表現。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜というわけではない（誤解を否定して本当の理由を述べる）

examples.label: B
examples.text: It's not that I hate studying, I just don't like studying for exams.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 勉強が嫌いなわけじゃない。ただ試験勉強が好きじゃないだけ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: It's not that I don't drink coffee, I just try not to have it after lunch.
examples.japaneseTranslation: コーヒーを飲まないわけじゃない。ただ昼食後は控えるようにしてるだけ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: It's not that I don't think fresh graduates will be able to learn on the job. I just think having a bit more training would be helpful.
examples.japaneseTranslation: そう言うのは正確ではない

examples.label: E
examples.text: It's not that I'm against online teaching. I just think that it'd be a lot harder to bring out the best in students if teachers can't see them face-to-face.
examples.japaneseTranslation: オンライン授業に反対なわけじゃない。ただ、先生が生徒の顔を直接見られないと、せいぜいを引き出すのがずっと難しくなると思う。

examples.label: F
examples.text: It's not exactly correct to say that.
examples.japaneseTranslation: そう言うのは正確ではない

words.nativeDefinition: Used to deny a simplistic or incorrect interpretation while offering a more nuanced explanation.
words.etymology: not that で「そういう意味ではない」と前置きし、後半で本音や本当の理由を述べる構文。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜というわけじゃない（相手の思い込みや大げさな解釈を否定する）

examples.label: B
examples.text: Why is she always asking me for the time? It's not like I'm better at checking my phone than she is.
examples.japaneseTranslation: なんで彼女はいつも俺に時間を聞くの？俺の方がスマホを見るのが上手いわけじゃないのに。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You have to stop telling people that I love extreme sports. I went skydiving once. It's not like I do crazy stuff every weekend or anything.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 俺がエクストリームスポーツが大好きだって人に言うのやめて。スカイダイビングは一度だけ。毎週末クレイジーなことしてるわけじゃない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I'm going to start my own YouTube channel this weekend. It's not like I'm getting anywhere just talking about it
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜という状況ではない

examples.label: E
examples.text: Don't worry about making mistakes. It's not like you're gonna die for them.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ミスを心配しなくていいよ。ミスしたからって死ぬわけじゃないでしょ。

examples.label: F
examples.text: It's not the case that.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜という状況ではない

words.nativeDefinition: Used to reject an assumption or exaggeration, emphasizing that something is not actually the case.
words.etymology: not like で「まるで〜かのように」と否定し、実際の状況とのギャップを示す。

words.japaneseMeaning: というより〜に近い（表現を修正・言い換える）

examples.label: B
examples.text: I didn't really break up with my X, it's more like we just drifted apart over time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 元カノと別れたというより、時間とともに自然と離れていった感じ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I didn't steal his idea. It's more like I borrowed his idea and put my own spin on it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼のアイデアを盗んだわけじゃない。借りて自分なりにアレンジしたって感じ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I'm not really on a first name basis with Brad Pitt. It's more like we spoke into each other a few times and I think he'd recognize me if you saw me.
examples.japaneseTranslation: そう言う方が正確だ

examples.label: E
examples.text: Making a mistake is not always a negative thing. It's more like it is an opportunity to learn a new thing.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ミスは必ずしもネガティブなことじゃない。新しいことを学ぶチャンスって感じの方が近い。

examples.label: F
examples.text: It's more correct to say that.
examples.japaneseTranslation: そう言う方が正確だ

words.nativeDefinition: Used to reframe or correct a description, suggesting a more accurate way to characterize something.
words.etymology: more like で「A というより B に近い」と、より正確な言い方へ修正する。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜って（形容詞）よね／〜なのが（形容詞）

examples.label: B
examples.text: It's kind of weird how drinking coffee at night makes me so sleepy.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 夜にコーヒー飲むとすごく眠くなるなんて、ちょっと変だよね。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Is it interesting how a smell can bring back so many memories?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 匂いでこんなに記憶が蘇るなんて、面白くない？

examples.label: D
examples.text: It's so lame how some people dish it out but can't take it themselves.
examples.japaneseTranslation: それは〜だ

examples.label: E
examples.text: It's really interesting how life can change in the blink of an eye. He was working at a convenience store until last year and now he's a politician.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 人生が一瞬で変わるなんて本当に面白いよね。去年までコンビニで働いてた人が、今は政治家になってる。

examples.label: F
examples.text: It's Azure to that.
examples.japaneseTranslation: それは〜だ

words.nativeDefinition: Used to comment on how surprising, odd, or noteworthy something is.
words.etymology: it's + 形容詞 + how の構文で、ある事柄の性質や程度に対する感想を述べる。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜だから（形容詞）／〜で（形容詞）なのは

examples.label: B
examples.text: I spent ages last night looking into how cryptocurrencies actually work. It's annoying because no matter how much I read about it, it still doesn't make sense to me.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昨夜、暗号通貨の仕組みを調べまくった。イライラするのは、どれだけ読んでも理解できないから。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Will says that he never takes a taxi home because he can't afford it. It's weird because he's always wearing really expensive stuff.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ウィルはタクシー代が払えないから帰らないって言う。変なのは、いつも高級な服を着てるのに。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Did you know Japan still has an emperor? It's confusing because Japan isn't an empire anymore.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 何が形容詞なの？

examples.label: E
examples.text: You should really invest in these stocks. It's great because they're still cheap right now, but we'll definitely grow rapidly over the next few years once everybody else finds out about them.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この株に投資すべきだよ。今はまだ安いし、みんな気づいたら今後数年でめちゃくちゃ伸びる。最高だよ。

examples.label: F
examples.text: What's adjective is?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 何が形容詞なの？

words.nativeDefinition: Used to express a feeling or judgment about something, followed by the reason.
words.etymology: it's + 形容詞 + because で、理由付きの評価・感情を述べる口語表現。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜なところが好き／〜なのが好き

examples.label: B
examples.text: I like how my co-worker randomly impersonates our boss.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 同僚が突然上司の真似をするのが面白くて好きなんだ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Of course I remember when we first met. I really liked how we hit it off straight off the bat.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 初めて会ったときのこと、もちろん覚えてるよ。すぐに意気投合できたのが本当に良かった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I love how my manager always puts up a fight before giving customers a refund, especially when it's obvious that they're wrong.
examples.japaneseTranslation: それが好き／それが大好き

examples.label: E
examples.text: I like how Frank always keeps me in the loop about anything that's taking longer than it should. There's nothing worse than people sitting on problems and not asking for help.
examples.japaneseTranslation: フランクがずっと遅れてることは全部教えてくれるのがいい。問題を抱え込んで助けを求めないのって最悪だから。

examples.label: F
examples.text: I like that. I love that.
examples.japaneseTranslation: それが好き／それが大好き

words.nativeDefinition: Used to express appreciation for a particular quality or aspect of something.
words.etymology: like/love + how で、ある点や様子への好意的な評価を表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: そうやって〜する／そういうやり方で

examples.label: B
examples.text: You need to set goals, put in the effort, achieve them, and repeat the process again and again. This is how you build self-confidence.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 目標を立てて、努力して、達成して、それを繰り返す。自信はそうやって築いていくものだ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I always hold the door open for the person behind me. That's how I was raised.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 後ろの人のためにいつもドアを開けてあげる。そう育てられたから。

examples.label: D
examples.text: You need to do more things that are outside of your comfort zone. That's how you learn and grow.
examples.japaneseTranslation: そういう方法だ／そういうやり方だ

examples.label: E
examples.text: My older brother would often drag me along to karaoke when I was a kid. That's all I started singing.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 子供の頃、兄がよくカラオケに連れていってくれた。歌い始めたきっかけはそれなんだ。

examples.label: F
examples.text: This is the way. That's the way.
examples.japaneseTranslation: そういう方法だ／そういうやり方だ

words.nativeDefinition: Used to explain the method or process by which something is achieved.
words.etymology: this/that's how で「こういう方法で」と手段・プロセスを説明する。

words.japaneseMeaning: こういう〜／ああいう〜（これから詳しく話す話題を導入する）

examples.label: B
examples.text: There were these things running around in my backyard in the middle of the night. Turns out it was just some squirrels, but I had to check to put my mind at ease.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 真夜中に裏庭を走り回ってる何かがいたんだ。リスだったみたいだけど、確認しないと安心できなくて。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You know what? I found this super cute girl on Tinder yesterday. She was like, way out of my league, but I got brave and liked her and we matched.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昨日 Tinder でめちゃくちゃ可愛い子を見つけたんだ。レベル高すぎたけど、勇気出していいね押したらマッチした。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I have this strange stare that if I pick up food dropped on the floor within three seconds, it's still okay.
examples.japaneseTranslation: これから詳しく話す話題

examples.label: E
examples.text: There's this word that perfectly describes what I'm feeling but I just can't remember it right now. It's on the tip of my tongue.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今の気持ちをぴったり表す言葉があるんだけど、思い出せない。舌の先まで来てるのに。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Something that I'm going to talk about more detail now.
examples.japaneseTranslation: これから詳しく話す話題

words.nativeDefinition: Used to introduce something you are about to talk about, often vaguely or emphatically.
words.etymology: this/these + 名詞 で、聞き手と共有する話題を「こういうやつ」と導入する口語表現。

examples.label: B
examples.text: To save time during the meeting, how about we send the materials out beforehand?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 会議の時間を節約するために、資料は事前に配布するのはどう？

examples.label: C
examples.text: How about we wait another month or so and see how this marketing campaign plays out? I think it's too early to change anything.
examples.japaneseTranslation: もう一ヶ月くらい待って、このマーケ施策の結果を見てから判断するのはどう？変更するには早すぎると思う。

examples.label: D
examples.text: This project is going nowhere right now. We don't have to go back to the drawing board, but how about we change tax slightly and bring in some people with new ideas.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 提案する

examples.label: E
examples.text: If you really don't like the spots on your face, how about you go to the skin clinic? Everybody that goes seems to really like it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 本当に顔のシミが気になるなら、皮膚科に行ってみたら？行った人みんな気に入ってるみたい。

examples.label: F
examples.text: I suggest.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 提案する

words.nativeDefinition: Used to make a suggestion or propose an idea.
words.etymology: how about で提案・勧誘を柔らかく行う口語表現。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜って（形容詞）よね！／めちゃくちゃ（形容詞）じゃない？

examples.label: B
examples.text: Have you seen that picture the Prime Minister fist bumping somebody who's trying to shake his hand? How funny is it?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 首相が握手しようとしてる人にフィストバンプしてる写真、見た？めちゃくちゃ面白くない？

examples.label: C
examples.text: I just found out that Australian-Lien's called Bell peppers, Cap's Cumbs. How cute is that? It sounds like Latin or something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: オーストラリア人はピーマンのことを Capsicum って呼ぶんだって。可愛くない？ラテン語みたい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: My crush came with me to the exam venue for Moral Support. How awesome is she?
examples.japaneseTranslation: とても形容詞な

examples.label: E
examples.text: How nice is this jacket? Can you believe I got it second hand?
examples.japaneseTranslation: このジャケットめちゃくちゃいいよね？中古で買ったの信じられる？

examples.label: F
examples.text: Something is very adjective.
examples.japaneseTranslation: とても形容詞な

words.nativeDefinition: A rhetorical exclamation emphasizing how adjective something is.
words.etymology: How + 形容詞 + is it? の倒置的な感嘆で、程度の高さを強調する。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜みたいなもの／〜のようなもの

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm finally getting the hang of skateboarding. It's like surfing on land.
examples.japaneseTranslation: やっとスケボーに慣れてきた。陸上サーフィンみたいなものだ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: If you've been a DisneyC, it's like DisneyLamp of For adults.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ディズニーCに行ったことがあるなら、大人向けのディズニーランドみたいなものだよ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I'm not really a hot dog person, but this one is actually amazing. It's like a burger that's been shaped to look like a hot dog.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 何かに似ている

examples.label: E
examples.text: Following on on our different seasons, there are different words that mean exactly the same thing. It's like saying chips instead of fries.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 季節によって違うって話の続きだけど、同じ意味の言葉が違う場合がある。フライの代わりにチップスって言うみたいな。

examples.label: F
examples.text: It's similar to something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 何かに似ている

words.nativeDefinition: Used to compare something to another thing, saying it is similar in nature.
words.etymology: it's like + 名詞/動名詞 で類似性を説明する比喩表現。

words.japaneseMeaning: まるで〜みたい／〜かのようだ

examples.label: B
examples.text: Stacey always seems to be super busy whenever I want to speak to her, it's like she's deliberately trying to avoid me or something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 話しかけたいときいつも超忙しそうなんだ。まるでわざと避けてるみたい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: All the stocks he bought last week are skyrocketing. It's like you can see into the future or something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 先週買った株が全部急騰してる。まるで未来が見えてるみたいだよ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: It's like you're always stressing about every little thing. Take it easy, man.
examples.japaneseTranslation: まるで〜のように

examples.label: E
examples.text: And that is really hard to read. It's like she really likes me, but doesn't want to meet me in person.
examples.japaneseTranslation: それを読むの本当に難しい。まるで彼女は俺のこと好きなのに、直接会いたくないみたい。

examples.label: F
examples.text: It's as if.
examples.japaneseTranslation: まるで〜のように

words.nativeDefinition: Used to suggest that something seems or feels as though a certain situation were true.
words.etymology: it's like + 節 で、推測や印象を「まるで〜のように」と表す。

words.headwordAudio: sbslike/015A it's kind of like sth_doing.mp3
words.japaneseMeaning: なんていうか〜みたいな／ちょっと〜っぽい

examples.label: B
examples.text: If you tried comb a child before, I tried it last week for the first time. It's kind of like really sweet tea, so it wasn't as bad as I thought it would be.
examples.japaneseTranslation: コンブチャ、初めて飲んだんだけど、甘いお茶みたいな感じで、思ったよりマシだった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Studying a language without understanding basic grammar isn't a great idea. It's kind of like playing sports without knowing the rules.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 基本文法を理解せずに語学を勉強するのは良くない。ルールを知らずにスポーツをするようなものだ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I've been playing this game called Little King Story lately. It's kind of like Pikmin but way funnier.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜と言えばそういうもの

examples.label: E
examples.text: This cake that I got for dessert is something else. It's kind of like a chocolate cake, but with layers of much in the middle.
examples.japaneseTranslation: デザートにもらったこのケーキ、別格だよ。なんていうかチョコレートケーキみたいだけど、中にムースの層がある。

examples.label: F
examples.text: I would describe it as something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜と言えばそういうもの

words.nativeDefinition: Used to describe something in approximate terms, comparing it loosely to something else.
words.etymology: kind of like で、はっきりした分類ではなく「なんとなく〜に似てる」と述べる。

words.japaneseMeaning: なんか〜みたい／ちょっと〜な感じ

examples.label: B
examples.text: Whenever I drink alcohol, it's kind of like my true self is revealed.
examples.japaneseTranslation: お酒を飲むと、なんか本当の自分が出てくる感じ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You've never been to Disneyland? You're missing out all the attractions and architecture there are just so beautiful man. It's kind of like you're in a different dimension.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ディズランド行ったことないの？アトラクションも建築もすごく綺麗なんだよ。別次元にいるみたい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: It's so strange how my lecture keeps asking me super hard questions. It's kind of like he's trying to embarrass me.
examples.japaneseTranslation: まるで〜のように

examples.label: E
examples.text: My room is so hot. It's kind of like I'm living inside an oven.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 部屋が暑すぎる。なんかオーブンの中に住んでるみたい。

examples.label: F
examples.text: It's as if.
examples.japaneseTranslation: まるで〜のように

words.nativeDefinition: Used to express that something feels or seems a certain way, with a sense of approximation.
words.etymology: kind of like + 節 で、印象や感覚をやわらかく「なんとなく〜みたい」と表現する。

words.japaneseMeaning: ほとんど〜みたいな／もう〜に近い

examples.label: B
examples.text: It's so hard for me to choose a favorite song from this album. It's almost like asking someone to pick their favorite child. They're all really good.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このアルバムで一曲選ぶの、ほとんど好きな子を選べって言われてるみたいで無理。全部良いんだもん。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Is driving an F1 car fun? You bet. It's almost like skydiving on the ground.
examples.japaneseTranslation: F1 車を運転するの楽しい？最高だよ。地上でスカイダイビングしてるみたい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: There was a crazy sale on flight so first thing this morning I booked a flight to Denmark. I just love traveling too much. It's almost like an addiction at this point.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ほぼ〜のようなもの

examples.label: E
examples.text: Everybody likes to act as if they know which crypto currencies are worth taking a chance on, but I still think it's almost like a coin flip.
examples.japaneseTranslation: みんな自分はどの暗号通貨に賭ける価値があるか知ってるふりしてるけど、俺に言わせればコインを投げるのとほぼ同じだと思う。

examples.label: F
examples.text: It's pretty much something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ほぼ〜のようなもの

words.nativeDefinition: Used to compare something closely to another thing, emphasizing strong similarity.
words.etymology: almost like + 名詞/動名詞 で、非常に近い類似性を強調する。

words.japaneseMeaning: もう〜も同然／まるで〜みたい

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm lying in bed eating hog and nuts right now. It's almost like you're going to heaven without dying.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今ベッドで豚肉とナッツ食べてる。死なずに天国に行ってるみたい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: These shoes are so comfortable. It's almost like I'm not wearing anything on my feet.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この靴めちゃくちゃ履き心地いい。足に何も履いてないみたい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Isn't it interesting how eyebrows stop growing when they reach a certain point? It's almost like they have a mind of their own, isn't it?
examples.japaneseTranslation: ほとんど〜のような気がする

examples.label: E
examples.text: I hate when Chris calls me it's all like he hits me I've only want to need something from me.
examples.japaneseTranslation: クリスから電話かかってくるの嫌なんだよね。なんか俺に何か必要なときだけ連絡してくるみたい。

examples.label: F
examples.text: It pretty much feels like.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ほとんど〜のような気がする

words.nativeDefinition: Used to express that a situation or feeling is very close to a certain state.
words.etymology: almost like + 節 で、状態や感覚が「もう〜も同然」とほぼ一致することを表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜みたいに／前にも言ったように

examples.label: B
examples.text: You shouldn't take his word for it. He must be blowing things out of proportion like he always does.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼の言うことを鵜呑みにしちゃダメ。いつもみたいに大げさに言ってるだけだよ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I try to kick the ball just like run all the dust but the ball doesn't travel anywhere near as fast.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ランニングの人みたいにボールを蹴ろうとしてるけど、全然速く飛んでかない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Are you still wondering why Tom wears sunglasses indoors? Maybe he just likes them. But like I said before, your guess is good as mine.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜のように

examples.label: E
examples.text: Like this, say, we must learn to walk before you can run. You should learn the basics of design first before finding your own style.
examples.japaneseTranslation: よく言うでしょ、走る前に歩き方を覚えなきゃいけないって。自分のスタイルを見つける前に、まずデザインの基礎を学ぶべきだ。

examples.label: F
examples.text: As
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜のように

words.nativeDefinition: Used as a discourse marker meaning 'as I said' or to draw a comparison ('as').
words.etymology: like は比較（〜のように）と引用・言及（前にも言ったように）の両方の用法がある。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜な気がする／〜みたいな感じがする

examples.label: B
examples.text: It feels like I've watched everything good on Netflix, and he defined something else to pass the time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Netflix で見る良い作品は全部見ちゃった気がする。他に時間つぶすものがない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: It feels like Steve's just been coasting along lately. I think he used to be a lot more passionate.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 最近スティーブはなんか適当にやってる感じ。以前はもっと情熱的だったのに。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I can't really prove this, but it feels like a lot of people think Columbia University is in Columbia, not the US.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜だと思う

examples.label: E
examples.text: It feels like all we ever do is argue about stupid stuff.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 俺たち、いつもバカみたいなことで喧嘩してる気がする。

examples.label: F
examples.text: I think that.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜だと思う

words.nativeDefinition: Used to express a subjective impression or sense that something is the case.
words.etymology: feel like + 節 で、確信ではなく主観的な印象・感覚を述べる。

words.headword: this is the kind/sort of thing
words.headwordAudio: sbslike/021A this is the kind_sort of thing.mp3
words.phonetic: ðɪs ɪz ðə kind/sort əv θɪŋ
words.japaneseMeaning: まさにそういうこと／そういう種類のもの

examples.label: B
examples.text: One of my old students texted me today telling me that she'd pass the exam. This is a kind of thing that makes me really happy.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昔教えた生徒から試験に合格したって連絡が来た。こういうのが本当に嬉しい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Wow, this new documentary about the history of life on Earth is really interesting. This is the sort of thing that really excites me.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 地球の生命史についての新しいドキュメンタリー、めちゃくちゃ面白い。まさにこういうのにワクワクする。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I have a client that keeps moving the goalpost. This is the kind of thing that happens when you don't have a written contract.
examples.japaneseTranslation: そういうタイプのもの

examples.label: E
examples.text: Look, this is the kind of thing that my bestie sends me. Yesterday it was a video of a chicken running while wearing sneakers, so dumb.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ほら、親友が送ってくるこういうやつ。昨日はスニーカー履いて走ってるニワトリの動画。めちゃくちゃバカ。

examples.label: F
examples.text: This is the type of thing that.
examples.japaneseTranslation: そういうタイプのもの

words.nativeDefinition: Used to characterize something as a particular type of thing that has a certain quality.
words.etymology: this is the kind/sort of thing that で、特定の性質を持つ典型例として紹介する。

words.headwordAudio: sbslike/022A a sth kind_sort of thing.mp3
words.japaneseMeaning: なんていうか〜みたいなもの／〜っぽいもの

examples.label: B
examples.text: A quality as a squirrel bear kind of thing. I don't know how else to describe it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: クオリッツみたいなリスと熊の中間みたいな感じ。他に言い方が思いつかない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This new phone is a flip phone sort of thing, but it has a touch screen.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この新しい携帯、折りたたみ式っぽいんだけどタッチスクリーン付き。

examples.label: D
examples.text: This drink looks like red wine, but it's actually a gin sort of thing. I don't know why it's red.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜とかそういうタイプのもの

examples.label: E
examples.text: I don't actually know what Chilato is, but it's an ice cream sort of thing.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ジラートが何なのか実はよくわからないけど、なんていうかアイスクリームっぽいもの。

examples.label: F
examples.text: or something type of thing.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜とかそういうタイプのもの

words.nativeDefinition: Used to describe something vaguely when you cannot think of a precise term.
words.etymology: a ... kind/sort of thing で、正確な名称が思い浮かばないときの曖昧な言い方。

examples.label: B
examples.text: This fast fashion thing is pretty bad for the environment so I've been trying to buy better quality closely.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ファストファッションって環境に良くないから、もっと質の良い服を買うようにしてる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm going to give this uber eat thing a try tonight. Excited.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今夜 Uber Eats 試してみる。楽しみ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I wonder how people do the whole minimalism thing? How much I declared in my room? It's still full of stuff.
examples.japaneseTranslation: よくわからないけどそういうもの

examples.label: E
examples.text: This Nintendo Ring Fit Adventure thing is awesome, I'm feeling the burn.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この Nintendo Ring Fit Adventure ってやつすごい。筋肉痛がきてる。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Something that I'm not quite familiar with.
examples.japaneseTranslation: よくわからないけどそういうもの

words.nativeDefinition: Used to refer to a trend, activity, or concept in a casual, sometimes dismissive way.
words.etymology: sth + thing で、流行や概念を「〜というやつ」とカジュアルに指す。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜かな／〜だと思う（自信なさげに）

examples.label: B
examples.text: I've been so busy lately that I haven't had any time to do things other than work. I need a right hand man who can handle business when I'm busy, I guess.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 最近忙しくて仕事以外の時間が全然ない。忙しいとき用の右腕が必要かな。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I think consciousness creates reality, so when viewed in a certain light, the world we're living in is almost like virtual reality, I guess.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 意識が現実を作ると思う。ある見方をすれば、俺たちの世界はほぼ VR みたいなものかな。

examples.label: D
examples.text: This box won't open. I need a key, I guess.
examples.japaneseTranslation: たぶん

examples.label: E
examples.text: I didn't get a bonus this year, but I did get a pay raise. That's a positive I guess.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今年ボーナスはもらえなかったけど、昇給はあった。プラス材料かな。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Maybe.
examples.japaneseTranslation: たぶん

words.nativeDefinition: Added at the end of a sentence to express uncertainty or a tentative conclusion.
words.etymology: I guess を文末に付け、推測や不確かさをやわらかく示すタグ用法。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜だと思う（個人的な見解を添える）

examples.label: B
examples.text: This project is in a good position, I think. So I'm going to head off a bit earlier tonight.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このプロジェクトは順調だと思う。だから今夜は少し早めに帰るつもり。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm pretty excited about tonight. The movie will be good, I think.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今夜楽しみ。映画も良さそうだと思う。

examples.label: D
examples.text: This red wine is penal noir, I think. It's definitely not Cabernet Sauvignon.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 私の意見では

examples.label: E
examples.text: Just send up your assignment already. It's not going to get any better, I think.
examples.japaneseTranslation: もう課題出しちゃいなよ。これ以上良くなるわけないと思う。

examples.label: F
examples.text: in my opinion.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 私の意見では

words.nativeDefinition: Added at the end of a sentence to express a personal opinion or belief.
words.etymology: I think を文末に付け、主観的な意見であることを示すタグ用法。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜だと思うけど（文中に挿入して意見であることを示す）

examples.label: B
examples.text: your English I think as a bit of a European accent where did you learn English?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 君の英語、I think ヨーロッパっぽいアクセントがあるけど、どこで習ったの？

examples.label: C
examples.text: Sorry to be blunt, but his presentation yesterday I think was the worst I'd ever seen. It was obvious that he was just going through the motions and it didn't seem to resonate with anyone.
examples.japaneseTranslation: はっきり言うと、昨日の彼のプレゼンは I think 今までで最悪だった。形だけで誰にも響いてなかった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: This I think is the best bar that I've been to in my life on a scale of 1 to 10. It's pretty close to a 10.
examples.japaneseTranslation: これはただの私見

examples.label: E
examples.text: This is I think the only CD that I still have around, I sold all the others on eBay.
examples.japaneseTranslation: これが I think 今残ってる唯一の CD。他は全部 eBay で売っちゃった。

examples.label: F
examples.text: This is just my opinion.
examples.japaneseTranslation: これはただの私見

words.nativeDefinition: I think inserted mid-sentence to qualify a statement as personal opinion.
words.etymology: I think を文中に挟み、述べる内容が個人的見解であると明示する。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I was waiting for the train last night, thinking about random stuff that had happened during the day, then I had a light bulb moment for a new product.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昨夜電車を待ってる間、一日の出来事をぼんやり考えてたら、新商品のアイデアがひらめいた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: On sunny weekends I like walking around going nowhere in particular.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 晴れた週末は、特に目的地もなくぶらぶら歩くのが好き。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I was flicking through some old photos today reminiscing about when I was a kid.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜している間に、誰かが〜していた

examples.label: E
examples.text: I was running on a treadmill listening to my favorite movie soundtrack. I felt like I was the main character in the movie.
examples.japaneseTranslation: トレッドミルで走りながら好きな映画のサントラを聞いてた。映画の主役になった気分だった。

examples.label: F
examples.text: And while doing so, somebody was doing.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜している間に、誰かが〜していた

words.nativeDefinition: A participial phrase describing what you were doing when something else happened.
words.etymology: 現在分詞を文頭・文中に置き、主な出来事と同時進行の背景動作を描写する。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜するのは（評価・コメントを添える）

examples.label: B
examples.text: Me reading too much into things that my girlfriend says to me is pretty normal. It might offend. The stuff she says is always so cryptic and wishy washi.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼女の言葉を深読みしすぎるのは普通。彼女の言うことはいつも曖昧で優柔不断だから。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My dog barking like that is a bit weird. He must be really hungry.
examples.japaneseTranslation: うちの犬があんな吠え方するのは変だね。すごくお腹空いてるに違いない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I hate Dean talking like that. It just sounds so smug.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 誰かが〜しているという事実

examples.label: E
examples.text: When I was in Italy, every time I came across and I'm familiar word I wrote it down. That was the beginning of me creating my own Italian vocabulary book.
examples.japaneseTranslation: イタリアにいたとき、知らない単語に出会うたびにメモした。それが自分だけのイタリア語単語帳を作り始めたきっかけ。

examples.label: F
examples.text: The fact that somebody is doing.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 誰かが〜しているという事実

words.nativeDefinition: A participial phrase commenting on someone's habitual or typical behavior.
words.etymology: sb/sth + doing で、主語の行動に対する評価や感想を前置きする口語構文。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜してる（人／物）（驚きや注目を伴う）

examples.label: B
examples.text: That cat chasing its own tail around is really funny. I thought only dogs did that.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 尻尾を追いかけ回ってるあの猫、面白いね。犬だけだと思ってた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I just watched a video of a guy eating 32 big max and one sitting insane.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 32 個のビッグマックを一気に食べてる男の動画を見た。狂ってる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Can you believe that woman dancing over there is my boss?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜している人／物

examples.label: E
examples.text: That kid playing the violin is only 13 years old. I'm trying to remember what I was doing at that age.
examples.japaneseTranslation: あのバイオリン弾いてる子、まだ13歳なんだ。自分があの年齢で何してたか思い出そうとしてる。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Somebody who is doing.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜している人／物

words.nativeDefinition: A participial phrase pointing out someone or something doing an action, often with surprise.
words.etymology: sb/sth + doing で、目の前の光景や状況を「あの〜してるやつ」と指し示す。

words.japaneseMeaning: これが〜してるところ（写真・動画を見せながら）

examples.label: B
examples.text: This is me having a great time when my friends at my last birthday party. It's a great photo, isn't it?
examples.japaneseTranslation: これが前回の誕生日パーティーで友達と楽しんでるところ。いい写真でしょ？

examples.label: C
examples.text: Look, this is my dog eating ice cream I dropped on the floor. Is it adorable?
examples.japaneseTranslation: ほら、うちの犬が床に落としたアイスクリーム食べてるとこ。可愛くない？

examples.label: D
examples.text: I'm going to show you a really hilarious video. This is me doing a Panama in front of the President during a meeting. It's funny how he's trying not to laugh, isn't it?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 誰かが〜している場面

examples.label: E
examples.text: This is me trying to speak Japanese for the first time. Pretty terrible, isn't it? I actually put this on YouTube when I was 19 and got tons of comments from Japanese viewers.
examples.japaneseTranslation: これが初めて日本語を話そうとしてるところ。ひどいでしょ？19歳のとき YouTube に上げたら、日本人からコメントめちゃくちゃもらった。

examples.label: F
examples.text: This is the scene that somebody doing.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 誰かが〜している場面

words.nativeDefinition: Used when showing a photo or video of yourself or someone doing something.
words.etymology: this is + doing で、映像や写真の内容を説明する際の定番表現。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜するのは〜だ（行為に対する評価を述べる）

examples.label: B
examples.text: This ramen shop putting whipped cream and soup, that's gross, who would order this.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このラーメン屋、スープに生クリーム入れるの？キモい。誰が注文するんだ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I don't mind making out wanting to play Jingle with me, but her telling me to get a life that's a bit harsh.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 一緒にジングルやりたがるのは構わない。でも「人生楽しめ」って言われるのはキツい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I can tolerate working extra hours, but having to work on weekends, that's a different story.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 誰かが〜しているのは形容詞

examples.label: E
examples.text: I think canceling a date a few days before it's supposed to happen is okay. Ghosting somebody, that's pretty rude.
examples.japaneseTranslation: デートの数日前にキャンセルするのは別にいいと思う。でも突然連絡を完全に断つのは、それはかなり失礼だよ。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Somebody doing his adjective.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 誰かが〜しているのは形容詞

words.nativeDefinition: Commenting on an action, then evaluating it with 'that's adj/sth'.
words.etymology: doing, that's ... の構文で、ある行為と別の行為を対比し評価を下す。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜したら〜だろうな（仮定の行為に対する評価）

examples.label: B
examples.text: A Starbucks opening right next to my apartment, that'd be amazing. I study there almost every day.
examples.japaneseTranslation: アパートの隣にスタバができるなんて、最高じゃん。ほぼ毎日そこで勉強してる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I don't really know what there is to do there, but you and I go to New Orleans for a few days. That'd be pretty fun.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ニューオーリンズに数日行くの、結構楽しそうじゃない？

examples.label: D
examples.text: bumping into my exit at restaurant, that'd be pretty awkward. We haven't spoken in years.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 誰かが〜するのは形容詞だろう

examples.label: E
examples.text: Me learning a new language, that'd be pretty hard at my age, still better late than ever.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 俺が新しい言語を学ぶのは、この年齢だと結構大変だろうな。それでも遅くないに越したことはないけど。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Somebody doing would be adjective.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 誰かが〜するのは形容詞だろう

words.nativeDefinition: Commenting on a hypothetical action and saying it would be a certain way.
words.etymology: doing, that'd be ... で、もしそうなったらどうなるかを評価する。

words.japaneseMeaning: （人に）〜させる／（人に）〜させられている

examples.label: B
examples.text: When I was a kid I loved reading but my parents would have me studying instead. At the time they wouldn't have thought that I'd become a novelist.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 子供の頃、読書が好きだったのに親は勉強させられた。小説家になるなんて思ってなかっただろうね。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My boss has me going crazy. Every time you refuse my work, he goes into all this needy gritty that nobody cares about.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 上司にイライラさせられてる。仕事を断るたびに誰も興味ない細かい話を始める。

examples.label: D
examples.text: One of the things that they had me doing when I first joined the company was buying coffee for the team.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人に）〜させる

examples.label: E
examples.text: Do you want to drop by for a few games of FIFA tomorrow? My mom usually has me doing chores until after lunch, so maybe some time after that.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 明日 FIFA 数戦しに寄ってかない？母が昼過ぎまで雑用させるから、そのあとなら。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Make somebody do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人に）〜させる

words.nativeDefinition: To cause or require someone to do something, often repeatedly or against their preference.
words.etymology: have + 人 + doing で、使役・継続的な要求を表す。have sb do より進行・反復のニュアンス。

words.japaneseMeaning: （人を）〜させる／（人を）〜な気分にさせる

examples.label: B
examples.text: I've been watching all these chibbly films and they've got me thinking why does chibbly food look so good?
examples.japaneseTranslation: ジブリ映画ばかり見てて、なんでジブリの料理はあんなに美味しそうに見えるんだろうって考えさせられる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: The hot weather's got me waking up super early every morning so I've been pretty tired by the time I get to work lately. I'll hit the sack early tonight.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 暑さのせいで毎朝超早く目が覚めるようになった。仕事着く頃には結構疲れてる。今夜は早く寝よう。

examples.label: D
examples.text: This song always gets me feeling some type of way. I can't explain exactly what it is though.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人に）〜させる原因になる

examples.label: E
examples.text: This video got me laughing like nuts.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この動画見て、めちゃくちゃ爆笑しちゃった。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Cause somebody's to do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人に）〜させる原因になる

words.nativeDefinition: To cause someone to start doing or feeling something, often as a reaction.
words.etymology: get + 人 + doing で、何かの影響で自然とその状態・行動に至ることを表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I love working in a bar because I get to drink whatever I feel like for free. Dan, day out.
examples.japaneseTranslation: バーで働いてるのが好きなのは、好きなものを無料で飲めるから。最高だよ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Yesterday was the best day of my life. I got to talk with you Jack member for his play. It was a dream come true.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昨日は人生最高の日。ジャック・メンバーと話せた。夢が叶った。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I got to drive a Tesla for the first time today was amazing. Everything felt super smooth.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜する機会がある

examples.label: E
examples.text: Because of work, I don't get to work out as much as I used to, so I'm going to start doing 50 minute exercises to get back into the swing of things.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 仕事のせいで、昔ほど運動できなくなったから、50分のエクササイズを始めて元のリズムに戻そうと思ってる。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Have the opportunity to do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜する機会がある

words.nativeDefinition: To have the opportunity or permission to do something.
words.etymology: get to do は機会・許可・特権を得て何かができることを表す口語。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Daniel was so awkward last night. I don't know where everybody got chatting about politics and stuff. I don't read the news, but I still somehow managed to get through it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ダニエル昨夜めちゃくちゃぎこちなかった。みんな政治の話してるのに、ニュース読まない俺でもなんとか乗り切った。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You're right, we have to get going or we'll be late. I'm glad you're on the ball.
examples.japaneseTranslation: その通り、急がないと遅刻する。頼りになるね。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I just booked a lesson with an accent coach. I'm so excited to get working on my pronunciation.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜し始める

examples.label: E
examples.text: Today I'm going to show you how to make a meat and vegetable pine just 50 minutes. Let's get cooking!
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今日は肉と野菜のパイの作り方を50分で紹介する。さあ、料理始めよう！

examples.label: F
examples.text: Start doing.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜し始める

words.nativeDefinition: To start doing something, often with energy or urgency.
words.etymology: get + 動名詞/動詞原形 で「さあ始めよう」と行動開始を促す口語。

words.headword: be going to have to do
words.headwordAudio: sbslike/037A be going to have to do.mp3
words.phonetic: bi goʊɪŋ tɪ hæv tɪ du
words.japaneseMeaning: 〜しなきゃいけなくなる／〜せざるを得ない

examples.label: B
examples.text: It'd be great if we had free health care in Japan, but somebody's going to have to pay for it, right? It's pretty pie in the sky.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 日本で無料医療があれば最高だけど、誰かが払わなきゃいけないでしょ？非現実的な話。

examples.label: C
examples.text: How are your French lessons coming along? I'm going to have to brush up on my Spanish soon too. Use it or lose it?
examples.japaneseTranslation: フランス語の勉強どう？俺もそろそろスペイン語を復習しなきゃ。使わないと忘れるよね。

examples.label: D
examples.text: New Zealand looks so beautiful. I'm going to have to visit there one day. It's on my bucket list.
examples.japaneseTranslation: いずれ誰かが〜しなければならない

examples.label: E
examples.text: Sorry guys, we're closing soon so I'm going to have to ask you to leave.
examples.japaneseTranslation: すみません、もうすぐ閉店するので、お帰りいただかなきゃいけません。

examples.label: F
examples.text: We'll probably have to do at some point in time. Unfortunately, somebody has to do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: いずれ誰かが〜しなければならない

words.nativeDefinition: Expressing that someone will inevitably need to do something in the future.
words.etymology: be going to have to do で、将来避けられない義務・必要を述べる。

words.headword: be going to need to do
words.headwordAudio: sbslike/038A be going to need to do.mp3
words.phonetic: bi goʊɪŋ tɪ nid tɪ du

examples.label: B
examples.text: If you want to quickly learn phrases that native English speakers use, you're going to need to get a really good textbook like distinction.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ネイティブが使うフレーズを早く覚えたいなら、Distinction みたいな良い教材が必要だよ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm going to need to learn how to drive sooner or later because I want to move to the US eventually.
examples.japaneseTranslation: いずれ運転免許取らなきゃ。いつかアメリカに移住したいから。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Unfortunately, we're going to need to make some budget cuts next year. This year's performance hasn't been great.
examples.japaneseTranslation: いずれ誰かが〜する必要がある

examples.label: E
examples.text: I know you're still on up on your X, but if you really want to move on with your life, you're going to need to cut ties with him.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 元カノのことまだ引きずってるのはわかるけど、本当に前に進みたいなら、彼とは縁を切る必要があるよ。

examples.label: F
examples.text: We'll probably need to do it some point in time. Unfortunately, somebody needs to do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: いずれ誰かが〜する必要がある

words.nativeDefinition: Expressing that someone will need to do something, often as a requirement.
words.etymology: be going to need to do で、今後必要になる行動を述べる。

words.headwordAudio: sbslike/039A be going to need sth.mp3

examples.label: B
examples.text: We're going to need a first cut of this video by tomorrow morning if you want to post it by the weekend.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 週末に投稿したいなら、明日の朝までにこの動画の初稿が必要だよ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I know you're busy but I'm going to need your help on this.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 忙しいのはわかるけど、これ手伝ってほしい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: To build a successful business, you're going to need time and money.
examples.japaneseTranslation: いずれ何かが必要になる

examples.label: E
examples.text: I'm gonna need new jobs soon. This one's fine, but I'm just a bit stuck in a rat.
examples.japaneseTranslation: そろそろ新しい仕事が必要かも。この仕事も悪くないけど、ちょっと行き詰まってる感じ。

examples.label: F
examples.text: We'll probably need something at some point in time. Unfortunately, somebody needs something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: いずれ何かが必要になる

words.nativeDefinition: Expressing that something will be needed.
words.etymology: be going to need + 名詞 で、今後必要になるものを述べる。

words.headwordAudio: sbslike/040A be going to be doing.mp3
words.japaneseMeaning: 〜することになる／（これから）〜する予定

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm going to be studying in the library for the whole afternoon because I've been exam coming up on Tuesday.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 火曜に試験があるから、午後ずっと図書館で勉強する予定。

examples.label: C
examples.text: In this video today, I'm going to be talking about how to make the best fresh watermelon juice.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今日の動画では、最高のスイカジュースの作り方について話していく。

examples.label: D
examples.text: It was a pretty last-minute decision, but I'm going to be living in Africa for the next few months. My friend asked me if I wanted to go and work for a charity with him.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜する予定だ

examples.label: E
examples.text: Apple is going to be announcing its new products in a few days, so you should keep your eyes peeled. They're going to be epic for sure.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Apple が数日以内に新製品を発表する予定だから、注目しておいた方がいい。間違いなくエピックになるよ。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Be going to do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜する予定だ

words.nativeDefinition: Used to describe a planned or ongoing future activity over a period of time.
words.etymology: be going to be doing で、将来の継続的な活動や予定を述べる。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Hey do you want to go have lunch? I know a good place.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ランチ行かない？いい店知ってるよ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Sir, I understand where you're coming from, but my hands are tied. Let me go talk to the store manager to see if there's anything we can do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: お気持ちはわかりますが、こちらでは対応できません。店長に話しに行ってみます。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Why don't we go play golf today? The weather couldn't be better.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 行って〜する

examples.label: E
examples.text: I just heard some girls complaining about how their boyfriend never buy them stuff or something along those lines. I'm like, shut up and go buy stuff for yourself.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼氏がプレゼント買ってくれないとか文句言ってる女の子の話を聞いた。俺は「黙って自分で買えよ」って思った。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Go and do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 行って〜する

words.nativeDefinition: Used to suggest going somewhere to do something.
words.etymology: go + 動詞原形 は口語で「〜しに行く」という提案・行動を表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: We've opened our new shop in San Francisco, so come visit us.
examples.japaneseTranslation: サンフランシスコに新店舗をオープンしたから、ぜひ来てね。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I wish my girlfriend would come see me, but that's just wishful thinking because she's really busy right now.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼女が会いに来てくれたらいいのに。今忙しいから無理なんだけど。

examples.label: D
examples.text: We'll be having a language exchange event tomorrow, so come join us if you have time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 来て〜する

examples.label: E
examples.text: Don't be a killjoy. Come drink with us after work.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ノリ殺しすぎ。仕事のあと一緒に飲みに来てよ。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Common Do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 来て〜する

words.nativeDefinition: An invitation to come and do something or visit somewhere.
words.etymology: come + 動詞原形 で「来て〜しよう」という招待・勧誘を表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜してくれない／どうしても〜しない

examples.label: B
examples.text: My app would open, I tried installing and reinstall it, but no luck.
examples.japaneseTranslation: アプリが開かない。何度も入れ直したけどダメ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: When I was in high school my parents wouldn't never let me stay out past nine. Do you think they were being overprotective?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 高校の頃、親は9時過ぎて外に出させてくれなかった。過保護すぎた？

examples.label: D
examples.text: I was going to push myself and finish this assignment tonight, but my laptop won't start. I hope it's not broken.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜しない／〜しなかった

examples.label: E
examples.text: Can I borrow your ladder? My cat won't come down from the tree.
examples.japaneseTranslation: はしご貸してくれる？うちの猫が木から降りてこないんだ。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Doesn't do. Didn't do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜しない／〜しなかった

words.nativeDefinition: Something refuses to work, function, or cooperate.
words.etymology: won't/wouldn't do で、物や人が意図通りに動作・協力しないことを擬人化して表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜だったらしい／〜だとわかった

examples.label: B
examples.text: I was so amazed by this video of a cat dancing with a duck. Turns out it was all fake.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 猫がアヒルと踊ってる動画に感動してたんだけど、全部フェイクだったらしい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Turns out that this guy was arguing with on Twitter about cooking steak was a chef. The worst part is that I knew I was wrong from the start. I should have quit while I was at head.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Twitter でステーキの焼き方で議論してた相手、シェフだったらしい。最初から間違ってるのわかってたのに。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I thought the good turnout at my concert was because people really loved my music. Turns out it was because Greg told everybody that drinks were free. How sad is that?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 驚くべきことに

examples.label: E
examples.text: Turns out my five-year-old daughter already knows Santa doesn't exist. What a shocker.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 5歳の娘、もうサンタがいないって知ってたらしい。衝撃だよね。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Surprisingly.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 驚くべきことに

words.nativeDefinition: Used to reveal an unexpected fact or outcome.
words.etymology: turns out で、後から判明した事実を「実は〜だった」と述べる。

words.japaneseMeaning: （気づいたら）〜している／〜してしまう

examples.label: B
examples.text: I found myself just coasting along doing nothing lately. I need to snap out of it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 最近なんもせずダラダラしてる自分に気づいた。早く切り替えないと。

examples.label: C
examples.text: In the last couple of months, I found myself stepping up and playing the role of manager. I'm really proud of myself.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ここ数ヶ月、気づいたらマネージャーの役割を引き受けてた。自分を誇りに思う。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I decided to quit my old job because the workload was getting out of hand and I found myself always having to put my hobbies and friends on hold.
examples.japaneseTranslation: なんとなく〜している

examples.label: E
examples.text: I often find myself keeping an eye on the stock market. I just don't want to miss the boat in the next big thing.
examples.japaneseTranslation: よく気づいたら株価をチェックしてる。次の大きなチャンスを逃したくないから。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Somehow I'm up doing.
examples.japaneseTranslation: なんとなく〜している

words.nativeDefinition: To realize you are doing something, often unintentionally or habitually.
words.etymology: find oneself doing で、意図せず・不知不識にその行動に至ったことを表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: 仮に〜だとしたら／例えば〜なら

examples.label: B
examples.text: Let's say I went to that speed dating thing last Friday. Do you really think I would have found my dream girl? I doubt it. That's why I never go to those things.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 仮に先週の婚活パーティーに行ったとして、本当に理想の女性に出会えたと思う？無理でしょ。だから行かないんだ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Let's say your wife becomes a breadwinner of the family and you don't have to work as much as you do now. What do you be happy with that?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 仮に奥さんが家計を支えて、今ほど働かなくていいとしたら、幸せ？

examples.label: D
examples.text: Let's say I was giving a presentation to a client, right? Do you think it would look bad if I read from a script?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 仮定する

examples.label: E
examples.text: Let's say your team is here and your parents are super generous and give you $10,000. What would you do?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 仮に20代で、親がすごく寛大で1万ドルくれて、何に使う？

examples.label: F
examples.text: Let's assume.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 仮定する

words.nativeDefinition: Used to introduce a hypothetical scenario for discussion.
words.etymology: let's say で仮定の状況を設定し、議論や思考実験を始める。

examples.label: B
examples.text: It's taking Liz way too long to decide if she wants to be a girlfriend or not. I have the feeling that she's treating you as an insurance policy, so I say it's time you force the issue.
examples.japaneseTranslation: リズが彼女になるか決めるのに時間かかりすぎ。保険として扱ってるだけだと思う。もうはっきりさせた方がいい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I say we draw a diagram rather than try to explain our idea in a very long essay after all a picture is worth a thousand words.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 長いエッセイで説明するより図を描こう。百聞は一見に如かずだ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I say we let Josh have free reign over what we have dinner tonight. His birthday is next week, but for all intents and purposes, this is birthday dinner.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜すべきだと思う

examples.label: E
examples.text: If you really like that code, I say stop over thinking it and just buy it. Even if it breaks the bank, you'll never find another one who's actually like it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 本当にそのコートが好きなら、考えすぎるのやめて買っちゃえ。背中をかき切っても、あんなにぴったりのは二度と見つからない。

examples.label: F
examples.text: I think somebody should do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜すべきだと思う

words.nativeDefinition: Used to propose what you think should be done.
words.etymology: I say + 節 で、強めの提案・意見を述べる口語表現。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜だと思う（個人的な見積もり・評価）

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'd say Bashing in Laundry is a lot more efficient than doing little loads of laundry every day.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 洗濯はまとめてやる方が、毎日少しずつより効率的だと思う。

examples.label: C
examples.text: A lot of economists act as if they can predict the future, but I'd say it's usually more art than science.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 経済学者は未来を予測できるかのように振る舞うけど、科学というより芸術に近いと思う。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I'd say Manchester City controlled the game pretty well today. What are your thoughts?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 私の意見では

examples.label: E
examples.text: Most billionaires make it sound like they made big bucks by being really smart and hardworking, but I'd say there's a pretty large element of luck involved in their success.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 大富豪はみんな、すごく賢くて勤勉だったから稼げたみたいに話すけど、成功にはかなり大きな運の要素があると思う。

examples.label: F
examples.text: in my opinion.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 私の意見では

words.nativeDefinition: Used to give a personal estimate or opinion.
words.etymology: I'd say で、控えめながらも自分の意見・推測を述べる。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜って言うだろう／〜と言われる

examples.label: B
examples.text: When we have grandchildren, they'll look at the fondre's using today and say, I can't believe you thought these were amazing.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 孫が今のフォンデを見て「こんなのすごいと思ってたなんて信じられない」って言うだろうね。

examples.label: C
examples.text: If you tried this cucumber with honey is an interesting combination, you're going to eat and say, it tastes like melon.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このキュウリにハチミツ、試して食べたら「メロンみたい」って言うよ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Why is this jacket so expensive? I think most normal people would look at this and say, why are you wearing a trash bag?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜と言うだろう

examples.label: E
examples.text: I want people to see my products and say they look like something from the future.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 人に僕の製品を見てもらって、未来から来たみたいだって言ってほしい。

examples.label: F
examples.text: and state were asked.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜と言うだろう

words.nativeDefinition: Used to describe what people would say in reaction to something.
words.etymology: and say で、他者の反応・評価を引用形式で予測する。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Most people will look at your half-shaped head and think, did he do it on purpose?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 半分剃った頭を見た人は「わざとやったの？」と思うだろう。

examples.label: C
examples.text: When I'm walking in a forest, sometimes I stop and think, what would these trees tell us if they could speak?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 森を歩いてると、たまに立ち止まって「木たちが話せたら何を言うだろう」と考える。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Last night I was playing with my cat and thought wouldn't it be a great idea to get another one?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜と思う／〜と気づく

examples.label: E
examples.text: I don't want people to see my Ferrari and think this guy has way too much money. It took me years to save up for it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: フェラーリを見て「この人お金持ちすぎ」って思われたくない。何年かかって貯めたんだから。

examples.label: F
examples.text: and ask or realize.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜と思う／〜と気づく

words.nativeDefinition: Used to describe what people would think in reaction to something.
words.etymology: and think で、他者の内心の反応を予測する。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜するだろう（相手の反応を予測する）

examples.label: B
examples.text: I really want to buy a new car but I don't think my wife would let me. She got super mad when she found out I bought a new walk without telling her last month, so I better not risk it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 新しい車買いたいけど、奥さんは許してくれないだろうね。先月こっそり時計買ったら激怒されたし。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Can you help us move our furniture to our new house? I pay you at least $2 for it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 引っ越し手伝ってくれない？200 ドルは払うよ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: You should definitely attend a seminar. It would change your whole perspective on life.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜するだろうと思う

examples.label: E
examples.text: You never met John, he has this huge affron as always wearing a red jacket, so you'd recognize it's him straight away.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ジョンに会ったことないでしょ。でっかいアフロでいつも赤いジャケット着てるから、すぐわかるよ。

examples.label: F
examples.text: I think somebody will do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜するだろうと思う

words.nativeDefinition: Used to predict how someone would react or behave in a situation.
words.etymology: would do で、仮定の状況での相手の反応を推測する。

examples.label: B
examples.text: American tends to draw the key in the word center, but British people would usually pronounce it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: アメリカ人は center の t を消すけど、イギリス人は普通発音する。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Localization refers to tingling your products to meet local needs. A good example of that would be Starbucks and Japan. They have stuff like cherry blossom, frappuccinos and hojishalates.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ローカライズの良い例は日本のスタバ。桜フラペチーノとか焙じ茶ラテとか。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I gave him my business card a long time ago, so I'm not sure if he'd still have it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜するものだと思う

examples.label: E
examples.text: My interpretation of this data would be that we would have to completely change our strategy to win the competition.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このデータの私の解釈だと、競争に勝つには戦略を完全に変えなきゃいけないことになる。

examples.label: F
examples.text: I think somebody does.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜するものだと思う

words.nativeDefinition: Used to describe typical or habitual behavior, or what is generally done.
words.etymology: would do で、一般的な傾向・習慣・典型例を述べる。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I suggest that you take notes during my class because the slides won't be uploaded on the student website.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 授業中メモ取った方がいいよ。スライドは学生サイトにアップされないから。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'd recommend you get some sleep over the weekend. You look so tired.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 週末は睡眠取った方がいい。すごく疲れて見える。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Taxes in Switzerland are really expensive so I wouldn't recommend them.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 提案する／お勧めする

examples.label: E
examples.text: I suggest you spend at least four weeks in Europe. There's so much to see.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ヨーロッパには最低4週間は滞在した方がいいと思う。見るものがたくさんあるから。

examples.label: F
examples.text: I suggest I recommend.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 提案する／お勧めする

words.nativeDefinition: Used to give advice or a recommendation.
words.etymology: I'd suggest/recommend + that で、助言・推薦を述べる丁寧な表現。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Man, this TV show is such a throwback. I remember how I'd sit in front of the TV for hours after school watching it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この番組懐かしい。放課後テレビの前に何時間も座って見てたもんだ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My college years were pretty rough. A most days I would work at a restaurant for a few hours before class and then a bar or club at night.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 大学時代は大変だった。授業前にレストラン、夜はバーかクラブで働く日が多かった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: My dad would feed my pet chickens left over a chicken nuggets when I was a kid. How fun is that?
examples.japaneseTranslation: （昔）よく〜した

examples.label: E
examples.text: At every training session my coach would always underline the importance of knowing your teammates like the back of your hand.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 練習のたびにコーチは、チームメイトを手の平のように知ることの大切さを強調してた。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Somebody used to do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （昔）よく〜した

words.nativeDefinition: Used to describe past habitual actions.
words.etymology: would do で過去の習慣・反復的行動を表す（used to に近い）。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'd lock down the lunch reservation as soon as possible, that place gets super busy.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ランチの予約は早めに確保した方がいい。あそこ超混むから。

examples.label: C
examples.text: They offered you a promotion with no pay raise. I definitely push for one. You can't do more work for the same amount of money.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昇進したのに給料上がらない？絶対交渉すべき。同じ給料で仕事増えるなんて。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I'd just the water before telling your wife that you don't really like her cooking. You could open up a can of worms, you know.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜すべき／私なら〜する

examples.label: E
examples.text: I'd be on my best behavior around Tesla. She's a very serious person.
examples.japaneseTranslation: テスラの前では真面目に振る舞うべきだと思う。すごく厳しい人だから。

examples.label: F
examples.text: You should do. I would do if I were you.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜すべき／私なら〜する

words.nativeDefinition: Used to say what you would do in someone's situation (giving advice).
words.etymology: I'd do で、助言として「私ならこうする」と述べる。

words.japaneseMeaning: （仮定の過去なら）〜しただろう

examples.label: B
examples.text: I wish YouTube had been around when I was a teenager. I would have been able to make so much money with it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 10 代の頃 YouTube があれば、めちゃくちゃ稼げたのに。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I can't believe you found the NBA match boring. You should have given me your tickets. I would have had a whale of a time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: NBA の試合退屈だなんて。チケット譲ってくれれば、最高に楽しめたのに。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Did your boyfriend give you a bunch of roses on your birthday? That's so nice, your actual note done that, Woody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 仮定の状況なら〜しただろう

examples.label: E
examples.text: I thought he put Frank in the team to be the Trump card. Why did he leave him on the bench for the whole game? I would have put him on by half time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: フランクを切り札としてチームに入れたと思ってた。なんで試合中ずっとベンチにいたの？俺ならハーフタイムまでには出す。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Somebody would have done it at the hypothetical situation and been true.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 仮定の状況なら〜しただろう

words.nativeDefinition: Used to express what would have happened in a hypothetical past situation.
words.etymology: would've done で、過去の仮定条件での結果を述べる。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜したはずだ／〜していただろう

examples.label: B
examples.text: My grandparents would listen to music using this old record player when they were kids.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 祖父母が子供の頃、この古いレコードプレーヤーで音楽を聴いてた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This job would have been used by ancient Egyptians to store food and water.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この壺は古代エジプト人が食料や水を保存するのに使ってたはず。

examples.label: D
examples.text: My cousin lived in Moscow for a few years, so he would have experienced first-hand how cold it can get there.
examples.japaneseTranslation: おそらく〜した

examples.label: E
examples.text: You started this company in 2001. That means I would have been like five or six when you started your own business.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この会社2001年に始めたんでしょ。つまり君が起業したとき、俺は5、6歳くらいだったってことだ。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Somebody probably did.
examples.japaneseTranslation: おそらく〜した

words.nativeDefinition: Used to infer that something probably happened or existed in the past.
words.etymology: would've done で、過去の状況からの推定・推測を表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜だと思っていた（実際は違った）

examples.label: B
examples.text: He really seems like he's in his element. Doesn't he? I would've thought that he'd hate dancing at a club.
examples.japaneseTranslation: すごく楽しそうだね。クラブで踊るの嫌がると思ってたのに。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Did you guys have to pay for your own food? I would have thought that having lunch with the boss would mean that the bill is taken care of.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 自分で食事代払ったの？上司とランチなら会社持ちだと思ってた。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I would have thought that you had to be tall to be a professional basketball player, but apparently not. This XMBA player is only 5 foot 3 inches.
examples.japaneseTranslation: そうだと思ったが、実際は違った

examples.label: E
examples.text: It's no one ever told you that you look like Cartman from South Park. I would have thought that you'd get that a lot.
examples.japaneseTranslation: サウスパークのカートマンに似てるって誰も言わなかったの？よく言われると思ってたのに。

examples.label: F
examples.text: I thought although that wasn't the case.
examples.japaneseTranslation: そうだと思ったが、実際は違った

words.nativeDefinition: Used to express surprise that something is not as expected.
words.etymology: I would've thought that で、当然そうだと思っていたが違った、という驚きを表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I think that flight star Australia would be fairly cheap in October. There's usually pretty good deals on flights to most places at that time of the year.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 10 月のオーストラリア行き航空券はそこそこ安いと思う。あの時期はどこもセールが多い。

examples.label: C
examples.text: How old do I think you are? Hmm, that's a tricky question. I think you're in your early 20s.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 何歳に見える？難しい質問。20 代前半かな。

examples.label: D
examples.text: There's so much hype around the cafe that just opened across from the office. I think that it would be pretty busy for the next few weeks.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜だと想定する

examples.label: E
examples.text: I think that a leopard print table will look pretty out of place on my simple living room, but let's just try it out.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ヒョウ柄のテーブルはシンプルなリビングには浮きそうだけど、とりあえず試してみよう。

examples.label: F
examples.text: I assume.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜だと想定する

words.nativeDefinition: Used to express a tentative assumption or guess.
words.etymology: I'd think that で、控えめな推測・想定を述べる。

examples.label: B
examples.text: You think Japan would be really technologically advanced. But the other day, I was asked to print a copy of my ID, glued onto a form and physically post it just to get a credit card.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 日本はすごく技術先進国だと思うけど、先日クレカ申込みで ID のコピーを印刷して糊付けして郵送しろって言われた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You think this blue robot character would be a raccoon or something but he's actually a cat?
examples.japaneseTranslation: この青いロボキャラ、ラaccoon かと思うけど、実は猫なんだ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: You think someone would have come up with a better way to land a plane by now? It's just so bumpy.
examples.japaneseTranslation: そう思うのも無理はないが、実際は違う

examples.label: E
examples.text: You think I'd be rich because I worked for a big company but no. We're pretty much just slaves to the executives that are making all the money.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 大企業で働いてるから金持ちだと思うでしょ。でも違う。上層部が儲けてる金の奴隷みたいなもんだ。

examples.label: F
examples.text: It's not the case, but it's understandable that you think.
examples.japaneseTranslation: そう思うのも無理はないが、実際は違う

words.nativeDefinition: Used when something is surprisingly not as one would expect.
words.etymology: you'd think that で「普通はそう思うよね」という期待と現実のギャップを示す。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜かもしれない／〜する可能性がある

examples.label: B
examples.text: I ordered my girlfriends birthday present really early because they said it could take up to six weeks to deliver.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼女の誕生日プレゼント早めに注文した。届くまで6週間かかるかもしれないって。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My manager could back me for promotion, but I'm not sure she will, and she told you anything.
examples.japaneseTranslation: マネージャーが昇進を後押ししてくれるかもしれないけど、わからない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: It feels like anything could happen in this match. Both teams have been on fire lately.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜するかもしれない

examples.label: E
examples.text: We could all be using electric cars in the next 20 years. Of course it's not a certain me, but you definitely can't rule it out.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 20年以内にみんな電気自動車を使ってるかもしれない。確実じゃないけど、可能性はゼロじゃない。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Somebody might do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜するかもしれない

words.nativeDefinition: Used to express possibility that something might happen.
words.etymology: could do で将来の可能性・不確実性を表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Some day AI could replace most jobs, but I don't think it's mature enough yet.
examples.japaneseTranslation: いつか AI が大部分の仕事を置き換えるかもしれないけど、まだ成熟してないと思う。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Elon Musk says humans can land on Mars within a decade. Do you think that's realistic?
examples.japaneseTranslation: イーロン・マスクは10年以内に火星に人類を送れるって言ってる。現実的？

examples.label: D
examples.text: I could make some videos about how to make yogurt at home, but I need to read up on how to actually do it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜できるかもしれない

examples.label: E
examples.text: Let me check my schedule. Okay, I can do the 25th for sure. I can do the 28th and 29th too, but I need to double check. There should be okay though.
examples.japaneseTranslation: スケジュール確認するね。25日は確実にいける。28日と29日もいけると思うけど、もう一度確認が必要。たぶん大丈夫だと思う。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Somebody may be able to do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜できるかもしれない

words.nativeDefinition: Used to say that something is capable of happening or being done.
words.etymology: could do で能力・可能性を表す（can より控えめ）。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I dropped my phone today and smashed the screen. I could buy a new one but I rather repaired because I don't like any of these new phones.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 携帯落として画面割っちゃった。新しいの買ってもいいけど、修理したい。新しいの好きじゃない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: We could walk there instead of cycling, it's not that far anyway.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 自転車じゃなくて歩いてもいいよ。そんなに遠くないし。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I could go for ramen right now, is anyone up for it?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜する選択肢がある

examples.label: E
examples.text: My job could definitely be done remotely, but my boss is against it for some reason. I don't get it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 俺の仕事は確実にリモートでできるけど、上司がなぜか反対してる。理解できない。

examples.label: F
examples.text: It's an option for somebody to do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜する選択肢がある

words.nativeDefinition: Used to suggest something as a possible option.
words.etymology: could do で選択肢としての可能性を述べる。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜かもしれない／〜の可能性がある

examples.label: B
examples.text: It could be that Wayne's just naturally fit, but it's more likely that he exercises pretty frequently. No pain no gain after all.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ウェインは生まれつきフィットしてるのかもしれないけど、頻繁に運動してる可能性の方が高い。ノーペイン・ノーゲイン。

examples.label: C
examples.text: There are a lot of reasons why kids avoid school. It could be that the child is being bullied. It could be that the child is struggling with their schoolwork. It could be a combination of different factors.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 学校をサボる理由はたくさんある。いじめられてるかもしれない。勉強についていけないかもしれない。複合的なことも。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I think any color will look good on you, but it might be that you're just always beautiful on me.
examples.japaneseTranslation: たぶん

examples.label: E
examples.text: Oh, you haven't received a confirmation email yet? And maybe that has gone into your spam folder. Could you please check and let me know.
examples.japaneseTranslation: あ、確認メールまだ届いてない？スパムフォルダに入ってるかもしれない。確認して教えてくれる？

examples.label: F
examples.text: maybe
examples.japaneseTranslation: たぶん

words.nativeDefinition: Used to suggest a possible explanation or reason.
words.etymology: it could/might/may be that で、原因や理由の可能性を述べる。

words.headwordAudio: sbslike/065A sb might as well do.mp3
words.japaneseMeaning: 〜した方がいい／どうせ〜するなら

examples.label: B
examples.text: I might as well submit this assignment now. The deadline is tomorrow, so there's only so much I can do at the stage anyway.
examples.japaneseTranslation: もう提出しちゃおう。締切明日だし、この段階でできることは限られてる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: That information could be confusing to the reader, so you might as well just leave it out of the article.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 読者を混乱させる情報なら、記事から省いちゃった方がいい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Still struggling to catch a taxi, you might as well walk.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜した方がいいかも

examples.label: E
