examples.text: You think we should start our own YouTube channel? Yeah, might as well. It'll probably be a good hobby
examples.japaneseTranslation: 自分の YouTube チャンネル始めるべきかな？うん、やってみよう。いい趣味になりそう。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Maybe somebody should do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜した方がいいかも

words.nativeDefinition: Used when there is no strong reason not to do something; might as well.
words.etymology: might as well do で「どうせなら」「やった方がマシ」と述べる。

words.headwordAudio: sbslike/066A sb might want to do.mp3
words.japaneseMeaning: 〜した方がいいかも／〜を検討した方が

examples.label: B
examples.text: You might want to think about making up with your girlfriend pretty soon. The longer you leave it, the harder it's going to be.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼女と仲直り、早めに考えた方がいいよ。時間が経つほど難しくなる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You might want to get your story straight if you want to cover up or you were last night. Nobody's going to believe anything you say if you keep changing the facts.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昨夜どこにいたか話を統一した方がいい。事実をコロコロ変えると誰も信じない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: We might want to make some changes to our marketing strategy because it doesn't seem to be working.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜した方がいいかも

examples.label: E
examples.text: You might want to learn how to code. I think that going forward is the only way you'll be able to keep pace with these young people.
examples.japaneseTranslation: プログラミングを学んだ方がいいかも。これからは若い人についていくにはそれが唯一の方法だと思う。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Maybe somebody should do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜した方がいいかも

words.nativeDefinition: A polite way to suggest that someone should consider doing something.
words.etymology: might want to do で、控えめな助言・提案を述べる。

words.headword: If sb/sth were to do, sb/sth wo
words.headwordAudio: sbslike/067A If sb_sth were to do, sb_sth wo.mp3
words.phonetic: ɪf somebody/something wər tɪ du, somebody/something woʊ
words.japaneseMeaning: もし〜したら／仮に〜するなら

examples.label: B
examples.text: If I were to bring one thing to a deserted island, I'd definitely bring my phone.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 無人島に一つ持っていくなら、絶対スマホ持っていく。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I think if I were to study abroad, it'd be any year or two.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 留学するなら、1〜2年は行きたい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I worked hard at my accounting job for years and one day I realized that if I were to put in the same amount of work running my own business, the sky would be limit.
examples.japaneseTranslation: もし誰かが〜するなら、誰かは〜する

examples.label: E
examples.text: If I were to be a Disney character, I would be Prince Charming from Cinderella, some handsome.
examples.japaneseTranslation: もしディズニーキャラになるなら、シンデレラの王子様かな。かっこいいし。

examples.label: F
examples.text: If somebody was going to do, somebody would do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: もし誰かが〜するなら、誰かは〜する

words.nativeDefinition: Used in formal hypothetical conditions: if someone were to do something, then...
words.etymology: If ... were to do の仮定法で、フォーマルな条件文を構成する。

words.headwordAudio: sbslike/068A (if sb could do,) sb_sth would.mp3
words.japaneseMeaning: 〜してくれたら助かる／〜がいい

examples.label: B
examples.text: I know you're busy, but if you get answer my questions by the end of today, that would be great.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 忙しいのはわかるけど、今日中に質問に答えてくれたら助かる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: If you could walk the dog from in this afternoon, I'd appreciate it a lot.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 午後犬の散歩お願いできる？本当に助かる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: What do I have to drink? Coffee would be nice thanks.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜してくれれば、〜する

examples.label: E
examples.text: It would be nice to know which style the client prefers before we start drafting the design.
examples.japaneseTranslation: デザインの草案を始める前に、クライアントがどのスタイルを好むか知れればいいのに。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Somebody can kindly do, somebody will do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜してくれれば、〜する

words.nativeDefinition: Used to make a polite request or express what would be nice.
words.etymology: if you could do, that would be great の丁寧な依頼・希望表現。

words.headword: more of a sth (than a sth)
words.headwordAudio: sbslike/069A more of a sth (than a sth).mp3
words.phonetic: mɔr əv ə ˈsəmθɪŋ (ðən ə ˈsəmθɪŋ)

examples.label: B
examples.text: I've been living in the UK for more than 10 years now, but I'm still more of a coffee drinker. I find it as more kick to it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 英国に10年以上住んでるけど、やっぱりコーヒー派。カフェイン効くし。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I don't think calling Michael Jackson in the musician is wrong per se, but I think he's more of an entertainer.
examples.japaneseTranslation: マイケル・ジャクソンをミュージシャンと呼ぶのは間違いじゃないけど、エンターテイナーに近い。

examples.label: D
examples.text: This isn't the exact number, it's more of a bar part figure. Are you sure you still want to send it to the client?
examples.japaneseTranslation: むしろ〜、〜

examples.label: E
examples.text: For some people, studying is like an obligation, but for me, it's more of a habit.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 人によっては勉強は義務みたいなものだけど、俺にとってはもっと習慣に近い。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Rather, something, something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: むしろ〜、〜

words.nativeDefinition: Used to say something is closer to one category than another.
words.etymology: more of a ... than a ... で、分類を修正・言い換える。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Dogs are more like friends and pets, don't you think?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 犬はペットというより友達に近くない？

examples.label: C
examples.text: Science is more like a blanket term for a variety of disciplines that a specific subject that you can study.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 科学は特定の科目というより、いろんな分野の総称に近い。

examples.label: D
examples.text: You should check out this cafe in Tokyo. It's actually more like a bar because they serve alcohol as well.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜に近い

examples.label: E
examples.text: I tried fraud for the first time yesterday, but contrary to popular belief, it didn't taste like chicken. It actually tastes more like fish.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昨日初めてカエルを食べたけど、鶏肉の味がするって言われてるけど違った。実際は魚に近い味。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Go straight to something and something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜に近い

words.nativeDefinition: Used to recharacterize something as being more similar to one thing than another.
words.etymology: more like ... than ... で、より正確な比較・言い換えを行う。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I can't believe my computer just lost two hours of my work. I'm actually more frustrated than sad.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 2時間分の作業が消えた。悲しいというよりイライラする。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My parents were more strict at meme. It was tough growing up, but I can see now that it was for my own good.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 両親は昔厳しかった。大変だったけど、今思えば自分のためだった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I'd say it's more cute than handsome.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 形容詞 A というより形容詞 B

examples.label: E
examples.text: Do you have a bit of a sweet tooth when it comes to drinks? You should try this cherry beer. It's more sour than sweet, but it's still really good.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 飲み物は甘いの好き？このチェリービール試してみて。甘いというより酸っぱいけど、すごく美味しい。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Adjective rather than adjective.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 形容詞 A というより形容詞 B

words.nativeDefinition: Used to compare two adjectives, saying one applies more than the other.
words.etymology: more adj than adj で、2つの形容詞のうちどちらが当てはまるかを比較する。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I wasn't interested in going to college at all. It's more that I just found learning new things really interesting.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 大学に行きたくなかったわけじゃない。新しいことを学ぶのが面白かっただけ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Making plans doesn't necessarily guarantee your success in business. It's more that it can improve your chances of being successful.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 計画を立てれば必ず成功するわけじゃない。成功確率を上げられる、という方が正確。

examples.label: D
examples.text: People who put in a lot of effort usually don't consciously put in the effort. It's more that they naturally drive themselves so that they can achieve what they want.
examples.japaneseTranslation: そう言う方が正確だ

examples.label: E
examples.text: I love my girlfriend, but I don't need her to survive. You know what I mean? It's more than I'm with her because it's fun, but I'm not that emotionally dependent on her.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼女は大好きだけど、生きるのに必要じゃない。わかる？一緒にいるのは楽しいからで、そんなに感情的に依存してるわけじゃない。

examples.label: F
examples.text: It's more correct to say that.
examples.japaneseTranslation: そう言う方が正確だ

words.nativeDefinition: Used to reframe the real reason or nature of something.
words.etymology: it's more that ... で、表面的な理由ではなく本質を言い換える。

words.headwordAudio: sbslike/073A sb_sth doesn't have as much (of.mp3

examples.label: B
examples.text: How about this candidate? He doesn't have as much experience as the other candidates, but his background is really interesting. I think it'll be a breath of fresh air for our company.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この候補者どう？他の人ほど経験はないけど、経歴が面白い。会社に新鮮な風を吹かせると思う。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Soy sauce ramen tastes good, but I think Miso is the best. Soy sauce ramen doesn't have as much flavor.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 醤油ラーメンも美味しいけど、味噌が一番。醤油は味が薄い。

examples.label: D
examples.text: To put it mildly, I used to be obsessed with basketball, but I hardly watch it now. I don't have as much free time anymore.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 同じ量の〜を持っていない

examples.label: E
examples.text: I think psychological factors like stress have the biggest effect on your skin. What you eat doesn't have as much of an impact.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ストレスみたいな心理的因子が肌に一番影響するんだと思う。食べるものほど影響はない。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Somebody doesn't have the same amount of something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 同じ量の〜を持っていない

words.nativeDefinition: Used to compare quantities, saying someone/something has less of something.
words.etymology: doesn't have as much (of) ... as で、量の比較を表す。

words.headwordAudio: sbslike/074A sb_sth doesn't have as many (of.mp3

examples.label: B
examples.text: John's YouTube channel is very similar to mine. It doesn't have as many videos but other than that it's pretty much in the same vein.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ジョンの YouTube チャンネル、俺のと似てる。動画数は少ないけど、方向性は同じ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Unfortunately, we don't have as many people in our team as we did last year, but if we divide and conquer and focus on working smarter will be able to finish the project on time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 残念ながら去年より人数が少ない。分担して賢くやれば間に合う。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I'd recommend the smart fridge, but if it's out of your budget, how about this one? It doesn't have as many of the bells and whistles, but it'll still do the trick.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 同じ数の〜を持っていない

examples.label: E
examples.text: This little cafe doesn't have as many types of drinks as Starbucks, but its atmosphere is something else.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この小さなカフェはスターバックスほどドリンクの種類はないけど、雰囲気が別格。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Somebody doesn't have the same number or something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 同じ数の〜を持っていない

words.nativeDefinition: Used to compare numbers, saying someone/something has fewer of something.
words.etymology: doesn't have as many (of) ... で、数の比較を表す。

words.headwordAudio: sbslike/075A there's not as much sth (as ....mp3

examples.label: B
examples.text: There's not as much color as I expected. Can we bump up the saturation contrast a little bit?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 色が思ったほど出てない。彩度とコントラスト上げられる？

examples.label: C
examples.text: The pay is much less than my previous job, but there's not as much pressure as stress so I feel a lot happier.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 給料は前の仕事よりずっと少ないけど、プレッシャーもストレスも少ない。ずっと幸せ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I thought I still had plenty of money in my bank account, but there wasn't as much left as I thought.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜が少ない

examples.label: E
examples.text: I low key regret joining Twitter. I should have just stuck with Instagram because there's not as much negative any there.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Twitter に入ったのちょっと後悔してる。インスタだけにしておけばよかった。あっちの方がネガティブな投稿が少ない。

examples.label: F
examples.text: There's last something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜が少ない

words.nativeDefinition: Used to say there is less of something than expected or compared to something else.
words.etymology: there's not as much ... as で、量が想定より少ないことを表す。

words.headwordAudio: sbslike/076A there're not as many sth (as ...mp3

examples.label: B
examples.text: There are not as many stray dogs and Japan as they were in the past. I wonder why?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 日本の野良犬、昔ほどいない。なんでだろう。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Detroit's population was over 1.8 million in 1950, but there are not as many people living there now because of urban decline.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 1950年にデトロイトの人口は180万以上だったけど、都市衰退で今は少ない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: This area is pretty convenient because there are a lot of convenience stores and supermarkets, but the downside is that they're not as many cafes.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜が少ない

examples.label: E
examples.text: I think there are not as many young people who want to become entrepreneurs as there should be in this country.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この国には起業したい若者が、あるべき数より少ないと思う。

examples.label: F
examples.text: There are fears something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜が少ない

words.nativeDefinition: Used to say there are fewer of something than before or compared to something else.
words.etymology: there're not as many ... as で、数が以前より少ないことを表す。

words.headwordAudio: sbslike/077A sb_sth doesn't do as much (as ..mp3

examples.label: B
examples.text: I don't cook as much lately because the project I'm currently assigned to is getting out of hand. I simply don't have time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 最近あまり料理しない。今のプロジェクトが手に負えなくて時間がない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I know a lot of people really care about what others think, but it doesn't stress me out as much. There's only so much you can do to change their minds.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 人の目を気にする人は多いけど、俺ほどストレスにはならない。変えられることには限界がある。

examples.label: D
examples.text: People nowadays don't read as much. It's probably because they can get information from the internet straight away.
examples.japaneseTranslation: あまり〜しない

examples.label: E
examples.text: Cows are terrible for the environment because they build out so much methane even cars don't pollute as much.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 牛はメタンガスを大量に出すから環境に悪い。車ですらそんなに汚染しない。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Somebody does less.
examples.japaneseTranslation: あまり〜しない

words.nativeDefinition: Used to compare frequency or degree of an action.
words.etymology: doesn't do as much (as) ... で、頻度・程度の比較を表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Some people find out that they build muscle as soon as they start working out. Some people take a bit longer, just hang in there.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 筋肉がすぐつく人もいれば、時間かかる人もいる。頑張って。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Some people want to learn and grow. Some people are just happy with the way they are. It's just a matter of preference. Either is better than the other.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 成長したい人もいれば、現状に満足してる人もいる。好みの問題。どちらも正解。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Our online community has a great mix of people. Some are good with numbers, some are teachers, some are experts in chemistry.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 他にも

examples.label: E
examples.text: It's pretty hard to tell you what Tokyo is like. Some places are really busy and crowded. Some are really quite in green.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 東京がどんなところか説明するの難しい。にぎやかで混んでる場所もあれば、静かで緑の多い場所もある。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Some others.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 他にも

words.nativeDefinition: Used to contrast two groups: some ... while others ...
words.etymology: Some ... some ... で、対照的な2つのグループを並列する。

examples.label: B
examples.text: James is something really insightful yesterday, which is that big coin may have no real use value, but neither does gold. He really hit the nail in the head with that one.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ジェームズが昨日言った洞察、ビットコインに実用価値がなくても金も同じ、って。的を射てた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This brand never uses additives or preservatives in any of its products, which is great, so I'd recommend it if you want to help the option.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このブランドは添加物・保存料を一切使わない。それが良い。健康志向ならお勧め。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Steve spent an entire afternoon walking me through hi-everything at this company works, which was really generous. I really own one.
examples.japaneseTranslation: そしてそれは

examples.label: E
examples.text: So many people still think that the male and the family should be bringing home the bacon, which I think is so outdated.
examples.japaneseTranslation: まだ家計を支えるのは男の仕事だと思ってる人が多いけど、それはすごく時代遅れだと思う。

examples.label: F
examples.text: And that.
examples.japaneseTranslation: そしてそれは

words.nativeDefinition: A non-restrictive relative clause adding extra information about the preceding statement.
words.etymology: which 節で、前文に付加的な情報を非限定用法で添える。

words.japaneseMeaning: その場合は〜／そうなったら

examples.label: B
examples.text: Our sales data might not be finalized before the presentation tomorrow, in which case I'll just give a guest to met.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 明日のプレゼンまでに売上データが確定しないかも。その場合は概算で話す。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You can cancel your order if it hasn't been shipped yet, in which case you'll have to fill up this form and send it to this email address.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 発送前なら注文キャンセルできる。そうなったらこのフォームに記入してこのメールに送って。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Scott might be on vacation in which case just touch base with Aaron.
examples.japaneseTranslation: その場合

examples.label: E
examples.text: You won't be able to improve your English without actually studying every day unless you live in an English-speaking country in which case you might naturally pick it up.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 英語圏に住んでない限り、毎日勉強しないと英語は上達しない。その場合は自然に身につくかもしれないけど。

examples.label: F
examples.text: And if that's the case.
examples.japaneseTranslation: その場合

words.nativeDefinition: Used to introduce a consequence that follows if a preceding condition is true.
words.etymology: in which case で、前の条件が成り立つ場合の結果を述べる。

words.japaneseMeaning: そういえば／それに関連して

examples.label: B
examples.text: I have a bit of time on my hand so I'm going to go for a walk to clear my mind. Speaking of which, are there any nice parks around here to wander around?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 時間あるから散歩して頭を空にしよう。そういえば、この辺に散策できる良い公園ある？

examples.label: C
examples.text: I haven't been able to play any video games lately, speaking of which, I watched the Wii G's Mansion 3's official trailer last night. It looked fantastic.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 最近ゲーム全然できてない。そういえば昨夜 Wii の Luigi's Mansion 3 のトレーラー見た。最高だった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Chris has gone home early because he's feeling unwell so I'm going to cancel the meeting. Speaking of which, you should think about heading off too. Didn't you say weren't feeling 100%.
examples.japaneseTranslation: その話題について

examples.label: E
examples.text: Looks like it's going to be nice weather all the next weekend. Speaking which are you free for a barbecue then? If you are, I'll pencil it in.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 来週末ずっと天気良さそう。そういえば、そのときバーベキュー空いてる？空いてたら予定入れとく。

examples.label: F
examples.text: on that topic.
examples.japaneseTranslation: その話題について

words.nativeDefinition: Used to shift to a related topic that was just mentioned or implied.
words.etymology: speaking of which で、直前の話題に関連する新しい話題へ移る。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I didn't previously appreciate the extent to which putting in a small amount of effort each day can make a big difference to my life.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 毎日少しずつ努力するだけで人生が大きく変わる、という程度を以前は理解してなかった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: The degree to which we love ourselves determines the degree to which we love others. I don't know who said this, but it's amazing.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 自分を愛する程度が、他人を愛する程度を決める。誰の言葉かわからないけど、素晴らしい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I think the probability of success is correlated to the degree to which you can flexibly adapt to changes.
examples.japaneseTranslation: どのくらい

examples.label: E
examples.text: The extent to what you can change people's minds using logic is pretty limited.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 論理で人の考えを変えられる程度はかなり限られてる。

examples.label: F
examples.text: How much?
examples.japaneseTranslation: どのくらい

words.nativeDefinition: Used to refer to the degree or extent of something.
words.etymology: the extent/degree to which ... で、程度・範囲を表す名詞句。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm at a stage in my life where I prefer to spend more money on experiences than on material things.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今は経験より物質に金を使いたい人生の段階にいる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You know that scene inspired it away where Hocka gives sunrise balls. I love that scene so much.
examples.japaneseTranslation: インサイド・アウトでサドネスが泣き玉を渡すシーン、あのシーン大好き。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I'll send you the link to this nice website where they talk about how to gather your thoughts when you're in a creative crisis. It really spells out each step.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜な状況

examples.label: E
examples.text: There's an interesting YouTube video where he talks about how to build an online cash cow. You should definitely check it out.
examples.japaneseTranslation: オンラインで金のなる木を築く方法について話してる面白い YouTube 動画がある。ぜひ見てみて。

examples.label: F
examples.text: something in which
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜な状況

words.nativeDefinition: A relative clause describing a place, stage, or situation where something happens.
words.etymology: sth where ... で、場所や状況を関係代名詞 where で修飾する。

examples.label: B
examples.text: At lunch, I ate sushi at the point where I couldn't move, but now I'm starving already.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ランチで寿司食べすぎて動けなくなるまで食べた。もうお腹空いてる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: If you want to be good at speaking English, you have to practice in practice at the point where you don't have to think about grammar and stuff.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 英語が上手くなるには、文法を考えなくていいくらい練習する必要がある。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I want to get drugs at the point where I don't remember anything.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜な段階まで

examples.label: E
examples.text: I used to be hooked on online games to the point where my dad banned me from playing them. They're so addictive.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昔オンラインゲームにハマりすぎて、父にプレイ禁止されたくらいだった。本当に中毒性ある。

examples.label: F
examples.text: to the stage that.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜な段階まで

words.nativeDefinition: Used to describe reaching such an extreme degree that a certain state results.
words.etymology: to the point where ... で、極端な程度に達することを表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜ということ（多くの人が考えない点）

examples.label: B
examples.text: Just because the business has a huge animal turnover doesn't necessarily mean it's actually profitable. What if it's profit margin was zero? This is something a lot of people don't consider.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 売上が大きいから儲かるとは限らない。利益率ゼロだったら？多くの人が考えない点。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This is something I wear to keep warm when the weather gets a bit colder. I know it looks a bit ugly, but it works.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 寒くなったとき暖かくするために着るやつ。ダサいけど機能する。

examples.label: D
examples.text: You know how Amazon opened up fully automated grocery store in the US? This is something that we'll see everywhere in the near future. How exciting is that?
examples.japaneseTranslation: これは〜ということ

examples.label: E
examples.text: This is something I bought in Greece ages ago. I don't actually know what it is, but it's kind of cute, isn't it?
examples.japaneseTranslation: これはずっと前にギリシャで買ったもの。何なのかよくわからないけど、なんか可愛いでしょ？

examples.label: F
examples.text: This is what.
examples.japaneseTranslation: これは〜ということ

words.nativeDefinition: Used to introduce something that many people overlook or that is noteworthy.
words.etymology: this is something (that) ... で、見落とされがちな点を指摘する。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜だと思う〜（個人的評価を添える）

examples.label: B
examples.text: A movie that I think is a classic example of a mafia film is the Godfather.
examples.japaneseTranslation: マフィア映画の古典的な例だと思うのはゴッドファーザー。

examples.label: C
examples.text: All the songs that I think are beautiful are from the 90s, those were the days.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 美しいと思う曲は全部90年代のもの。良き時代だった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I'm not really looking forward to presenting speaking in front of a large crowd of something that I think a lot of people find terrifying and I'm no different. I just want to get it over with.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 私が信じる〜

examples.label: E
examples.text: People that I think are cut out for this job are those who have passion and perseverance.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この仕事に向いてると思う人は、情熱と粘り強さがある人たち。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Something that I believe.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 私が信じる〜

words.nativeDefinition: A relative clause expressing personal opinion about something.
words.etymology: that I think ... で、関係節の中に個人的意見を挿入する。

examples.label: B
examples.text: It's really important to design your business in a way that makes its employees happy.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 従業員が幸せになるようなビジネス設計が本当に大事。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I always try to work in a way that doesn't harm my mental health. It's something that's very important to me.
examples.japaneseTranslation: メンタルヘルスを害さない働き方を常に心がけてる。自分にとってすごく大事。

examples.label: D
examples.text: You know how some people explain simple things in a way that makes it sound really complicated? It's so annoying.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜な方法で

examples.label: E
examples.text: I don't think he means to come across like that, but he acts in a way that people find obnoxious.
examples.japaneseTranslation: わざとそう振る舞ってるわけじゃないと思うけど、人からウザいと思われるやり方で行動する。

examples.label: F
examples.text: in a manner that.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜な方法で

words.nativeDefinition: Used to describe the manner or method in which something is done.
words.etymology: in a way that ... で、方法・様態を関係節で修飾する。

examples.label: B
examples.text: There are times when I crave a Starbucks, Frappuccino really badly, but there's no Starbucks on my town. How sad is that?
examples.japaneseTranslation: スタバのフラペチーノがめちゃくちゃ飲みたくなるときがある。うちの町にスタバがない。悲しい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: There are times when I wish I lived on a farm, but then I remember how much work it would be. The grass is always green on the other side, isn't it?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 農場で暮らしたいと思うときもある。でも仕事量を考えると…。隣の芝生は青い。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I hate cramming, but there are times when I have to. If you don't have any time to study before the exam, that's the only way to pass, right?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 時々

examples.label: E
examples.text: Why did you have to tell my wife that I bought new golf clubs? There are times when I wish you weren't friends with her.
examples.japaneseTranslation: なんで妻に新しいゴルフクラブ買ったって言う必要あったの？あなたと彼女が友達じゃなかったらいいのにって思うときがある。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Sometimes...
examples.japaneseTranslation: 時々

words.nativeDefinition: Used to describe occasions when a certain feeling or situation occurs.
words.etymology: there're times when ... で、時折起こる状況や感情を述べる。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜という事実（本当の理由・核心）

examples.label: B
examples.text: New York is special, but not because it's so big. It's the fact that it's so diverse. Everywhere I walk in the city, I see people have different backgrounds talking and collaborating with each other.
examples.japaneseTranslation: NY が特別なのは大きさじゃない。多様性という事実。どこ歩いても異なる背景の人が話して協力してる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I don't have a YouTube channel to make money. It's the fact that I can use it to communicate with different people that motivates me.
examples.japaneseTranslation: YouTube で稼ぎたいわけじゃない。色々な人と交流できるという事実がモチベーション。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I have a PhD, but I find it's not what I learned from studying that impressive people the most. It's the fact that I work so hard to get it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜だから

examples.label: E
examples.text: I like everything that Mason keeps in a mix, maybe it's the fact that their Fox emblem is really adorable.
examples.japaneseTranslation: キッチンエイドのミキサーは全部好き。多分あのキツネのエンブレムが本当に可愛いから。

examples.label: F
examples.text: It's because.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜だから

words.nativeDefinition: Used to emphasize the true reason or key factor behind something.
words.etymology: it's the fact that ... で、表面的な理由ではなく核心を強調する。

words.headwordAudio: sbslike/090A it's not the fact that ....mp3
words.japaneseMeaning: 〜というわけではない（その理由が本当ではない）

examples.label: B
examples.text: It's not the fact that I find them boring. It's just that I can't sit through anybody talking for two hours straight.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 退屈だから嫌いなわけじゃない。2時間誰かの話を聞き続けるのが無理なだけ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: It's not the fact that this job will pay so well that I want to work here. It's because it'll help me learn more skills and gain more experience.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 給料が良いからここで働きたいわけじゃない。スキルと経験を積めるから。

examples.label: D
examples.text: It's not the fact that AI is going to take over the world that scares me. It's the fact that people aren't prepared for it that keeps me up at night.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜だからではない

examples.label: E
examples.text: I go to a club every weekend, but it's not the fact that I enjoy clubbing. I just go to take my mind off things sometimes.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 毎週末クラブ行くけど、クラブが好きだから行ってるわけじゃない。たまに気を紛らわすため。

examples.label: F
examples.text: It's not because.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜だからではない

words.nativeDefinition: Used to deny that the stated reason is the real one.
words.etymology: it's not the fact that ... で、誤解されがちな理由を否定する。

words.headword: it's not just the fact that ...
words.headwordAudio: sbslike/091A it's not just the fact that ....mp3
words.phonetic: ɪts nɑt ʤɪst ðə fækt ðət
words.japaneseMeaning: 〜だけじゃない（他にも理由がある）

examples.label: B
examples.text: It's not just a fact that you call me during a meeting, but that you kept calling even after I messaged you. That's why I'm angry.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 会議中に電話してきただけじゃない。メッセージ送った後もかけ続けた。だから怒ってる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: It's not just a fact that she's gorgeous, but she's also witty, funny and easy going basically my dream girl.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 美人なだけじゃない。機知に富んでて面白くて気さく。理想の女性。

examples.label: D
examples.text: We brought him into the team for a few reasons. It's not just a fact that he's strong, quick, fit and whatnot, but also because he's got the attitude and mentality that we want our players to have.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜だけが理由ではない

examples.label: E
examples.text: I feel so relieved after quitting my job. It's not just the fact that I no longer have to deal with my boss, but also because nobody expects me to be anywhere or do something at a certain time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 仕事辞めてすごくホッとしてる。上司と関わらなくなっただけじゃなく、いつどこにいて何をしなきゃいけないか誰にも期待されないから。

examples.label: F
examples.text: It's not only because.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜だけが理由ではない

words.nativeDefinition: Used to say the reason is not only the stated one but also something else.
words.etymology: it's not just the fact that ... で、理由が一つだけではないことを強調する。

words.japaneseMeaning: それだけは（理解できる／同意できる）

examples.label: B
examples.text: I don't get most things that Greg talks about, but for some reason, that I understand.
examples.japaneseTranslation: グレグの話の大半はわからない。でもそれだけは理解できる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: What? Is that a fried grasshopper? No way. I usually don't mind bizarre foods, but that I would never eat. I simply hate insects.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 揚げたバッタ？無理。変な食べ物は平気だけど、それだけは絶対無理。虫が嫌い。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I think the Earth is a square. I've heard some pretty weird conspiracy theories, but that I've never heard before.
examples.japaneseTranslation: それはそうだ

examples.label: E
examples.text: Yes, we need to spend less money. That I agree with.
examples.japaneseTranslation: うん、出費を減らす必要がある。それだけは同意する。

examples.label: F
examples.text: I do that.
examples.japaneseTranslation: それはそうだ

words.nativeDefinition: Used to single out one thing among many that you do or don't understand/agree with.
words.etymology: that I do で、他はダメでも「それだけは」と特定の点を強調する。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I didn't know that learning a new language would be this tricky. I don't remember it taking this long for things to sink in when I was learning French as a kid.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 新しい言語を学ぶのがこんなに難しいなんて知らなかった。子供の頃フランス語習ったときはこんなに時間かからなかった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: How can building IKEA furniture be this hard? I'm literally falling the instructions.
examples.japaneseTranslation: IKEA の家具組み立て、こんなに大変なの？説明書通りにやってるのに。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I didn't think that I had to wait this long for a single cup of coffee. It's been like 15 minutes now.
examples.japaneseTranslation: こんなに形容詞

examples.label: E
examples.text: A dog this big shouldn't be kept in a house this small, it definitely needs to live somewhere roomier.
examples.japaneseTranslation: こんなに大きな犬をこんなに小さい家で飼うべきじゃない。もっと広いところに住ませる必要がある。

examples.label: F
examples.text: As adjective as this.
examples.japaneseTranslation: こんなに形容詞

words.nativeDefinition: Used to emphasize the surprising degree of an adjective in the current situation.
words.etymology: this + 形容詞 で、今の状況の程度の高さに驚きを表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: そんなに〜じゃない／そこまで〜ではない

examples.label: B
examples.text: I don't understand where there's so much hype around that cafe. I've tried to signature pancake before, but it wasn't that good.
examples.japaneseTranslation: あのカフェ、なんであんなに話題？看板パンケーキ食べたけど、そんなに美味しくなかった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Is he really that sick? He's been posting on Instagram all day.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 本当にそんなに具合悪いの？インスタは一日中更新してるけど。

examples.label: D
examples.text: A big Mac isn't that big and it's pretty expensive. I'll just order two cheeseburgers.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 想像ほど形容詞ではない

examples.label: E
examples.text: I don't think it's that hard to strike up a conversation with a girl that you like. Just think of them as a friend.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 好きな女の子に話しかけるの、そんなに難しくないと思う。友達だと思えばいいだけ。

examples.label: F
examples.text: As adjectives you'd expect.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 想像ほど形容詞ではない

words.nativeDefinition: Used to downplay or question the degree of an adjective.
words.etymology: that + 形容詞 で、期待ほど程度が高くないことを表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Are you interested in politics at all?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 政治に興味ある？

examples.label: C
examples.text: If you enjoyed this video at all, and be great if you could hit the like button.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 少しでもこの動画が良かったら、いいね押してもらえると嬉しい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I highly recommend exercising at least twice a week if it all possible.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 少しでも／全く

examples.label: E
examples.text: Did you talk to your parents at all about your plan to drop out of college?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 大学中退の計画、両親に話した？

examples.label: F
examples.text: to any extent.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 少しでも／全く

words.nativeDefinition: Used to ask if something applies even slightly, or to emphasize 'in any way'.
words.etymology: at all で、疑問文では「少しでも」、否定文では「全く」を強調する。

words.headwordAudio: sbslike/096A a bit of a sth.mp3

examples.label: B
examples.text: I was pretty set on quitting my job, but now I'm having second thoughts. It's a bit of a risk in this environment, isn't it?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 辞める決心してたけど、今は二の足。今の環境だとリスクがあるよね。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I was a bit of a workahawk when I worked in Hong Kong. I worked until I turned the morning every single day and I didn't even think there was anything wrong.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 香港にいた頃、ちょっと仕事中毒だった。毎日朝まで働いて何も問題ないと思ってた。

examples.label: D
examples.text: These phones were supposed to take center stage at the Expo, but it looks like they won't be finished in time. Apparently there was a bit of a hiccup at the factory.
examples.japaneseTranslation: なんとなく〜な

examples.label: E
examples.text: Lots of arguments at the start of relationship are a bit of a warning sign that things probably won't work out.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 付き合い始めの頃から喧嘩が多いのは、うまくいかなさそうって少し警告してる感じ。

examples.label: F
examples.text: sort of a something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: なんとなく〜な

words.nativeDefinition: Used to describe something as somewhat or to a certain degree a particular thing.
words.etymology: a bit of a + 名詞 で、程度を控えめに「多少〜な」と述べる。

words.japaneseMeaning: こんなにたくさんの〜／こういう〜

examples.label: B
examples.text: All these angry people in the comments section of social media need to calm down. Anybody that reads what they read are just like, care factor zero.
examples.japaneseTranslation: SNS のコメント欄の怒ってる人たち、落ち着けよ。読んでも「知ったこっちゃない」って感じ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You adopted all these cats and now you're complaining about the mess that they make, you can't have your cake and eat it too.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 猫をこんなにたくさん飼っておいて、散らかすの文句言うなよ。両方は無理。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I don't want to go out for dinner tonight because I have all this leftover curry that I cooked yesterday.
examples.japaneseTranslation: たくさんの〜

examples.label: E
examples.text: Thanks for the invite to the party, but I think I'll pass this time. I've got to this homework that I need to finish tonight.
examples.japaneseTranslation: パーティー誘ってくれてありがとう。今回はパスするわ。今夜終わらせなきゃいけない宿題があるから。

examples.label: F
examples.text: a lot of something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: たくさんの〜

words.nativeDefinition: Used to refer to a large amount of something present or accumulated.
words.etymology: all this/these + 名詞 で、目の前にある大量のものを指す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: You should get on to TikTok if you want to understand young people a bit better. They all seem to love it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 若者を理解したいなら TikTok 入った方がいい。みんなハマってる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: The problem of climate change won't be solved by a small number of people. We all need to do our part.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 気候変動は少数の人では解決できない。みんなで協力する必要がある。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I'll need you all to help me carry this couch upstairs. I won't be able to do it alone.
examples.japaneseTranslation: みんな／全部

examples.label: E
examples.text: Why is the stack of dishes on the wash? These ought to be clean before we get out of here.
examples.japaneseTranslation: なんで食器が洗濯機の中にあるの？ここ出る前にきれいになってなきゃ。

examples.label: F
examples.text: We you, they, these those.
examples.japaneseTranslation: みんな／全部

words.nativeDefinition: Used with all to mean everyone or everything in a group.
words.etymology: we/you/they/these/those all + 動詞 で、グループ全体を強調する。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I managed to fix up most of the leaks in my house today, but I'm going to leave the floorboards for tomorrow. That's a whole other problem.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今日は家の漏れのほとんど直した。床板は明日に回す。それは全く別の問題。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I thought that because I've studied Japanese, learning Chinese would be pretty straightforward, but it turns out it's a whole other thing.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 日本語勉強してたから中国語も簡単だろうと思ったけど、全く別物だった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: On the face of it, she's definitely my type, whether she's the one or not as a whole other issue that I need to think on.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 全く別の〜

examples.label: E
examples.text: I really want to study abroad, but whether my parents would allow me, that's a whole other story.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 留学したいけど、親が許すかどうかは全く別の話。

examples.label: F
examples.text: a completely different something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 全く別の〜

words.nativeDefinition: Used to say something is an entirely separate issue or problem.
words.etymology: a whole other + 名詞 で、別次元の問題であることを強調する。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜というわけではない（但し書き）

examples.label: B
examples.text: I think she get a college degree if you want to become a business consultant. That's not to say that it'll be impossible without it, but it just that it's usually required by employers.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ビジネスコンサルタントになるなら学位取った方がいいと思う。無理ってわけじゃないけど、雇い主は普通求める。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm pretty relaxed about this interview. That's not to say I'm nothing on the line, but I've got a few others lined up in case this doesn't work out.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この面接は割と余裕。何も失うわけじゃないけど、ダメなら他にも候補ある。

examples.label: D
examples.text: You should try listening a bit more. That's not to say that you should just sit through meanings without saying anything, but maybe just speak after others have spoken.
examples.japaneseTranslation: それは意味しない

examples.label: E
examples.text: I like sushi more than burgers, but that's not to say that I never eat burgers.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 寿司の方がハンバーガーより好きだけど、ハンバーガーを全然食べないってわけじゃない。

examples.label: F
examples.text: That doesn't mean.
examples.japaneseTranslation: それは意味しない

words.nativeDefinition: Used to clarify that what was said should not be taken as an absolute statement.
words.etymology: that's not to say that ... で、前の発言を絶対視しない但し書きを添える。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Isn't it interesting how the word risk means so many different things to different people? Some people just call it opportunity.
examples.japaneseTranslation: risk という言葉、人によって意味が全然違うの面白くない？機会と呼ぶ人もいる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: The word made is not a politically correct term now, so you should use House Helper instead.
examples.japaneseTranslation: maid は今ポリティカルに正しくない。House Helper を使うべき。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I'm allergic to the word Sunish. Is it Sun or not Sun?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜という用語

examples.label: E
examples.text: The word bi-weekly drives me insane is that every two weeks or twice a week, so confusing.
examples.japaneseTranslation: bi-weekly という言葉、イライラする。2週間に1回なの？週2回なの？超わかりにくい。

examples.label: F
examples.text: The term something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜という用語

words.nativeDefinition: Used when discussing the meaning, usage, or connotations of a particular word.
words.etymology: the word + 名詞 で、特定の語の意味や使い方について議論する際の表現。

examples.label: B
examples.text: My phone's running out of battery already. Thank God I brought my charger.
examples.japaneseTranslation: もうバッテリー残り少ない。よかった、充電器持ってきた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Thank God, Winters just around the corner. I can't wait to go snowboarding.
examples.japaneseTranslation: よかった、もうすぐ冬。スノーボード行きたくて待ちきれない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Thank God my mom didn't grill me on the final exam. That's the last thing I want to talk about.
examples.japaneseTranslation: よかった

examples.label: E
examples.text: Thank God I didn't visit my girlfriend's parents empty handed. I didn't know they were so strict with manners and stuff
examples.japaneseTranslation: よかった、彼女の両親のところに手ぶらで行かなかった。マナーとかすごく厳しいなんて知らなかった。

examples.label: F
examples.text: I'm glad.
examples.japaneseTranslation: よかった

words.nativeDefinition: An exclamation expressing relief or gratitude about something.
words.etymology: Thank God ... で、安堵や感謝を表す感嘆。宗教的でなく口語的な用法も多い。

examples.label: B
examples.text: You know how Travis always seems really happy? I asked him what his secret was and he said he doesn't know either.
examples.japaneseTranslation: トラビスいつも超ハッピーに見えるでしょ？秘密聞いたら、本人もわかってないって。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You know how Kathy and Richard have been at Lagerheads for a while now? Did anybody know why?
examples.japaneseTranslation: キャシーとリチャードずっと仲悪いでしょ？理由知ってる人いる？

examples.label: D
examples.text: You know how I keep saying that I'm going to quit and open up my own bakery? Well, it's finally happening.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜だと知ってるでしょ

examples.label: E
examples.text: You know I've been looking for a new TV for a while. I'm still trying to work out whether I want something State of Yart or something that's a bit cheaper that I can replace in a few years.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 新しいテレビ探してるの、ずっと続いてるでしょ？最新モデルにするか、数年後に買い替えられるもう少し安いのにするか、まだ迷ってる。

examples.label: F
examples.text: I assume you're where that.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜だと知ってるでしょ

words.nativeDefinition: Used to refer to something the listener likely already knows about.
words.etymology: you know how ... で、聞き手と共有の話題を「知ってるでしょ？」と導入する。

examples.label: B
examples.text: You know that girl I just joined our team last week. Apparently she's already thinking about resigning.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 先週チームに入ったあの子、知ってる？もう辞めようとしてるらしい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You know those Nike sneakers I was talking about on Friday, they're selling like hotcakes now.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 金曜に話してた Nike のスニーカー、今めちゃくちゃ売れてる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: You know that weird place that we had lunch out when we were in Paris? You're not going to believe this, but the owner is on my foot salt team. It's a small world, isn't it?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜を覚えてる

examples.label: E
examples.text: You know that new kid cat I was talking about last week? It's already sold out everywhere. I'm so sad.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 先週話してたあの新しいキットカット、知ってる？もうどこでも売り切れ。悲しい。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Remember somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜を覚えてる

words.nativeDefinition: Used to remind the listener of a person or thing they should know.
words.etymology: you know + 名詞 で、聞き手が知っているはずの人・物を「あの〜」と指す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: To be honest, I don't really want to go to the work function this weekend, but sometimes you have to do things that you don't enjoy to maintain good relationships, you know?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 正直、今週末の社内イベント行きたくない。でも人間関係のために嫌なこともやらなきゃいけない、でしょ？

examples.label: C
examples.text: I would have preferred a guitar that isn't bright purple, but it was a gift so beggars can't be choosers, you know? It's the thought that counts.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 紫のギターじゃなくて良かったけど、プレゼントだから文句言えない、でしょ？気持ちが大事。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I know Sean is busy but if you tell someone you're going to show up at a certain time you actually have to be there, you know?
examples.japaneseTranslation: そうでしょ？

examples.label: E
examples.text: No matter how crowded it is, they shouldn't treat customers like that, you know?
examples.japaneseTranslation: どんなに混んでても、お客さんにそんな扱いするべきじゃないでしょ？

examples.label: F
examples.text: Agreed?
examples.japaneseTranslation: そうでしょ？

words.nativeDefinition: A tag question seeking agreement, often used for empathy or confirmation.
words.etymology: you know を文末に付け、共感や同意を求めるタグ用法。

examples.label: B
examples.text: You know Steven Spielberg, right? Yeah, he's got another film coming out next month.
examples.japaneseTranslation: スティーヴン・スピルバーグ知ってる？来月また新作出る。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You know Mark, right? We're going to grab a drink after work if you want to join.
examples.japaneseTranslation: マーク知ってる？仕事終わりに飲み行くんだけど、来る？

examples.label: D
examples.text: I'm going to study for a few months in Amsterdam next year. You know Amsterdam, right?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜に詳しい？

examples.label: E
examples.text: I can't remember the name but that bar that we went to last year was really good. You know the one I'm talking about right? The one on the rooftop in the city?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 名前は覚えてないけど、去年行ったあのバーすごく良かった。言ってるのわかる？街の屋上にあるあそこ？

examples.label: F
examples.text: You're familiar with somebody, correct?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜に詳しい？

words.nativeDefinition: Used to check if the listener is familiar with someone or something.
words.etymology: you know ..., right? で、聞き手の了解を確認してから話を進める。

words.japaneseMeaning: 一体何が〜なの？／何が〜なの？

examples.label: B
examples.text: What is it that I need to do so that I can retire by the time I'm 50? I've got a few things of the works but I don't think any of them will help me get there.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 50 歳までに退職するには一体何をすればいい？いくつか考えてるけど、届くかわからない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: What is it that you don't like about this apartment? It seems perfect to me.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このアパート、何が気に入らないの？完璧に見えるけど。

examples.label: D
examples.text: What is it about summer that makes me have no appetite?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 一体何が〜なの？

examples.label: E
examples.text: What is it about nighttime that makes me crave sweets?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 夜になるとなんで甘いものが食べたくなるの？

examples.label: F
examples.text: What is that thing that?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 一体何が〜なの？

words.nativeDefinition: A cleft sentence emphasizing what aspect of something is being asked about.
words.etymology: What is it that ...? の分裂文で、焦点を当てた疑問を構成する。

words.headwordAudio: sbslike/108A what can sb do that ....mp3

examples.label: B
examples.text: What could I do that will make me a more well-rounded basketball player?
examples.japaneseTranslation: よりバランスの取れたバスケ選手になるには何ができる？

examples.label: C
examples.text: What can we do that encourages employees to voice their opinions?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 従業員が意見を言いやすくするには何ができる？

examples.label: D
examples.text: For whatever reason, our official website looks kind of dodgy. What can we do that makes it look more legit and professional?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜できることはある？

examples.label: E
examples.text: What can I create that no one else has before?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 誰もこれまで作ったことのないものを、俺は何が作れる？

examples.label: F
examples.text: Is there anything that somebody can do that?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜できることはある？

words.nativeDefinition: A question asking what action would achieve a certain result.
words.etymology: What can sb do that ...? で、目的達成のための手段を問う。

words.japaneseMeaning: どうして〜できるの？／よくも〜

examples.label: B
examples.text: How could you tell Darren that the work he did over the weekend is useless? It's a bit of a slap in the face, isn't it?
examples.japaneseTranslation: どうしてダレンに週末の仕事が無駄だって言えたの？平手打ちも同然でしょ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: How could you forget your wedding anniversary three years in a row? No wonder your wife seems so upset.
examples.japaneseTranslation: どうして結婚記念日を3年連続で忘れるの？奥さん怒るのも無理ない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: How can Mike just take all the credit for the work that you've done? I used to think that it was a really nice guy.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜するなんてひどい

examples.label: E
examples.text: How could I forget to bring the tickets? I put them in my backpack last night so I wouldn't even have to remember.
examples.japaneseTranslation: なんでチケット持ってくるの忘れたの？思い出さなくていいよう、昨夜バックパックに入れたのに。

examples.label: F
examples.text: It's terrible or disappointing that somebody does.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜するなんてひどい

words.nativeDefinition: An exclamation expressing shock, disapproval, or disbelief at someone's action.
words.etymology: How can/could sb do? で、非難・驚き・失望を表す強い疑問。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I can't believe Henry wants us to add more stuff to the presentation. How can we finish this project by the deadline now? It's overkill anyway.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ヘンリーがプレゼンにもっと追加しろって。締切に間に合うの？やりすぎ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This universe is huge. How could anyone believe that we're alone?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 宇宙は巨大だ。一人きりだなんて信じられる？

examples.label: D
examples.text: We all know that Japan's population will continue to decline in its innovation is slowing down. How can you expect us to be optimistic about the future of this country?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜できるとは思えない

examples.label: E
examples.text: I was attacked by the same crow three days in a row. How could this happen?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 同じカラスに3日連続で襲われた。どうしてこんなことが？

examples.label: F
examples.text: I don't think it's possible for somebody to do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜できるとは思えない

words.nativeDefinition: Expressing disbelief that something is possible or can be done.
words.etymology: How can ... do? で、可能性への疑問・非難を表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: 誰が〜する？／そんなことする人いる？

examples.label: B
examples.text: You're going to try and sell your C-Show collection, who would buy something like that.
examples.japaneseTranslation: C 級のコレクション売ろうとしてるの？そんなの買う人いる？

examples.label: C
examples.text: Oh my god, who would steal the seat from my bike? What would you even do with it?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 信じられない、自転車のサドル盗まれた。何に使うの？

examples.label: D
examples.text: I can't believe everybody thinks that I'm getting married next month, which start a rumor like that.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜する人がいるとは驚き

examples.label: E
examples.text: A lot of people think they can fully automate education by using videos and stuff, but who'd want to go to a school like that? That'd be no fun.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 動画で教育を完全自動化できると思ってる人は多いけど、そんな学校に行きたい人なんている？全然楽しくないでしょ。

examples.label: F
examples.text: I'd be surprised if somebody did.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜する人がいるとは驚き

words.nativeDefinition: Expressing disbelief that anyone would do something.
words.etymology: Who would do? で、行為の不可思議さ・非現実性を強調する。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I can't stand people who proposed during someone else's wedding. Why would you steal the bride and groom's thunder like that?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 他人の結婚式でプロポーズする人許せない。なんで新郎新婦の晴れ舞台を奪うの？

examples.label: C
examples.text: Did he bite your gold medal? Why did he do that? Do you think it was a chocolate medal?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 金メダル噛んだの？なんで？チョコのメダルだと思った？

examples.label: D
examples.text: Why would you show but when you're only leading by a goal, you're just asking for trouble.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜する理由がわからない

examples.label: E
examples.text: I feel so hurt, why would you pull my leg like that?
examples.japaneseTranslation: すごく傷ついた。なんでそんな冗談言うの？

examples.label: F
examples.text: It makes no sense for somebody to do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜する理由がわからない

words.nativeDefinition: Asking why someone would do something, expressing confusion or disapproval.
words.etymology: Why would sb do? で、動機の不可解さを問う。

words.japaneseMeaning: なんでわざわざ〜するの？

examples.label: B
examples.text: Why force yourself to do what you don't like to do? You don't have all the time in the world so don't waste it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 嫌いなことを無理してやるなよ。時間は有限だから無駄にするな。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Need to work on your expectation management. Why promise to get the job done when you know that there's no way you'll have time?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 時間ないのに約束するなよ。期待値管理を学べ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Why cook dinner when it's cheaper to buy?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜する意味は？

examples.label: E
examples.text: Why take a cab? The weather's nice and it'll be quicker to walk there.
examples.japaneseTranslation: なんでタクシー乗るの？天気いいし、歩いた方が早いよ。

examples.label: F
examples.text: What's the point of doing?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜する意味は？

words.nativeDefinition: A rhetorical question questioning the point or logic of doing something.
words.etymology: Why do ...? で、非効率・不合理な行為への疑問を表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Can you stop wasting my time with those stupid questions? Why not Google it first?
examples.japaneseTranslation: バカな質問で時間無駄にするな。Google すれば？

examples.label: C
examples.text: You all know the cars, so why not try asking for a bit more money? They need you a lot more than you need them.
examples.japaneseTranslation: カード知ってるでしょ。もう少し金額上乗せして交渉したら？彼らの方が君を必要としてる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: There are so many people that say they can teach people how to make big bucks using social media. I'm just like, if you really have that know how, then why not just make yourself rich.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜したらどう？

examples.label: E
examples.text: If you actually think that you're right, why not stick your guns and clearly explain why.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 本当に自分が正しいと思うなら、信念を曲げずに、はっきり理由を説明すればいいじゃん。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Why don't you do?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜したらどう？

words.nativeDefinition: A suggestion to do something, often implying it is obvious or better.
words.etymology: Why not do? で「〜したらどう？」と提案する。Why don't you do? に近い。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I get that you wanted to study English abroad, but why Maltod? Do they even speak English there?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 留学したいのはわかる。でもなんでマルタ？そこ英語話すの？

examples.label: C
examples.text: There are millions other people you could have chosen. Why me?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 選べる人は何百万人もいるのに。なんで俺？

examples.label: D
examples.text: I know I said I need to use this fine, but why tomato?
examples.japaneseTranslation: なぜ〜を選んだ？

examples.label: E
examples.text: Why a motorbike? There's still one safe. Everyone I know that draws one has at least one closed shave.
examples.japaneseTranslation: なんでバイク？まだ安全じゃないよ。知ってるバイクに乗ってる人は、全員何かしら怪我してる。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Why did somebody choose something?
examples.japaneseTranslation: なぜ〜を選んだ？

words.nativeDefinition: Questioning why a particular choice was made.
words.etymology: Why + 名詞? で、特定の選択に対する疑問を表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Why not Denise? She's got more than 10 years of experience under a belt. So I think this job is right for her.
examples.japaneseTranslation: デニスはどう？10年以上の経験あるし、この仕事にぴったり。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You've been looking for a nice plain white t-shirts? Why not Uniclo? Their stuff is pretty cheap and durable.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 白い T シャツ探してる？ユニクロはどう？安くて丈夫。

examples.label: D
examples.text: You feel like a coffee? Why not 7-11?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜はどう？

examples.label: E
examples.text: You can't decide between strawberry and vanilla, why not both.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ストロベリーとバニラで迷ってるなら、両方はどう？

examples.label: F
examples.text: What about something?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜はどう？

words.nativeDefinition: Suggesting something as an alternative choice.
words.etymology: Why not + 名詞? で、代替案を提案する。

examples.label: B
examples.text: My girlfriend is such a wet blanket. There's some new bars around the corner that I want to check out with her tonight, but she's nagging me to help her with her assignment.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼女は本当にノリを壊す人。今夜一緒に行きたい新しいバーがあるのに、課題手伝えってうるさい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: There's a lot of things that I want to accomplish in life.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 人生で達成したいことがたくさんある。

examples.label: D
examples.text: There's a bunch of different sneakers I want to buy, but they're all expensive.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜がある

examples.label: E
examples.text: There's so many different characters in Smash Bros. It's like a Mishmash of characters from all sorts of Nintendo games.
examples.japaneseTranslation: スマブラにはキャラがめちゃくちゃ多い。いろんな任天堂ゲームのキャラがごちゃ混ぜになってる感じ。

examples.label: F
examples.text: There are something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜がある

words.nativeDefinition: Used to introduce the existence of something, often as a problem or desire.
words.etymology: there's + 名詞 で、存在・問題・願望を導入する。

examples.label: B
examples.text: This isn't the best one that I've ever had, but it's a good bang for the buck though.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今までで最高ってわけじゃないけど、コスパはいいよ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This March-L-Lother jacket is very simple, but I like it though.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このマッキントッシュのジャケット、シンプルだけど好き。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Learning English seems tough, but this book will make it easy though.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜だけど

examples.label: E
examples.text: I understand why you don't like working with Owen, but George is right though. Two heads are better than one, so just be patient with him until the project is done.
examples.japaneseTranslation: オーウェンと働くのが嫌な理由はわかるけど、ジョージの言うことは正しいよ。二人の方が一人よりマシだから、プロジェクトが終わるまで我慢して。

examples.label: F
examples.text: dough
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜だけど

words.nativeDefinition: Used to concede a point while adding a contrasting positive opinion.
words.etymology: but ... though で、譲歩しつつ反対の意見を though で添える口語。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I exercise hungry yesterday and felt awful afterwards so I wouldn't recommend it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昨日空腹のまま運動して、その後気分最悪。お勧めしない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My roommate came home drunk without shoes last night and he doesn't know where they are. I'll find he's at.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ルームメイト昨夜酔っ払って靴なしで帰ってきた。どこ行ったか知らない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I woke up there seeing the middle of the night so I drank at glass of water and ended up going down the YouTube rabbit hole until 4.30 this morning.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 形容詞な状態で〜する

examples.label: E
examples.text: My grandma and my father died pretty young at 76, but my grandma and my mom's died still alive and kicking at 103.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 祖父と父は76歳で若くして亡くなったけど、母方の祖母は103歳でもまだ元気。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Do being adjective
examples.japaneseTranslation: 形容詞な状態で〜する

words.nativeDefinition: Used when someone does something while in a certain state or condition.
words.etymology: do + 形容詞 で、特定の状態で何かをすることを表す口語（do hungry = 空腹のまま等）。

words.headword: one of the more adj sth
words.headwordAudio: sbslike/120A one of the more adj sth.mp3
words.phonetic: wən əv ðə mɔr adj ˈsəmθɪŋ

examples.label: B
examples.text: Let's go to the stake house opposite our office tonight. I think it's one of the better restaurants around here.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今夜オフィス向かいのステーキハウス行こう。この辺でより良いレストランの一つだと思う。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Nancy is one of the more annoying friends that I have. She definitely has a few screws loose.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ナンシーはよりうっとうしい友達の一人。少し常識外れ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: One of the bigger problems with the internet, I think, is it so hard to tell if information comes from a legit source or not.
examples.japaneseTranslation: かなり形容詞な〜の一つ

examples.label: E
examples.text: I wouldn't say that your dog is skinned in bones, but he's definitely one of the skin of your dogs that I've seen.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 君の犬が骨と皮っぽいとは言わないけど、見た中で一番痩せてる部類だね。

examples.label: F
examples.text: One of the pretty adjective something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: かなり形容詞な〜の一つ

words.nativeDefinition: Used to rank something among the more adjective items in a group.
words.etymology: one of the more + 形容詞 + 名詞 で、グループ内での相対的評価を表す。

words.headwordAudio: sbslike/121A if sb_sth would've done, sb_sth.mp3
words.japaneseMeaning: もし〜していたら、〜しただろう

examples.label: B
examples.text: If I were to say that I wanted to be a doctor when I was in high school, my classmates and teachers would have laughed at me because my grades were a joke.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 高校の頃医者になりたいって言ったら、成績がひどかったからクラスメイトも先生も笑っただろう。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I knew that if I would have stayed in the same town, my life would have remained really boring, so I threw a caution that when and moved the other side of the country.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 同じ町にいたら人生退屈なままだった。思い切って国の反対側に引っ越した。

examples.label: D
examples.text: If you would have told me in my 20s that I would be the president of a big corporation at 33, I wouldn't have believed you.
examples.japaneseTranslation: もし〜していたら、〜しただろう

examples.label: E
examples.text: If I would have known all this information earlier, learning French would have been a lot easier.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この情報をもっと早く知ってたら、フランス語の学習はずっと楽だったのに。

examples.label: F
examples.text: If somebody had done something, somebody would have done.
examples.japaneseTranslation: もし〜していたら、〜しただろう

words.nativeDefinition: A hypothetical past condition and its result (non-standard would have).
words.etymology: If ... would have done の口語的仮定法（標準的には had done + would have done）。

words.headword: if sb did, sb would've done
words.headwordAudio: sbslike/122A if sb did, sb would've done.mp3
words.phonetic: ɪf ˈsəmˌbɑdi dɪd, ˈsəmˌbɑdi ˈwʊdəv dən
words.japaneseMeaning: もし〜していたら、〜しただろうに

examples.label: B
examples.text: If you said that during the interview, the outcome would have been totally different. It's too late now.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 面接でそう言ってたら、結果は全然違ってた。もう遅い。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I would have joined the Study of Broad Program at my university if money wasn't an issue for me.
examples.japaneseTranslation: お金の問題じゃなければ、大学の留学プログラムに参加してた。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Did nobody show up to Rogers Fairwell Party? That's a shame. If I knew that he was leaving today, I definitely would have gone.
examples.japaneseTranslation: もし〜していたら、〜しただろう

examples.label: E
examples.text: Imagine what would have happened to your life if you didn't study hard when you were a student. It's scary, isn't it?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 学生の頃もっと勉強してなかったら、人生どうなってたか想像してみて。怖くない？

examples.label: F
examples.text: If somebody had done, somebody would have done.
examples.japaneseTranslation: もし〜していたら、〜しただろう

words.nativeDefinition: A standard past hypothetical: if someone did, someone would have done.
words.etymology: If + 過去形, would have done の仮定法過去で、反事实の条件と結果を表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: There are only three things you need to do to establish the foundation for learning English. Memorize vocabulary, understand grammar, and learn pronunciation.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 英語学習の基礎を築くには3つだけ。語彙を覚える、文法を理解する、発音を学ぶ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: There are a few kid things that you need to learn to be good at playing soccer, run, trap, pass, head and shoot. Professional players mix and match these skills in their own style.
examples.japaneseTranslation: サッカーが上手くなるには、走る、トラップ、パス、ヘディング、シュート。プロは自分のスタイルで組み合わせる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: You'll have to be able to do at least these three things if you want to get promoted. Coach people well, communicate clearly and finish tasks on time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜すること、〜すること、そして〜すること

examples.label: E
examples.text: This is hopefully further down the track, but before I retire there, a few things I want to learn how to do. Play golf, play an instrument, and learn Chinese.
examples.japaneseTranslation: まだ先の話だけど、退職する前に学びたいことがいくつかある。ゴルフ、楽器、中国語。

examples.label: F
examples.text: To do, to do, and to do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜すること、〜すること、そして〜すること

words.nativeDefinition: Listing multiple actions in sequence, often as essential steps.
words.etymology: do, do, and do の並列で、必要な複数の行動を列挙する。

words.japaneseMeaning: 〜すべきことは〜だ／やることは〜

examples.label: B
examples.text: I bet your parents said that what you have to do is go to a good university and get a good job. But forget about that. The world has changed a lot since their generation.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 親は良い大学行って良い仕事つかめって言っただろう。忘れろ。世の中は彼らの世代から変わった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: If you just started your own YouTube channel, the only thing you have to do is just keep making more and more videos. That's it. Don't worry too much about analytics and stuff.
examples.japaneseTranslation: YouTube 始めたばかりなら、やることは動画をどんどん作り続けること。それだけ。分析とか気にしすぎないで。

examples.label: D
examples.text: What I always do at the end of each day is write up my to-do list for the next day. It helps to lay the groundwork for a productive day.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜することだ

examples.label: E
examples.text: The first thing I did when I arrived in Tokyo was go check out should be a scramble crossing because I'd seen it on TV but never had a chance to visit.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 東京に着いた最初にしたことは、渋谷のスクランブル交差点を見に行ったこと。テレビで見たことがあったけど、行ったことなかったから。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Something is to do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜することだ

words.nativeDefinition: Identifying what the essential or required action is.
words.etymology: sth is (to) do で、本質的・必要な行動を特定する。

examples.label: B
examples.text: People often ask me, how long does it take to get good at golf? I understand why they ask that question, but it's like asking, how long is a piece of string?
examples.japaneseTranslation: よく聞かれる、ゴルフ上達にどれくらいかかる？わかるけど、糸の長さを聞いてるようなもの。

examples.label: C
examples.text: The problem that we're trying to solve is how do we make international travel more environmentally friendly and sustainable?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 解決しようとしてる問題は、国際旅行をもっと環境に優しく持続可能にするにはどうすればいいか？

examples.label: D
examples.text: Before we dive into the mechanism of memory, the first thing I want to explain to you is, why do we forget?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 間接疑問

examples.label: E
examples.text: The reason why I put this book together is to help students understand what is statistics all about.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この本をまとめた理由は、学生が統計学って何だろうって理解できるようにするため。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Do indirect question.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 間接疑問

words.nativeDefinition: Embedding a direct question within a statement.
words.etymology: do, 直接疑問文 の構文で、疑問文を文中に埋め込む。

examples.label: B
examples.text: You only live once, so do what you want and have fun.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 人生一度きり。好きなことやって楽しめ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I don't understand why general education is required in college. Like let me study what I want, man.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 大学の一般教養、なんで必修なの？学びたいこと学かせろよ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Say what you want about me, but I want to let you say bad things about my friends.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 望むことをする

examples.label: E
examples.text: I work out all week just to eat what I want on the weekends.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 週末好きなものを食べるために、平日ずっと運動してる。

examples.label: F
examples.text: Do it somebody wants to do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 望むことをする

words.nativeDefinition: To do whatever one wants or desires.
words.etymology: do what sb wants で、自分の望むことを自由に行う。

examples.label: B
examples.text: With losing weight, the keys to be realistic. I'm gonna take a lot of people make when they first start out as set unrealistic goals for themselves.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ダイエットに関しては、現実的であることが鍵。初心者がよく失敗するのは非現実的な目標を立てること。

examples.label: C
examples.text: With Greenland and Iceland, most people think that Iceland is really cold while Greenland is a lot warmer. It's actually the other way around.
examples.japaneseTranslation: グリーンランドとアイスランド、多くの人はアイスランドの方が寒いと思うけど、実は逆。

examples.label: D
examples.text: With dating, I think it's important to be up front about what you're looking for in the other person.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜に関しては

examples.label: E
examples.text: With children, you can't expect them to be studying all the time. It's human nature to want to have fun, especially at that age.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 子供に関しては、いつも勉強してると期待できない。楽しみたいのは人間の本性だし、特にあの年齢は。

examples.label: F
examples.text: When it comes to something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 〜に関しては

words.nativeDefinition: When it comes to a particular topic or area.
words.etymology: With + 名詞 で「〜に関しては」と話題を導入する（when it comes to に近い）。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Don't beat yourself up about John getting through his work quicker than you. It takes working here for years to know everything inside out like he does.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ジョンが仕事早いのを落ち込むな。彼みたいに全部熟知するには、ここで何年も働く必要がある。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Anyone can become an entrepreneur, but it takes making mistakes and learning from them again and again.

examples.label: E
examples.text: It takes understanding how to make the most of YouTube algorithms to get a lot of use consistently.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 安定して再生数を稼ぐには、YouTube のアルゴリズムを最大限活用する方法を理解する必要がある。

examples.label: F
examples.text: There are choirs doing.

words.japaneseMeaning: 精一杯〜する／できる限り〜する

examples.label: B
examples.text: We lost the game but we did the best we could, so no regrets.

examples.label: C

examples.label: D

examples.label: F

examples.japaneseTranslation: できる限り〜

words.etymology: the best sb can で、能力の限りを尽くすことを表す。

words.headwordAudio: sbslike/130A the best sb can do.mp3
words.japaneseMeaning: これ以上はできない／精一杯のこと

examples.label: B
examples.text: Nobody is 100% sure if Earth is the only planet that has life, but is definitely a possibility. That's the best we can say at this point in time.

examples.label: C

examples.label: D
examples.text: You never know when the next earthquake will happen so the best you can do is to always be prepared for it.

examples.label: E
examples.text: The camera on the new Samsung Galaxy is great. I think it's the best you can get right now.

examples.label: F
examples.text: Awesome, but it can do.

examples.label: B

examples.label: B

examples.japaneseTranslation: この景色、最高だよ。街を見下ろしていると世界の頂点にいる気分。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Where the champions it still hasn't sunk in yet.

words.japaneseMeaning: 限界はない、可能性は無限大

examples.label: B

examples.text: Having to put on a suit and tie in the morning is such a drag especially when you're in a rush.

examples.text: I'm looking forward to some downtime tonight. It's been a big day today.

examples.label: B
examples.text: I know I should stand up to my manager when he starts shouting at me for no reason, but it's hard when he's the one that calls the shots.

examples.label: B

words.japaneseMeaning: （人を）気持ち悪がらせる、ゾッとさせる

examples.label: B

examples.label: C

examples.japaneseTranslation: ゴキブリほど気持ち悪いものはない。

examples.label: D

examples.label: B
examples.text: I know Sam is a bit annoying sometimes, but I think Trugging is named through the mud in front of everybody is a bit uncold for.

examples.label: C

examples.text: Can I treat you to dinner tonight? You've been really helpful to me recently, so I just want to do something for you.

examples.label: C

examples.label: B
examples.text: Then, it was born with the silver spoon in his hand. You can say that again. He told me that he's never worked a day in his life.

examples.label: C
examples.text: No news is good news? You can say that again. Every time I get a text from Liz, it seems like something terrible has happened to her.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 「知らない方がいいこともある？」→「その通り。リズからのメッセージのたびに何か悲惨なことが起きてる気がする。」

examples.label: B
examples.text: Oh no! Is it sold out already? That's a shame. Let me quickly check Mercury in Yahoo Auctions.
examples.japaneseTranslation: え、もう売り切れ？残念だね。ヤフオクで早速チェックしてみる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Netflix acts my favorite show. That's a shame.

examples.label: B
examples.text: She's really picky, so I'm going to let her decide where we're grabbing coffee. If we're up to me, we just go to a convenience store.

examples.label: B
examples.text: I have no clue how to beat this final boss. He must have some sort of Achilles heel, but I just can't find it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このラスボスをどう倒せばいいか全く見当がつかない。弱点があるはずなのに見つけられない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I have no clue why every man is dog in my class uses an iPhone. I think Android are much better.
examples.japaneseTranslation: なぜクラスの男子全員がiPhone使ってるのかさっぱりわからない。Androidの方がいいと思う。

examples.label: D

examples.label: B
examples.text: Did Fred think that I was angry at him? I didn't mean to come across like that. I was just tired.
examples.japaneseTranslation: フレッド、私が怒ってると思ったの？そんな印象を与えたつもりはない。ただ疲れてただけ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I met him in person he was very nasty. He didn't come across like that on Twitter.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 直接会ったらすごく意地悪だった。Twitter上ではそんな印象じゃなかった。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm gonna prep for the next semester by becoming an early bird again. It's just around the corner.
examples.japaneseTranslation: また早起き生活に戻して新学期の準備をする。もうすぐだから。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I can't believe my birthday is just around the corner. How has it been a year already?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 誕生日もうすぐだなんて信じられない。もう1年経ったの？

examples.label: D

words.japaneseMeaning: （人を）がっかりさせる、期待を裏切る

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm a little under the weather, so I'm going to cancel the workshop today. I didn't want to let people down, but better safe than sorry.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 体調が優れないから今日のワークショップはキャンセルする。参加者をがっかりさせたくなかったけど、安全が第一だ。

examples.text: She's cancelled dinner for the fifth time this month. I feel a little bit let down to be honest.

examples.label: D

examples.text: I'm sure you had your reasons, but the bottom lines that you left me high and dry when I need you the most.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 理由はあったんだろうけど、要点は一番必要な時に見捨てられたってことだ。

examples.label: D

examples.label: B
examples.text: Don't overthink things, just enjoy life. Everyone has their ups and downs.

words.japaneseMeaning: （仕事以外の）自分の生活がある

examples.label: B
examples.text: I see him at the office all the time. I don't think he has a life.
examples.japaneseTranslation: いつもオフィスにいる。プライベートの生活がないんじゃないか。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I can't just drop everything and come when you ask, I have a life you know.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 呼ばれたらすぐに来られるわけないよ。自分の生活もあるんだから。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Have meaningful things to do with one's life.

words.nativeDefinition: To have meaningful activities and interests outside work.
words.etymology: have a life（人生がある）で、プライベートな充実した生活を持つことを表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Don't worry about doing any research into this. I'm on it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 調査は心配しないで。僕がやっておくから。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Don't worry about sprucing up the presentations slides. I'm already on it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: プレゼンのスライドを整えるのも心配しないで。もう対応中だよ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Somebody's working on it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人が）対応中だ

words.nativeDefinition: Someone is already working on it.
words.etymology: on it（それに取り組んでいる）で、既に対応中であることを表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I want to be a couch potato today, but my friends keep bugging me to hang out.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今日はソファポテトになりたいのに、友達が遊びに誘ってくる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I have some ground rules at home about how much TV my kids can watch each day. I don't want them to become couch potatoes.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 子供がテレビばかり見てソファポテトになるのは嫌だから、1日の視聴時間にルールを設けてる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Lazy person.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 怠け者；ソファで過ごす人

words.nativeDefinition: A lazy person who spends a lot of time sitting and watching TV.
words.etymology: couch（ソファ）＋ potato（ジャガイモ）で、動かずソファに座りっぱなしの人を表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: （人を）度肝を抜かせる、びっくりさせる

examples.label: B
examples.text: My dad gave me a surprise gift. It totally came out of left field and blew my mind.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 父がサプライズプレゼントをくれた。完全に不意打ちで、度肝を抜かれた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I remember when I got the PlayStation 1, the graphics blew my mind back in the day. It was amazing!
examples.japaneseTranslation: PlayStation 1を手に入れた時、グラフィックにびっくりした。当時は本当にすごかった！

examples.label: D
examples.text: A standard somebody.

examples.label: B
examples.text: Although I've been studying English for three years now, I still have to google words every now and then.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 英語を3年勉強してるけど、まだたまに単語をググる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My daughter visits me every now and then to drop off my favorite foods.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 娘がたまに好きな食べ物を持ってきてくれる。

examples.label: D
