words.nativeDefinition: Occasionally; from time to time.
words.etymology: now and then（今とそれから）で、時折という意味。

examples.label: B
examples.text: He seems a bit cold because he's not very good at small talk, but he's actually always looking out for other people.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 雑談が苦手で冷たく見えるけど、実はいつも周りの人の面倒を見てる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Can you please look out for Brian? He's a newbie, so he still needs coaching.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ブライアンの面倒見てくれない？新人だからまだ指導が必要なんだ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Care for somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人の）面倒を見る；気にかける

words.nativeDefinition: To watch over and care for someone.
words.etymology: look out（見張る）＋ for（〜のために）で、誰かのことを気にかける。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Can you fix up those numbers? Actually forget about that. They're spot on.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 数字直してくれる？…いや、忘れて。バッチリ合ってる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Did I tell you to grab drinks for the night? Forget about that. We got plenty at home.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 飲み物買ってきてって言ったっけ？忘れて。家にたくさんあるから。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Disregard that.
examples.japaneseTranslation: それは忘れて；気にしないで

words.nativeDefinition: To disregard or cancel a previous request or instruction.
words.etymology: forget about（〜について忘れる）で、先ほどの指示を取り消す。

words.japaneseMeaning: （人を）動揺させる、ビクビクさせる

examples.label: B
examples.text: That movie had me rattle the all day, I'm never watching horror movies ever again.
examples.japaneseTranslation: あの映画、一日中ビクビクし続けた。ホラー映画はもう二度と見ない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Don't pay attention to the opponent's supporters over there. They're just trying to rally you before the game.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 向こうの応援団のことは気にしないで。試合前に動揺させようとしてるだけ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Warrior upset somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人を）動揺させる；イライラさせる

words.nativeDefinition: To unsettle or make someone nervous.
words.etymology: rattle（ガタガタ鳴らす）で、心を揺さぶることを表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: ファンガール、熱狂的な女性ファン

examples.label: B
examples.text: It's so cute to see an actress fan growing over other actresses.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 女優が他の女優に熱狂するのを見るの、可愛いよね。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I don't really know what this YouTuber's selling point is, but he seems to have a lot of fan girls.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このYouTuberの売りが何かよく分からないけど、ファンガールが多いみたい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: The haven and overexcited way, overly enthusiastic fan.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 熱狂的なファン

words.nativeDefinition: An overly enthusiastic female fan of a celebrity.
words.etymology: fan（ファン）＋ girl（女の子）の造語。

words.headword: your guess is as good as mine
words.headwordAudio: vibe/035A your guess is as good as mine.wav
words.phonetic: jʊr gɛs ɪz ɛz gʊd ɛz maɪn
words.japaneseMeaning: 私にも分からない、お互い様

examples.label: B
examples.text: I can't tell you how much your next bonus will be. Your guess is good as mine.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 次のボーナスがいくらかは教えられない。私にも分からない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I hope that'll snow tomorrow, but your guess is good as mine.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 明日雪が降るといいけど、私にも分からない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I'm not sure either.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 私にも分からない

words.etymology: your guess is as good as mine（あなたの推測も私の推測と同じ）で、同じく不明という意味。

words.japaneseMeaning: （その場に）留まる、しばらくいる

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm not going to stick around forever so if you want to ask me something you should do it soon rather than later.
examples.japaneseTranslation: いつまでもここにいるわけじゃないから、聞きたいことがあれば早めに。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm not surprised next step around until 3 in the morning. He's a real party animal.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼が朝3時までいるのは驚かない。本物のパーティーアニマルだから。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Remain.
examples.japaneseTranslation: そのままいる；残る

words.etymology: stick（くっつく）＋ around（周りに）で、近くに留まる。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Is there standard wording I should use to write this letter? It's legal document, so I don't want to just wing it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この手紙に使うべき決まった言い回しはある？法的書類だから、適当には書けない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I like the wording of this email. Can you send us to the rest of the team?
examples.japaneseTranslation: このメールの言い回しが好き。チーム全員に転送してくれる？

examples.label: D
examples.text: Frazing.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 言い回し；表現

words.nativeDefinition: The choice and arrangement of words in speech or writing.
words.etymology: word（言葉）＋ -ing（〜すること）で、言葉の選び方・表現を指す。

words.japaneseMeaning: 現段階では、この時点では

examples.label: B
examples.text: I don't want to move the London permanently at this stage. I might have a change of heart in a couple of years.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 現段階ではロンドンに永住するつもりはない。数年後に気が変わるかも。

examples.label: C
examples.text: At this stage, we haven't picked a place for lunch. We'll figure it out later.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 現段階ではランチの場所は決まってない。後で考えよう。

examples.label: D
examples.text: At this point in time.

words.etymology: stage（段階）で、現在の進行段階を指す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I wish somebody would text me for real, I need to kill time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 誰か本気でメッセージくれないかな。時間つぶしたい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I have to kill time for 20 hours at the airport. I hope I can live with that.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 空港で20時間も時間をつぶさなきゃ。耐えられるといいけど。

examples.label: D
examples.text: be occupied while waiting.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 時間をつぶす

words.etymology: kill（殺す）＋ time（時間）で、待ち時間を潰すという表現。

words.japaneseMeaning: ぼったくり、法外な値段

examples.label: B
examples.text: I can't believe you paid 500,000 yen for that ancient iPod, would rip off.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 古いiPodに50万円払ったなんて信じられない。ぼったくられたよ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Did you pay $15 for a cup of coffee? What a rip off! You should really cut back on your spending. It all adds up.
examples.japaneseTranslation: コーヒー1杯に15ドル？ぼったくりだよ！支出を抑えないと。小さな出費も積もるよ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: We'll socialize.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ぼったくり；法外な値段

words.nativeDefinition: Something that is overpriced; a swindle.
words.etymology: rip off（引き剥がす）から、不当に高い値段を請求することを表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I never really broke up with my axe. We just sort of drifted apart in Lost Touch.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 別れたわけじゃない。ただ自然と疎遠になって、連絡が取れなくなった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I think Oscar and I began drifting apart when he moved overseas.
examples.japaneseTranslation: オスカーとは彼が海外に引っ越してから疎遠になり始めたと思う。

examples.label: D

words.japaneseMeaning: （ネットで）バズる、急速に拡散する

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm pretty good at making videos like Ovaro, but it's still definitely not an exact science.
examples.japaneseTranslation: バズる動画は作れる自信あるけど、正確な科学ではない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I can't believe Paul's post went viral again, he's on fire this week.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ポールの投稿がまたバズった。今週は絶好調だね。

examples.label: D

examples.japaneseTranslation: 急速に広まる

words.headwordAudio: vibe/043A take sth_sb at face value.wav
words.japaneseMeaning: 額面通りに受け取る、表面的に信じる

examples.label: B
examples.text: I wouldn't take what Nicole says at face value. She's an absolute drama queen.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ニコールの言うことは額面通りに受け取らない方がいい。完全なドラマクイーンだから。

examples.label: C
examples.text: A lot of people take things at face-fallion instead of critically evaluating them, and I think that's a serious problem.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 批判的に評価せず、額面通りに受け取る人が多い。それは深刻な問題だと思う。

examples.text: Trust something or somebody without question.

examples.label: B
examples.text: If you make a website for me, all pull some strings to get you a ticket to the concert. Deal?
examples.japaneseTranslation: サイト作ってくれたら、コンサートのチケット手配するよ。取引成立？

examples.label: C
examples.text: Just follow me and I'll follow back. It's a deal.
examples.japaneseTranslation: フォローしてくれたらフォロー返す。約束だよ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: agreement

words.japaneseMeaning: 分かってる？、ついて来れてる？

examples.label: B
examples.text: Let me dumb it down for you. A gravitational singularity is location where the space time curvature becomes infinite. Are you with me?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 分かりやすく言うね。重力特異点っていうのは、時空の曲率が無限大になる場所。分かってる？

examples.label: C
examples.text: Are you with me? This song just makes my ears bleed.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 分かってる？この曲、耳が痛い。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Do you understand what I'm saying?

words.etymology: with me（一緒に）で、話の内容について理解しているか確認する表現。

words.japaneseMeaning: （人に）恋に落ちる、好きになる

examples.label: B
examples.text: I've only known for a few weeks, but I think I'm falling for a crazy, right?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 数週間しか知らないけど、彼女に恋してる気がする。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Nothing is more bitter sweet than falling for somebody and not having that low returned.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 誰かに恋して、その気持ちが返ってこないのほど苦しいものはない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: FONNAGLASS OF SONBRARY
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人に）恋に落ちる

words.etymology: fall for（〜に落ちる）で、恋愛感情を抱くことを表す。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/047A get the hang of sth.wav

examples.label: B
examples.text: It's still early days, but Joe's already starting to get the hang of surfing very impressive.
examples.japaneseTranslation: まだ初期段階だけど、ジョーはもうサーフィンのコツを掴み始めてる。すごい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I finally got in the hang of driving. I thought it'd be really hard, but it's actually pretty straightforward.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 運転のコツをようやく掴んだ。難しいと思ってたけど、実は割とシンプル。

examples.label: D
examples.text: A choir, a general sense or something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かの）コツをつかむ

words.nativeDefinition: To learn how to do something through practice.
words.etymology: hang（掛かり）で、物事のやり方をつかむ・慣れることを表す。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/048A keep an eye on sth_sb.wav
words.japaneseMeaning: （〜を）見張る、気にかけて見ておく

examples.label: B
examples.text: This app is so sick, I can keep an eye on my kitten remotely while I'm away.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このアプリすごい。留守の間もリモートで子猫を見守れる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Can you please keep an eye on my kids for me? They've been bouncing off the walls today.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 子供の面倒見てくれる？今日はすごくはしゃいでる。

examples.label: D

words.nativeDefinition: To watch or monitor something or someone carefully.
words.etymology: eye（目）をkeep（保つ）で、注意深く監視することを表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: My computer's getting a bit laggy. I'm not sure if I should upgrade it or just buy a new one.
examples.japaneseTranslation: パソコンがちょっと重くなってきた。アップグレードすべきか、新しいの買うべきか。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This website is kind of shady and super laggy. I have no idea why you recommended it.

examples.label: D
examples.text: sluggish
examples.japaneseTranslation: 動作が遅い；もっさりした

words.nativeDefinition: Slow to respond; experiencing delay, especially in computing.
words.etymology: lag（遅延）＋ -y で、反応が遅い状態を表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: We have to keep our videos punchy and fun so that our viewers don't get bored and move on to watching something else.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 動画はテンポよく楽しくしないと、視聴者が飽きて他の動画に移っちゃう。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This meeting is dragging on a bit so I'm going to move on to the next topic soon.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この会議、少し長引いてるから、そろそろ次のトピックに移る。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Advance
examples.japaneseTranslation: 前に進む；切り替える

words.nativeDefinition: To proceed to something else; to continue after a setback.
words.etymology: move（動く）＋ on（先へ）で、次の段階に進むことを表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I want to put my best foot forward at the interview tomorrow morning, so I'm going to hit the sack a bit early tonight.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 明日の面接でベストを尽くしたいから、今夜は早めに寝る。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm going to hit the Saxon so I'll speak to you tomorrow.
examples.japaneseTranslation: もう寝るから、明日話そう。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Go to bed.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 寝る；就寝する

examples.label: B
examples.text: He's pretending to be happy, but deep inside it must hurt a lot.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 幸せそうに振る舞ってるけど、心の奥ではすごく傷ついてるに違いない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: We're not together anymore, but deep inside, I still miss her.

examples.label: D

words.etymology: deep（深い）＋ inside（内側）で、心の奥底を表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I tried making up on my girlfriend last night, but she wouldn't listen to a word I said.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昨夜彼女と仲直りしようとしたけど、一言も聞いてくれなかった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I want to extend an olive branch to our so we can make up and be friends again.
examples.japaneseTranslation: オリーブの枝を差し出して、仲直りしてまた友達になりたい。

examples.label: D

examples.label: B
examples.text: What comes to mind when you see this photo?
examples.japaneseTranslation: この写真を見て何が思い浮かぶ？

examples.label: C
examples.text: Nothing comes to mind right now, but I'll let you know as soon as I think of any great ideas.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今は何も思い浮かばないけど、いいアイデアが浮かんだらすぐ連絡する。

examples.label: D

examples.japaneseTranslation: （人の）頭に浮かぶ

words.etymology: come to mind（心に来る）で、思い浮かぶことを表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: （問題に）取り組む、 tackle する

examples.label: B
examples.text: When you feel that a problem is too big to tackle, you should break it down into smaller parts.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 問題が大きすぎると感じたら、小さな部分に分けて取り組むべきだ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My thinking is that we should talk a one problem at a time. Multitasking never works.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 一度に一つの問題に取り組むべきだと思う。マルチタスクはうまくいかない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Do you have a something?
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かに）取り組む；対処する

words.nativeDefinition: To deal with or take on a problem or task.
words.etymology: tackle（タックル）はラグビーから、問題に正面から取り組む意味に。

words.japaneseMeaning: ベビーブーマー世代、（スラングで）年配の人

examples.label: B
examples.text: Why is our new CEO trying to weed out all the boomers? Age is just a number, right?
examples.japaneseTranslation: なんで新CEOはベビーブーマーを排除しようとしてるの？年齢はただの数字でしょ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This whole concept of mandatory FaceTime in the office is such a boomer thing. I hope it goes away soon.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 出社必須のビデオ通話なんて、完全にベビーブーマー的な発想だ。早くなくなってほしい。

words.nativeDefinition: A person born in the baby boomer generation; slang for an older person.
words.etymology: baby boomer（第二次世界大戦後の人口増加期に生まれた世代）の略。

examples.label: B
examples.text: She kept sending the pleading emoji to me, so I took it the wrong way and thought she had a crush on me.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼女がずっとお願いの絵文字を送ってきたから、勘違いして片思いされてると思った。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I have a crush on Kate, can you please keep it between us?
examples.japaneseTranslation: ケイトに片思いしてる。内緒にしてね。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Have a feeling of romantic love for somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人に）片思いしている

examples.label: B
examples.text: In my opinion, people shouldn't think that they can get away with saying whatever pops in their head on the internet just because they're anonymous.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 匿名だからといって、頭に浮かんだことを何でも言っていいわけじゃない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: It was completely unrelated, but I brought it up because it just popped into my head out of the blue.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 全く関係ないけど、ふと思い浮かんだから話した。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Sunni occurred to somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人の）頭にふと浮かぶ

words.nativeDefinition: To suddenly occur to someone.
words.etymology: pop（突然現れる）＋ into one's head（頭の中に）で、ふと思い浮かぶ。

words.japaneseMeaning: 正直に言うと、ぶっちゃけ

examples.label: B
examples.text: Not gonna lie. I think people who take it out on random people and social media are usually very quite in real life. Kind of scary, isn't it?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 正直に言うと、SNSで知らない人に当たり散らす人は、リアルではかなり大人しいことが多い。怖くない？

examples.label: C
examples.text: Not gonna lie. I was pretty stressed out yesterday, so I decided to go to the gym to blow off some steam. It was a great idea.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 正直に言うと、昨日すごくストレス溜まってたから、ジム行って発散した。正解だった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: To be honest.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 正直に言うと

words.nativeDefinition: To be honest; frankly.
words.etymology: not gonna lie（嘘はつかない）＝正直に言うと、という前置き。

words.japaneseMeaning: （うまく）進む、順調に進展する

examples.label: B
examples.text: Good to hear that your relationship is coming along well. Music to my ears.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 関係がうまく進んでるって聞いてよかった。最高のニュースだ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Have a new job coming along.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 新しい仕事も順調に進んでる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Pegrace!
examples.japaneseTranslation: 一緒に来る；付いてくる

words.nativeDefinition: To progress or develop well.
words.etymology: come along（一緒について来る）から、物事が進展する意味に。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I love this cat's grumpy face so adorable.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この猫の不機嫌な顔、可愛すぎる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Alexa refuses to answer my questions. I guess she's a bit grumpy today.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Alexaが質問に答えてくれない。今日は機嫌悪いのかな。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Bad tempered.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 不機嫌な；機嫌が悪い

words.nativeDefinition: Bad-tempered; irritable.
words.etymology: grump（不機嫌な人）＋ -y で、機嫌が悪い様子を表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: 待ちきれない、楽しみでたまらない

examples.label: B
examples.text: I can't wait to see you again.
examples.japaneseTranslation: また会えるの待ちきれない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I can't wait to get my hands on my new phone. The screen on this one's been cracked for ages.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 新しいスマホを手に入れるのが待ちきれない。この画面、何ヶ月も割れてる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: be very excited.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 待ちきれない；とても楽しみだ

words.nativeDefinition: To be very excited and eager.
words.etymology: can't wait（待てない）で、強い期待と興奮を表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: 一見して、最初の印象では

examples.label: B
examples.text: I thought the house was pretty big at first glance, but after I went inside I realized it was quite cramped.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 一見して家は大きそうだったけど、中に入ったらかなり狭かった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: At first glance, I thought I could at least ask her out on a date, turns out she's way out of my league.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 一見してデートに誘えるかなと思ったけど、全然レベルが違った。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Based on initial impressions
examples.japaneseTranslation: 一目見た感じでは；第一印象では

words.nativeDefinition: Based on first impressions before closer examination.
words.etymology: at first glance（最初の一瞥で）で、初見の印象を表す。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/064A give sb a hard time.wav
words.japaneseMeaning: （人を）困らせる、厳しく当たる

examples.label: B
examples.text: Man, this final exam is giving me a really hard time. I just want to get it over with.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 期末試験が本当に苦手。早く終わらせたい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Some people are giving you a hard time? Look on the bright side. They love you, but probably don't know how to express it love.
examples.japaneseTranslation: みんなに厳しく当たられてる？良い面もあるよ。愛されてるけど、表現の仕方がわからないだけかも。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Treat somebody harshly.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人を）ひどく扱う；いじめる

words.nativeDefinition: To treat someone harshly or cause someone difficulty.
words.etymology: give a hard time（大変な時間を与える）で、困らせることを表す。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/065A let sb off the hook.wav

examples.label: B
examples.text: I don't have to do the dishes today because my wife let me off the hook. I'm walking on Sunshine.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 妻が許してくれたから、今日は皿洗いしなくていい。天国だ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I can't believe you let Robert off the hook again. He stood you up so many times already.
examples.japaneseTranslation: またロバート許したの？何度もドタキャンしてるのに。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Allow somebody to avoid responsibility or consequences.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人を）許す；責任を免除する

words.nativeDefinition: To allow someone to avoid punishment or responsibility.
words.etymology: let off the hook（釣り針から外す）＝責任から解放する比喩。

words.japaneseMeaning: （人から）連絡をもらう、便りを聞く

examples.label: B
examples.text: I haven't heard from Kylie in a while. What's she up to these days?
examples.japaneseTranslation: カイリーからしばらく連絡ない。最近何してるんだろう。

examples.label: C
examples.text: It's really good to hear from you. I was worried that something had happened to you.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 連絡くれて本当に良かった。何かあったんじゃないかと心配してた。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Be contacted by somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人から）連絡がある

words.nativeDefinition: To receive communication from someone.
words.etymology: hear from（〜から聞く）で、連絡を受けることを表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I never thought that taking selfies would become my bread and butter.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 自撮りが生計の手段になるなんて思わなかった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I take a selfie every day to keep track of changes in my skin.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 毎日自撮りして、肌の変化を記録してる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Take a picture of oneself.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 自撮りする

words.nativeDefinition: To take a photograph of oneself.
words.etymology: selfie（自撮り）の動詞形。

words.japaneseMeaning: 考え直す、二の足を踏む

examples.label: B
examples.text: I was having second thoughts about my Tinder profile picture, but that ship is sailed. I've already been banned.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Tinderのプロフィール写真を考え直してたけど、もう手遅れ。BANされてた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: It's okay to have second thoughts about your future, it's not set in stone.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 将来について考え直すのは普通のこと。決まったわけじゃない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Reyes, reconsider.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 考え直す；迷いが生じる

words.nativeDefinition: To reconsider a decision.
words.etymology: second thoughts（二番目の考え）で、再考することを表す。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/069A I wouldn't worry about sth.wav

examples.label: B
examples.text: I wouldn't worry about Terry's bad mood, he's usually like to sound Mondays.
examples.japaneseTranslation: テリーの機嫌の悪さは心配しなくていい。月曜はいつもああだから。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I wouldn't worry about making the poster-inch perfect. I think it's already close enough.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ポスターを完璧にする必要はないと思う。もう十分近い。

examples.label: D
examples.text: You don't have to be concerned about something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かを）心配しなくていい

words.nativeDefinition: Used to tell someone not to worry about something.
words.etymology: wouldn't worry（心配しないだろう）で、心配不要を伝える。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/070A leave sth up to sb.wav

examples.label: B
examples.text: Interior design is your strong suit, so I'll leave the decorating up to you.
examples.japaneseTranslation: インテリアデザインはあなたの得意分野だから、装飾は任せるよ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'll leave the catering for the wedding up to you.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 結婚式のケータリングも任せる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Give somebody the responsibility for something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かを）人に任せる

words.nativeDefinition: To give someone the responsibility for something.
words.etymology: leave up to（〜に委ねる）で、責任を任せることを表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: This girl that I really like just told me that she only sees me as a friend. It's a bit of a bummer, but I'll get over it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 好きな子に友達以上とは思ってないって言われた。ちょっと残念だけど、乗り越える。

examples.label: C
examples.text: It's a bit of a bummer that we have to be in the office where the weather is so gorgeous.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 天気が最高なのに出社しなきゃいけないの、ちょっと残念。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Disappointment
examples.japaneseTranslation: 残念だ；がっかりだ

words.nativeDefinition: Something disappointing or unfortunate.
words.etymology: bummer（残念）で、がっかりする出来事を表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: What's your take on that brainstorming session that we had earlier? I personally think it was a waste of time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: さっきのブレスト、どう思った？個人的には時間の無駄だった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'd be interested to get Jim's take on what the latest stock market crash means for the future of the US economy.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ジムの見解を聞きたい。最近の株価暴落が米国経済にどう影響するか。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Somebody's opinion on something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人の）（何かに対する）意見

words.nativeDefinition: Someone's opinion or perspective on something.
words.etymology: take（見方）で、個人の意見・見解を表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: （人に）優しくする、手加減する

examples.label: B
examples.text: You can't bear your head in the sand and go easy on Nick just because he's your friend. He punched somebody in the face.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ニックは友達だからといって手加減しちゃダメ。人の顔を殴ったんだよ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: She's a bit highly strong at the moment, so I'm going to go easy on her so that she doesn't feel more stressed.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼女は今神経質だから、手加減してあげよう。ストレス増やしたくない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Treat somebody gently.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人に）手加減する；優しくする

words.nativeDefinition: To treat someone gently or leniently.
words.etymology: go easy（優しく）＋ on（〜に）で、手加減することを表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: You can't count on him to do legit work. He's a few sandwiches short of a picnic.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼にまともな仕事を期待するのは無理。頭がちょっとおかしい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: When I travel, I bring my own shampoo and stuff because I think counting on hotel freebies is risky.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 旅行の時は自分のシャンプー持参する。ホテルの無料品に頼るのはリスクだ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Reliant something or somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何か・人を）頼りにする

words.nativeDefinition: To rely on someone or something.
words.etymology: count on（数に入れる）＝頼りにするという意味。

examples.label: B
examples.text: You're bringing wine to the party tonight, sweet.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今夜パーティーにワイン持ってくるの？いいね。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You're right, this is a pretty sweet restaurant. I should know better than to judge a book by its cover.
examples.japaneseTranslation: その通り、このレストランかなりいいね。見た目で判断しちゃダメだった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Great!
examples.japaneseTranslation: 素敵だ；いいね

words.nativeDefinition: Slang for great, awesome, or wonderful.
words.etymology: sweet（甘い）から、気持ちいい・最高というスラング用法に。

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜を）保留にする、一時停止する

examples.label: B
examples.text: Until my boss gives me the green light, I'm going to put my travel plans on hold.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 上司のGOサインが出るまで、旅行計画は保留にする。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I decided to put my homework on a hold and watch the bachelor instead.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 宿題を保留にして、バachelorette観ることにした。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Pause something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かを）保留にする；一時停止する

words.nativeDefinition: To postpone or pause something.
words.etymology: on hold（保留中）で、一時的に止めることを表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: （人と）イチャイチャする、ナンパする

examples.label: B
examples.text: I put two and two together and finally figured out she was just flirting with me. It's sad because I was really serious.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 状況を整理したら、彼女はただイチャイチャしてただけだと分かった。本気だったのに悲しい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Every time you try to flirt with a girl online, you always start speaking gibberish, like, you're la la la la la la la
examples.japaneseTranslation: オンラインで女の子とイチャイチャしようとするたび、意味不明なこと言い出すんだよね。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Interact with somebody in a playfully romantic way.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人と）軽く恋愛モードで話す

words.nativeDefinition: To behave in a playfully romantic way toward someone.
words.etymology: flirt（いちゃつく）で、軽い恋愛的なやり取りを表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: 参考までに、一応言うと

examples.label: B
examples.text: For what it's worth, I think you should get back to basics. I know you want to run your own business, but you don't even know what profit means.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 参考までに、基本に戻るべきだと思う。起業したいのは分かるけど、利益の意味すら知らないでしょ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: For what it's worth, it's never too late. You should start working towards your dream if that's what you really want to do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 参考までに、遅くても遅くない。本当にやりたいなら、夢に向かって動き始めるべき。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I'm not sure if this is useful.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 参考までに；役に立つか分からないが

words.nativeDefinition: Used to introduce an opinion that may not be valued.
words.etymology: for what it's worth（それがどれだけの価値があるかはともかく）で、控えめに意見を述べる。

words.japaneseMeaning: 要するに、一言で言えば

examples.label: B
examples.text: In a nutshell, how will you get along with your mother-in-law? Will make or break your marriage?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 要するに、義母とうまくやっていけるかが結婚の成否を左右する。

examples.label: C
examples.text: There's a lot of career advice I could give you, but in a nutshell, you should always play to your strengths.
examples.japaneseTranslation: キャリアアドバイスはたくさんあるけど、要するに自分の強みを活かすべき。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Simply put.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 要するに；簡潔に言えば

words.nativeDefinition: In summary; briefly stated.
words.etymology: in a nutshell（ナッツの殻の中に）＝簡潔にまとめる比喩。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm at the end of my rope, no matter where I place this couch, it just always seems out of place.
examples.japaneseTranslation: もう限界。ソファをどこに置いても場違いに見える。

examples.label: C
examples.text: She looks so out of place and her princess party dress while the others are dressed in business suits.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 他の人がスーツなのに、プリンセスドレスで場違いすぎる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Not in a proper place.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 場違いな；馴染まない

words.nativeDefinition: Not fitting in; seeming inappropriate for the situation.
words.etymology: out of place（場所から外れて）で、場に合わないことを表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: （話が）分からなくなった、置いていかれた

examples.label: B
examples.text: This blog post is too worthy. You've lost me. Maybe try to keep it simple.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このブログ記事、難しすぎる。置いていかれた。もっとシンプルにして。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Wait, you've lost me. Why did you reply? Hi, I'm Song Goku. When I just simply ask you, how are you?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 待って、置いていかれた。なんで「こんにちは、孫悟空です」って返信したの？

examples.label: D
examples.text: I don't understand what you are saying.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 分からなくなった；置いていかれた

words.nativeDefinition: I don't understand what you're saying.
words.etymology: lost me（私を見失った）＝理解できなくなったという意味。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Are you going to hang on to all this stuff just because it might come in handy one day? I don't get it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: いつか役に立つかもって理由で全部持ってるの？理解できない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: After you open a beer with it, Jacob told me with a straight face, a new my fidget spinner would come in handy eventually.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ビール栓を開けた後、フィジェットスピナーが役に立つって真面目な顔で言われた。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Beautiful!
examples.japaneseTranslation: 役に立つ；重宝する

words.nativeDefinition: To be useful in a particular situation.
words.etymology: come in handy（手元に来る）＝必要な時に役立つ。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I have a lot of old photos that have been collecting dust on the shelf. I'm sure they will bring back so many memories.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 棚で埃をかぶってる古い写真、きっとたくさんの思い出を呼び起こしてくれる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This song brings back so many memories and feelings.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この曲、たくさんの思い出と感情を呼び起こす。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Remind somebody of past memories.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昔を思い出させる

words.nativeDefinition: To cause someone to remember past experiences.
words.etymology: bring back（呼び戻す）＋ memories（思い出）で、記憶を蘇らせる。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Am I coming to the party tonight? You bet. What time is it kicking off?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今夜パーティー行く？もちろん。何時から？

examples.label: C
examples.text: You bet I'm going to tell him what I'm thinking. I couldn't care less about how he's going to react.
examples.japaneseTranslation: もちろん本音言うよ。彼の反応なんてどうでもいい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Of course.
examples.japaneseTranslation: もちろん；間違いない

words.nativeDefinition: Certainly; of course.
words.etymology: you bet（あなたの賭け）＝間違いない、もちろんという強い同意。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I started playing Pokemon Go for the first time in so far I'm having a blast.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 久しぶりにポケモンGO始めたけど、めちゃくちゃ楽しい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I cut up with Kevin last night and had a blast.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昨夜ケビンと遊んで、めちゃくちゃ楽しかった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Have fun!
examples.japaneseTranslation: とても楽しむ

words.nativeDefinition: To have a very enjoyable time.
words.etymology: have a blast（爆発する）＝大いに楽しむという比喩。

words.japaneseMeaning: （人を）ゾッとさせる、うんざりさせる

examples.label: B
examples.text: When I see people biting their nails, it makes me cringe like crazy.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 爪を噛む人を見ると、ゾッとする。

examples.label: C
examples.text: The monkey emoji makes me cringe so much.
examples.japaneseTranslation: サルの絵文字、ゾッとしすぎる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Make somebody feel disgusted.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人を）ゾッとさせる；恥ずかしがらせる

words.nativeDefinition: To make someone feel embarrassed or disgusted.
words.etymology: cringe（身震いする）を他者に引き起こす＝ゾッとさせる。

words.headword: for the first time in ages
words.headwordAudio: vibe/088A for the first time in ages.wav
words.phonetic: fər ðə fərst taɪm ɪn ˈeɪʤɪz

examples.label: B
examples.text: I bought clothes for the first time in ages.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 久しぶりに服買った。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I met up with my cousin last night for the first time in ages.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昨夜、久しぶりにいとこに会った。

examples.label: D
examples.text: for the first time in a long time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 久しぶりに初めて

words.nativeDefinition: For the first time in a long while.
words.etymology: in ages（長い年月の間）で、久しぶりという意味。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Everyone thinks that Kelly's a really hard worker because she always sends emails at midnight, but the reality is that she's just night out.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ケリーは真夜中にメール送ってるから仕事熱心だと思われてるけど、実はただの夜型。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Are you actually a night owl or do you just slack off during the day?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 本当に夜型？それとも昼間サボってるだけ？

examples.label: D
examples.text: Person who prefers to be active late at night.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 夜型の人

words.nativeDefinition: A person who prefers to stay up late at night.
words.etymology: night owl（夜のフクロウ）で、夜型の人を表す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: My wife gave me the go-ahead to buy the car because it was a good deal.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 妻がGOサイン出してくれた。お得な買い物だったから。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Is the new iPhone a good deal compared to similar Android devices?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 新しいiPhone、似たAndroidと比べてお得？

examples.label: D
examples.text: Good value.
examples.japaneseTranslation: お買い得；いい取引

words.nativeDefinition: Good value for the price.
words.etymology: good deal（良い取引）で、お得な買い物を表す。

words.japaneseMeaning: ボーッとする、上の空になる

examples.label: B
examples.text: I always space out when my boss goes on and on about his wife and kids.

examples.japaneseTranslation: 兄が何してるか分からない。ただ漂ってるだけで、何にも本気で取り組んでない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I've just been drifting along at work for a couple of days, so I'll have to pick up the pace soon, the deadline is around the corner.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 仕事で数日流れに任せてた。締切が近いから、そろそろペース上げないと。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Progress without direction.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 流れに任せて進む

words.nativeDefinition: To progress without clear direction or purpose.
words.etymology: drift（漂う）＋ along（一緒に）で、方向性なく進む。

words.japaneseMeaning: 危険にさらされて、賭けている

examples.label: B
examples.text: As a famous person, you shouldn't put your reputation online by doing anything shady.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 有名人は、怪しいことをして評判を危険に晒すべきじゃない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I just don't understand people who take selfies on cliff ledges. I wouldn't put my life on the line for a stupid photo.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 崖の縁で自撮りする人が理解できない。バカな写真のために命を賭けたくない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: and risk.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 危険にさらされている

words.nativeDefinition: At risk; in a vulnerable position.
words.etymology: on the line（線の上）＝危険に晒されている状態。

words.japaneseMeaning: 危うく〜するところだった、九死に一生

examples.label: B
examples.text: I had a real close shave walking across the road yesterday. A car just appeared out of nowhere and almost hit me from behind.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昨日道を渡ってて危うくひかれるところだった。車が突然現れて後ろから。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I've had a few close shaves likely, so I'm going to visit a temple tomorrow to purify myself.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 最近何度か危うい目に遭ったから、明日お寺行って祓う。

examples.label: D
examples.text: How a narrow escape from danger.
examples.japaneseTranslation: かろうじて危機を逃れる

words.nativeDefinition: A narrow escape from danger or disaster.
words.etymology: close shave（ギリギリの剃り）＝かろうじて逃れた比喩。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I have to stay back late tonight. I've been roped into this crazy project that needs to get wrapped up by tomorrow morning.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今夜残業。明日朝までに終わらせなきゃいけないクレイジーなプロジェクトに無理やり巻き込まれた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I heard that you managed to rope you to enjoy the basketball club. Good stuff!
examples.japaneseTranslation: ユージンをバスケ部に入れたんだ。すごい！

examples.label: D
examples.text: Involve somebody in something against somebody's will.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人を）無理やり巻き込む

words.nativeDefinition: To involve someone in something against their will.
words.etymology: rope in（ロープで引き込む）＝無理やり参加させる。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I heard Bill Loss's watch on the train yesterday. That's pretty rough.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昨日電車でビルの時計を盗まれたって聞いた。かなりひどい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: It's pretty rough how Adam has to play second fiddle to Sophia. She's just good at sucking up to the powers that be.
examples.japaneseTranslation: アダムがソフィアの二番手なのはかなり厳しい。上の人に媚びるのが上手いだけ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Pretty unfortunate or harsh.
examples.japaneseTranslation: かなりつらい；かなり不運な

words.nativeDefinition: Pretty difficult or harsh.
words.etymology: pretty（かなり）＋ tough（厳しい）で、相当きつい状況。

words.japaneseMeaning: （様子を）探る、慎重に確かめる

examples.label: B
examples.text: I heard through the great one that Joe and Kylie are having a baby, but I'll feel them out to confirm it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 噂でジョーとカイリーが赤ちゃんを待ってるって聞いた。本人たちに探って確認する。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Suit's made studying loss seem interesting and easy, but I'm still trying to feel it out.
examples.japaneseTranslation: スーツは法律を面白く簡単に見せるけど、まだ様子を探ってる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Carefully gain knowledge is something or somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何か・人の）様子を探る

words.nativeDefinition: To carefully assess something or someone.
words.etymology: feel out（探る）＝慎重に様子を確かめる。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I don't suck these comments are just banner, but it's hard not to take them to hard sometimes.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このコメントは冗談だと思うけど、時々本気で受け取っちゃう。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I used to think Mike was really vanilla, but it turns out he loves a bit of banner.
examples.japaneseTranslation: マイクは地味そうに見えたけど、実は冗談好きだった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Good humor, playful, or teasing conversation.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 軽い冗談；からかい合い

words.nativeDefinition: Playful, teasing conversation.
words.etymology: banter（軽口）で、楽しい言い合いを表す。

words.headword: be careful what you wish for
words.headwordAudio: vibe/129A be careful what you wish for.wav
words.phonetic: bi ˈkɛrfəl wət ju wɪʃ fər
words.japaneseMeaning: 望むものには注意を、後悔するかも

examples.label: B
examples.text: You want your boss to notice you more? Be careful you wish for it. It might not be a bad thing to fly into the radar for a bit.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 上司にもっと目立ちたい？望むものには注意。しばらく目立たない方がいいかも。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Are you sure you want to know everything about your boyfriend? Be careful what you wish for, sometimes ignorance is bliss.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼氏のすべて知りたいの？望むものには注意。知らない方が幸せなこともある。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Carefully consider the consequences of what you're asking for.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 望むものの結果をよく考えなさい

words.nativeDefinition: Be careful what you ask for; it may have unwanted consequences.
words.etymology: be careful what you wish for（願うものには注意）という警告。

words.japaneseMeaning: 良い時も悪い時も、どんな時も

examples.label: B
examples.text: My girlfriend and I have been together through thick and dense, so of course I was caught off guard when she dumped me.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼女とは良い時も悪い時も一緒だった。だから振られた時は不意打ちだった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: As husband and wife, you have to be there for each other through thick and thin. Simple as that.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 夫婦は良い時も悪い時も互いのそばにいなきゃいけない。それだけ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Even during tough times.
examples.japaneseTranslation: どんな困難な時でも

words.etymology: through thick and thin（厚い時も薄い時も）＝順境も逆境も。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm not sure if we're actually on the right track, but just play along and say we're in good shape. I know the boss will get panicky otherwise.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 正しい方向に進んでるか分からないけど、順調だって言って合わせて。上司がパニックになるから。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I need my parents to think of my girlfriend so can you play along just until the end of lunch? I'll explain why later.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 両親に彼女の存在を信じ込ませたいから、ランチ終わるまで合わせて。後で説明する。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Comment is below.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人に）合わせて振る舞う

words.nativeDefinition: To pretend to agree or cooperate.
words.etymology: play along（一緒に演じる）＝本音ではなく合わせる。

words.japaneseMeaning: 実務で学ぶ、現場で覚える

examples.label: B
examples.text: I prefer loading on the job over reading textbooks.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 教科書より実務で学ぶ方が好き。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I thought that getting a crash course from Amy would be helpful, but honestly, learning on the job was so much easier.
examples.japaneseTranslation: エイミーから短期集中で教わるのもいいけど、正直現場で覚える方がずっと楽だった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Learn while working.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 実務を通じて学ぶ

words.nativeDefinition: To learn skills while working rather than through formal study.
words.etymology: on the job（仕事中に）＝実際の仕事を通じて学ぶ。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I wish it would stop raining, I'm sick of being cooped up at home alone.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 雨やんでほしい。一人で家に閉じ込められてうんざり。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I love my grandma, but being cooped up with her all day would probably drive me up the wall. She's a broken record.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 祖母は好きだけど、一日中一緒に閉じ込められたらイライラする。同じ話の繰り返しだし。

examples.label: D
examples.text: confined.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 閉じ込められた；狭い空間にいる

words.nativeDefinition: Confined indoors for a long time.
words.etymology: cooped up（鶏小屋に閉じ込められた）＝長時間室内にいる。

words.japaneseMeaning: 警告サイン、危ない兆候

examples.label: B
examples.text: If a restaurant isn't packed on a Friday night, it's generally a pretty big warning sign.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 金曜の夜にレストランが空いてたら、だいたい大きな警告サイン。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Is this dogmark crash warning sign that a recession is coming? I hope it's just a tempest in a T-pot.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このドットコムバブル崩壊は不況の警告サイン？大げさすぎるかも。

examples.label: D
examples.text: alert
examples.japaneseTranslation: 警告の兆候

words.nativeDefinition: A sign that something may be wrong or risky.
words.etymology: warning（警告）＋ sign（兆候）で、危険の前触れ。

words.japaneseMeaning: くつろぐ、足をのばして休む

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm really looking forward to putting my feet up over Christmas. It's been a tough few weeks.
examples.japaneseTranslation: クリスマスは足をのばして休むのが待ち遠しい。ここ数週間大変だった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: It wouldn't hurt to put your feet up every once in a while. You seem really tired.
examples.japaneseTranslation: たまには足をのばして休んでもいいんじゃない。すごく疲れてるみたい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Relax.

words.nativeDefinition: To relax, especially by putting one's feet up.
words.etymology: put one's feet up（足を上げる）＝くつろんで休む。

words.japaneseMeaning: 精神的な支え、モラルサポート

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'll definitely be at your next game for moral support if you think it'll help you.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 次の試合、精神的な支えになるなら絶対行く。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My sister always gives me a hand with anything as well as more support.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 姉はいつも手伝ってくれるし、精神的な支えもくれる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Psychological Support.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 精神的な支え

words.nativeDefinition: Support and encouragement rather than practical help.
words.etymology: moral（精神的な）＋ support（支援）で、実利ではなく精神面の支援。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm very traditional so I need my girlfriend's parents to give me the okay before I ask her to marry me.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 伝統的だから、プロポーズ前に彼女の両親からOKもらう必要がある。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My mom finally gave me the OK to buy a new game. I'm so happy.
examples.japaneseTranslation: やっと母が新しいゲーム買っていいってOK出してくれた。嬉しい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Give somebody a approval.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人に）承認を与える

words.nativeDefinition: To give approval or permission.
words.etymology: give the OK（OKを与える）＝正式に許可する。

words.japaneseMeaning: 初稿、最初のバージョン

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'll get you a first cut by Monday. It shouldn't take me too long to mock something up.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 月曜までに初稿渡す。モックアップ作るのそんな時間かからない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My video editing skills have come a long way since I gave you the first cut up a few months ago.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 数ヶ月前に渡した初稿から、動画編集スキルはだいぶ上達した。

examples.label: D
examples.text: initial draft
examples.japaneseTranslation: 最初の草案；初稿

words.nativeDefinition: An initial draft or version of something.
words.etymology: first cut（最初のカット）＝編集・制作の初版。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm not interested in opera but my girlfriend drag me along.
examples.japaneseTranslation: オペラに興味ないけど、彼女が無理やり連れて行った。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I started driving along to this meeting, but I think I'll learn a lot from it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この会議に無理やり連れてこられたけど、学ぶことは多そう。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Bring somebody against somebody's will.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人を）無理やり連れて行く

words.nativeDefinition: To bring someone somewhere reluctantly.
words.etymology: drag along（引きずって連れて行く）＝嫌がる人を連れて行く。

words.japaneseMeaning: （努力せず）流れに任せる、適当に進む

examples.label: B
examples.text: I wish I could just coast the line at work and still get promoted every year. I know that's impossible.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 仕事で適当に流してても毎年昇進できるといいんだけど、無理だよね。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You can choose to coast along your life, but you shouldn't expect much from it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 人生を流れに任せることもできるけど、あまり期待しない方がいい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Progress with minimal effort.
examples.japaneseTranslation: あまり努力せずに進む

words.etymology: coast along（慣性で進む）＝最小限の努力で進む。

words.japaneseMeaning: スポーツマンシップのある人、いい人

examples.label: B
examples.text: Jack threw a giant pie at the president's face and the president just cracked up. He's such a good sport.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ジャックが大統領の顔にパイ投げて、大統領は大笑い。本当にいい人。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Nancy is a really good sport, she doesn't like singing but she still came to our karaoke party and sang together with us.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ナンシーは本当にいい人。歌は嫌いだけど、カラオケパーティーに来て一緒に歌ってくれた。

examples.label: D
examples.text: A griable person.
examples.japaneseTranslation: スポーツマンシップのある人

words.nativeDefinition: A good-natured person who takes jokes well.
words.etymology: good sport（良いスポーツ）＝負けても寛大な人。

words.japaneseMeaning: 話し方がうまい、雄弁な

examples.label: B
examples.text: Kevin so well spoke at all the time. He never says anything for the sake of saying something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ケビンはいつも話し方がうまい。ためらいで何か言うことはない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Don't take everything this guy says is gospel just because he's so well spoken.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 話し方がうまいからといって、この人の言うことを全部鵜呑みにしちゃダメ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Eliequin.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 話し方が上手な；洗練された

words.nativeDefinition: Articulate and speaking clearly and well.
words.etymology: well（上手に）＋ spoken（話された）で、話し方が洗練されている。

words.japaneseMeaning: （人の）後ろ盾に付きまとう、監視する

examples.label: B
examples.text: Mom, stop breathing down my neck. I want to study at my own pace.
examples.japaneseTranslation: お母さん、後ろから監視しないで。自分のペースで勉強したい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You should be careful when you talk to her. She has a jealous boyfriend and is always breathing down her neck.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼女と話す時気をつけて。嫉妬深い彼氏が常に監視してる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Monitor somebody too closely.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人の）行動を細かく監視する

words.nativeDefinition: To monitor someone too closely; to pressure someone.
words.etymology: breathe down one's neck（首に息を吹きかける）＝過度に監視する。

words.japaneseMeaning: ビクビクする、神経質な

examples.label: B
examples.text: My dogs really jump at the moment, so try not to make any loud noises.
examples.japaneseTranslation: うちの犬、今すごくビクビクしてる。大きな音出さないで。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Why are you so jump today? Too much coffee?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今日なんでそんなビクビクしてるの？コーヒー飲みすぎ？

examples.label: D
examples.text: An easier anxious.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 神経質な；びくびくしている

words.nativeDefinition: Nervous and easily startled.
words.etymology: jumpy（飛び跳ねる）＝すぐ驚く様子。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/145A get sth under one's belt.wav
words.japaneseMeaning: （経験などを）積む、身につける

examples.label: B
examples.text: Now that I've got five years of experience as a breeze down in my belt, I can finally open my own cafe.
examples.japaneseTranslation: バリスタとして5年の経験を積んだから、ついに自分のカフェを開ける。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This game is where the shot. I've got two wins under my belt already. I can show you the ropes of how to play if you want.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このゲーム熱い。もう2勝積んでる。やり方教えようか？

examples.label: D
examples.text: Gain or accumulate something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かを）経験として積む

words.nativeDefinition: To gain experience or achieve something.
words.etymology: under one's belt（ベルトの下）＝身につけた・達成した比喩。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Thank you for your feedback, it will feed into the next revision.
examples.japaneseTranslation: フィードバックありがとう。次の改訂に反映される。

examples.label: C
examples.text: What we discussed today will definitely feed into our future plans.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今日話したことは、今後の計画に確実に反映される。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Can I be to something?
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かに）影響を与える；つながる

words.nativeDefinition: To contribute to or influence something.
words.etymology: feed into（〜に供給する）＝次の段階に反映される。

words.japaneseMeaning: 雰囲気を壊す人・もの

examples.label: B
examples.text: The remix of DJ Play Last Night was such a buzzkill, who plays anguasongs at a club.
examples.japaneseTranslation: クラブでアンサンブル曲流すなんて、雰囲気壊しすぎ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I've decided not to invite Sam the dinner because he's always a buzzkill.
examples.japaneseTranslation: サムはいつも雰囲気壊すから、ディナーに招待しないことにした。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Someone or something that spoils an otherwise enjoyable event.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 興を削ぐ人・物

words.nativeDefinition: Someone or something that spoils the mood.
words.etymology: buzz（盛り上がり）＋ kill（殺す）＝盛り上がりを台無しにする。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/148A put one's mind to sth.wav
words.japaneseMeaning: （〜に）本気で取り組む、集中する

examples.label: B
examples.text: You can bang on me as an ASMR or put my mind to it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 本気で取り組めば、ASMR配信者になれる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You can achieve anything if you believe in yourself and put your mind to it. Where there's a will, there's a way.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 自分を信じて本気で取り組めば、何でも達成できる。意志あれば道は開ける。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Direct a lot of ones effort to something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かに）全力を注ぐ

words.nativeDefinition: To focus one's effort and determination on something.
words.etymology: put one's mind to（心を向ける）＝本気で集中する。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Sorry if it's the bill for this role because she has retail experience.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 小売経験があるから、この役に合うと思う。

examples.label: C
examples.text: We are looking for passionate volunteers like you to join our team. Do you know anyone who fits the bill?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 情熱的なボランティアを探してる。条件に合う人知らない？

examples.label: D
examples.text: Match the requirements.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 条件に合う；要求を満たす

words.nativeDefinition: To match the requirements or needs.
words.etymology: fit the bill（請求書に合う）＝要件を満たす。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/150A too good to be true.wav
words.japaneseMeaning: 良すぎて信じられない

examples.label: B
examples.text: I smell a rat, I just want to trip to Hawaii in a lottery that I don't remember entering. It sounds too good to be true.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 怪しい。覚えてない宝くじでハワイ旅行当選。良すぎて信じられない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I know it sounds too good to be true, but you can make it killing in the next few days if you follow my instructions.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 良すぎて信じられないって分かってるけど、指示通りにすれば数日で稼げる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: So great as to be difficult to believe.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 良すぎて信じられない

words.nativeDefinition: So good that it seems impossible or suspicious.
words.etymology: too good to be true（本当であるには良すぎる）＝疑わしいほど良い。

words.japaneseMeaning: 第一世界の問題、贅沢な悩み

examples.label: B
examples.text: It's a bit of a first-world problem, but it annoys me so much when there's too much cash in my wallet.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 贅沢な悩みだけど、財布に現金が多すぎるとすごくイライラする。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I've switched off notifications for everything and now I have FOMO. Definitely a first world problem.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 通知全部オフにしたらFOMOになった。完全に第一世界の問題。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Chirvio problem.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 先進国の些細な問題

words.nativeDefinition: A trivial problem that only affects privileged people.
words.etymology: first world（先進国）＋ problem（問題）＝裕福な人だけの些細な悩み。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/152A get sth out of sth.wav

examples.label: B
examples.text: I want to get the most out of my college life so I'm going to participate in as many campus activities as I can.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 大学生活を最大限楽しみたいから、キャンパス活動にできるだけ参加する。

examples.label: C
examples.text: To get something out of this game, we're going to have to go all out.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このゲームから何か得るには、全力を尽くさないと。

examples.label: D

words.nativeDefinition: To obtain benefit or value from something.

examples.label: B
examples.text: I blew off my doctor's appointment today just because I didn't feel like it. I felt terribly guilty afterwards so I will never do it again.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今日医者の予約サボった。行く気がしなかった。後ですごく罪悪感。二度としない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I hit you up when I was near your office, but you blew me off. I know you're busy, so no hard feelings.

examples.label: D

words.etymology: blow off（吹き飛ばす）＝無視する・ドタキャンする。

examples.label: B

examples.label: B

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜について）被害妄想的な、過度に心配性な

examples.label: B
examples.text: Conference call system says that I'm on mute, but I'm still a bit paranoid that people can hear me.

examples.label: C

words.japaneseMeaning: （人と）すぐ仲良くなる、相性がいい

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm pretty stoked that I clicked with my roommate straight away.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ルームメイトとすぐ仲良くなれて、すごく嬉しい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I find it really hard to click with people that are just booksmart. They're just not very pragmatic.

examples.label: B
examples.text: I know exactly how this is going to play out. I'm going to play this doppy character and get pigeon hold forever.

examples.label: C
examples.text: Did you start your own business? Congratulations! I'm excited to see how things play out.

examples.label: D
examples.text: Traveler

words.japaneseMeaning: 話は変わるけど、脱線するけど

examples.label: B
examples.text: This is a little off topic, but I love the green tea latte at Starbucks.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 話は変わるけど、スタバの抹茶ラテ大好き。

examples.label: C
examples.text: He texted me, Mr. Donut don't answer yum, so I said tell me about it. Oh, off topic, but I just noticed Donut has two spellings.

examples.label: D
examples.text: This is a relevant to the subject, but...

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜を）一気見する、べったり見る

examples.label: B
examples.text: Instead of binging Netflix like I normally do, I'm going to change it up and go for a height this weekend.
examples.japaneseTranslation: いつものNetflix一気見じゃなくて、今週末はハイキングに変える。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I just managed to get my hands on this new series so I'm going to binge all of it tonight.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 新シリーズ手に入れたから、今夜全部一気見する。

examples.label: D

examples.label: B
examples.text: She's upset with me right now because I pulled her leg in front of all her friends. I'm going to apologize to smooth things over.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 友達の前でからかったから、今怒られてる。仲直りして誤解解く。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Are you pulling my leg? Surely snakes living in this park are an urban legend.
examples.japaneseTranslation: からかってるの？この公園に蛇がいるなんて都市伝説でしょ。

examples.label: D

words.japaneseMeaning: 舞台裏で、表向きは見えないところで

examples.label: B
examples.text: There are lots of checks and balances behind the scenes that prevent staff from stealing money.

examples.label: C
examples.text: You shouldn't forget that there are many people working behind the scenes to keep things moving.

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜の）ために生きる、〜が生きがい

examples.label: B
examples.text: Does Johnny really live for work? I don't buy it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ジョニー本当に仕事のために生きてるの？信じられない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I live for travel so the fact that Nancy Hazard is a deal breaker for me.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 旅行が生きがいだから、ナンシーが旅行嫌いなのは致命的。

words.japaneseMeaning: 本音を言うと、正直に言うと

examples.text: Real talk, I was a bit iffy about the last video, but this video is so fire hands down.

examples.label: C

words.headwordAudio: vibe/165A use it or lose it.wav

examples.label: B
examples.text: Learning a foreign language is like building a muscle. Use it or lose it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 外国語学習は筋肉と同じ。使わなければ衰える。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My golf swing used to be great, but I'm a bit rusty because I haven't played for months now. I guess you use it or lose it.

words.japaneseMeaning: （恋人として）手放したくない人、逸材

examples.label: B
examples.text: Your boyfriend is all the chores and also earns grip with your salary, he's definitely a keeper.

examples.label: B
examples.text: Catching up with Julian took me back to when I used to work in the accounting department.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ジュリアンと再会して、経理部で働いてた頃を思い出した。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/168A what are sb's thoughts (on sth).wav

examples.label: B
examples.text: What are you thought some buying a pink car?
examples.japaneseTranslation: ピンクの車買うの、どう思う？

examples.label: C

words.japaneseMeaning: チャンスを逃す、乗り遅れる

examples.label: B
examples.text: The stock I was looking at last week went from $10 to $100 in just a couple of days. I really think I missed the boat.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 先週見てた株が数日で10ドルから100ドルに。完全にチャンス逃した。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I really want to buy an intended switch, but they're sold out across the board. I miss the boat on this one.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Switch買いたいけど、全国売り切れ。今回は乗り遅れた。

examples.label: C

examples.label: B
examples.text: Before we eat, I'm going to say a few words about why we're all here today.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 食事の前に、今日みんながここに集まった理由について一言述べる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Thanks for inviting me to say a few words at this wonderful event. Could us to you and your team.

examples.label: B
examples.text: Can you let me hear ear for a few minutes? There's something going to get off my chest and you're the only person I can trust.

examples.label: C

words.japaneseMeaning: コスパがいい、費用対効果が高い

examples.label: B
examples.text: Check out these webinars to one of the most cost-effective ways to get up to speed with AI.
examples.japaneseTranslation: AIを素早くキャッチアップする最もコスパのいい方法の一つがウェビナー。

examples.label: C
examples.text: hindsight is 2020, but I should have spent more time studying during college. It's more cost-effective than clubbing every night.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 後から思うと、大学時代もっと勉強すべきだった。毎晩クラブよりコスパいい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Eternal elf Gawn
examples.japaneseTranslation: 費用対効果が高い

words.etymology: cost（費用）＋ effective（効果的）＝費用対効果が高い。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I just jumped on the zoom band Wangen. To be honest, I have no idea how it's different from Skype.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Zoomの流行に乗った。正直Skypeと何が違うか分からない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You jumped on the podcast bandwagon 2, great-minds think alike. I usually listen to it while doing my numbing work.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ポッドキャストの流行にも乗った。英雄所见略。単純作業中に聞いてる。

words.japaneseMeaning: 習うより慣れろ、練習が完璧を作る

examples.text: You can't cut corners if you want to succeed at the top level. Practice makes perfect. That's the truth.
examples.japaneseTranslation: トップレベルで成功したいなら手を抜いちゃダメ。練習が完璧を作る。それが真実。

examples.label: C

examples.label: B
examples.text: I want to join you guys for golf this weekend, but I need to run it by my better half first.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 週末ゴルフ参加したいけど、まずパートナーに相談しないと。

examples.label: C
examples.text: That sounds like a great idea, but I think you're barking up the wrong tree. You should run it by George.

examples.label: B
examples.text: I can't believe I bumped into your cousin on a bus and Athens. It's a small world, isn't it?
examples.japaneseTranslation: アテネのバスで君のいとこに偶然会うなんて。世間狭いね。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Are you also from Alaska? Wow, it's a small world!
examples.japaneseTranslation: 君もアラスカ出身？世間狭い！

examples.label: D
examples.text: People in different places are connected.

examples.label: B
examples.text: The company is back on track now. The new CEO did pretty much nothing to turn it around, but he took all the credit for it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 会社は軌道に乗った。新CEOはほとんど何もしてないのに、全部の手柄を横取りした。

examples.label: C

examples.label: D

words.japaneseMeaning: （いつもと）変える、気分転換する

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm going to work in London for you just to switch it up. I'm really hyped for it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 気分転換にロンドンで働くことにした。すごくワクワクしてる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Let's push it up and have lunch outside today.

words.japaneseMeaning: コンフォートゾーン、安心できる領域

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm in my comfort zone at work right now, but I've hit a wall in terms of my learning and development.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 仕事では今コンフォートゾーンにいるけど、学習・成長面では行き詰まってる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You need to get out of your comfort zone if you want to broaden your horizons.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 視野を広げたいなら、コンフォートゾーンから出ないと。

examples.label: D

words.japaneseMeaning: 一歩下がる、距離を置いて考える

examples.text: Sometimes it's good to step back and see things through different lens.

examples.label: C

examples.label: D

words.japaneseMeaning: （インターホンで）入れる、解錠する

examples.label: B
examples.text: Let me know when you get here all buzzy in.

examples.label: C
examples.text: Our landlord doesn't want residents to buzz in Uber eats drivers so we have to collect our food from the lobby.
