words.japaneseMeaning: 法外に高い、財布を破る

examples.label: B
examples.text: $800 for a plain white t-shirt, I'll break the bank.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 白いTシャツ1枚800ドル？財布が破れる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: A great watch doesn't necessarily have to break the bank. There are a lot of good ones that are $500.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 良い時計は必ずしも法外に高くなくていい。500ドルで十分良いのたくさんある。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Cost too much.
examples.japaneseTranslation: とても高い；財布を痛める

words.nativeDefinition: To cost too much; to be very expensive.
words.etymology: break the bank（銀行を破る）＝非常に高価な比喩。

words.japaneseMeaning: （ソフトが）バグだらけ、動作が不安定

examples.label: B
examples.text: That new operating system is a bit buggy, so don't install it yet.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 新OSちょっとバグ多いから、まだインストールしない方がいい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Is the app buggy on your phone too or is it just mine?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 君のスマホでもアプリバグる？それとも僕だけ？

examples.label: D
examples.text: go lane
examples.japaneseTranslation: バグだらけの；不具合のある

words.nativeDefinition: Having software errors or glitches.
words.etymology: bug（虫＝バグ）＋ -y で、不具合がある状態。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/185A to put it another way.wav
words.japaneseMeaning: 別の言い方をすると、つまり

examples.label: B
examples.text: Who is coming to the office this Friday or to put another way who wants to grab a drink with me after work?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 金曜誰がオフィス来る？別の言い方をすると、仕事後に飲みに行きたい人は？

examples.label: C
examples.text: Your confidence comes only from your experience. To put it another way, unless you take action, you'll never be confident.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 自信は経験から来る。別の言い方をすると、行動しなければ自信は生まれない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: In other words.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 言い換えると；別の言い方をすると

words.nativeDefinition: In other words; to rephrase.
words.etymology: put it another way（別の方法で言う）＝言い換える。

words.japaneseMeaning: （散歩などで）気を紛らわす、乗り越える

examples.label: B
examples.text: You have to write your essay from scratch again because you forgot to save it? Walk it off, I'm sure it'll be quicker this time around.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 保存忘れてエッセイ最初から書き直し？散歩して気を紛らわせて。今度は早いよ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm so stuffed. I'm going to walk it off. I'll be back in 30 minutes.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 食べ過ぎた。散歩して消化する。30分で戻る。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Endure. Walk in order to feel better.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 歩いて気分を紛らわす；耐えて歩く

words.nativeDefinition: To recover from disappointment by walking; to endure.
words.etymology: walk it off（歩いて取り除く）＝歩いて気分を立て直す。

words.headword: there's only so much sb can do
words.headwordAudio: vibe/187A there's only so much sb can do.wav
words.phonetic: ðɛrz ˈoʊnli soʊ məʧ ˈsəmˌbɑdi kən du
words.japaneseMeaning: （人に）できることには限界がある

examples.label: B
examples.text: You may think that shooting investors always have the minus touch, but there's only so much they can do in this economic crisis.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 投資家は常にミダステッチだと思われがちだけど、この経済危機でできることには限界がある。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'll try my best to get you a new boyfriend, but there's only so much I can do.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 新しい彼氏探すの手伝うけど、できることには限界がある。

examples.label: D
examples.text: There's a limit to how much action somebody can take.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人に）できることには限界がある

words.nativeDefinition: There is a limit to what someone can do.
words.etymology: only so much（それだけ）＝限られた範囲しかできない。

words.japaneseMeaning: （人を）ゴーストする、連絡を突然絶つ

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm thinking about why I got ghosted probably because of my cute and squishy tummy.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ゴーストされた理由考えてる。可愛いお腹のせいかも。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Socky goes to me halfway through such a downer.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 途中でゴーストされるの、本当にがっかり。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Stop contacting somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人と）連絡を絶つ

words.nativeDefinition: To suddenly stop all communication with someone.
words.etymology: ghost（幽霊）＝突然姿を消すように連絡を断つ。

examples.label: B
examples.text: This is just my two cents worth, but if we want to buy this house, we need to pull the trigger today.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 個人的な意見だけど、この家買うなら今日決断しないと。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Just my two cents, but I think you should speak up a bit more during meetings.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 個人的な意見だけど、会議でもっと発言した方がいいと思う。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Somebody's a pinion.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人の）意見；見解

words.nativeDefinition: One's modest opinion.
words.etymology: two cents（2セント＝些細な意見）＝控えめな意見。

examples.label: B
examples.text: He says this is a picture perfect tablet, but I think there's so much room for improvement. These kinds of tablets are a dime it doesn't.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 完璧なタブレットだって言うけど、改善の余地はたくさんある。

examples.label: C
examples.text: There's a lot of room for improvement in my defensive skills, so I'm going to knuckle down and practice more.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 守備スキルには改善の余地がたくさんある。もっと練習に専念する。

examples.label: D
examples.text: scope for enhancement.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 改善の余地

words.nativeDefinition: Scope for improvement or enhancement.
words.etymology: room（余地）＋ for improvement（改善のため）＝改善の余地。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I had a gut feeling that the video you sent me was going to freak me out. Turns out I was right and now I can't unsee it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 送ってくれた動画、ビビると思った。当たり。もう忘れられない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Someone said a power outlet looks like a pig's nose and I can't unsee it anymore.
examples.japaneseTranslation: コンセントが豚の鼻に見えるって言われて、もう忘れられない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Can't forget the side of something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かを）見たことを忘れられない

words.nativeDefinition: Unable to forget something one has seen.
words.etymology: unsee（見なかったことにする）の否定＝見たものを消せない。

words.headword: do not sit right/well with sb
words.headwordAudio: vibe/192A do not sit right_well with sb.wav
words.phonetic: du nɑt sɪt right/well wɪθ ˈsəmˌbɑdi
words.japaneseMeaning: （〜が）しっくりこない、納得できない

examples.label: B
examples.text: According to Smash Bros. Mario is 26 years old, that doesn't sit right with me.
examples.japaneseTranslation: スマブラでマリオ26歳って、しっくりこない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: The fact that I'm not going to live forever doesn't sit well with me. I want to be a mortal.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 永遠に生きられないって、納得できない。不死になりたい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: It's difficult to accept somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人に）しっくりこない；受け入れがたい

words.nativeDefinition: To feel that something is wrong or unacceptable.
words.etymology: not sit right（正しく座らない）＝違和感がある。

words.japaneseMeaning: プレッシャーがかかっている

examples.label: B
examples.text: We really need to be firing on all cylinders from next week if we want to launch this new product on time. The pressure is on.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 来週から全速力でいかないと、新製品を期日通りに出せない。プレッシャーがかかってる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You need to be on top of everything in this chapter by tomorrow if you want to pass the exam. The pressure's on.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 明日までにこの章全部マスターしないと試験に落ちる。プレッシャーがかかってる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: The stakes are high.
examples.japaneseTranslation: プレッシャーがかかっている

words.nativeDefinition: There is strong pressure to succeed or perform.
words.etymology: pressure is on（圧力がかかっている）＝高いプレッシャー状態。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm normally kind of a control-free, but this time around I've decided to take a back seat.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 普段はコントロール好きだけど、今回は脇に回ることにした。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You should take a back seat when the boss is in the room. He's grumpy today.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 上司がいる時は脇に回った方がいい。今日機嫌悪い。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Take a less important position.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 脇に回る；重要度を下げる

words.nativeDefinition: To take a less prominent or active role.
words.etymology: take a back seat（後部座席に座る）＝主役から退く。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I know you really want to buy the house, but you shouldn't get ahead of yourself. You should see it first hand before making a decision.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 家、本当に買いたいのは分かるけど、先走らないで。決める前に直接見るべき。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I know firsthand that there are a lot of executives who only care about themselves and makes my blood boil just thinking about it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 直接知ってる。自分勝手な役員がたくさんいる。考えるだけで腹が立つ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: personally
examples.japaneseTranslation: 直接；自分自身で

words.nativeDefinition: Directly; through personal experience.
words.etymology: firsthand（第一の手）＝直接体験・目撃。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/196A learn sth the hard way.wav

examples.label: B
examples.text: The flu is not something to take lightly. I learned this the hard way last month.
examples.japaneseTranslation: インフルエンザは軽く見ちゃダメ。先月痛いほど学んだ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: When the acid bubble bursts, I learned a hard read that all good things must come to an end.
examples.japaneseTranslation: バブル崩壊で、良いことにも終わりがあると痛いほど学んだ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Learn something through a negative experience.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 苦い経験を通じて学ぶ

words.nativeDefinition: To learn something through a painful or difficult experience.
words.etymology: the hard way（難しい方法）＝苦い経験を通じて学ぶ。

words.japaneseMeaning: （自分の）分野に専念する、余計な口出しをしない

examples.label: B
examples.text: The best way to not get fired in this environment is to stay in your lane and play your cards right.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この環境でクビにならない最善の方法は、自分の分野に専念して正しい判断をすること。

examples.label: C
examples.text: What I do in my spare time is none of your business, staying your lane.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 私の余暇の過ごし方はあなたの知ったことじゃない。余計な口出ししないで。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Mind once on business.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 自分の分野にとどまる；余計な口出しをしない

words.nativeDefinition: To stay focused on one's own responsibilities.
words.etymology: stay in one's lane（自分の車線に留まる）＝自分の役割に専念。

words.headword: can't put one's finger on sth
words.headwordAudio: vibe/198A can't put one's finger on sth.wav
words.phonetic: kænt pʊt wənz ˈfɪŋgər ɔn ˈsəmθɪŋ
words.japaneseMeaning: （原因が）はっきり分からない、特定できない

examples.label: B
examples.text: There's something wrong with this drawing, but I can't quite put my finger on it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この絵、何かおかしいけど、はっきり言えない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You look a bit different today. I can't put my finger on why.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今日なんか違うね。なぜかはっきり分からない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Can't work out the exact reason for something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かの）正確な理由が掴めない

words.nativeDefinition: Unable to identify exactly what is wrong.
words.etymology: put one's finger on（指を置く）＝正確に特定する。

examples.label: B
examples.text: You're stumped by what he's saying too, dido.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 君も彼の言うことが分からない？同感。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You hate rich people who talk down to other people? Dido.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 金持ちが見下すの嫌？同感。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Agreed.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 同感；その通り

words.nativeDefinition: Me too; I agree.
words.etymology: ditto（同上）＝イタリア語から、同じく・同感。

words.japaneseMeaning: （マンネリに）はまっている、行き詰まっている

examples.label: B
examples.text: I feel like I'm stuck in a bit of a workrat. I need a career change soon.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 仕事のマンネリにはまってる気がする。近いうちに転職したい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm in a deep rut, I watch the same stuff over and over again on Amazon Prime.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 深いマンネリ。Amazon Primeで同じものばかり見てる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: be in a situation that never changes.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （行き詰まった）状況から抜け出せない

words.nativeDefinition: To be stuck in a boring, unchanging routine.
words.etymology: in a rut（轍の中）＝同じ道を行き来する＝マンネリ。

words.japaneseMeaning: （ルール・条件を）ころころ変える

examples.label: B
examples.text: I thought Jimmy and I were on the same page but he started moving the goal posts.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ジミーと同じページにいると思ってたのに、条件をころころ変え始めた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: It's annoying how the client keeps moving the goal post, but when all said and done, they have the final say.
examples.japaneseTranslation: クライアントがゴールを動かし続けるのはうんざり。最終的には彼らの言う通りだけど。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Alter previously agreed upon terms of rules.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ルールや条件を後から変える

words.nativeDefinition: To change the rules or expectations after agreement.
words.etymology: move the goalposts（ゴールポストを動かす）＝合意後に基準を変える比喩。

words.headword: hit the nail on the head
words.headwordAudio: vibe/202A hit the nail on the head.wav
words.phonetic: hɪt ðə neɪl ɔn ðə hɛd
words.japaneseMeaning: 的を射ている、まさにその通り

examples.label: B
examples.text: He said it was just an educated guess, but he's hit the nail on the head. This disease really did stick around for two years.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 推測だと言ってたけど、的を射てた。この病気は本当に2年続いた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Sorry to put you on the spot, but can you share with everybody what you told me earlier? I think you really hit the nail on the head.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 急に聞くのは悪いけど、さっき話してくれたことをみんなに共有して？的を射てると思う。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Be exactly right.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 的を射ている；まさにその通り

words.nativeDefinition: To be exactly right.
words.etymology: hit the nail on the head（釘の頭を正確に打つ）＝的確に当てる。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/203A the grass is always greener.wav
words.japaneseMeaning: 隣の芝生は青い、他の方が良さそうに見える

examples.label: B
examples.text: I know it seems like being top dog is great, but the grass is always greener on the other side.
examples.japaneseTranslation: トップが素晴らしいように見えても、隣の芝生は青い。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My workplace is a bit boring now, but I don't want to be too quick to jump ship. The grass is always greener.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 職場はちょっと退屈だけど、すぐ転職したくない。隣の芝生は青い。

examples.label: D
examples.text: The alternative always seems better.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 隣の芝生は青い

words.nativeDefinition: Other situations always seem better than one's own.
words.etymology: the grass is always greener（草はいつもより青い）ということわざ。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I don't actually want to be a fly in the wall. It's just a figure of speech. I just wish I could have been at that meeting.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 本当に壁のシミになりたいわけじゃない。比喩表現。あの会議にいたかった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: By bells and whistles, I meant unnecessary features, not literally, is just a figure of speech.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ベルズ・アンド・ホイッスルは不要な機能のこと。文字通りじゃない。比喩表現。

examples.label: D
examples.text: None that are afraid.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 比喩表現

words.nativeDefinition: An expression not meant to be taken literally.
words.etymology: figure of speech（話の形）＝文字通りではない表現。

words.japaneseMeaning: （自分の）天職で、居心地がいい

examples.label: B
examples.text: Outs is really in his element when he's taking online English lessons. It's pretty obvious that he gets a kick out of it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: アウスはオンライン英語レッスンで本領発揮してる。楽しんでるのが明らか。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Even though Matt can't carry a tunid all, he somehow in his element during carryoky.
examples.japaneseTranslation: マットは歌下手なのに、カラオケではなぜか居心地がいい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: In an environment where one is comfortable.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 自分の居場所にいる；得意分野で

words.nativeDefinition: In a situation where one feels comfortable and confident.
words.etymology: in one's element（自分の元素の中）＝最も得意な環境。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Everyone, let's stick together. We're almost there.
examples.japaneseTranslation: みんな、一緒に行こう。もうすぐ着く。

examples.label: C
examples.text: We need to really stick together to overcome this problem.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この問題を乗り越えるには、本当に団結する必要がある。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Support each other.
examples.japaneseTranslation: お互いを支え合う

words.nativeDefinition: To remain united and support each other.
words.etymology: stick together（一緒にくっつく）＝団結する。

examples.label: B
examples.text: She takes days to reply to my messages. I think it's a bit of a red flag.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 返信に何日もかかる。ちょっと危険信号だと思う。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This restaurant is empty. It's a bit of a red flag, isn't it?
examples.japaneseTranslation: このレストラン空いてる。危険信号じゃない？

examples.label: D
examples.text: Warning!
examples.japaneseTranslation: 危険信号

words.nativeDefinition: A warning sign of potential problems.
words.etymology: red flag（赤い旗）＝危険の警告信号。

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜を）過剰に利用する、搾り取る

examples.label: B
examples.text: He's really milking his injury, isn't he? I'm pretty sure he just twisted his ankle, but now he's saying he can't even type.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 怪我を過剰に利用してるよね？足首捻っただけなのに、タイピングもできないって。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I think so many politicians are milking their positions for personal gain. We should definitely get to the bottom of it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 政治家が地位を私的に利用してる。調査すべき。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Take advantage of something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かを）搾り取る；利用し尽くす

words.nativeDefinition: To exploit something excessively for personal gain.
words.etymology: milk（搾る）＝最大限利用・搾り取る比喩。

words.headword: go back to the drawing board
words.headwordAudio: vibe/209A go back to the drawing board.wav
words.phonetic: goʊ bæk tɪ ðə drɔɪŋ bɔrd
words.japaneseMeaning: 最初からやり直す、白紙に戻る

examples.label: B
examples.text: I can't believe we have to go back to the drawing board. We were making such good progress.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 最初からやり直しなんて信じられない。順調に進んでたのに。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I don't think we have to go back to the drawing board. You'll just need to tweak a few things here and there to get the design right.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 最初からやり直す必要はない。デザインを少し調整すればいい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Return to the start of the process.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 最初からやり直す

words.nativeDefinition: To start over from the beginning.
words.etymology: go back to the drawing board（設計台に戻る）＝最初から設計し直す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I was going to write a long-winded comment on this YouTube video, but then I realized it was nothing but a waste of time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: YouTubeに長々としたコメント書こうとしてたけど、時間の無駄だと気づいた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm not a big fan of as long when it explanations. I'm like, for God's sake, just cut to the chase.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 長々とした説明は好きじゃない。早く本題に入って。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Extremely lengthy.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 非常に長い；冗長な

words.nativeDefinition: Using too many words; excessively lengthy.
words.etymology: long（長い）＋ winded（息切れする）＝長くて退屈な。

examples.label: B
examples.text: If you think your way is better, stick to your guns and spend some time explaining why.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 自分の方法が良いと思うなら、信念を貫いて、なぜか説明に時間を使って。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I admire how Joe sticks to his guns even when the whole world is calling him crazy.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 世界中が狂ってると言われても、ジョーは信念を貫く。尊敬する。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Refuse to change one's opinion or belief.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 自分の信念を曲げない

words.nativeDefinition: To maintain one's position despite opposition.
words.etymology: stick to one's guns（銃に固執する）＝意見を曲げない。

words.japaneseMeaning: パーティー嫌い、雰囲気を壊す人

examples.label: B
examples.text: Have you always been a party pooper? Or did you just wake up on the wrong side of the bed today?
examples.japaneseTranslation: ずっとパーティー嫌い？それとも今日だけ機嫌悪い？

examples.label: C
examples.text: You can such a party pooper. She brings your boy boyfriend to everything.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 本当に雰囲気壊す。どこに行くにも彼氏連れてくる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Person who spoils a cheerful mood.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 興を削ぐ人

words.nativeDefinition: A person who spoils others' fun.
words.etymology: party（パーティー）＋ pooper（台無しにする人）。

words.japaneseMeaning: 見た目が良い、目に優しい

examples.label: B
examples.text: I thought being easy on the eyes was a surefire way to become popular on Instagram, but she seems to be an exception.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 見た目が良ければInstagramで人気になると思ったけど、彼女は例外みたい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: textbook free kick by messy so easy on the eyes.
examples.japaneseTranslation: メッシのフリーキック、見てて気持ちいい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: aesthetically pleasing.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 見た目が美しい

words.nativeDefinition: Pleasant to look at; attractive.
words.etymology: easy on the eyes（目に優しい）＝見ていて心地よい。

words.headword: don't have to ask me twice
words.headwordAudio: vibe/214A don't have to ask me twice.wav
words.phonetic: doʊnt hæv tɪ æsk mi twaɪs

examples.label: B
examples.text: Do I want to go home early today? Don't have to ask me twice.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今日早く帰りたい？二言は要らない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Another shot of Kyla, don't add that to me twice. Let's get wasted.
examples.japaneseTranslation: カイラ、もう一杯？二言は要らない。酔い潰れよう。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Absolutely.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 二言はいらない；喜んで

words.nativeDefinition: Absolutely; without hesitation.
words.etymology: don't have to ask twice（二度聞く必要がない）＝即座に同意。

words.japaneseMeaning: ピカピカ、徹底的にきれい

examples.label: B
examples.text: It's amazing how everything in your room is ship shape and squeaky clean. How do you do that? Your dogs are always running riot.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 部屋がピカピカに整頓されてるのすごい。犬が暴れ回ってるのに、どうやってるの？

examples.label: C
examples.text: I've just spent two hours making the kitchen squeaky clean, so try not to mess it up too quickly.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 2時間かけてキッチンをピカピカにした。すぐ汚さないで。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Completely clean.
examples.japaneseTranslation: きれいさっぱり；汚れひとつない

words.nativeDefinition: Completely clean.
words.etymology: squeaky（きしむ）＋ clean（きれい）＝きしむほどきれい。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/216A move on with one's life.wav
words.japaneseMeaning: （人生を）前に進める、立ち直る

examples.label: B
examples.text: Emma broke my heart when she left me, but sometimes that's the way the cookie crumbles. I guess I just have to move on with my life.
examples.japaneseTranslation: エマに振られて心が痛い。でもそういうもの。人生を前に進めるしかない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: At least there's one takeaway from this breakup. I just need to keep moving on with my life no matter what happens.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この別れから学べることは一つ。何が起きても人生を前に進め続ける。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Continue living one's life in the usual way.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 普段通りの生活を続ける

words.nativeDefinition: To continue living after a setback.
words.etymology: move on with one's life（人生を前に進める）＝ setback 後も生き続ける。

words.japaneseMeaning: （順調に）進む、問題なく推移する

examples.label: B
examples.text: Everything is ticking along nicely here. Business is usual.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ここはすべて順調に進んでる。平常運転。

examples.label: C
examples.text: We managed to keep the business taking long during the financial crisis.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 金融危機中も事業を順調に維持できた。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Progress without any serious problems.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 大きな問題なく順調に進む

words.nativeDefinition: To proceed steadily without problems.
words.etymology: tick along（カチカチと進む）＝時計のように順調に進む。

words.japaneseMeaning: 音沙汰なし、連絡がない状態

examples.label: B
examples.text: I expected Carly to give me a buzz this week, but it's just been radio silence for a while now.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今週カーリーから連絡あると思ったけど、しばらく音沙汰なし。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This radio silence from my girlfriend is killing me. I need to do something to clear the air.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼女からの音沙汰なしが辛い。何かして関係を修復しないと。

examples.label: D
examples.text: A period of time when one hears nothing.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 連絡が途絶えた期間

words.nativeDefinition: A period of no communication.
words.etymology: radio silence（ラジオの沈黙）＝通信途絶の軍事用語から。

words.japaneseMeaning: （人を）正直にさせる、監視して不正を防ぐ

examples.label: B
examples.text: Having to submit receipts claim expenses is a bit fiddly, but it keeps employees honest.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 領収書提出は面倒だけど、社員を正直に保つ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: The only person that can keep chemonuses or mom.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 正直にさせられるのは母だけ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Ensure that somebody maintains integrity.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人が）正直でいるよう促す

words.nativeDefinition: To ensure someone maintains honesty or integrity.
words.etymology: keep honest（正直に保つ）＝不正を防ぐ仕組み。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/220A bring sth to the table.wav
words.japaneseMeaning: （〜に）価値をもたらす、貢献する

examples.label: B
examples.text: A lot of people don't like Ken because he has an in your face attitude, but I still keep him on the team because he brings a lot to the table.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ケンは押しが強いから嫌われがちだけど、チームに大きな価値をもたらすから残してる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: She was like, my boyfriend brings nothing to the table, he can't do chores or even make money. I kind of feel sorry for her boyfriend.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼氏は何も貢献しない、家事もできないし稼げもしない。彼氏が可哀想。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Can tribute something?
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かを）貢献する；持ち込む

words.nativeDefinition: To contribute something valuable.
words.etymology: bring to the table（テーブルに持ってくる）＝交渉に価値を提供。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I have no idea how property prices are going to move this year. I don't have a crystal ball.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今年の不動産価格がどう動くか分からない。水晶玉は持ってない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: There's no way to pick the winning horse. I need a crystal ball for that.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 勝ち馬を選ぶ方法はない。水晶玉が必要。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Ability to predict the future.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 未来を予測する力

words.nativeDefinition: The ability to predict the future.
words.etymology: crystal ball（水晶玉）＝占いで未来を見る道具の比喩。

examples.label: B
examples.text: How did you know I took the last cookie? I thought I covered my tracks really well.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 最後のクッキー取ったの、どうやって分かった？完璧に隠したつもりだった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: After going on YouTube at work, I always clear my browsing history to cover my tracks.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 仕事中YouTube見た後、必ず履歴消して痕跡を消す。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Consuel evidence of one's actions.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 証拠を隠す

words.nativeDefinition: To hide evidence of one's actions.
words.etymology: cover one's tracks（足跡を隠す）＝証拠を隠蔽する。

examples.label: B
examples.text: You want to bring in Ronald to prop up the team? Sounds like a plan.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ロナルドを呼んでチームを支える？良い案だね。

examples.label: C
examples.text: The government's been doing a good job of propping up the economy so far, but we're not out of the woods yet.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 政府は経済を支えてきたけど、まだ危機は去ってない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Give something support.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かを）支える；立て直す

words.nativeDefinition: To support or strengthen something.
words.etymology: prop up（支えで立てる）＝支援・支える。

words.japaneseMeaning: 噂をすれば影、言及した人が現れた

examples.label: B
examples.text: Lisa is such a hard task master, so I don't want to work with her anymore. Speak at the devil. Here she comes.
examples.japaneseTranslation: リサは厳しい上司だから一緒に働きたくない。…噂をすれば影、来た。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I haven't seen Martin for ages. Speak of the devil, he's just arrived.
examples.japaneseTranslation: マーティンに久しぶり。噂をすれば影、ちょうど到着した。

examples.label: D
examples.text: The person we were talking about has appeared unexpectedly.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 噂をすれば影

words.nativeDefinition: Said when someone you were talking about appears.
words.etymology: speak of the devil（悪魔の話をすると）＝噂をすれば影の英語版。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I can't work till retirement having to put up with a lot of garbage only to get paid peanuts just doesn't make sense.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 退職まで我慢して、ゴミみたいな扱いで雀の涙の給料なんて意味ない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Richard orders Uber eats every day, and he's loaded so that's penis for him.
examples.japaneseTranslation: リチャードは毎日Uber Eats頼む。金持ちだから雀の涙でも平気。

examples.label: D
examples.text: A very small amount of money.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ほんの少しの金

words.nativeDefinition: A very small amount of money.
words.etymology: peanuts（ピーナッツ）＝ごくわずかな金額の比喩。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/226A move in the right direction.wav
words.japaneseMeaning: 正しい方向に進んでいる

examples.label: B
examples.text: I haven't got my dream job yet, but I feel like I'm moving in the right direction. I'm looking into a few different options for my next move.
examples.japaneseTranslation: まだ理想の仕事は見つかってないけど、正しい方向に進んでる気がする。

examples.label: C
examples.text: There were a few issues in the software that were holding up the team's progress, but we're now moving in the right direction.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ソフトの問題でチームの進捗が止まってたけど、今は正しい方向に進んでる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Progress well.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 良い方向に進む

words.nativeDefinition: To make progress toward a goal.
words.etymology: move in the right direction（正しい方向に動く）＝目標に向かって進む。

words.japaneseMeaning: （人の）手が縛られている、何もできない

examples.label: B
examples.text: I want to bail out homeless people with my hands are tied. It's frustrating.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ホームレスの人を助けたいけど、手が縛られてる。 frustrating。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I know you want to refund, but my hands are tied. It's against the store's policy.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 返金したいのは分かるけど、手が縛られてる。店のポリシーだから。

examples.label: D
examples.text: But somebody can do is limited.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人には）できることが限られている

words.nativeDefinition: Unable to act because of restrictions.
words.etymology: hands are tied（手が縛られている）＝行動できない状態。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I hired him because he checked all the boxes for the role, but I had no idea he'd turn out to be a pain in the neck.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 条件をすべて満たしてたから雇った。面倒な人になるとは思わなかった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: It's really hard for me to find a girl that checks all the boxes I have really high standards.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 条件全部満たす女性を見つけるの難しい。基準高いから。

examples.label: D
examples.text: fulfill all the requirements.
examples.japaneseTranslation: すべての条件を満たす

words.nativeDefinition: To meet all required criteria.
words.etymology: check all the boxes（すべての箱にチェック）＝要件をすべて満たす。

examples.label: B
examples.text: She's unfalled or boyfriend to get back at him for unfollowing her. That's so childish.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼氏がアンフォローした仕返しに彼女がアンフォローした。子供っぽい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Remember, if you betray me, I'll get back at you.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 裏切ったら、必ず仕返しする。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Game Revenge over somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人に）仕返しする

words.nativeDefinition: To take revenge on someone.
words.etymology: get back at（〜に戻る）＝仕返しする。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I want a new phone for Christmas. Actually scratch that. I'd prefer a TV.
examples.japaneseTranslation: クリスマスに新しいスマホが欲しい。…いや、なし。TVの方がいい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Can I have a latte? No scratch set. I'll have a sweet tea.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ラテください。…いや、なし。スイートティーにする。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Ignore that.
examples.japaneseTranslation: それはなし；取り消し

words.nativeDefinition: To cancel or disregard what was just said.
words.etymology: scratch that（それを消す）＝直前の発言を取り消す。

words.japaneseMeaning: （不本意に）払い出す、出費する

examples.label: B
examples.text: Yesterday my boss dragged me to his go-to cafe and I had to cough up $40 out of pocket for mediocre curry.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昨日上司に行きつけのカフェ連れて行かれて、自腹で4000円払った。微妙なカレー。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I can't believe international students have to cough up more than 30K a year for tuition.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 留学生が年間30K以上の授業料を払わなきゃいけないなんて信じられない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: so Maybe
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かを）払い出す；出す

words.nativeDefinition: To pay unwillingly, especially a large amount.
words.etymology: cough up（咳をして吐き出す）＝不本意に金を出す。

words.japaneseMeaning: 今しかない、チャンスは今だけ

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm almost 30 so it's probably now or never if I want to work overseas.
examples.japaneseTranslation: もう30近い。海外で働くなら今しかないかも。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This beautiful necklace will never be on sale again, so if you have a burning desire to get it, it's now or never.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このネックレス二度とセールない。欲しいなら今しかない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: The Last Opportunity.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今しかない；最後のチャンス

words.nativeDefinition: The last opportunity; act immediately or never.
words.etymology: now or never（今か決してない）＝最後のチャンス。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/233A get on sb's bad side.wav
words.japaneseMeaning: （人の）機嫌を損ねる、嫌われる

examples.label: B
examples.text: Watch out for the manager. It's really easy to get on his bad side.
examples.japaneseTranslation: マネージャーに気をつけて。簡単に機嫌を損ねる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm always walking on egg shells around Alvin because I don't want to get on his bad side.
examples.japaneseTranslation: アルビンの前では常に気を使う。機嫌を損ねたくない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Become disliked by somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人の）機嫌を損ねる

words.nativeDefinition: To cause someone to dislike you.
words.etymology: get on one's bad side（悪い面に乗る）＝機嫌を損ねる。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Sorry for not getting back to you earlier. I wasn't brushing you off. I've just been slammed at work.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 返信遅くてごめん。無視したわけじゃない。仕事に追われてた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I was pretty shattered when she brushed me off in front of everyone.
examples.japaneseTranslation: みんなの前であしらわれて、本当にショックだった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Ignore something or somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何か・人を）無視する；あしらう

words.nativeDefinition: To dismiss or ignore someone or something.
words.etymology: brush off（ブラシで払う）＝軽くあしらう。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm really excited about this vocabulary book that's in the works. I think it'll be a game changer for English learners.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 制作中の語彙本、すごく楽しみ。英語学習者に革命を起こすと思う。

examples.label: C
examples.text: The next toy story is in the works I can't wait.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 次のトイ・ストーリー制作中。待ちきれない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Currently being produced.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 進行中；準備中

words.nativeDefinition: Currently being planned or developed.
words.etymology: in the works（仕掛かり中）＝進行中のプロジェクト。

examples.label: B
examples.text: It took me a while to start studying properly, but I'm finally in the groove.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 勉強に入るのに時間かかったけど、ようやく調子が乗った。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm going to have a late lunch. I'm in the groove at the moment so I don't want to hit pause yet.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 遅めのランチにする。今調子乗ってるから、一旦止めたくない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Performing well.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 好調に機能している；ノリに乗っている

words.nativeDefinition: Performing well; in a productive rhythm.
words.etymology: in the groove（溝の中）＝リズムに乗っている状態。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/237A have a bird's-eye view (of sth).wav
words.japaneseMeaning: （〜を）俯瞰で見る、全体を把握する

examples.label: B
examples.text: Having a bird's eye view of the game helps me identify up and coming players effectively.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 試合を俯瞰で見ると、有望な選手を効果的に見つけられる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Once I had a bird's eye view of life, I realized all the stars were aligned for my success, so I decided to bite the bullet and quit my job.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 人生を俯瞰したら、成功の条件が揃ってた。覚悟決めて退職した。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Have a broad view of something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かの）全体像を把握している

words.nativeDefinition: To have a broad overview of something.
words.etymology: bird's-eye view（鳥の目線）＝高所から全体を見る。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'll make sure that the project is in a good position before I head off on vacation.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 休暇前にプロジェクトを好調な状態にしておく。

examples.label: C
examples.text: We were in such a good position at half time, but we completely choked in the second half.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ハーフタイムでは好調だったのに、後半完全に崩れた。

examples.label: D
examples.text: in a positive state.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 有利な立場にある

words.nativeDefinition: In a favorable or advantageous state.
words.etymology: in a good position（良い位置に）＝有利な状態。

words.headword: put in a good word (for sb)
words.headwordAudio: vibe/239A put in a good word (for sb).wav
words.phonetic: pʊt ɪn ə gʊd wərd (fər ˈsəmˌbɑdi)
words.japaneseMeaning: （人の）口添えをする、推薦する

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'll put it in a good word for you, but at the end of the day you still have to do well in the interview.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 口添えするけど、結局面接で結果出さないと。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Could you put in a good word for me? He blocked me, but I'm pretty sure we just got our wires crossed.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 口添えしてくれる？ブロックされてる。すれ違いだと思う。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Speak positively about somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人の）ことを良く言う

words.nativeDefinition: To speak favorably about someone to another person.
words.etymology: put in a good word（良い言葉を入れる）＝推薦の口添え。

words.japaneseMeaning: バランスの取れた、多才な

examples.label: B
examples.text: We should recruit well-rounded players so that we can try out different tactics.
examples.japaneseTranslation: バランスの取れた選手をスカウトして、色々な戦術を試したい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This well-rounded fitness program covers all bases. It teaches you about all sorts of things such as food, mental health and exercise.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このフィットネスプログラムはバランスが取れてる。食事、メンタル、運動全部カバー。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Well balanced.
examples.japaneseTranslation: バランスの取れた；多才な

words.nativeDefinition: Having a broad range of skills or qualities.
words.etymology: well（良く）＋ rounded（丸い）＝多方面にバランスが取れた。

words.japaneseMeaning: 思い切ってやる、踏み切る

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm going to take the plunge and apply to graduate school in Australia. I'll figure out how to pay my tuition later.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 思い切ってオーストラリアの大学院に応募する。学費は後で考える。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I've just watched this video called Investing 101. It was so ego boosting. I decided to take the plunge and go all in on investment.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 投資101の動画見て、思い切って全額投資することにした。

examples.label: D
examples.text: commit oneself.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 思い切ってやる；決断する

words.nativeDefinition: To commit to a significant decision despite risk.
words.etymology: take the plunge（飛び込む）＝思い切って決断する。

words.japaneseMeaning: 心機一転する、改心する

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm going to turn over a new leaf this year. I said the same thing last year too, but I mean it this time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今年は心機一転する。去年も同じこと言ったけど、今度こそ本気。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Just seems to have turned a new leaf. Now we always go to the rules before asking me questions.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼、心機一転したみたい。質問する前にルールを確認するようになった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Change behavior in a positive way.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 心機一転する；行動を改める

words.nativeDefinition: To change one's behavior for the better.
words.etymology: turn a new leaf（新しい葉をめくる）＝新たなスタート。

words.japaneseMeaning: 手に負えなくなる、収拾がつかなくなる

examples.label: B
examples.text: Yesterday's game got really out of hand because of the one side of Raff's watching it was like pulling teeth.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昨日の試合、一方のファンが原因で手に負えなくなった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I think I might get a haircut tomorrow, it's getting a bit out of hand.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 明日散髪行こうかな。髪の毛、手に負えなくなってきた。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Become at a control.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 手に負えなくなる；制御不能になる

words.nativeDefinition: To become uncontrollable.
words.etymology: get out of hand（手から出る）＝制御不能になる。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I got into Pokemon as a kid since then, it's been close to my heart.

words.japaneseMeaning: 強硬に出る、手加減しない

examples.label: B

words.headwordAudio: vibe/246A add it to the pile.wav
words.japaneseMeaning: （やることリストに）追加する

examples.label: B
examples.text: I know you want this done right now, but I'm working on a few things at the moment. All at it to the pile for later this afternoon.

examples.label: C

examples.label: B

examples.japaneseTranslation: みんながトイレットペーパーを買い込んでる時、僕は株を買い込んでた。天才。

examples.label: C

examples.japaneseTranslation: パーティー始まる前に飲み物大量に買い込もう。

examples.label: D

examples.label: B
examples.text: It sounds like they're having a pretty heated discussion in there, so I wouldn't go in just yet.

examples.label: C

examples.label: D

words.japaneseMeaning: （点と点を）つなげる、全体像を把握する

examples.label: B
examples.text: Steve Jobs said, you can't connect the dots looking forward, you can only connect them looking backwards.

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'll come clean and tell my boss that I was playing Animal Crossing instead of working. He'll connect the dots sooner or later anyway.

words.japaneseMeaning: （努力して）出世する、這い上がる

examples.label: B
examples.text: It's unheard of for someone to work their way up to C.L. and 3 years.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 3年でCEOまで這い上がるなんて前例がない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You could be just as successful as this guy. He started the bottom work his way up.

examples.label: D
examples.text: Advanced is a result of one's own efforts.

examples.label: B

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜を）完了する、チェックを入れる

examples.text: I've been spinning wheels for the last few weeks, but I finally managed to get a few things checked off this morning.

examples.label: C

words.japaneseMeaning: （喜びで）飛び跳ねる、大喜びする

examples.label: B
examples.text: I finally reached 5,000 followers on Twitter. I'm jumping for joy.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Twitterフォロワー5000人達成！飛び跳ねて喜んでる。

examples.label: C

words.japaneseMeaning: 人生は続く、生活は続く

examples.label: B
examples.text: Today's not my day, my girlfriend broke up with me. I lost my wallet and I fractured my leg, but life goes on.

words.japaneseMeaning: （目立たず）潜伏する、大人しくする

examples.label: B
examples.text: I've heard that my boss is not happy with me, so I'm going to lie low for a while.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 上司が不機嫌らしいから、しばらく大人しくしてる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You don't remember? You were so wasted last night, you threw your boss's phone at the river. I'd like to love for a week or so if I were you.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 覚えてない？昨夜酔い潰れて上司のスマホ川に投げた。1週間潜伏する。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Some of the media don't tell the whole story and just gloss over the negative parts.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 一部のメディアは全体像を語らず、ネガティブな部分だけ軽く触れる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: We can't just gloss over the fact that you punch the hole in the wall. It's massive.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 壁に穴を開けた事実をごまかせない。デカいんだから。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Can see you'll something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かを）軽く流す；触れない

words.nativeDefinition: To treat something superficially; to avoid discussing it fully.
words.etymology: gloss over（光沢を塗る）＝表面だけ処理して本質を避ける。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Kim got pretty fired up because her boss brushed the cider comments.
examples.japaneseTranslation: キム、上司がサイドのコメントを軽視して激怒した。

examples.label: C

words.headword: as good as it's going to get

examples.label: B

words.japaneseMeaning: 最後に笑う、最終的に勝つ

examples.label: B
examples.text: He lost all his money in the stock market three years ago, but now he's a billionaire running 15 companies. He got the last laugh.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 3年前に株で全財産失った彼が、今は15社経営の億万長者。最後に笑った。

words.japaneseMeaning: 分けて conquer する、分担する

examples.label: B
examples.text: My wife and I usually divide in concord the housework.

words.japaneseMeaning: ショック、驚き（当たり前のことも）

examples.label: B
examples.text: duh, of course the new YouTube app is the number one free app, not exactly a shocker.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 新YouTubeアプリが無料アプリ1位？当たり前の結果でしょ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: It was a nice cafe, but $15 for a cup of coffee was a bit of a shocker.
examples.japaneseTranslation: いいカフェだったけど、コーヒー1杯15ドルはちょっとショック。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Sorry, I didn't mean to spill the beans. I thought you'd already told Bill that you're quitting next week.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 秘密漏らすつもりなかった。来週辞めること、もうビルに言ったと思った。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Okay, spill the beans. Who are you with last night?
examples.japaneseTranslation: さあ、白状して。昨夜誰と一緒だった？

words.japaneseMeaning: お楽しみに、続報を待って

examples.label: B
examples.text: We're developing a brand new app right now. Stay tuned.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 新しいアプリ開発中。お楽しみに。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I've been speaking for a while now, so I'm going to leave it there. Stay tuned for further updates next week.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 長い話になったので、ここで終わり。来週の続報をお楽しみに。

examples.label: B
examples.text: There's no margin for air when you're making airplanes. That's why we only hire the cream of the crop.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 飛行機作りにミスの余地はない。だから最高の人材だけ雇う。

examples.label: C

examples.japaneseTranslation: 次のラウンド進出には勝利必須だったのに0-10で負けた。この段階でミスの余地はほぼゼロ。

words.japaneseMeaning: （組み合わせて）合わせる、ミックスする

examples.label: B
examples.text: These pants were an absolute steal. I also love how I can mix and match them with most of my other clothes.

examples.label: C
examples.text: I make some matches old school videos with futuristic socks I make something creative. Look, it's an absolute mess.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 古い動画と未来的なソックスを組み合わせて何か作った。完全にカオス。

examples.label: B

examples.japaneseTranslation: 最先端のトレーニング施設があるから、このチームに入ることにした。

examples.label: C

words.headwordAudio: vibe/267A take what one can get.wav

examples.text: This T-shirt is a bit too oversized for me, but this is the only one left so I'll take what I can get.

examples.label: C
examples.text: Oh, you don't have Capitino here? Well, I don't want to go in a wild goose chase for coffee, so I'll take what I can get.
examples.japaneseTranslation: カプチーノない？まあ、コーヒー探しに奔走するのも面倒。あるもので我慢する。

words.headword: what you see is what you get

examples.label: B
examples.text: Picture can be a bit high headed sometimes, but at least what you see is what you get.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ピクチャーは時々高飛車だけど、少なくとも見たままが全部。

examples.label: C

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜に）線引きする、限界を決める

examples.label: B
examples.text: People who enjoy their lives often find it difficult to draw the line between work and life. I thought it was the opposite.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 人生を楽しむ人は仕事と生活の線引きが難しい。逆だと思ってた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: He draws the line between business friends and real friends.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼はビジネス上の友人と本当の友人の線引きをはっきりしてる。

examples.label: B
examples.text: When it comes to fashion, Japan is ahead of the curve for sure.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ファッションに関しては、日本は確実に時代の先を行ってる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: No job is safe in the 21st century, so we all need to keep learning to say ahead of the curve.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 21世紀に安全な仕事はない。時代の先を行くために学び続けないと。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Advanced

examples.label: B
examples.text: You need to free up some time to lay the groundwork for your next big opportunity.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 次の大きなチャンスのために、基盤を築く時間を確保する必要がある。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Our company is currently laying the groundwork for more efficient operations.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 会社は今、より効率的な運営の基盤を築いている。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Create a Foundation.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 基盤を築く

words.japaneseMeaning: 少しずつ、ちょこちょこ

examples.label: B
examples.text: What's the deal with this? I've been losing Twitter followers and drips and drabs. Maybe I'm better off biting my tongue.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Twitterフォロワーが少しずつ減ってる。黙ってた方がいいかも。

examples.label: C
examples.text: The dad is getting sent to me in Drives and Drives right now, so I'll drip for you to you as I get it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: データが少しずつ送られてきてる。届き次第共有する。

examples.label: D
examples.text: and small sporadic amounts.

words.nativeDefinition: In small amounts over time.
words.etymology: in dribs and drabs（一滴一滴と）＝少量ずつ。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Can we push back the meeting by an hour or so?
examples.japaneseTranslation: ミーティング1時間くらい延期できる？

examples.label: C
examples.text: I need to get my wisdom teeth removed. I just stop pushing it back and get it over with.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 親知らず抜く必要がある。延ばし続けて、早く終わらせよう。

examples.label: D
examples.text: to lay something
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かを）延期する

words.nativeDefinition: To postpone something to a later time.
words.etymology: push back（後ろに押す）＝日程を延期する。

words.japaneseMeaning: （気分を）立て直す、切り替える

examples.label: B
examples.text: I've been on autopilot for about a year now, but I'm going to snap out of it soon. It's time to shake things up in my life.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 1年くらいオートパイロット状態。そろそろ切り替える。人生に変化を。

examples.label: C
examples.text: When I don't feel like getting out of bed, I snap out of it by playing my favorite song.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ベッドから出たくない時、好きな曲をかけて気分を切り替える。

examples.label: D
