words.headword: in the blink of an eye
words.headwordAudio: vibe/275A in the blink of an eye.wav
words.phonetic: ɪn ðə blɪŋk əv ən aɪ
words.japaneseMeaning: 瞬く間に、あっという間に

examples.label: B
examples.text: You should spend more time with your kids while they're still like hanging out with you. The grow up and the blink of an eye.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 子供と一緒にいられるうちに過ごしな。あっという間に大人になる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: It's beautiful outside today, but don't be fooled, Melbourne weather can change in the blink of an eye.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今日は外が綺麗だけど、メルボルンの天気は瞬く間に変わる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Very quickly.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 一瞬のうちに；あっという間に

words.nativeDefinition: Very quickly.
words.etymology: in the blink of an eye（瞬きの間に）＝非常に速く。

examples.label: B
examples.text: You should stick it out till you hit your stride. It's definitely worth it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 本調子になるまで粘れ。絶対に価値がある。

examples.label: C
examples.text: After my fifth cup of coffee, I finally hit my stride. I feel a bit jittery though.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 5杯目のコーヒーでようやく本調子。少しジッターするけど。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Begin to perform effectively after a slow start.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 調子に乗り始める；本調子に入る

words.nativeDefinition: To begin performing at one's best after a slow start.
words.etymology: hit one's stride（歩幅に入る）＝最適なペースに入る。

examples.label: B
examples.text: His attention to detail is great on the flip side he's sometimes stressed about stuff that's really not important.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 細部への注意力は素晴らしい。逆に言えば、重要じゃないことでストレス抱えてる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'd rather be by myself than hang out with people, but on the flip side I feel lonely sometimes.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 一人の方が好きだけど、逆に言えば時々寂しい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: On the other hand.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 一方では；反対に

words.nativeDefinition: On the other hand; looking at it differently.
words.etymology: on the flip side（裏側では）＝反対の側面から見ると。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/278A it's the thought that counts.wav
words.japaneseMeaning: 気持ちが大切、心配りが大事

examples.label: B
examples.text: I couldn't care less about what I get for Christmas. It's a thought that counts.
examples.japaneseTranslation: クリスマスに何もらうかどうでもいい。気持ちが大切。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Please don't buy me anything pricey for my birthday. It's a thought that counts.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 誕生日に高いもの買わないで。気持ちが大切。

examples.label: D
examples.text: It's the kindness behind the act that matters most.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 気持ちが大切；心遣いが重要だ

words.nativeDefinition: It's the thought and effort behind a gift that matters.
words.etymology: it's the thought that counts（考えが重要）＝気持ちが大切。

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜を）除外する、選択肢から外す

examples.label: B
examples.text: I want to take a trip overseas, but I don't want to roll out a stication either. Is there a way I can kill two birds with one stone?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 海外旅行したいけど、国内旅行も除外したくない。一石二鳥の方法ない？

examples.label: C
examples.text: I've ruled out moving to Europe. I'm thinking about Americans, dead.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ヨーロッパ移住は除外した。アメリカを考えてる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: exclude something from consideration.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かを）除外する；可能性を否定する

words.nativeDefinition: To exclude something from consideration.
words.etymology: rule out（除外する）＝可能性を消す。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm a late bloomer, my time will come soon.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 僕は大器晩成型。いずれ時が来る。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This player didn't start playing well until last season, some players are just late bloomers.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この選手、昨シーズンまで調子出なかった。遅咲きの選手もいる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Person who becomes successful later in life.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 大器晩成型の人

words.nativeDefinition: A person who achieves success later than expected.
words.etymology: late bloomer（遅く咲く花）＝後から開花する人。

examples.label: B
examples.text: You shouldn't buy a nice watch, straight off the bat when you start working.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 働き始めたら、いきなり高級時計買うべきじゃない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: She asked me about my salary straight off the bat on her first date. It was pretty full on.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 初デートでいきなり給料の話。かなりハードだった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: immediately
examples.japaneseTranslation: すぐに；最初から

words.nativeDefinition: Immediately; right from the start.
words.etymology: straight off the bat（バットから真っ直ぐ）＝最初からすぐに。

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜から）良いところだけ取る、都合のいいデータだけ選ぶ

examples.label: B
examples.text: This expert is really biased. He always cherry picks dad at his sport as a pinion.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この専門家、偏ってる。いつも自分のスポーツの都合のいいデータだけ選ぶ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I don't watch TV because the media tend to cherry pick things to show us. It's really sneaky.
examples.japaneseTranslation: TV見ない。メディアは見せたいものだけ選ぶ。本当に sneaky。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Select the most desirable part of something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かから）良いところだけ選ぶ

words.nativeDefinition: To select only the best or most favorable items.
words.etymology: cherry-pick（サクランボを選ぶ）＝都合の良いものだけ選ぶ。

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜に）何かある、何か引っかかる

examples.label: B
examples.text: There's something about this picture that makes me cringe. I'm not sure what it is though.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この写真、何かゾッとする。何か分からないけど。

examples.label: C
examples.text: There's something about this video. I don't know what, but there's something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この動画、何かある。何か分からないけど、何かある。

examples.label: D
examples.text: There's something strange about something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かに）何か特別なものがある

words.nativeDefinition: There is something special or appealing about something.
words.etymology: there's something about（〜について何かがある）＝説明できない魅力。

words.headword: have a whale of a time
words.headwordAudio: vibe/284A have a whale of a time.wav
words.phonetic: hæv ə weɪl əv ə taɪm

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm a big bookworm, so I always have a whale of a time at the library.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 本好きだから、図書館ではいつもめちゃくちゃ楽しんでる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I had a whale of a time on the ski trip. I think I had a lot to do with the friends that I went with.
examples.japaneseTranslation: スキー旅行、めちゃくちゃ楽しかった。一緒に行った友達のおかげ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Have a lot of fun.
examples.japaneseTranslation: とても楽しむ

words.nativeDefinition: To have a very enjoyable time.
words.etymology: have a whale of a time（クジラほどの時間）＝大いに楽しむ。

words.japaneseMeaning: （家に閉じこもって）ストレスが溜まる、キャビン・フィーバー

examples.label: B
examples.text: I thought drinking wine and watching Netflix at home all day was a good life, but I'm getting cabin fever already.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 一日中家でワイン飲んでNetflix見るのがいい人生だと思ってた。もうストレス溜まってきた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You're getting cabin fever? That's the first. I thought you loved spending time at home.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ストレス溜まってる？初めて聞いた。家にいるの好きだと思ってた。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Stress from being confined for a long time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 閉じ込められてストレスを感じる

words.nativeDefinition: Restlessness from being confined indoors too long.
words.etymology: cabin fever（小屋の熱）＝長期間の閉じこもりによるストレス。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm going to spend a few months getting the lay of the land before I start my side hustle. I just want to understand the market a bit better.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 副業始める前に、数ヶ月様子見して市場を理解したい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I don't want to hold off on starting my side hustle any longer. There's a huge demand for online yoga instructors right now.
examples.japaneseTranslation: もう副業を始めるのを待てない。オンラインヨガインストラクターの需要がすごい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Side job.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 副業

words.nativeDefinition: A secondary job in addition to one's main employment.
words.etymology: side（横）＋ hustle（必死に働く）＝本業以外の仕事。

words.japaneseMeaning: もっと自分の生活を持て、趣味を見つけろ

examples.label: B
examples.text: Stop criticizing people to make yourself feel better, get alive.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 自分を良くするために他人を批判するのやめろ。もっと自分の生活を持て。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I really think you need to get a life. All you've been doing is talking shop recently.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 本当にもっと自分の生活を持った方がいい。最近仕事の話ばかり。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Do more exciting things.
examples.japaneseTranslation: もっと充実した生活を送れ

words.nativeDefinition: Used to tell someone to get a more interesting life.
words.etymology: get a life（人生を手に入れろ）＝もっと充実した生活を。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'll have a word with Scott soonish, but not today. I like to prepare myself mentally before having awkward conversations.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 近いうちにスコットと話す。今日じゃない。気まずい会話の前に心の準備が必要。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I can't wait for the sequel. Do you think it's coming soon,ish?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 続編、近いうちに来ると思う？

examples.label: D
examples.text: in the near future.
examples.japaneseTranslation: そろそろ；近いうちに

words.nativeDefinition: Fairly soon; in the near future.
words.etymology: soon（すぐ）＋ -ish（〜っぽい）＝近いうちに。

words.japaneseMeaning: 二度見する、もう一度見る

examples.label: B
examples.text: I did a double date because that face rang a bell. I swear I know her.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 二度見した。あの顔、どこかで見た。知ってる気がする。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I did a double take when I saw my boss's pace lip, he's definitely overpaid.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 上司の給料、二度見した。明らかに過払い。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Take a second look.
examples.japaneseTranslation: もう一度見直す

words.nativeDefinition: To look again because something is surprising.
words.etymology: double take（二回見る）＝驚いて二度見する。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I heard the client decided to meet his halfway, what a turn of events.
examples.japaneseTranslation: クライアントが折衷案を受け入れたって聞いた。なんて展開。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I thought my boss wasn't happy with my performance, but he was singing my praises to me all day. What a great turn of events!
examples.japaneseTranslation: 上司は不満だと思ってたけど、一日中褒めてくれた。なんて展開！

examples.label: D
examples.text: Change in circumstances.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 事態の変化

words.nativeDefinition: An unexpected change in circumstances.
words.etymology: turn of events（事態の転換）＝予想外の展開。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm feeling a little blusk, so I'm going to take a nap.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ちょっとだるいから、昼寝する。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My girl's feeling blot today. I'm thinking of ways to cheer her up right now.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼女今日だるそう。元気づける方法考えてる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Feeling unwell or unmotivated.
examples.japaneseTranslation: だるい；やる気が出ない

words.nativeDefinition: Feeling unwell, tired, or unmotivated.
words.etymology: blah（だるい感じ）＝元気がない・モヤモヤした状態。

words.japaneseMeaning: （それ）やったよ、もう経験した

examples.label: B
examples.text: Icebreaker? Been there done that and sick of them.
examples.japaneseTranslation: アイスブレイカー？やったよ、もううんざり。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I think I have to pull an old knight or the office tonight, been there, done that.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今夜オフィスで徹夜か。やったよ、もう経験した。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I have experienced that before.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 経験済みだ；もう知ってる

words.nativeDefinition: I've experienced that before.
words.etymology: been there, done that（そこに行って、それをした）＝経験済み。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I heard that she passed the time counting the number of watermelon seeds all day. I'm at a loss for words.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 一日中スイカの種を数えて時間を潰してたって聞いた。言葉がない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I was worried that I won't know how to pass the time once I retire, but I decided that I'll cross that bridge when I get there.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 退職後の時間の潰し方が心配だったけど、その時が来たら考える。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Spend free time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 時間をつぶす

words.nativeDefinition: To spend time doing something while waiting.
words.etymology: pass the time（時間を過ごす）＝待ち時間を潰す。

words.japaneseMeaning: 終わったことは終わり、覆水盆に返らず

examples.label: B
examples.text: We lost the game but what's done is done. We can only move forward and try to win the next game.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 試合に負けたけど、終わったことは終わり。前に進んで次を勝とう。

examples.label: C
examples.text: We can't change the past. What's done is done. We can only change the future.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 過去は変えられない。終わったことは終わり。未来だけ変えられる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: What has happened cannot be changed.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 済んだことは仕方がない

words.nativeDefinition: What has happened cannot be changed.
words.etymology: what's done is done（終わったことは終わった）＝覆水盆に返らず。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/295A go through a rough patch.wav
words.japaneseMeaning: （困難な）時期を乗り越える、苦しい時期を経験する

examples.label: B
examples.text: I was going through a rough patch of work, but I started to see the light at the end of the town.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 仕事で苦しい時期を経験してたけど、光が見え始めた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm going through a rough patch in life right now, but I'll get through it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今人生で苦しい時期を経験してる。でも乗り越える。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Experience a period of hardship.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 困難な時期を経験する

words.nativeDefinition: To experience a difficult period.
words.etymology: rough patch（荒れた区間）＝困難な時期。

examples.label: B
examples.text: It's probably a long shot, but I'm going to try and quit smoking cold turkey.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 可能性は低いけど、タバコを突然やめるつもり。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I quit eating meat cold turkey. I'm officially a vegan now.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 肉を突然やめた。正式にヴィーガン。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Stop sending abruptly.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かを）突然やめる

words.nativeDefinition: To stop something abruptly without gradual reduction.
words.etymology: cold turkey（冷たい七面鳥）＝突然完全にやめる。

words.japaneseMeaning: （最高の）パフォーマンスを出す、本気を出す

examples.label: B
examples.text: You'll have to bring your A game this year if you want to be in the mix for promotion next year.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 来年昇進の候補に入りたいなら、今年は本気出さないと。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Binsom abroad is a game, scoring a hat trick.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ビンソン、本気出してハットトリック決めた。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Deliver once best performance.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 最高のパフォーマンスを出す

words.nativeDefinition: To perform at one's very best.
words.etymology: bring one's A game（A級のゲームを持ってくる）＝最高のパフォーマンス。

words.japaneseMeaning: 遅くてもやらないよりマシ、遅れても未着手よりはいい

examples.label: B
examples.text: I finally started to start my own YouTube channel, better late than ever.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ようやくYouTubeチャンネル始めた。遅くてもやらないよりマシ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: It took me three years to notice that I have a built in dishwasher in my apartment. Better late than ever, I guess.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 3年かけて気づいた。アパートにビルトイン食洗機がある。遅くても未発見よりマシ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: It is better to do something late than to never do it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 遅くてもやらないよりマシ

words.nativeDefinition: It's better to do something late than never.
words.etymology: better late than never（遅い方が決してないよりマシ）ということわざ。

words.japaneseMeaning: 期待しないで、当てにしないで

examples.label: B
examples.text: You're waiting for the government to come up with a silver bullet. Don't hold your breath, you shouldn't rely on it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 政府が銀の弾丸を出すのを待ってる？期待しないで。当てにしちゃダメ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I know you have a soft spot for Fiona, but don't hold your breath. There's so many hot guys around her.
examples.japaneseTranslation: フィオナに好意持ってるのは分かるけど、期待しないで。周りにイケメンだらけ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Don't expect it to happen.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 期待しないで；無理だよ

words.nativeDefinition: Don't expect it to happen.
words.etymology: don't hold your breath（息を止めるな）＝期待するな。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I used to feel invincible when I was young, but now I feel like a normal human.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 若い頃は無敵な気がしてた。今は普通の人間。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I know wearing a face mask doesn't make me invincible, but at least gives me a peace of mind.
examples.japaneseTranslation: マスク着けても無敵にはならないけど、少なくとも安心感はある。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Incapable of being conquered.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 無敵の；征服できない

words.nativeDefinition: Impossible to defeat or overcome.
words.etymology: in-（否定）＋ vincible（征服可能）＝征服できない。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I think a lot of women say no to the Amps because if you leave any wiggle room some crease will march right in and say something offensive.
examples.japaneseTranslation: DMを断る女性多いのは、少しでも余地を残すと変な男が入ってきて失礼なこと言うから。

examples.label: C
examples.text: You shouldn't drive a hard bargain for your salary. There won't be much wiggle from anyway, and you may burn your bridges too.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 給料で強気に交渉しすぎない方がいい。そもそも余地少ないし、橋を焼くかも。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Scope reflexibility.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 余地；融通の幅

words.nativeDefinition: Flexibility or room for adjustment.
words.etymology: wiggle（小刻みに動く）＋ room（余地）＝調整の余地。

words.headword: have a mind of its own
words.headwordAudio: vibe/302A have a mind of its own.wav
words.phonetic: hæv ə maɪnd əv ɪts oʊn
words.japaneseMeaning: （勝手に）動く、自分の意志がある

examples.label: B
examples.text: My Alexa has a mind of its own. She just randomly started reading me a poem yesterday. She freaks me out sometimes.
examples.japaneseTranslation: Alexa、勝手に動く。昨日突然詩を読み始めた。時々怖い。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My Siri has a mind of it's sounds she said, I'm sorry to hear that during a few and a rest of your day. Cut it out!
examples.japaneseTranslation: Siriも勝手に動く。「お気の毒に」って言った。やめて！

examples.label: D
examples.text: Operate unpredictably and independently of human control.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 勝手に動く；予測不能に動く

words.nativeDefinition: To operate independently and unpredictably.
words.etymology: have a mind of its own（独自の考えを持つ）＝人の制御外で動く。

examples.label: B
examples.text: How is the Apposterboy for Ignorance? Can you unpack your statement? He seems to know so much.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 無知の象徴ってどういう意味？詳しく説明して。彼、すごく知識あるように見える。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I just can't get my head around why she's a pushergo for intervals. She's so obviously an extrovert.
examples.japaneseTranslation: なんで彼女が内向型の象徴なのか分からない。明らかに外向的。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Very good example.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 典型例；象徴的な人

words.nativeDefinition: A person who is a perfect example of a type.
words.etymology: poster boy/girl（ポスターの男/女）＝広告の象徴的人物。

words.japaneseMeaning: （人に）順を追って説明する、丁寧に教える

examples.label: B
examples.text: This math question is pretty tricky. Can you please walk me through how you got to the right answer?
examples.japaneseTranslation: この数学の問題難しい。正解に至った過程を順を追って説明してくれる？

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'll walk you through how I made this video in the afternoon. On second thought, just tomorrow morning work for you instead.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この動画の作り方、午後説明する。…やっぱ明日の朝の方がいい？

examples.label: D
examples.text: methodically explain something to somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人に）丁寧に説明する

words.nativeDefinition: To explain something step by step to someone.
words.etymology: walk through（一歩ずつ歩く）＝段階的に説明する。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/305A sweep sth under the carpet.wav
words.japaneseMeaning: （問題を）隠蔽する、先送りにする

examples.label: B
examples.text: I have a hunch that the government is trying to sweep a lot of things under the carpet, but who knows?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 政府がいろいろ隠蔽しようとしてる気がする。誰にも分からないけど。

examples.label: C
examples.text: We shouldn't sweet the problem under the carpet. We should just get our story straight and explain what happened.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 問題を隠蔽しちゃダメ。話を整理して、何が起きたか説明しよう。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Hide something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かを）隠蔽する

words.nativeDefinition: To hide a problem rather than deal with it.
words.etymology: sweep under the carpet（カーペットの下に掃く）＝問題を隠す。

words.headword: there's more to sth than meets
words.headwordAudio: vibe/306A there's more to sth than meets.wav
words.phonetic: ðɛrz mɔr tɪ ˈsəmθɪŋ ðən mits
words.japaneseMeaning: （〜には）見た目以上のものがある

examples.label: B
examples.text: I know John seems boring, but there's more to end the meets the eye.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ジョン地味に見えるけど、見た目以上のものがある。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Being a goalkeeper looks easy, but I'm sure there's more to it than meets the eye.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ゴールキーパーは簡単そうに見えるけど、見た目以上のものがあるはず。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Something or somebody is more valuable, interesting, or complex, and they seem to be.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 見た目以上に価値がある；奥が深い

words.nativeDefinition: Something is more complex or valuable than it appears.
words.etymology: more than meets the eye（目に見える以上）＝見かけ以上の価値。

words.headword: bang one's head against the wal
words.headwordAudio: vibe/307A bang one's head against the wal.wav
words.phonetic: bæŋ wənz hɛd əˈgɛnst ðə wɔl
words.japaneseMeaning: （〜に）頭をぶつけ続ける、徒労に終わる

examples.label: B
examples.text: I spent hours trying to teach David how to drive, but I feel like I'm banging my head against the wall.
examples.japaneseTranslation: デイビッドに運転教えるのに何時間もかけた。壁に頭ぶつけてる気分。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Getting up is a great for meeting new people, but I'm banging my head against the wall when it comes to actually getting myself a girlfriend.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 合コンは出会いにいいけど、彼女作るのは壁に頭ぶつけてる感じ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Make no progress despite repeated effort.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 同じことを繰り返しても進展しない

words.nativeDefinition: To make no progress despite repeated effort.
words.etymology: bang one's head against the wall（壁に頭をぶつける）＝徒労。

words.japaneseMeaning: （痛みを）一気に終わらせる、長痛より短痛

examples.label: B
examples.text: Breakups are always hard, but if you don't love them anymore, you should just rip off the bandaid and move on.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 別れはいつも辛い。もう愛してないなら、一気に終わらせて前に進むべき。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Don't make things up to soften the blow. You should just rip out the band and tell them the truth.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 傷つかないようにごまかさないで。一気に真実を言うべき。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Resolve something difficult quickly.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 痛みを一気に受けて解決する

words.nativeDefinition: To resolve something painful quickly rather than prolonging it.
words.etymology: rip off the band-aid（絆創膏を一気にはがす）＝長痛より短痛。

words.japaneseMeaning: （矛盾した）メッセージ、行動と言葉の不一致

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm getting mixed messages from my ex. Do you think she wants to get back together or just be friends?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 元カノから矛盾したメッセージが来る。復縁したいの？友達のまま？

examples.label: C
examples.text: The flyers as the store opened today, but it's actually having a closing sale now. I'm getting mixed messages here.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今日オープンって書いてあるのに閉店セール中。矛盾したメッセージ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Contradictory Information
examples.japaneseTranslation: 矛盾した情報

words.nativeDefinition: Conflicting or contradictory messages.
words.etymology: mixed messages（混ざったメッセージ）＝矛盾した情報。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I don't want to weigh in on your argument, but I think Tina might be right. Your claim is pretty shaky.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 君の口論に口出ししたくないけど、ティナが正しいかも。君の主張、かなり弱い。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Do you think I'll see him pushy if I win with my opinion?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 意見述べたら押し付けがましく思われる？

examples.label: D
examples.text: Forceful Lake Intribute.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 意見を述べる；力強く発言する

words.nativeDefinition: To give one's opinion on a matter.
words.etymology: weigh in（計量に参加する）＝議論に意見を加える。

words.headword: know sth like the back of one's
words.headwordAudio: vibe/311A know sth like the back of one's.wav
words.phonetic: noʊ ˈsəmθɪŋ laɪk ðə bæk əv wənz
words.japaneseMeaning: （〜を）熟知している、手の平のように知っている

examples.label: B
examples.text: I know New York like the back of my hand. I live there for more than five years.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ニューヨークは手の平のように知ってる。5年以上住んでた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I know Roman history like the back of my hand. I studied it for a year at university.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ローマ史は手の平のように知ってる。大学で1年勉強した。

examples.label: D
examples.text: No something very well.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かを）熟知している

words.nativeDefinition: To know something extremely well.
words.etymology: like the back of one's hand（手の甲のように）＝非常によく知っている。

examples.label: B
examples.text: American football isn't for me, but the Super Bowl is a different kettle of fish.
examples.japaneseTranslation: アメフトは好きじゃないけど、スーパーボウルは別物。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Asian history is very interesting. It's a very different kettle of fish to the history of Europe.
examples.japaneseTranslation: アジア史は面白い。ヨーロッパ史とは全く別物。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Different things all together.
examples.japaneseTranslation: まったく別の話；別種のこと

words.nativeDefinition: A completely different situation or thing.
words.etymology: different kettle of fish（別の魚の入れ物）＝全く別の話。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Can you random end before he drinks anymore is going to say something stupid in front of our client again?
examples.japaneseTranslation: これ以上飲む前に彼を抑制して。またクライアントの前でバカなこと言う。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This guy keeps throwing shit at me and can someone rain him in?
examples.japaneseTranslation: この男、物投げてくる。誰か抑制して。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Control something or somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何か・人を）抑制する；制御する

words.nativeDefinition: To control or restrain someone or something.
words.etymology: rein in（手綱を引く）＝制御・抑制する。

words.japaneseMeaning: （厳格な）決まり、絶対的なルール

examples.label: B
examples.text: It's not a hard and fast rule, but you shouldn't wear white socks with black shoes.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 絶対的なルールじゃないけど、黒靴に白ソックスは避けるべき。

examples.label: C
examples.text: As a rule of thumb, when you're writing essays, you shouldn't make it up as you go along. It's not a hard and fast rule though.
examples.japaneseTranslation: エッセイは適当に書かない方がいい。絶対的なルールじゃないけど。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Smooth stick raw
examples.japaneseTranslation: 厳格なルール

words.nativeDefinition: A strict rule that must always be followed.
words.etymology: hard and fast（厳格で速い）＝厳格なルール。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'm going to blow ahead with this meeting even though Greg can't make it. He's sick so it can't be helped.
examples.japaneseTranslation: グレッグ来れないけど、会議は推進する。病気だから仕方ない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Many of us just plow ahead without understanding what we're working towards and I think that's very risky.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 多くの人が何に向かってるか分からずに突き進んでる。非常にリスキー。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Proceed with something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かを）押し進める

words.nativeDefinition: To proceed with something despite obstacles.
words.etymology: plough ahead（plow ahead）（耕しながら前進）＝障害があっても進む。

words.headword: you win some, you lose some
words.headwordAudio: vibe/316A you win some, you lose some.wav
words.phonetic: ju wɪn səm, ju luz səm
words.japaneseMeaning: 勝つ時もあれば負ける時もある

examples.label: B
examples.text: What's going on with my life? I can't believe it started raining just as I walked outside. You win some you lose some I guess
examples.japaneseTranslation: 外に出たら雨。人生、勝つ時も負ける時もある。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Can you believe she got caught Jay walking at midnight? You and some you lose some.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 真夜中にjaywalkingで捕まった？勝つ時も負ける時もある。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Unfortunately, things happen sometimes.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 勝つこともあれば負けることもある

words.nativeDefinition: You win some and lose some; that's life.
words.etymology: you win some, you lose some（勝つ時も負ける時も）＝人生の浮き沈み。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I think the situation in the middle is for reach boiling point any minute, but I'm hoping that things come down.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 中東の状況、もうすぐ限界点に達しそう。落ち着くことを願う。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My argument with my neighbor is about to reach boiling point, but I'm still cool as a cucumber because I know I'm right.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 隣人との口論、もうすぐ限界点。でも自分は正しいと分かってるから冷静。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Arrived at the point at which a situation becomes critical.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 限界点に達する

words.nativeDefinition: To reach a critical point where action is urgently needed.
words.etymology: reach boiling point（沸点に達する）＝限界点に達する。

words.headword: there's a method to sb's madnes
words.headwordAudio: vibe/318A there's a method to sb's madnes.wav
words.phonetic: ðɛrz ə ˈmɛθəd tɪ ˈsəmˌbɑdiz madnes
words.japaneseMeaning: （〜には）方法がある、理にかなった理由がある

examples.label: B
examples.text: It may seem like I'm just taking action for no reason, but there's a method to my madness.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 理由なく行動してるように見えるけど、俺には方法がある。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Being around successful people and learning from them is very important. There's always a method to their madness.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 成功者の周りにいて学ぶのは重要。彼らにはいつも方法がある。

examples.label: D
examples.text: There's a rationale behind somebody's actions.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人の）行動にも筋は通っている

words.nativeDefinition: There is a logical reason behind someone's seemingly irrational behavior.
words.etymology: method to one's madness（狂気の中の方法）＝一見無意味にも理屈がある。

words.headword: get back into the swing of thin
words.headwordAudio: vibe/319A get back into the swing of thin.wav
words.phonetic: gɪt bæk ˈɪntu ðə swɪŋ əv θɪn
words.japaneseMeaning: （再び）調子を取り戻す、リズムに戻る

examples.label: B
examples.text: I've been on vacation for a few weeks so it might take me a while to get back into swing things again.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 数週間休暇だったから、調子を取り戻すのに時間かかるかも。

examples.label: C
examples.text: The player has just come back from an injury so I think I'll need a bit more time to get back into the swing of things.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 選手は怪我から復帰したばかり。もう少し調子を取り戻す時間が必要。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Return to somebody's previous level.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 以前の調子に戻る

words.nativeDefinition: To return to one's previous level of activity or skill.
words.etymology: get back into the swing of things（物事のリズムに戻る）＝再び調子を取り戻す。

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜を）マイクロマネジメントする、細かく管理しすぎる

examples.label: B
examples.text: Jenny asked me for status updates every hour. I'm so sick of her micromanaging me.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ジェニー、毎時間進捗報告求めてくる。マイクロマネジメントにうんざり。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I try not to micromanage my kids homework because I really hated it when my parents did that to me.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 子供の宿題はマイクロマネジメントしない。親がそうされたのが嫌だった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Accessively monitor or control something or somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何か・人を）細かく管理しすぎる

words.nativeDefinition: To control every small detail excessively.
words.etymology: micro-manage（微細に管理）＝過度に細かく管理。

words.headword: wouldn't miss sth for the world
words.headwordAudio: vibe/321A wouldn't miss sth for the world.wav
words.phonetic: ˈwʊdənt mɪs ˈsəmθɪŋ fər ðə wərld
words.japaneseMeaning: （〜は）絶対に見逃さない、何があっても行く

examples.label: B
examples.text: Ariana Grande's concert? I would miss it for the world.
examples.japaneseTranslation: アリアナ・グランデのコンサート？絶対に見逃さない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I wouldn't miss your graduation for the world. I'll take leave one way or another.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 君の卒業式、絶対に見逃さない。休暇取る。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Definitely go to something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 絶対に見逃さない

words.nativeDefinition: Would definitely not miss something for anything.
words.etymology: wouldn't miss for the world（世界のためにも見逃さない）＝絶対に行く。

words.japaneseMeaning: （バランスを取る）難しい作業、綱渡り

examples.label: B
examples.text: Making good food in an affordable price is a balance in act that most restaurants need to master.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 美味しい料理を手頃な値段で提供するのは、レストランがマスターすべき綱渡り。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Finding time for work, life and family is a tough balancing act.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 仕事・生活・家族の時間を見つけるのは難しい綱渡り。

examples.label: D
examples.text: activity or action that needs a delicate balance.
examples.japaneseTranslation: バランスの取れた行動

words.nativeDefinition: An activity requiring careful balance of competing demands.
words.etymology: balancing act（バランスを取る行為）＝複数の要求のバランス。

words.headword: bring out the best in sb
words.headwordAudio: vibe/323A bring out the best in sb.wav
words.phonetic: brɪŋ aʊt ðə bɛst ɪn ˈsəmˌbɑdi
words.japaneseMeaning: （人の）良いところを引き出す

examples.label: B
examples.text: Given that the best of machines spires me to always shoot for the stars.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ベストの機械は常に星を目指す気持ちを引き出してくれる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This new coach is right out the best in him. He's playing with so much more confidence.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 新コーチが彼の良いところを引き出してる。自信を持ってプレーしてる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Hope somebody showed their best qualities.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人の）最高の一面を引き出す

words.nativeDefinition: To help someone show their best qualities.
words.etymology: bring out the best in（〜の最高を引き出す）＝良い面を引き出す。

words.japaneseMeaning: （注目を）独占する、一人占めする

examples.label: B
examples.text: Sam, stop hogging the spotlight and let somebody else speak for once.
examples.japaneseTranslation: サム、スポットライト独占やめて。他の人にも話させて。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I went to Disneyland yesterday and Mickey was hogging the spot like as always.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 昨日ディズニーランド行った。ミッキーが相変わらず注目独占。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Deliberately draw attention.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 注目を独占する

words.nativeDefinition: To monopolize attention.
words.etymology: hog the spotlight（スポットライトを独占）＝注目を独占。

words.headword: how long is a piece of string
words.headwordAudio: vibe/325A how long is a piece of string.wav
words.phonetic: haʊ lɔŋ ɪz ə pis əv strɪŋ
words.japaneseMeaning: （答えは）人それぞれ、定まらない

examples.label: B
examples.text: Simon asked me how many pairs of socks he should own such a strange question. How long's the piece of string?
examples.japaneseTranslation: ソックス何足持つべき？変な質問。答えは人それぞれ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I get this question quite often. How long does it take to build a website? How long to piece of string?
examples.japaneseTranslation: ウェブサイト作るのにどれくらいかかる？よく聞かれる。答えは人それぞれ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: I'm not sure.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 分からない；長さはいくらでもある

words.nativeDefinition: Used when a question has no definite answer.
words.etymology: how long is a piece of string（紐の長さは？）＝答えが定まらない質問。

words.japaneseMeaning: （180度）方向転換する、方針を変える

examples.label: B
examples.text: Sorry about doing a 180 at the 11th hour, but I actually think it's best that we include more graphics in this article.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 直前に180度方向転換してごめん。記事にもっとグラフィック入れた方がいいと思った。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My life did a complete 180 at the age of 50 so it's never too late to change your life.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 50歳で人生が完全に180度変わった。人生を変えるのに遅くない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Completely change.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 180度変わる；完全に方向転換する

words.nativeDefinition: To completely change direction or opinion.
words.etymology: do a one-eighty（180度回転）＝完全に方向転換。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Thanks for filling in for me, Esther. You really said my bacon.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 代わりに入ってくれてありがとう、エスター。本当に助かった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: He said my bacon so many times, but he always acts like it's not a big deal, so modest.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 何度も助けてくれるのに、大したことないフリ。控えめ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: risk somebody
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人を）窮地から救う

words.nativeDefinition: To save someone from trouble.
words.etymology: save one's bacon（ベーコンを救う）＝危機から救う。

words.headword: there are plenty of fish in the
words.headwordAudio: vibe/328A there are plenty of fish in the.wav
words.phonetic: ðɛr ər ˈplɛnti əv fɪʃ ɪn ðə
words.japaneseMeaning: 世の中には他にもたくさんいる（恋人）

examples.label: B
examples.text: My fiance got cold feet and dumped me right before the wedding. I was pretty upset, but there are plenty of fish in the sea, right?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 結婚前に逃げ出されて落ち込んだ。でも世の中には他にもたくさんいる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm sorry to hear you guys broke up. I know it seems like you was one and a million, but they're plenty fish in the sea.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 別れたの残念。唯一無二に思えたけど、他にもたくさんいる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: There are many other potential future partners.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 他にもたくさん候補がいる

words.nativeDefinition: There are many other potential partners.
words.etymology: plenty of fish in the sea（海には魚がたくさん）＝他にも選択肢がある。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/329A get out of sb's hair.wav
words.japaneseMeaning: （人から）離れて、うるさくしないで

examples.label: B
examples.text: The easiest way to get my kids out of my hair for a few hours is to put an eye pad in front of them.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 子供を数時間うるさくしない最善の方法は、iPadの前に置くこと。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My dad's been a backseat driver recently, he keeps nagging me about literally everything from like seriously just get out of my hair.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 父が最近バックシートドライバー。何でも文句。本当に離れて。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Stop being a nuisance to somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人の）邪魔をやめる

words.nativeDefinition: To stop bothering someone.
words.etymology: get out of one's hair（髪から出る）＝邪魔をやめる。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/330A underline the importance of sth.wav

examples.label: B
examples.text: I'd like to underline the importance of utilizing digital marketing and our advertising activities.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 広告活動でデジタルマーケティングの活用の重要性を強調したい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I really want to underline the importance of digging deep to create your own unique skills.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 独自のスキルを深く掘り下げる重要性を強調したい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Emphasize the importance of something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かの）重要性を強調する

words.nativeDefinition: To emphasize the importance of something.
words.etymology: underline（下線を引く）＝重要性を強調。

words.headword: two heads are better than one
words.headwordAudio: vibe/331A two heads are better than one.wav
words.phonetic: tu hɛdz ər ˈbɛtər ðən wən
words.japaneseMeaning: 二人の方が一人より良い、知恵を出し合え

examples.label: B
examples.text: Whenever I hit a roadblock, I try to ask somebody I trust for help. After all, two heads are better than one.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 行き詰まったら信頼できる人に助けを求める。結局、二人の方が一人より良い。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Two heads are better than one, except when I'm working with Paul. We are both chatty so we tend to go off track whenever we team up.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 二人の方が一人より良い。…ポールと組む時は例外。二人ともおしゃべりで脱線する。

examples.label: D
examples.text: It is easier for two people to solve a problem than for one person.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 二人の方が一人より良い

words.nativeDefinition: Two people working together is better than one.
words.etymology: two heads are better than one（二つの頭は一つより良い）ということわざ。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/332A have a few screws loose.wav
words.japaneseMeaning: （ちょっと）おかしい、ネジが緩んでる

examples.label: B
examples.text: Tom may seem like he has a few screws loose, but he's actually very sharp.
examples.japaneseTranslation: トム、ネジが少し緩んでるように見えるけど、実はすごく鋭い。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I heard that people who can take outside the box often have a few screws loose. God only knows I guess.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 枠にとらわれない人はネジが緩んでることが多いって聞いた。神のみぞ知る。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Big centric.
examples.japaneseTranslation: おかしい；常識はずれ

words.nativeDefinition: Slightly crazy or eccentric.
words.etymology: have a few screws loose（ネジがいくつか緩んでる）＝少しおかしい。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/333A put sb's mind at ease.wav

examples.label: B
examples.text: Traveling in the Philippines can be dangerous, so to put your mind in ease, we'll make sure we stick with you at all times.

examples.label: D
examples.text: Pawn to something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かを）熟考する

words.nativeDefinition: To think carefully about something.
words.etymology: mull over（じっくり考える）＝慎重に検討。

words.headword: the apple doesn't fall far from
words.headwordAudio: vibe/364A the apple doesn't fall far from.wav
words.phonetic: ðə ˈæpəl ˈdəzənt fɔl fɑr frəm
words.japaneseMeaning: 梨の落ちるところに近い、親の背を越す子なし

examples.label: B
examples.text: Just like his father, his fashion is always on point. I guess the apple doesn't fall for it in the tree.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 父親と同じくファッションセンス抜群。梨の落ちるところに近い。

examples.label: C
examples.text: His dad was a professional soccer player too, the Apple doesn't fall far from the tree.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 父もプロサッカー選手。梨の落ちるところに近い。

examples.label: D
examples.text: The child is similar to the parent.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 親似子

words.nativeDefinition: Children tend to resemble their parents.
words.etymology: the apple doesn't fall far from the tree（リンゴは木から遠くに落ちない）ということわざ。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/365A the apple of sb's eye.wav

examples.label: B
examples.text: Kyle always turns up to practice bright and early and motivates his teammates. He's the apple of my eye.
examples.japaneseTranslation: カイルはいつも早く来てチームを鼓舞する。目の中の玉。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I can stick my neck out for my daughter at any time because she's the apple of my eye.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 娘のためならいつでも首を突っ込む。目の中の玉だから。

examples.label: D
examples.text: The person that somebody cherishes most.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人の）最愛の人

words.nativeDefinition: The person someone loves most.
words.etymology: apple of one's eye（目のリンゴ）＝最も大切な人。

words.headword: you can't teach and old dog new
words.headwordAudio: vibe/366A you can't teach and old dog new.wav
words.phonetic: ju kænt tiʧ ənd oʊld dɔg nu
words.japaneseMeaning: 老いた犬に新しい芸は教えられない

examples.label: B
examples.text: I've been suggesting we go digital, but my boss still wants me to print everything. You can't teach an old dog new tricks.
examples.japaneseTranslation: デジタル化提案してるけど、上司は全部印刷したがる。老犬に新技。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Props for my grandma for learning how to use a computer, who said it can't teach an old dog new tricks.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 祖母がPC使えるようになった。老犬に新技と言われてたのに。

examples.label: D
examples.text: It is difficult to teach someone new things.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 年を取ると新しいことは学びにくい

words.nativeDefinition: It is difficult to teach older people new skills.
words.etymology: you can't teach an old dog new tricks（老犬に新技は教えられない）ということわざ。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Long story short, the old carrot and stick approach doesn't work for millennial employees.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 要するに、古いアメとムチはミレニアル社員には効かない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Our company's new caret and stick motivation approached and go down well.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 会社の新しいアメとムチの動機付け、あまり受け入れられてない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: reward and punishment.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ご褒美と罰

words.nativeDefinition: A system of reward and punishment to motivate.
words.etymology: carrot and stick（ニンジンと棒）＝報酬と罰の組み合わせ。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/368A have a high pain threshold.wav

examples.label: B
examples.text: This soldier was like, I've been shot, but it didn't hurt at all. He has such a high pain threshold.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この兵士「撃たれたけど全然痛くなかった」。痛みにすごく強い。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I know he has a high pain threshold, but that doesn't mean you can be hard on him all the time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 痛みに強いのは分かるけど、いつも厳しく当たっていいわけじゃない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Have a large tolerance for physical or emotional pain.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 痛みに強い

words.nativeDefinition: Able to endure a lot of physical or emotional pain.
words.etymology: pain threshold（痛みの閾値）＝痛みを我慢できる限界。

examples.label: B
examples.text: In light of its improved financial performance, I think it's a good idea to invest in this company while we still can.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 財務実績の改善を踏まえ、まだ投資できるうちにこの会社に投資すべき。

examples.label: C
examples.text: In light of this feedback, we need to make our exam marking cred here a more clear cut. The students don't really seem to understand it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このフィードバックを踏まえ、採点基準をもっとはっきりさせる必要がある。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Taking something into account.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かを）踏まえて；考慮して

words.nativeDefinition: Taking something into account; because of.
words.etymology: in light of（〜の光の中で）＝〜を考慮して。

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜と）歩調を合わせる、ペースを保つ

examples.label: B
examples.text: Your work is getting very sloppy. You just started, so don't worry about keeping pace with the others. Quality over quantity, okay?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 仕事がだらしなくなってる。始めたばかりだから、他の人と歩調合わせなくていい。質が量より重要。

examples.label: C
examples.text: In this sink or swim environment, it's important to make yourself stand out rather than to keep pace with others.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この環境では目立つ方が大事。他の人と歩調合わせるより。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Make progress at the same speed as something or somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かと）同じペースで進む

words.nativeDefinition: To progress at the same rate as something else.
words.etymology: keep pace with（〜とペースを保つ）＝同じ速度で進む。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Fred became a laughing stock at the office after the same bar kicked him out twice in one night.
examples.japaneseTranslation: フレッド、一晩でバーから2回追い出されて、オフィスの笑い者になった。

examples.label: C
examples.text: One spelling mistake can make you a laughing stock on Twitter so proofread before you tweet.
examples.japaneseTranslation: スペルミス1つでTwitterの笑い者になれる。ツイート前に校正しろ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Person subjected to ridicule.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 笑いものにされる人

words.nativeDefinition: A person who is ridiculed by others.
words.etymology: laughing stock（笑いの種）＝嘲笑の対象。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/372A take a leap of faith.wav
words.japaneseMeaning: （信念を持って）飛躍する、勇気を出す

examples.label: B
examples.text: She took a leap of faith and quit her job and went on to pursue her dreams. That made a big difference.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼女は信念を持って飛躍して、仕事辞めて夢を追った。大きな違いを生んだ。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I took a leap of faith and went on a blind date with this girl so far so good.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 信念を持って飛躍して、ブラインドデート行った。今のところ順調。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Take a risk.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 思い切って賭けてみる

words.nativeDefinition: To take a risk based on trust rather than certainty.
words.etymology: leap of faith（信念の飛躍）＝確信なく信じて飛び込む。

examples.label: B
examples.text: My blog is a mishmash of food, video games, music, friends, and things like that. It's almost like real life.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 俺のブログは食べ物、ゲーム、音楽、友達のごちゃ混ぜ。リアルライフみたい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Her fashion is a mismatch of tacky clothes, but it always feels consistent.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 彼女のファッション、ダサい服の寄せ集めだけど、いつも一貫してる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: App Hazard Collection
examples.japaneseTranslation: ごちゃ混ぜ；寄せ集め

words.nativeDefinition: A confused mixture of different things.
words.etymology: mishmash（ごちゃ混ぜ）＝混ざり合ったもの。

words.headword: put one's own spin on sth
words.headwordAudio: vibe/374A put one's own spin on sth.wav
words.phonetic: pʊt wənz oʊn spɪn ɔn ˈsəmθɪŋ
words.japaneseMeaning: （〜に）自分ならではの工夫を加える

examples.label: B
examples.text: I check out the work of other creators not to imitate them but to be inspired. This lets me put my own spin on my ideas.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 他のクリエイターの作品は真似じゃなく、インスピレーション。自分ならではの工夫を加えられる。

examples.label: C
examples.text: To put my own spin on it, I didn't use a recipe, but it was a bad idea for somebody who's just scratched the surface of cooking.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 自分ならではの工夫を加えたつもり。レシピ使わなかった。料理初心者には悪手だった。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Give something one's personality.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かに）自分らしさを加える

words.nativeDefinition: To add one's own personal style to something.
words.etymology: put one's own spin on（自分のスピンを加える）＝個性を加える。

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜を）詳しく説明する、はっきり言う

examples.label: B
examples.text: We have a company policy that spells out all the rules and stuff, but our company is so big that it's difficult to apply it across the board.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 会社のポリシーですべてのルールを詳しく説明してる。会社が大きすぎて全体適用が難しい。

examples.label: C
examples.text: This is the last straw. I've spelled out the directions to my house so many times, but he still manages to get lost every single time.
examples.japaneseTranslation: もう限界。家への道順、何度も詳しく説明したのに、毎回迷う。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Explain something clearly and in detail.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かを）明確に説明する

words.nativeDefinition: To explain something clearly and in detail.
words.etymology: spell out（綴り出す）＝一文字一文字説明する＝詳しく説明。

words.japaneseMeaning: （計画が）崩れる、車輪が外れる

examples.label: B
examples.text: The wheels have come off for Joker Vitch. What's happening?
examples.japaneseTranslation: ジョーカービッチ、何が起きてる？車輪が外れた？

examples.label: C
examples.text: The wheels came off pretty much from the start of the interview. I think I blew it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 面接、最初から車輪が外れた。ダメにした。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Things go wrong.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 事態が崩れる；うまくいかなくなる

words.nativeDefinition: Things start to go wrong; a plan falls apart.
words.etymology: the wheels come off（車輪が外れる）＝計画が崩壊する。

words.headword: get all one's ducks in a row
words.headwordAudio: vibe/377A get all one's ducks in a row.wav
words.phonetic: gɪt ɔl wənz dəks ɪn ə roʊ
words.japaneseMeaning: （すべてを）準備万端にする、整理整頓する

examples.label: B
examples.text: Your pronunciation isn't perfect? Don't worry. You don't have to get all your ducks in a row before you start to practice speaking.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 発音が完璧じゃなくていい。話し始める前に全部準備万端にする必要ない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm going to spend the day in the library getting all my ducks in a row for the exam tomorrow.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 明日の試験のために、一日図書館で全部準備万端にする。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Get oneself completely prepared.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 万全の準備を整える

words.nativeDefinition: To get everything organized and prepared.
words.etymology: get one's ducks in a row（アヒルを一列に並べる）＝準備万端にする。

words.headword: give sb a piece of one's mind
words.headwordAudio: vibe/378A give sb a piece of one's mind.wav
words.phonetic: gɪv ˈsəmˌbɑdi ə pis əv wənz maɪnd
words.japaneseMeaning: （人に）思いっきり文句を言う、かっこつける

examples.label: B
examples.text: I want to see this guy in person to give him a piece of my mind. He's trod my timeline all week.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 直接会って思いっきり文句言いたい。一週間タイムライン荒らしてきた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I'm going to get the store manager piece of my mind. I've been waiting for my dessert for over an hour.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 店長に思いっきり文句言う。デザート1時間以上待ってる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Speak angrily to somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （人に）思いっきり文句を言う

words.nativeDefinition: To angrily tell someone what one thinks of them.
words.etymology: give a piece of one's mind（心の一片を与える）＝激怒して意見を言う。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Why is our manager such a sacred cow? I'm going to call a spade to spade. He has no idea what he's doing.
examples.japaneseTranslation: なんでマネージャーは聖域なの？率直に言うけど、何やってるか分かってない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: The CEO's accessories are sacred cow at this company. Nobody even talks about it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: CEOの特権はこの会社の聖域。誰も話題にしない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Something that is unreasonably immune from criticism.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 批判できない聖域

words.nativeDefinition: Something immune from criticism or change.
words.etymology: sacred cow（神聖な牛）＝インドの聖牛から、批判されないもの。

words.japaneseMeaning: （〜から）手を引く、関わりを断つ

examples.label: B
examples.text: I quit smoking last year and I haven't even had a craving yet, I think I can completely wash my hands of it.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 去年禁煙して、渇望すらない。完全に手を引けた。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I wash my hands up in because there is such a negative person. Negativity is contagious.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ネガティブな人だから手を引いた。ネガティブは伝染する。

examples.label: D
examples.text: eliminate once involvement with something or somebody.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何か・人との）関わりを断つ

words.nativeDefinition: To refuse to be involved with something anymore.
words.etymology: wash one's hands of（手を洗う）＝関与を断つ（ピラトゥスから）。

examples.label: B
examples.text: If you want to ease into a new role at work, expectation management is very important. You don't want to put pressure on yourself.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 新しい役に慣れるなら、期待値管理が重要。自分にプレッシャーをかけたくない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Effective expectation management boils down to early communication and agreement about what a reasonable outcome looks like.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 効果的な期待値管理は、早期のコミュニケーションと合理的な成果の合意に帰着する。

examples.label: D
examples.text: proactive regulation of anticipated outcomes.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 期待値の管理

words.nativeDefinition: Proactively setting and adjusting expectations.
words.etymology: expectation（期待）＋ management（管理）＝期待値の調整。

words.japaneseMeaning: （雰囲気を）台無しにする人、湿っぽい人

examples.label: B
examples.text: As I was having biscuits, I overheard a high school girl saying, biscuits are for old people. What a wet blanket!
examples.japaneseTranslation: ビスケット食べてる時、高校生が「ビスケットは年寄り向け」って。台無し。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Sorry, I don't mean to be a web blanket, but I won't be able to come for a drink for an hour or two. I'm in the middle of something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 台無しにするつもりないけど、1〜2時間は飲みに行けない。今何かやってる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Someone that spoils other people's fun.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 興を削ぐ人

words.nativeDefinition: A person who spoils others' fun.
words.etymology: wet blanket（濡れた毛布）＝火を消すように雰囲気を台無しにする。

words.headword: put one's foot in one's mouth
words.headwordAudio: vibe/383A put one's foot in one's mouth.wav
words.phonetic: pʊt wənz fʊt ɪn wənz maʊθ

examples.label: B
examples.text: Can you please wordsmith my script a bit? I don't want to put my foot in my mouth during the presentation.
examples.japaneseTranslation: スクリプト、言い回し直してくれる？プレゼンで失言したくない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I thought I really put my foot in my mouth during that interview, but I ended up getting hired. I guess all's well then, as well.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 面接で失言したと思ったけど、採用された。結果オーライ。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Say something foolish.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 失言する；余計なことを言う

words.nativeDefinition: To say something embarrassing or inappropriate.
words.etymology: put one's foot in one's mouth（足を口に入れる）＝失言する。

words.japaneseMeaning: 有其父必有其子、親の背を見て子は育つ

examples.label: B
examples.text: They say like, rather like, son, but I don't want to go bald.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 有其父必有其子って言うけど、ハゲたくない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: My dad and grandpa are so full of themselves, like father like son.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 父も祖父も自己中。有其父必有其子。

examples.label: D
examples.text: A sun shows similar interest or behavior to his father's.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 父親似の息子

words.nativeDefinition: A son's behavior resembles his father's.
words.etymology: like father, like son（父のように、息子のように）ということわざ。

words.headword: take the bull by the horns
words.headwordAudio: vibe/385A take the bull by the horns.wav
words.phonetic: teɪk ðə bʊl baɪ ðə hɔrnz
words.japaneseMeaning: （困難に）正面から立ち向かう、思い切ってやる

examples.label: B
examples.text: I really look up to him. He always takes the bull by the horns and faces the challenge.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 本当に尊敬してる。いつも困難に正面から立ち向かって挑戦と向き合う。

examples.label: C
examples.text: I always admire how you take the bull by the horns and work on your weaknesses.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 弱点と向き合って正面から立ち向かう姿、いつも尊敬してる。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Deal courageously with the situation.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 困難に正面から立ち向かう

words.nativeDefinition: To confront a difficult situation with courage.
words.etymology: take the bull by the horns（雄牛の角を掴む）＝困難に勇敢に立ち向かう。

examples.label: B
examples.text: This actor is definitely a diamond in the rough. Once we train him up a bit, his career will go gangbusters.
examples.japaneseTranslation: この俳優、ダイヤの原石。育てれば、キャリアは大成功する。

examples.label: C
examples.text: The stock market is going gangbusters. What's going on?
examples.japaneseTranslation: 株市場、絶好調。何が起きてる？

examples.label: D
examples.text: succeed or do very well.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 大成功する；絶好調だ

words.nativeDefinition: To succeed or perform extremely well.
words.etymology: go gangbusters（ギャングバスターズのように）＝大成功する。

examples.label: B
examples.text: Ryan was pretty upset when his dog kicked the bucket last week. You just can't tell because he's putting on a brave face.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ライアンの犬が先週亡くなって、かなり落ち込んでる。表には出さないけど。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Talking about your grandpa kicking the bucket was a bit below the belt, my apologies.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 祖父が亡くなった話、少し言い過ぎだった。ごめん。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Die.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 死ぬ

words.nativeDefinition: To die (informal).
words.etymology: kick the bucket（バケツを蹴る）＝死ぬ（俗語）。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/388A blow sth out of proportion.wav
words.japaneseMeaning: （〜を）大げさにする、過大評価する

examples.label: B
examples.text: Stop blowing things out of proportion. A slight brown your toast is not the end of the world.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 大げさにするのやめて。トーストが少し焦げただけで世界の終わりじゃない。

examples.label: C
examples.text: The news is often blown out of proportion, so take it with a grain of salt.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ニュースはよく大げさにされる。話半分に聞け。

examples.label: D
examples.text: exaggerate something.
examples.japaneseTranslation: （何かを）大げさにする

words.nativeDefinition: To exaggerate the importance or severity of something.
words.etymology: blow out of proportion（比例から吹き飛ばす）＝大げさにする。

examples.label: B
examples.text: I've been running on fumes lately, so I'm going to sit back and relax tonight.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 最近ずっとヘトヘトだから、今夜はのんびりする。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Sorry, I dropped the ball today. I sometimes forget to do things when I'm running on fumes.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 今日ミスった。ヘトヘトの時、忘れることがある。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Exhaustil.
examples.japaneseTranslation: ギリギリまで消耗している

words.nativeDefinition: Extremely tired with almost no energy left.
words.etymology: running on fumes（煙で走る）＝燃料がほぼ空の状態。

words.japaneseMeaning: 隠れた名店・名作、知る人ぞ知る

examples.label: B
examples.text: This RPG is a hidden gem I played it so much as a kid.
examples.japaneseTranslation: このRPG、隠れた名作。子供の頃めちゃくちゃプレイした。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Finding hidden gems to eat at is my thing. I might try and turn it into a career down the road.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 隠れた名店を見つけるのが得意。いずれキャリアにしたい。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Great thing or place that not a lot of people know about.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 隠れた名所；知る人ぞ知る素晴らしいもの

words.nativeDefinition: Something excellent that is not well known.
words.etymology: hidden gem（隠れた宝石）＝知る人ぞ知る良いもの。

words.japaneseMeaning: （準備なしに）即興で、思いつきで

examples.label: B
examples.text: I don't want to make any comments off the cuff because I might shoot myself in the foot.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 即興でコメントしたくない。自分の足を引っ張るかも。

examples.label: C
examples.text: Can you give me the questions before the interview? I don't want to speak off the cuff.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 面接前に質問もらえる？即興で話したくない。

examples.label: D
examples.text: Without preparation.
examples.japaneseTranslation: 即興で；準備なしで

words.nativeDefinition: Without preparation; spontaneously.
words.etymology: off the cuff（袖口から）＝メモなしに即興で。

words.headwordAudio: vibe/392A put sth_sb on the map.wav
words.japaneseMeaning: （〜を）有名にする、地図に載せる

examples.label: B
examples.text: The Toyota 2000 GT put Japanese sports cars on the global map.
examples.japaneseTranslation: トヨタ2000GTが日本のスポーツカーを世界地図に載せた。

examples.text: Each year really puts the out on the map in Japan when I tell people I'm from Seattle they're all like, oh each year oh!

examples.label: D

examples.japaneseTranslation: （何か・人を）有名にする

words.nativeDefinition: To make something or someone famous.
words.etymology: put on the map（地図に載せる）＝有名にする。

words.headword: learn to walk before one can ru

words.phonetic: lərn tɪ wɔk ˌbiˈfɔr wən kən ˈɑˈrju
words.japaneseMeaning: 走る前に歩け、基礎から始めろ

examples.label: B
examples.text: I want to write an novel so badly, but I know I've got to learn to walk before I can run. I know how to read it, but I know how to write it.

examples.label: C

examples.label: D

examples.japaneseTranslation: 歩けるようになってから走れ

words.nativeDefinition: Learn basic skills before attempting advanced ones.
words.etymology: learn to walk before one can run（走る前に歩く）ということわざ。

words.japaneseMeaning: 絵に描いた餅、非現実的な夢

examples.label: B

examples.japaneseTranslation: ユニバーサルベーシックインカムは現実的な解決策？それとも絵に描いた餅？

examples.label: C

words.headword: put sth on the back burner
